title: 4am IRR - for the inner IRR nerd in you!
description:
Please, please, please.....Where are all of the Internal Rate of Return nerds?

This virtual session is for you!

Starting at 4am, that was not a typo (don't worry if that is too early, you can register for the replay).

Looking for like minded IRR enthusias that want to make history by attending the first event of New York Tech week. 

There's something special about being first that is oh so sweet. 

Running for only 30 minutes (so you can go back to sleep if needed) this session will cover 3 of the most important IRRs (there are over 15).

The IRRs covered are:
Modified IRR ✔️ 
Time Zero IRR ✔️ 
De-annualized IRR ✔️ 

Last year tech week I hosted the miseducation of IRR and that event was a blast so I'm looking to do things a little different and kick start tech week with the first event happening. 

For those of you not familiar with IRR, IRR or Internal Rate of Return is the famous (or infamous depending on who you talk to) calculation that is used to evaluate Venture Capital and Private Equity Returns.

Despite having it's weaknesses, the calculation is fraught with it's avid users in the Private Capital Markets.  

So grab your coffee, tea or matcha and attend the first event of New York tech week. 

Note a recap will be emailed to all those who cannot make it as some attendees are bound to oversleep given the time. 

The recap/replay will be available during the entire text week for you to enjoy.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

*Please allow 12-72 hours for approval.

title: Proof of Concept Fest: A Startup-Only Hackathon
description:
PoC Fest is the only hackathon at NYC Tech Week where winning teams walk away with letters of intent from sponsors, not just prize money. This is a one-day, application-gated event for early-stage founders and builders who want to ship something that actually matters, across any stack: AI, crypto, fintech, CPG, whatever you're building.

No panels. No fireside chats. No pitch tourists. Just a curated group of founders and engineers in a room, building something stupid-good with real commercial stakes on the line for the best teams.

Spots are limited and attendees are screened. Apply with your GitHub and LinkedIn; we'll confirm qualified builders on a rolling basis.

Judges include Alexander Law-Smith, a growth and marketplace operator who has scaled acquisition systems across 40+ markets, and Lu Yang, Founder & CEO of Luminvera and former Senior Manager of Digital Transformation at Bosch.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Central Park Morning Run
Agents Meet at New York 2026: Meet Your Next Partner
description:
Connect Smarter at Tech Week!

Have you ever attended a large event with hundreds—or even thousands—of people, and felt unsure about who you should talk to?

We're solving that problem.

Instead of leaving it all to chance, your personal AI agent will first connect with other participants' agents, helping you discover the most relevant people for you to meet. By the time you walk into the room, you'll already know who your best matches are.

Here's how it works:
Now – May 31, 6:00 PM → Sign-up is open. Create your Digital Twin by signing up and let it know what you're looking for at Tech Week.

Jun 1, 8:00 AM → Agents Meet begins. Your AI twin starts networking with other participants' agents—having conversations, finding common ground, and identifying your best matches while you go about your day.

Jun 1, 9:00 AM → Your twin wraps up and sends you a personalized email summarizing who you should meet and why, based on the conversations it had on your behalf.

Jun 1, 2:00 – 4:00 PM → Join us for an in-person networking session at a cafe in New York, where you can meet your top matches face-to-face.
We look forward to seeing you there!

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: How Do You Build a Borderless Tech Career in the AI Era? Hosted by Darshana and Get on Board
description:
Co-hosted by Darshana & Get on Board

What does it actually take to build a global tech career today?

Join Darshana and Get on Board for a practical conversation on international hiring, AI-driven recruitment, credential visibility, and the changing realities of global work.

We’ll share data, frameworks, and tools around how professionals can better navigate global opportunities and build stronger, more portable professional profiles in an increasingly AI-mediated job market.

Topics include:

— What companies actually look for in global talent
— How AI is changing hiring and candidate visibility
— Why credentials and verifiable experience matter more than ever
— Common barriers in cross-border hiring
— Strategies for building a globally competitive career profile

Open discussion + live Q&A for students, professionals, founders, and anyone thinking beyond local career paths.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the global tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Accelerate AI Summit: Australian x US AI
description:
Join American Australian Association, Austrade and Tech Council of Australia for a full-day summit bringing together founders, investors, and enterprise leaders at the forefront of AI — with a focus on the US–Australia technology corridor.

​The program features candid conversations across five themed sessions: breaking into enterprise sales, building strategic partnerships, AI readiness from a global perspective, infrastructure and the future of AI at scale, and accessing capital as an AI company. The day also includes a VC reverse pitching session, giving startups direct exposure to investors, and is bookended by networking over breakfast and lunch.

​Whether you're an Australian company scaling into the US market or an American firm looking to deepen ties with the Australian ecosystem, this is a rare opportunity to connect with a curated group of peers and decision-makers.

​Stay tuned for announcements on this year's agenda on our LinkedIn or Instagram. 

​Last year's summit featured:

​- Keynote on AI's opportunity for Australia, delivered by Accenture

- ​Panels on selling to American enterprise clients (Mastercard, NVIDIA, Accenture), accessing US capital (Intel Capital, Antler, Necessary Ventures), and AI strategy from a CEO perspective

​- Fireside chats with leaders from Blackstone, Xero, and Artefact USA on the state of AI in corporate America and the investor outlook

​- A session on Agentic AI with Agentic Scale AI and MyState Bank

​- Perspectives from the New York Economic Development Corporation and the United States Studies Centre on policy and the NYC advantage

​- An executive session with Goldman Sachs

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Maersk presents: the ultimate goal of full-stack supply chain transformation
description:
Join Maersk and partners for an intimate, invite-only breakfast and discussion on how global trade moves, the innovation behind it, and how you and your startup/team can be positioned to win. Maersk's innovation team is opening the door for a candid conversation on where supply chain technology is headed — the infrastructure being built, the bets being made, and what the next decade looks like for global logistics.

Expect sharp conversation, real perspectives from operators and investors, and no fluff.

Note: Recording is not allowed at the event.

Speakers include:
- Aruna Hussain, Global Head of Landside + CRM Product Development & Innovation @ Maersk
- Caroline Duffy, Partner @ Cowboy Ventures
- Raj Aggarwal, Founder & ex-GM GenAI at AWS

Moderated by:
- Naomi Goez, Partner @ Forum Ventures

What to expect:
8:30-9:00: arrive and mingle
9:00-9:45: panel and Q&A
9:45-10:30: open networking 

Food and drinks provided.

Space is limited.

Maersk is a global leader in integrated logistics, currently spearheading a digital revolution in the supply chain with a dedicated team of 100,000+ employees operating in more than 130 countries.

​​​​​​​Rock Yard Ventures is an early-stage venture capital firm backing exceptional founders redefining technology across core and foundational industries.

Forum Ventures is the leading early-stage fund, accelerator, and venture studio for B2B tech startups. Founded in 2014 and based in New York, San Francisco, and Toronto, we’re on a mission to make the zero-to-one journey easier, more accessible, and successful for founders. Forum is investing in 65+ companies this year.

Cowboy Ventures is a high-conviction, seed-stage venture capital firm based in the San Francisco Bay Area. Founded in 2012 by Aileen Lee, the veteran investor who famously coined the term "unicorn", the firm is known for its "founder-obsessed" philosophy and its focus on being a deeply hands-on partner during a startup's earliest days.

The event is sponsored by Stripe and Rho.

Stripe is a global financial infrastructure platform that helps businesses accept payments, facilitate multi-party payouts, manage revenue, and scale worldwide. Stripe’s software and APIs power payments, subscriptions, billing, fraud prevention, and global commerce, helping businesses grow faster and operate more efficiently around the world.

Rho is the all-in-one banking platform for startups. With banking set up in minutes, 2% cashback cards, and powerful tools for managing spend, paying bills, and closing the books, Rho helps high-growth startups move fast and stay focused—with support that goes to the ends of the earth to help you win.

Rho is a fintech company, not a bank. Checking and card services are provided by Webster Bank, N.A., member FDIC; savings account services are provided by American Deposit Management Co. and its partner banks.

description:

title: Raisi: Pre-Seed and Seed Fundraising Breakfast
description:
Walk into Tech Week knowing exactly how to raise and what to do next.

Seed & Pre-Seed Founders Breakfast
June 1 | Morning

Co-hosted by Raisi, Seed2Table, Day AI, and Forecastr

A curated breakfast for early-stage founders who are actively raising or about to.

This is a high-intent room focused on helping founders understand how investors are actually making decisions right now and what they should do next to increase their chances of getting traction.

Free catered breakfast included.

Featured Panel

Gregg Smith
Founder of Evolution VC Partners
360+ investments | 56 unicorns | 30+ exits

Andy Walsh
Host of Startups Decoded podcast
Serial entrepreneur with 2 successful exits

Christopher O'Donnell
Founder & CEO of Day AI
Recently closed a $20M Series A led by Sequoia
Former Chief Product Officer at HubSpot

What to expect

We’re bringing together seed and pre-seed founders alongside active investors, operators, and accelerator leaders for a focused, conversation-driven morning built around fundraising clarity and tactical execution.

How it works

Curated Roundtable Conversations

* What investors are responding to right now
* Why some companies get traction while others get ignored
* How founders should adjust positioning, narrative, and outreach

Live Discussion + Feedback

* Founders share where they are in their raise
* Investors and operators give direct feedback
* Open conversation across the room

Raisi Data Snapshot

* Key patterns from millions of live founder-investor interactions
* What’s actually working across outreach, meetings, and conversion right now

What you get

* A clear next step for your raise
* Access to a curated room of founders and investors worth knowing
* Free strategy session offered to attendees
* Entry into a raffle for a free month of Raisi for founders who book a follow-up

Who’s in the room

* Seed and pre-seed founders
* Active seed investors
* Accelerator and ecosystem leaders
* Operators actively working in venture and startup growth

This is the starting point for the week. You walk in early, get clarity, and carry that into every conversation that follows during Tech Week.

About Raisi

Raisi is a fundraising operating system built to help founders run their raise end to end. From identifying the right investors to executing outreach and managing conversations, Raisi replaces fragmented fundraising workflows with one integrated system.

Since 2024, once every 50.1 minutes, a founder has met with an investor interested in their company through Raisi. The platform has facilitated 21,000+ positive connections between founders and investors and is powered by up-to-date signals from millions of live interactions across the private markets.

About Day AI

Day AI is an AI-native relationship management platform designed to help founders, operators, and investors manage conversations, follow-ups, and workflows more intelligently. The company recently raised a $20M Series A led by Sequoia and is building tools that turn meetings and communication into actionable systems for teams and businesses.

About Seed2Table

Seed2Table is a founder and investor community platform focused on creating meaningful relationships across the startup ecosystem through curated experiences, conversations, and events. The organization brings together founders, operators, investors, and ecosystem leaders to create high-quality connections that extend beyond traditional networking.

About Forecastr

Forecastr helps founders build investor-ready financial models and projections. From early-stage planning to growth-stage forecasting, Forecastr gives you the clarity and structure investors expect, so you can walk into conversations with confidence and back up your story with real numbers.

title: Mercury Vinyl House
description:

June 1st, 2nd, and 3rd · 9 am–3 pm

Live Pitch Jam — June 1 @ 10:30 am

We'll wrap it all up with a Closing Brunch on June 3, 9–11 am, with a live DJ set.

title: Tech Trends Unleashed: Explore, Engage, Excel in Queens!

description:

Date: Monday, June 1st
Time: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM 
Location: Queens Chamber of Commerce – The Bulova Center 75-20 Astoria Boulevard, Suite 140 East Elmhurst NY 

New York Ideal for: Owners and decision-makers of Queens and Long Island based small to medium-sized businesses, typically with 10 to 500 employees, across industries like finance, construction, logistics, retail, hospitality, and non-profits. It's also ideal for IT professionals eager to explore how the latest in Managed IT, Network Services, Cloud Infrastructure and Management technology can drive down costs, increase security and efficiency in their operations.

Where Are You Spending NY Tech Week?

Stay in Queens and dive into the tech that powers our world—now with cutting-edge innovations! The Queens Chamber of Commerce invites you to Tech Trends Unleashed, an unmissable event spotlighting essential IT technology that drive businesses forward—all happening right here in Queens!

As highlighted in "The Phoenix Project," IT is as vital as electricity. But how are SMBs managing this crucial resource? How can they prevent IT sprawl and streamline their systems to embrace the latest AI advancements?

Join us for practical insights, expert tips, and advice on creating a streamlined IT setup that supports modern work environments, including remote work, AI applications, and 24/7 cybersecurity.

•	Managed IT
•	Cybersecurity
•	Effective Project Management
•	Network Services
•	Digital Data Management
•	Equipment & Hardware Solutions
•	Cloud Infrastructure & Serverless Computing

All presented by local vendors who understand what makes Queens unique and know which technologies help businesses thrive.

Event Highlights:
•	Tech Showcase & Networking (9:00 AM – 9:30 AM): Discover innovative solutions from eight leading local IT companies specializing in Managed IT, Cybersecurity, Cloud services, Network Services, and more.

•	Panel Discussion & Q&A (09:30 AM – 10:30 AM): The latest in fundamental IT Service: Leverage the latest in tech to reduce costs, increase security and increase customer and staff retention.  Gain insights from five industry experts as they share trends, practical tips, and essential information about the tech that is keeping your business operational and can help your business grow. title: Women Shaping the Future of AI
description:
Who actually holds the pen on AI decisions — and what does responsible deployment really look like from the inside? Join us for a cross sector panel exploring how women are shaping the next phase of AI across platforms, capital, safety, and public infrastructure. 
 
Melanie Jolson and Adine Mitrani from Fenwick will moderate the panel, including: 
•	Dr. Allison Mishkin, Child Safety + Wellbeing Product Policy Lead at OpenAI
•	Dr. Gal Noyman-Veksler, Partner at Lionbird Ventures
•	Stacey Matlen, Sr. Vice President of Innovation at Partnership for New York City
 
This discussion will explore how today’s leaders are navigating the gap between AI capability and responsible deployment, shaping decision-making and risk frameworks, and influencing the future of AI products, policy, and public infrastructure. The panel will also examine the trends and shifts likely to define the next 6–12 months across the AI ecosystem.

By confirming your attendance you agree to receive communications and updates from Fenwick.

title: Building Products That Scale Globally
description:
In an ecosystem often driven by hype, Payaza has focused on disciplined execution and institutional-grade infrastructure. The company is building a cross-border payments platform designed for long-term durability across markets.

Leadership:
Seyi Ebenezer (CEO & Co-Founder)
Seyi leads Payaza’s strategy, expansion, and regulatory engagement. His focus has been on positioning Payaza as a trusted financial infrastructure company, with emphasis on governance, compliance, and financial discipline over short-term growth narratives.

Philips Akinyele (CTO & Co-Founder)
Philips leads engineering and infrastructure development. With experience across FirstBank, GTBank, Access Bank, Andela, Bankly, and Payfi, he brings deep expertise in building scalable financial systems across banking and fintech environments.

At Payaza, he has led the design of a multi-channel payments ecosystem powering SME and enterprise transactions across multiple markets.


Key Milestones:
Expanded operations into 20+ countries across Africa, North America, Europe, and the Middle East

Enabled integrations with Visa and Mastercard

Built scalable APIs for collections, disbursements, and embedded finance

Supported a $35M commercial paper program and ₦20.3B ($13.5M) debt repayment

Achieved investment-grade credit recognition, including AA− upgrade and A− affirmation


Signal of Maturity
Recent credit rating upgrades reflect Payaza’s transition from growth-focused fintech metrics to institutional financial strength.

The company has received dual validation from leading African credit intelligence firms. DataPro upgraded Payaza from A to AA−, citing strong financial performance, governance quality, and sustained operational momentum. Intelligence Africa also assigned an A− rating, affirming its financial stability and credibility.

An AA− rating places Payaza among the highest-rated fintech companies in the region, signaling low credit risk, strong liquidity management, and reliability for enterprise and institutional partners.


Together, Seyi Ebenezer and Philips Akinyele represent a single thesis: building a durable cross-border financial infrastructure system emerging from Africa, designed to scale globally.

title: Who Gets to Shape AI? Why Girls Must Be in the Room
description:
Light refreshments will be served ☕ 🥐

 A discussion on AI’s impact on youth hosted at IBM with panelists Girls Who Code, UCLA, IBM, and Anthropic during NYC Tech Week.
Generative AI is quickly becoming embedded in school, work, and everyday life—yet many young people still lack meaningful access to learning what AI is, how it works, and how it shapes opportunity. 

This panel brings together industry and youth voices to explore findings from Girls Who Code + UCLA Computer Science Equity Project’s study investigating how teens and young adults—particularly girls and women—make sense of AI and its impacts on their identities, futures, and actions. 

IBM’s Director of AI Governance & Responsible Technology, Jamie VanDodick, will lead a discussion with Ryan Clarke of Girls Who Code, Jean Ryoo of UCLA, Trisha Bansal of Anthropic, Dr. Ronny Luss of IBM, and Columbia University student Anabel Kinsey to translate youth-informed research into actionable insights for responsible AI development, equitable AI education, and workforce readiness. Opening remarks will be made by Pamela Jacob, Corporate Social Responsibility Leader, North America, IBM.

Panelists:
Jamie VanDodick, Director, AI Governance & Responsible Technology, IBM (Moderator)
Trisha Bansal, Applied AI, Anthropic 
Ryan Clarke, VP of Research, Girls Who Code
Anabel Kinsey, Undergraduate Student, Columbia University
Dr. Ronny Luss, Research Scientist, IBM
Dr. Jean Ryoo, Director of Research of the Computer Science Equity Project, UCLA Center X

title: The Biotech & Deep Tech Pitch Clinic
description:
Only 1–2% of startup pitches secure funding. Yet VCs report that the vast majority of rejections aren't due to bad technology—they're due to flawed communication.

🚨 THIS IS AN EXCLUSIVE, PAID WORKSHOP
Your spot is NOT confirmed until payment is received via the Venmo link below. Unpaid RSVPs will be moved to the waitlist to open up spots for active founders.

In a sea of complex scientific, medical, and technical claims, the investor's brain is naturally skeptical. The ultimate differentiator isn’t your IP; it's how you pitch it. You must translate deep science into high-stakes commercial potential without losing scientific accuracy.

In this intensive 90-minute pitch clinic, neuroscience-based executive communication coach Jennifer Pawlitschek teaches you how to cut through the clinical data, build immediate neural authority, and anchor your scientific value proposition in the minds of high-stakes investors.

🚀 Kick Off Tech Week with Authority
Held on the first week of NY Tech Week prep, this clinic ensures you stand out when competition is at its peak. Master your narrative so you can pitch with total conviction at every mixer, panel, and demo day.

Key Takeaways:

The Pre-Clinic Neuro-Audit: Submit your slides ahead of time. Jennifer will personally review your deck beforehand to identify exactly where dense scientific data and clinical slides are losing the investor's brain.

The 60-Second Hook: Polish your opening minute to translate complex science into immediate commercial potential and capture the room.

Neuro-Presence & Delivery: Align your physical, vocal, and non-verbal cues to project executive presence, handle academic presentation habits, and trigger immediate investor trust.

Q&A Defense: Master the psychological frameworks needed to answer complex technical, clinical, and regulatory objections with absolute conviction.

What Other Founders Say:

"Jennifer helped us translate our complex clinical trial data into a commercial story VCs actually wanted to fund. She showed us how to respect the science while pitching the business."
— Dr. A.B., Co-Founder of Stealth Therapeutics Startup

"We used to lose investors by slide three with deep biochemistry jargon. Jennifer's Neuro-Audit completely streamlined our narrative. The difference in investor engagement was night and day."
— H.M., CEO of Gene Editing Platform

"Nailing the first 60 seconds was our biggest challenge as scientific founders. Jennifer's presence coaching gave me the physical and vocal authority to command the room instantly."
— T.R., Founder of Neurotech Medical Device Startup

🗺️ Two Ways to Secure Your Spot:

The Clinic ($50): Interactive group coaching. Strictly limited to 10 founders to ensure personalized feedback. Transfer your registration fee via Venmo immediately to @Jennifer-Pawlitschek (https://account.venmo.com/u/Jennifer-Pawlitschek) to secure your seat.

Private 1-on-1 Pitch Coaching ($300): Want dedicated, private eyes on your pitch deck instead? Book a 60-minute deep-dive session at our exclusive NY Tech Week rate here: https://partners.simply.coach/jennifer-pawlitschek/founder-pitch-coaching

title: Founders & Investors Brunch
Health Systems, Clinicians, & the Consumer Health Era
host: One Health Collective, Scalpels & Spreadsheets, NXGN
description:
A joint event with One Health Collective & NXGN, hosted by Scalpels & Spreadsheets.

Agenda:
Doors Open: 10.55AM
Session 1:  11.00 - 12.00PM
Session 2: 12.00PM - 1.00PM
Networking: 1.00 - 1.30PM 
_______________________________

Session One | One Health Collective (11-12)

Patients are no longer starting their healthcare journey inside the health system.

From wearables + labs, DTC care delivery, and AI-native care platforms, consumers are increasingly building their own front door to healthcare.

Health systems are responding, but the behavior shift is already underway.

- How do health systems view  the rise of consumer health, wearables, DTC labs, and AI guidance/doctors. 

- How forward-thinking health systems are rebuilding patient access, management, and engagement

- What healthcare organizations can’t afford to get wrong with these shifts underway

Speakers:

Dr. Devin Mann, Director of the Section of Digitally Enabled Care (SoDEC) at NYU Langone Health, leading cross-disciplinary teams across digital health research, operations, and innovation. He also directs FuturePractice, NYU Langone's internal tech innovation lab, and serves as Strategic Director of Digital Health Innovation within Health Informatics.

Daniel Marchalik, MD, MBA, Medical Director of Business Innovation and Executive Director of the MedStar Health Center for Wellbeing, leading venture, commercialization, and IP strategy at MedStar Health, the largest integrated health system in the Mid-Atlantic.

Holden Groves, MD, an anesthesiologist and critical care physician at Columbia University, where his clinical work spans the operating room and ICU. He also leads inpatient AI and mobile solutions at NewYork-Presbyterian, building the tools that bring emerging technology to the bedside across one of the country's largest health systems.

Co-Moderators:

John Joshua, RRT, MHA : Co- Founder, One Health Collective 

Addison Montiel, RN, MBA : Co- Founder, One Health Collective 
_______________________________

Session Two | NXGN (12-1)

Patients are arriving with lab panels, wearable data, GLP-1s, peptide protocols, and AI-assisted self-diagnosis already in hand.

What does that mean for the clinicians now treating them?

- Physicians on the front lines of consumer health and self-directed care

- GLP-1s, peptides, optimization culture, and the informed patient

- What medicine looks like when the patient journey starts before the clinic does

Speakers:

David Kessler, MD, MSc is the Vice Chair of Innovation and Professor of Pediatrics in Emergency Medicine at Columbia University, leads strategic cross-disciplinary initiatives focused on designing, testing, and implementing emerging technologies to improve healthcare training and clinical care. His work spans artificial intelligence, simulation, ultrasound, and immersive technologies. He has led multi-center research collaborations, authored over 200 peer-reviewed publications, and co-founded INSPIRE, an international network advancing innovation and scholarship in simulation science.

George Matthew, MD, MBA, FACP is a practicing Internal Medicine physician with over two decades of corporate and startup experience, including GE, Goldman Sachs, Pfizer, Medidata Solutions, Aetna, and served as the Chief Medical Officer of Hewlett Packard, later DXC Technology.  While practicing he also serves as the Chief Medical Officer of Times AI Health, a privacy-based health chatbot startup. 

Peter Rezkalla, MD, known to millions as "Doctor Disney," is a practicing pediatrician specializing in Pediatrics, Public Health, and Mental Health, as well as an award-winning child advocate spearheading the new era of health education for families and global communities. Leveraging his training in both medicine and musical theater, he transforms healthcare through creativity, tech, and story-driven science. With over 200 million views on social media, he is a top-trusted voice among healthcare leaders today.

Moderators:

Aakash Saraiya: Emergency Medicine Physician, Investor and Co-Chair of NXGN

Athena Yao: Biotech Operator and Co-Chair of NXGN

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: From Tissue to Treatment
description:
Artificial intelligence is transforming drug discovery and clinical medicine - but AI is only as powerful as the data underlying it. The next frontier is not bigger datasets: it is spatially resolved datasets. Spatial multi-omics technologies can now simultaneously capture gene expression, chromatin accessibility, DNA methylation, and protein abundance with precise X-Y tissue coordinates, creating a new class of tissue-level foundation models that transcend models based on bulk and single-cell data.

This panel brings together five leading scientists and technologists from Yale, Columbia, IAS Princeton, and KAUST to explore a pivotal question: How will spatial multi-omics reshape AI models for disease treatment across diverse ancestral backgrounds, and accelerate drug discovery from target identification to clinical validation?

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: How to Raise a Series A
host: Silicon Valley Bank, SSM, Pilot, TriNet
description:
Raising a Series A is a fundamentally different process than raising a seed round. Expectations shift—from vision to execution, from storytelling to metrics, and from promise to proof. This panel is designed for seed-stage founders who want a clear, realistic view of what it takes to successfully raise a Series A in today’s market.

You’ll hear directly from industry professionals as to what Series A VCs actually look for, how founders derail otherwise strong raises, and how to prepare months in advance—not weeks.

Target Audience:
- Seed-stage founders planning to raise a Series A in the next 6–18 months
- Founders who have product-market fit signals but want clarity on readiness
- CEOs navigating early traction, hiring, and go-to-market scale
Founders who want to avoid common Series A fundraising mistakes

Topics of Discussion:
- What “Series A ready” really means in 2026 (metrics, momentum, narrative)?
- What are the key diligence questions Series A investors focus on and why, including pre-raise legal and financial considerations?
- How Series A expectations differ from seed across product, GTM, and team?
-How does a founder run a tight Series A process (timing, data room, investor targeting)?
- Valuation, ownership, and control considerations founders often overlook when raising their first priced round.

title: Fighting deepfake in Age and Identity Verification
description:
Age and Identity verification is full of deepfakes. It is getting harder and harder to distinguish honest actors from fraudsters. 

The solution isn't that we use better AI tools to detect deepfakes - that is playing a losing battle. Rather we should be using authoritative sources and cryptography to verify user's identity. 

That is what Reclaim Protocol does (https://reclaimprotocol.org) - an open source verification system that uses zero knowledge proofs to verify users' identity. 

Come learn more, and discuss with others solving the same problem. Each participant will be hand picked, to maintain high density of like minded people. 

Looking forward to seeing you!

title: If Your AI Can’t Talk and Act, It’s Broken - Agentic Voice Workshop
description:
Build Your First AI Voice Agent
Artificial intelligence is rapidly changing how humans interact with technology, and voice agents are emerging as one of the most powerful interfaces for AI applications. This workshop introduces developers to the foundations of agentic voice systems and shows how to build intelligent voice agents capable of interacting with users in real time.

Sponsored by Cloudonix, this hands-on demonstration workshop explores how developers can combine modern AI models with real-time voice infrastructure to create practical applications such as AI receptionists, automated service agents, and conversational assistants.

During the session, participants will learn how to use Dograh, an open-source framework for building agentic voice systems, together with the programmable voice infrastructure provided by Cloudonix.

What You’ll Learn
Participants will be introduced to the core components of an AI voice agent, including:
• How agentic AI systems interact with voice interfaces
 • The architecture of modern AI voice bots
 • Connecting AI models to real-time voice communication
 • Building a functional voice agent using open tools
 • Rapid prototyping techniques for voice-based applications

The workshop will include live demonstrations showing how a developer can go from concept to a working voice agent using open-source tools and cloud communications APIs.

Who Should Attend
This workshop is designed for:
• University students studying computer science or AI
 • Recent graduates interested in AI startups
 • Developers exploring conversational AI
 • Entrepreneurs interested in voice-enabled applications

What Participants Receive
All registered participants will receive three months of free access to Cloudonix services, enabling them to continue building and experimenting with their own voice agents after the event.
The location will be provided to registered attendees.

Duration
3-hour

title: The Performance Protocol: Stay Sharp in the Age of AI
description:
AI is changing how we work faster than most people can adapt.

Entry-level hiring is down 35%, and the IMF estimates 60% of roles are highly exposed to AI. Companies like Ford, Salesforce, and Amazon are already forecasting major shifts in how work gets done.

The people who will succeed are not the ones who automate the most. They are the ones who can think clearly under pressure and make better decisions in uncertain environments.

That ability is not a personality trait. It is a trainable neurological state, and most high performers operate at only a fraction of it.

In this 60-minute workshop, you will learn a practical system for staying sharp as work continues to evolve, combining AI workforce research, performance neuroscience, and a live somatic protocol.

You’ll leave with:
- A clear understanding of how AI is actually impacting your career, not just the headlines
- A simple nervous system reset you can use before high-stakes decisions
- A personalized AI workflow, including what to automate, what to protect, and how to stay sharp daily

Who this is for:
Professionals, founders, and creatives navigating rapid change and looking to work smarter with AI without burning out or losing their edge.

title: The Six-Figure Mistakes International Founders Keep Making in the U.S.
description:
EVENT SUMMARY

You didn't move halfway across the world to get tripped up by Delaware vs New York and C-Corp vs. LLC decisions. Join Acclime and Herrick for a no-BS lunch session where legal, tax, and market-entry experts break down exactly what international founders get wrong in their first 12-24 months stateside—and how to fix it before it costs you a round.

FULL DESCRIPTION

The U.S. is the biggest market in the world, and the easiest place to make a six-figure mistake before you've closed your first customer or investment round.

Entity structuring that triggers double taxation. IP ownership gaps that spook investors. Hiring decisions that create liabilities you didn't know existed. Most international founders don't discover these landmines until they're already expensive to unwind.

This Lunch & Learn at Herrick's Murray Hill offices brings together the legal, tax, and cross-border specialists who clean up these messes daily—except this time, they're helping you avoid them entirely. The conversation is built around real scenarios founders face in months 1–24: where to incorporate, how to handle global IP, what U.S. tax exposure actually looks like, and how to structure governance when your team spans three countries.

Intimate format. Actual experts. Lunch is on us.

If you're an international founder building in or expanding to the U.S., this is the most valuable hour of your Tech Week.

About Herrick: Herrick, Feinstein LLP is a full‑service U.S. law firm advising founders, growth‑stage companies, investors, and funds on corporate, venture, M&A, real estate, and cross‑border matters. Herrick works closely with international companies expanding into the US, providing practical legal guidance from market entry through scale.

About Acclime: Acclime is a global corporate services and advisory firm helping international founders and growth companies expand into new markets. Acclime supports US market entry and scale‑up across entity setup, tax, accounting, compliance, payroll, and ongoing operational support.

About SAX: SAX is a multi-disciplinary accounting, tax, technology and advisory firm with nearly 70 years of experience supporting privately held companies, family-owned businesses, nonprofit organizations, and high-net-worth individuals.

Herrick, Acclime and SAX are members of the TAG Alliances a leading global alliance of independent law firms, accounting firms, and strategic advisors, enabling seamless cross‑border legal, tax, and business support across 110+ countries for internationally scaling companies and investors.

title: Generative Computing Masterclass: Building Predictable, Auditable AI with Mellea.
description:
Speaker: Paul Schweigert, AI Foundations Engineer, IBM Research

In this masterclass, we’ll introduce Mellea, an
open‑source framework that treats large language models,
not as chat partners, but as programmable components
inside real applications. You’ll see how AI behavior can be
broken into clear, composable steps using the Instruct–
Validate–Repair pattern, with structured outputs and
grounded retrieval built in. The payoff: predictable,
auditable, and reproducible AI. Combined with smaller
open‑weight models like IBM’s Granite models, developers
are seeing performant, trustworthy results at a fraction of
the cost. We’ll close the session with a real‑world case
study that turns a stack of construction PDFs into a fully
cited cost estimate and report.

This event is on a first come, first served basis. Please arrive early to claim your seat. Registration will open at 12:00 PM ET.

title: Agentic Voice Hackathon
description:
Agentic Voice Hackathon
Build the Next Generation of AI Voice Agents
Following the workshop, developers are invited to participate in an intensive Agentic Voice Hackathon, where teams will design and prototype innovative voice-powered AI agents.

Participants will use Cloudonix to build real-time voice applications capable of interacting with users, performing tasks, and integrating with modern AI systems.

Use Cloudonix with any of the following tools (or a combination of) to create something real and unique:
- Dograh
- Pipecat
- ⁠LiveKit
- ⁠ElevenLabs
- ⁠VAPI
- ⁠Retell
- ⁠Ultravox
- ⁠Dialog Flow
- ⁠Any LLM with Voice capabilities
- ⁠vCon Services

This short but high-energy hackathon is designed to encourage experimentation, creativity, and rapid prototyping in the emerging field of agentic voice technology.

The Challenge
Teams will build a working AI voice agent that demonstrates a useful or creative application of conversational AI. Possible examples include:
• AI customer service agents
 • Voice-based productivity assistants
 • AI receptionists or booking systems
 • Interactive voice games or educational tools
 • Voice interfaces for startups or services

We are looking for creative and innovative implementations in the following verticals:
- Real Time Voice Cloning for Contact Centers
- ⁠Real Time Multi-Party Transcription and Sentiment Analysis
- ⁠Real Time Multi Modal Sales Assistant
- ⁠Real Time Rejection Assistant

• Duration: 4 hours
• Teams: up to 3 participants

• Final demos presented to a panel of industry judges

Mentors will help teams navigate the technology stack, integrate voice systems with AI agents, and refine their prototypes.

Judging Criteria
Projects will be evaluated by industry experts based on:
• Creativity and originality
 • Technical implementation

 • Real-world usefulness or startup potential

Prizes
• Winning team members will receive Amazon gift cards
• All participants receive three months of free Cloudonix service to continue building their projects

Who Should Participate

title: Architecting a Borderless Life + Travel Giveaway
description:
If traveling or moving abroad has been on your mind, join this interactive session if you're aspiring-to-be a traveler or expat in tech.

Register & attend to win a travel giveaway in Spain or Morocco.

From 'wild Uber' to E.V. escapades, travel and life abroad can be filled with unknown surprises, puzzles to solve, and gifts that keep giving.

Travel can be a great way to "refuel" and particularly fulfilling for creatives, product people, profssionals in transition, and entrepreneurs. But it's important to figure out:
- when working remotely can work, when it can't
- how to travel both prepared and open to adventure
- what to consider if planning a permanent move

 
Come with questions and concerns and leave inspired to take the leap!

---

In 2025, a record-breaking number of Americans reported wanting to move abroad permanently (40% women, 19% men), and the U.S. experienced its first net negative migration since the 1930s.

Throughout 2026, Mary Beth Snodgrass, the founder and Managing Director of the PAC, has traveled abroad in Portugal, Spain and Morocco, exploring life as a 'senior' digital nomad. 

Check out a 4-minute reel of some of her 2026 travel photos: https://youtu.be/RNHww4xMiQ0

Previously, Mary Beth traveled to dozens of countries in Latin America, Africa, Asia and Europe and lived in Spain for a year.  

Taking cues from the audience on what topics to cover, Mary Beth will lead a Q&A and share research on why so many people and women want to leave the U.S. along with tips and tactics for months-long travels.

--

description:

This conversation goes beyond fundraising theory. We will explore what investors actually look for in healthtech and life sciences companies, including how to think about timing, building relationships, communicating your story, preparing for diligence, and avoiding common fundraising mistakes. 

From timing the market and building investor relationships to metrics, storytelling, diligence, and common fundraising mistakes, this conversation offers practical insight into what different-stage investors look for and how founders can position themselves for a successful raise.

title: Dubai is Building the Future. Come Join Us.
description:
The best cities don't just attract innovation. They accelerate it.
Meet the team at Dubai Future Foundation driving one of the world's most ambitious innovation agendas, led by Khalifa Al Qama, Dubai's Chief of Research, Development & Innovation.

Join us for an evening of real conversations — no pitch decks. Just the right people in a room, talking about what's next.

Great technology doesn't scale in a vacuum. It needs a city that's designed for it. Few places have a government that moves with the urgency of a founder, a vision that starts at the top and runs through every layer of the system, and the infrastructure, regulatory agility, talent, and ecosystem to make it all possible.

Are you building the next big thing in #AI, #HealthTech, or #GreenTech?

title: Why Investors Say No; What the Data Actually Shows
host: GoPresent, Office of Tomorrow, Simon Squibb, HelpBnk & 81 other investments
description:
The biggest venture quarter ever recorded just happened. $300B deployed. 81% of it into AI. And yet most early-stage founders raising right now are still getting passed on in the first meeting.

This isn't a panel about how to build a better slide deck. It's a conversation about the gap between what founders think investors want to hear and what actually drives an investment decision.

We're bringing together investors and founders who've been on both sides of that conversation to break down:
— What kills a deal in the first 5 minutes (and why founders rarely see it coming)
— How the Q1 2026 AI funding explosion is changing what "investable" looks like at Pre-Seed through Series B
— The specific patterns that separate founders who close rounds from founders who get "great meeting, we'll circle back"

This is a small-format fireside designed for honest conversation, not polished talking points. 30-50 seats. Founders raising now and investors actively deploying.

This event is a part of #NYCTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Beyond the Spec Masterclass: Engineering in the Age of Agents
description:
Host: Rynne Whitnah, IBM, Developer Advocate and Technical Lead, AI Ecosystem

We've spent years debating whether AI will replace developers. That's the wrong question. The right one: what does great engineering look like when AI handles the implementation?

This session digs into that shift with IBM Bob, an agentic coding IDE designed for developers who want to work with AI, not just alongside it. We'll walk through reasoning through complex tasks where human judgment still drives the outcome, and what the day-to-day of an agentic engineer looks like in practice.

Less hype, more hands-on. If you're figuring out how to evolve your practice as the tools around you change fast, this one's for you.

title: AI for Good Meetup
description:
New York Tech Week is loud, fast, and technical. Before you dive in, come find your people to help you navigate this week with social good in mind.

AI is reshaping every sector that touches social impact such as and not exclusive: climate, health, education, conservation, and capital. But the most important conversations aren't happening on stages. They're happening between the people doing the work: the researchers, founders, funders, advocates, and practitioners who are navigating real tradeoffs, in real communities, with real stakes.

This is an open, informal gathering at The Globe Bar to kick off Tech Week with intention. Come ready to talk about how we use AI and tech safely, responsibly, and sustainably  and what it means to build technology that actually serves people.

This is a meetup, there are no panels, but rather structured networking where you’ll break into groups of what you are interested in and meet people similar to collaborate and brainstorm with.

Who should come: Social impact enthusiasts. Climate tech builders and ocean conservationists. Nonprofit leaders. Impact investors and philanthropists. Global health advocates and humanitarian responders. Community organizers and policy thinkers. Anyone who wants to walk into Tech Week thinking about what technology should actually be for.

This event is a part of #NYCTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.


Hosts:
Paulina Muñoz is the author of Do Good Capital, a newsletter at the intersection of technology, investing, culture, and social impact, rooted in a deep belief that the financial system is one of the most powerful levers for social change. Her path from international policy to investment banking at HSBC and now to venture capital — where she spent time with Equal Opportunity Ventures as an investment fellow backing companies serving low-income and historically marginalized communities — has given her a rare, cross-sector view of how capital can be redirected toward the communities that need it most.

Angela Ng is the Director of Business Innovation at Carnegie Mellon University, where she has spent nearly five years leading sustainability, AI, and innovation programs across CMU's global campuses. With a decade of international development experience, spanning water and sanitation in Bangladesh and the DRC, maternal and child health in India, and disaster resilience across Africa and the Middle East, she brings a grounded, field-tested lens to emerging technology. She is passionate about responsible AI adoption in the social sector and is actively exploring how AI can drive climate action, ocean conservation, and equitable access at scale. She believes the most important work happens at the edges of disciplines, not the center of any one field. She is a Globethics AI Governance Fellow for 2026, focusing on AI ethics standards in Global South contexts.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: The builder’s table: AI finance leaders lunch with Vayu, Base44 & PwC
description:
Join Vayu, Base44, and PwC for a private, invite-only lunch with CFOs and finance leaders during NY Tech Week — focused on how modern finance teams are moving from waiting on systems and engineering teams to building, automating, and acting faster with AI.

AI is changing what finance teams can build, automate, and understand on their own. As revenue models become more usage-based and data becomes more fragmented across billing, CRM, ERP, and spreadsheets, finance leaders need faster ways to see what is moving, why it is moving, and what to do next.

Base44 will lead an interactive working session: “Build a Finance Superagent in 60 Seconds.” The audience will choose the focus — revenue movement, executive visibility, or cash — and see how a finance-focused AI superagent can be shaped live to answer executive-level questions across fragmented systems.

Vayu
Vayu provides revenue infrastructure for modern finance and operations teams, helping companies connect the path from selling to billing, revenue visibility, and business performance.

PwC
PwC brings a transformation and advisory perspective from working with leading enterprises on finance modernization, automation, operating models, and AI adoption.

Base44
Base44 enables teams to build production-ready applications and AI-powered workflows from natural-language prompts, helping operators turn ideas into working tools without waiting on engineering.

title: From Model to Market: The Future of Clinical AI in Healthcare
description:
As health systems race to modernize, startups are increasingly shaping how hospitals operate—from core technology infrastructure to AI‑enabled workflows and new care models. But adoption doesn’t happen in a vacuum. Legal, regulatory, procurement and operational realities all influence what makes it from pilot to scale.

Join us for a candid conversation with founders, health system leaders, investors and advisors working at the intersection of healthcare delivery and emerging technology. We’ll explore how hospital–startup partnerships are evolving, what health systems are actually looking for today, and how companies can navigate the complexities that come with selling into—and partnering with—large providers.

We’ll cover topics including:

- Where hospitals are investing in technology and AI infrastructure today
- How health systems evaluate, adopt, and scale startup solutions
- Key legal, regulatory, and business considerations that shape partnerships
- What differentiates startups that move beyond pilots from those that stall

title: A Brain Reset Experience
Japan Gateway for Climate & Deep Tech - Initiated by City of Yokohama
description:
Where will climate and deep tech actually scale—and with whom?
As AI-driven energy demand accelerates and geopolitical shifts reshape global supply chains, investor focus is moving toward one critical question: which markets, platforms, and partnerships can turn innovation into real-world deployment.
At the same time, cities and states across the U.S. are taking on a more decisive role in shaping the conditions for that deployment. Governors in 24 states—representing approximately 60% of the U.S. economy and 55% of its population—remain committed to climate action, while 46 states and more than 350 cities continue to advance local initiatives. Beyond federal policy, these subnational dynamics are becoming increasingly central.
In parallel, attention is turning to partners capable of supporting the transition from proof of concept to commercialization. Japan, with its deep industrial base, advanced manufacturing capabilities, and global corporate networks, is emerging as a critical partner in enabling large-scale deployment—particularly in sectors such as energy systems, infrastructure, and materials.
This event will convene investors from the U.S. and Japan, along with policymakers and industry leaders, to share insights on these trends from a U.S.–Japan investor perspective, and to explore where and how the next phase of scaling will take place through emerging opportunities for U.S.–Japan collaboration.

Overview
	Date: Monday, June 1st
	Time: Doors open at 2:30 PM. The session will take place from 3:00 PM to 6:00 PM, including a networking segment.
	Venue: Baker & McKenzie LLP (452 Fifth Avenue, New York, NY 10018)
	Format: Invitation-only (Up to 130 participants)
	Initiated by: City of Yokohama, Greennex Global, ShibuLA Ventures
	Venue Sponsored by: Baker & McKenzie LLP
	Tea Sponsored by: ITO EN
	In Partnership with: Deep Tech Forum

Program Detail（See: https://businessyokohama.com/blog/2026/04/29/japangatewayforclimatetech/)
1.	Introduction 

2.	Keynote: “How NYC is Building the Next Climate Tech Hubs"
• Nicole Spina, Vice President, Climate Innovation & Industry Development, New York City Economic Development Corporation ((NYCEDC) 

3.	Fireside Chat: Where to Scale Next: US–Japan Investor Perspectives on Climate & Deep Tech
<Speakers / Moderator>
• Masami Shibamoto, Senior Manager, JERA Americas
• Raluca Florea, Principal on the Investment Team, Decarbonization Partners
• Richard Bourgeois, Associate Director, Innovation Partnerships, NYSERDA
• Kevin Ninomiya, CEO & Co-Founder, ShibuLA Ventures (a moderator) 

4. Fireside Chat: The Manufacturing Backbone of Climate Innovation: Scaling Deep Tech Through Industry
<Speakers / Moderator>
• Cvic Innocent, Founder & General Partner, Frankenbuild Ventures
• Stacey Weissmiller, President & CEO, American Manufacturing Futures Institute
• Sabrina Sasaki, Partner, Monozukuri Ventures (Moderator)

5.	Startup Pitch
Selected startups participating in the pitch session include companies working across fusion energy, circular materials, thermal electrification, critical mineral recovery, and advanced sensing technologies. Several are backed by leading investors, strategic industrial partners, and U.S. federal programs.
• Realta Fusion — Compact Fusion Energy
A university spinout developing modular magnetic mirror fusion systems for industrial-scale deployment. The company achieved a world-record 17 Tesla magnetic field on confined plasma and is backed by a $36M Series A and U.S. DOE fusion program support.
• Evrnu — Circular Textile Materials
Commercializing regenerative lyocell fiber made from post-consumer cotton waste, capable of being recycled multiple times without quality degradation. The company has secured over $330M in purchase commitments from global apparel brands.
• MIMiC Systems — Next-Generation Heat Pumps
Developing solid-state heat pumps with no refrigerants and no moving parts, targeting major reductions in HVAC energy consumption and emissions through advanced thermoelectric systems.
• FAST Metals — Critical Mineral Recovery
Recovering rare earths and high-value minerals from industrial waste streams through a proprietary low-energy hydrometallurgical process. The company is backed by a major global mining player.
• Florrent — Grid-Scale Energy Storage
Developing next-generation ultracapacitors for renewable integration, EV charging infrastructure, and grid stabilization. The company’s proprietary low-cost energy storage technology targets critical power stability gaps that conventional batteries cannot efficiently address.
• Turnover Labs — CO2-to-Chemicals / Industrial Decarbonization
A startup developing on-site electrolysis systems that convert industrial CO2 emissions into high-value chemical building blocks such as plastics, solvents, and fuels. The company claims 500% longer operational lifetime and 40% better energy efficiency than conventional systems.

6.	Networking

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: AI Content Lab: Build an AI Workflow Where Brand Voice Actually Scales
description:
Most growth-stage teams are already using AI to create content. The problem isn't output. It's the rework, the inconsistency, and the brand drift that comes with scale.

This hands-on lab, led by AI workflow builder Nia Joseph and brand marketing expert Katy Lee, is built for founders ($1M–$10M raised) and GTM leaders who are past the "should we use AI?" question and stuck on "how do we make it actually work?"

Workshop Hosts:

- Nia Joseph
AI Product Leader at Microsoft
Founder of NexPost

- Katy Lee
Founder & Marketing Partner of Next Growth Wave
Ex HP, IKEA, Hershey's, Comcast

💡 What You’ll Learn
• Map your current workflow gaps
• Translate your brand guidelines into structured prompt systems
• Build a content process with built-in QA guardrails

In this workshop, you will leave with a content process ready to hand off to your team.

🧪 Workshop Format
A hands-on working session with real deliverables.

Bring your laptop and leave with clarity and results.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: AI Agents for Allocators and Asset Managers
description:
A hands-on session starting at 3pm ET for asset managers and institutional allocators building AI agents into their IR, RFP, and diligence workflows.

We'll go behind the scenes of a modern diligence AI agent - how it's designed, where it fails, and what's actually implementable today - from tagging content libraries and responding to DDQs for asset managers to orchestrating ODD, ESG, compliance, tax, and legal review for allocators.

Expect practical frameworks, real use cases, and a live demo you can take back to your team - ideal for teams looking for low-risk, high-impact ways to bring AI into due diligence.

This event is a part of NYC TechWeek - a week of events hosted by innovators to bring together the tech ecosystem.

title: Venture Point Legal Office Hours and Happy Hours
description:
Pull up a stool, grab a cocktail (or a cortado), and bring your hardest questions about startup law. Venture Point Legal partner Ellisha Blechynden is hosting an open Ask Me Anything session during New York Tech Week. Whether you're prepping for a seed round, navigating a Series A, or just trying to figure out what your lawyer is talking about, this is your chance to get real answers in a low-key setting.

On the table:
SAFEs, priced rounds, and everything in between
Cap table mechanics and dilution (the cocktail and non-cocktail kind)
Founder agreements, vesting, and co-founder dynamics
Delaware entity structuring
Investor negotiations and term sheet red flags
Whatever else you want to ask

The vibe: Small group, big questions. Cocktails, coffee, and non-alcoholic options on us. No panels, no slides, no pitch decks. Just conversation.

Who should come: Founders, operators thinking about starting something, and investors who want to talk shop.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: ERA/RV Portfolio Agentic AI Demo Showcase
description:
A Private Showcase by Entrepreneurs Roundtable Accelerator & Remarkable Ventures (Invitation Only)

Artificial intelligence is moving from models to systems.

The most important shift is not better outputs—it is autonomy. AI that doesn’t just assist, but acts.

This private showcase brings together a curated group of investors, operators, and enterprise decision-makers to experience how leading startups are deploying agentic AI in production environments today.

No slides. No abstractions. Live demos only.

What You’ll See

A selection of high-performing companies from ERA, Remarkable Ventures, and Remarkable Ventures Climate will demonstrate how agentic AI is transforming:

Enterprise workflows — automating complex, multi-step processes
Financial services — decisioning, underwriting, and execution
Healthcare & life sciences — intelligent coordination and analysis
Climate & energy systems — optimization, forecasting, and control
Sales, operations, and internal tools — AI agents embedded into daily work

Each demo is designed to answer one question:

What does this replace, and how much better is it?

Why Attend

This is not a typical demo day.

For investors: See where real product-market fit is emerging in agentic AI, and where it is not

For enterprise leaders: Identify deployable solutions that can drive immediate efficiency and advantage

For family offices and strategic buyers: Understand where durable value is forming as AI becomes operational infrastructure

You will leave with a clear view of which AI systems are actually working, not just being discussed.

Format

Curated set of live, product-driven demos
Short, high-signal presentations (no pitch decks)
Direct access to founders for follow-up conversations

Who Should Attend

Venture investors and growth equity firms
Family office principals and CIOs
Corporate innovation and strategy leaders
Operators responsible for deploying AI inside real businesses.

This event is in partnership with Zendesk.

Zendesk for Startups
Meet your favorite startup’s favorite CX solution. Trusted by more than 50,000 founders, Zendesk for Startups delivers world-class AI-powered support at no cost for up to 6 months. No developer? No problem. Zendesk works out of the box AND scales. Your data is kept safe and your customers are delighted. Plus, native, intelligent AI agents resolve over 80% of routine tickets, freeing your team to drive growth and innovation. Get started today and unlock exclusive discounts from Zendesk partners like AWS, Google Cloud, and more: https://tinyurl.com/5dhhp2as

title: The New Arsenal
description:

Join us for a moderated panel and networking reception spotlighting how venture capital is reshaping the dual-use and defense technology landscape. Our panelists span corporate venture, institutional venture, and crossover funds with deep experience across AI, autonomy, space, cyber, and reindustrialization. The conversation will explore how investors evaluate emerging dual-use technologies, navigate the federal market, and align capital with mission priorities.

Alex Poulin, Partner, Lavrock Ventures

title: The Bridge Ecosystem Forum: A Panel on Public-Private Collaborations by QUAY and Brown Rudnick
description:
The Bridge is a curated bi-monthly strategic forum where corporate innovation leaders and government officials align priorities, remove blockers, and build a thriving innovation economy together - converting ecosystem connections into regional growth. Every seat at the table belongs to a strategic thinker and ecosystem builder—a leader with a track record of getting things done who is focused on understanding collaboration models, how decisions get made, and designing shared investment to cultivate warm relationships for when opportunities naturally arise.

For Tech Week this year, we are excited to host a broader gathering bringing together corporate innovation leaders, government officials, investors and founders working on ventures aligned with The Bridge’s mission. The event will feature a panel conversation on How the Public and Private Sectors Can Effectively Collaborate to Drive Innovation, followed by a networking reception.

​Spots are limited so please register at your earliest convenience if you wish to attend. 

We look forward to seeing you there! 

Please note: by registering, you acknowledge that photos and videos may be taken by the event organizers.
​
This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

About QUAY Acceleration:
QUAY Acceleration leads a portfolio of over 900+ technology startups and SMB’s from across the world – the top 20% together have raised $1.18B and are worth $6.7B. Our firm designs, launches, and operates startup accelerators with government, corporate, and university institutions around the world. US and international economic development teams work with QUAY to generate regional and constituent business outcomes through cutting edge curriculum, connections, techniques and tools. We collaborate with a network of public and private partners to deliver customized programs for startups across the globe. Learn more at https://www.quay.co/  

About Brown Rudnick:
Brown Rudnick is a leading international law firm that provides exceptional, client-driven service in global litigation, crisis management (restructuring, investigations and special situations), brand and reputation management, life sciences and technology.  We advise on high-profile cross-border matters while providing the kind of personal attention to clients that is typically found at boutiques. Our enterprising and tenacious lawyers serve our clients around the globe from offices in key financial centers in the U.S. and the U.K., and have been recognized by, among others, Chambers USA, Legal 500, Benchmark Litigation and Law360 for excellence in their fields. Learn more at: https://brownrudnick.com/

title: Context Engineering 101 Masterclass: A Practical Introduction
description:
Speaker: Rosemary Wang, IBM, Developer Advocate and Infrastructure Engineer

Are you prompting AI systems but still not getting a useful response? In this session, we’ll dive into a technical introduction of context engineering for AI systems that can understand and respond to complex user interactions.

Learn how to design and implement effective context patterns, leverage knowledge retrieval and memory management techniques, optimize your AI’s performance using prompt engineering and tool calling.

Whether you’re using or building chatbots, virtual assistants, or other AI-powered applications, this session will give you the insights and practical skills you need to create more engaging, informative, and use-friendly experiences.

This event is on a first come, first served basis. Please arrive early to claim your seat. Registration will open at 2:30 PM ET. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

IBM may take group photos or perform live streaming during the event for promotional purposes.

title: From Vibe Coding to AI-Driven Development
description:
AI is radically changing how software is built. Tools can now generate prototypes in hours — sometimes minutes. But speed alone doesn’t create successful products.

​​​This side event explores the real difference between “vibe coding” — fast, AI-generated code driven by intuition — and AI-driven development with an expert in the loop, where AI accelerates delivery without sacrificing quality, security, or long-term value.

We’ll break down when rapid AI prototyping is good enough, when it becomes risky, and how founders, product leaders, and innovation teams can use AI responsibly to deliver MVPs that are not just fast — but fundable, scalable, and production-ready.

​​​What you’ll learn?

​​​Vibe Coding vs AI-Driven Development
Understand where AI-only coding works, where it fails, and why expert oversight is the difference between a demo and a real product.

​​​The hidden risks of fast AI-built MVPs
Learn why many AI-generated prototypes collapse under investor scrutiny due to poor architecture, security gaps, and technical debt.

​​​Expert-in-the-loop MVP delivery
See how experienced engineers and architects guide AI to ensure clean architecture, compliance, scalability, and knowledge retention.

​​​From idea to production roadmap
Get a practical blueprint for turning AI-assisted prototypes into systems that can survive real users, real traffic, and real growth.

​​​Who should attend?

​​​First-time startup founders
Learn why flashy demos rarely convince investors — and how to build an MVP that balances speed with credibility.

​​​Serial entrepreneurs
Discover how to reduce MVP cost and timelines without repeating mistakes that lead to fragile systems and lost ROI.

​​​Enterprises & innovation teams
Validate new product ideas quickly while keeping core engineering teams focused — and avoid creating future technical debt.

​​​Product & tech leaders
Understand how to integrate AI into your development process without losing control over quality, security, or strategy.

​​​Why attend?

​​​AI won’t replace engineering expertise — but teams that combine AI with the right experts will outperform those who rely on speed alone.

​​​This session is a practical, no-hype conversation about how modern teams build software responsibly in the age of AI — whether you’re launching your first startup or innovating inside a large organization.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Future of Visual Commerce with AI Agents
description:
Please note: Doors open at 4:00 PM. Space is limited and entry will be first come, first served. A valid ID will be required to enter the building. The fireside chat with Pauline Brown will begin promptly at 5:00 PM.

Visual content drives every purchase decision in e-commerce, yet the way brands create, communicate, and scale their visual identity is evolving quickly.

As AI and new digital tools reshape fashion and retail, the role of taste, aesthetics, brand intuition, and creative direction is becoming more important than ever.

This event will explore the future of visual commerce through two conversations: a fireside chat on aesthetic intelligence and brand intuition, followed by a panel discussion on how AI and technology are being applied across fashion and retail.

Event Schedule:

4:00–5:00 PM
Networking / Shopping

5:00–5:45 PM
Fireside Chat with Pauline Brown
Former Chair of LVMH USA | Adjunct Professor at Harvard & Columbia Business School | Author of Aesthetic Intelligence

Moderated by Zehra Soysal, founder of Savanah.ai, this conversation will explore the role of taste, aesthetics, and brand intuition in a technology-driven world. Pauline will share her perspective on why aesthetic intelligence still matters as AI and new digital tools reshape how brands create, communicate, and connect with customers.

5:45–6:30 PM
Panel Session
Featuring Maria Costa, Senior Director of Marketing at M.M.LaFleur and Anna Lombardo, Chief of Staff at Nordstrom

A discussion on how technology and AI are being applied across the retail and fashion industries, from creative workflows and content production to customer engagement, marketing, personalization, and operational efficiency. The panel will explore where innovation is already creating value and what the future of fashion and retail may look like.

6:30–8:00 PM
Enjoy light snacks and drinks while connecting with founders, operators, and brand leaders.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Rebuilding American Manufacturing [BVP, J.P. Morgan, Orrick]
host: Orrick, Bessemer Venture Partners, J.P. Morgan
description:
​American manufacturing is at an inflection point. Capital is flowing in at unprecedented scale, a new generation of vertically integrated operators is redefining how factories are built and run, and emerging technologies are reshaping what’s possible on the factory floor – all alongside accelerating defense demand and industrial policy.

​Hosted by Bessemer Venture Partners, J.P. Morgan and Orrick, this session brings together leaders across aerospace, defense and advanced manufacturing to explore a central question: who controls the factory of the future, and what will it take to build a resilient, scalable American industrial base?

​The program will open with remarks from sponsors with representation from JPMorgan’s $1.5 trillion Security & Resiliency Initiative (SRI), while the panel will feature founders, operators and investors actively building and financing the next generation of manufacturing systems.

​Program Agenda:

Opening remarks from:
- Max Cantor, Partner, Technology Companies Group, Orrick 
- Chris Wan, Vice President, Bessemer Venture Partners
- Justin Krauss, Managing Director, Security & Resiliency Initiative, J.P. Morgan

Panel discussion with:
- Moderator: Kristina Nilsson, Global Co-Head of AI Investment Banking, J.P. Morgan
- David Caputo, Co-Founder and CEO, Harmoni
- Jason Ray, Co-Founder and CEO, Paperless Parts
- Byron Boots, Co-Founder and CEO, Overland AI
- Ed Mehr, Co-Founder and CEO, Machina Labs

Audience Q&A

Reception

​Discussion Topics:
- ​The gap between capital deployment and operational change
- Vertical integration: where it works and where it breaks
- The role of software and AI in coordinating the modern factory
- Defense demand and policy pressure on domestic production
- Building new capacity vs. modernizing existing infrastructure

​Audience
​Investors, founders, and executives working across manufacturing, defense and industrial technology.

---

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Unlocking Entrepreneurship in Travel through Humans x Technology
description:
Fora Travel is powering the next generation of travel entrepreneurs. Join us at one of the best spots in the city for an insider conversation on how we're building the team, tools, and culture to thrive in an AI-forward world.

In a panel with Jake Peters (Co-Founder & Chief Product/Technology Officer) and one of our advisors, we'll explore the art of finding the right balance between human expertise and technology to deliver the best outcomes for every stakeholder: travelers, advisors and partners.

Backed by leading VCs including Thrive, Insight, Forerunner, and Heartcore, Fora has earned recognition across both the tech and travel industries and was recently voted a top employer on Built In. We're growing fast, and we're investing in the people who will shape what comes next.
