title: Global Expansion & Cross-Border Investing
description:
A curated gathering focused on fundraising, scaling companies, and deploying capital across borders.

Hosted by Yorkseed, with the introduction of Valois — our private relationship and capital layer designed for high-trust connections within the global venture ecosystem.

This session brings together founders, investors, and operators navigating international markets — from entering new regions and raising globally to structuring cross-border partnerships and investments.

The conversation will explore:
→ Cross-border fundraising and capital strategy
→ Expanding into new markets and ecosystems
→ Building globally from day one
→ The realities of operating across multiple regions
→ Long-term relationship building between founders and capital allocators

More importantly, this is intentionally designed as a high-signal room. The focus is not mass networking, but meaningful conversations between people actively building, investing, and moving capital internationally.

We are also soft-launching Yorkseed during NY Tech Week.

Yorkseed is a curated global venture network and operating layer for founders, investors, family offices, and institutional partners. Beyond events, Yorkseed is designed to help members build long-term strategic relationships, access curated deal flow, navigate fundraising more intelligently, and operate within a trusted global ecosystem.

The platform includes:
→ A vetted global member network across founders and investors
→ Curated introductions and investor matching
→ Deal flow and syndicate infrastructure
→ AI-powered diligence and fundraising intelligence tools
→ Founder and investor operating hubs designed for real execution, not vanity networking

Approved Yorkseed members receive priority access to select gatherings, curated introductions, platform features, and future opportunities across the network.

To maintain the quality of the ecosystem, applications go through a review and due diligence process to ensure strong alignment across founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders.

Apply via app.yorkseed.co

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the global tech ecosystem.

title: Cofounder Matchmaking
description:
What if founders could identify compatibility before building a company together?

One of the leading reasons startups fail is cofounder incompatibility yet most entrepreneurs don’t discover misalignment until stress, growth, and decision-making pressure begin impacting the business.

That’s why Relationality and DefinedTalent’s visionary impact are coming together for a special networking and launch event focused on redefining how we build teams, partnerships, leadership, and culture from the very beginning.

During this collaborative event, DefinedTalent will officially introduce the ReDefine T.H.I.S. framework a new people-centered approach built around:
Talent. Human Resources. Innovation. Strategy.

Alongside the launch of Relationality where attendees will experience the power of of the company’s groundbreaking compatibility platform, designed to identify decision-making styles and predict long-term working compatibility between founders, collaborators, and leadership teams.

As part of the experience, participants will receive FREE access to the Relationality Test prior to the event giving attendees the unique opportunity to better understand themselves and immediately identify whether a potential cofounder, collaborator, or strategic connection they meet at the event may truly be aligned…or not.

This is more than a networking event.
It’s a conversation around alignment, leadership, culture, innovation, and building relationships that actually last.

Whether you’re a founder, entrepreneur, leader, creative, investor, or community builder — this event is designed to help you connect with intention, purpose, and compatibility.

Come network.
Come connect.
Come ReDefine what alignment can look like in business and beyond.

Learn more at www.relationalityforwork.com and www.definedtalent.com

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: From the Hudson to the Thames: Exploring International Expansion
host: British Consulate, Osborne Clarke, Blick Rothenberg, Eisner Advisory Group LLC
description:
Request to join us on Monday 1 June (4–6pm) for an exclusive panel bringing together founders, operators, and advisors to unpack the realities of scaling across the US–UK corridor. The discussion will cut through the noise on international expansion with a dive into market entry strategy, product and operations localization, regulatory and data considerations, plus the culture factors that make cross border growth work.

Featuring a headline speaker from Duolingo and advisors with deep experience supporting venture backed companies, the session offers grounded, actionable insight for teams navigating transatlantic growth.

Hosted by EisnerAmper, Blick Rothenberg, Osborne Clarke, and the British Consulate General New York’s Tech Team, the event convenes a strong network of operators and specialists across the ecosystem.

A networking reception will follow, connecting founders, investors, and advisors active in the US–UK tech landscape.

Location will be shared with approved attendees.  Please note - entry will not be permitted unless attendees have registered in advance.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Fashion Tech Panel Event @ FIT
description:
This event is part of a series: New York (Fashion) Tech Week - Presented by The Pattern Maker. https://thepatternmaker.substack.com/

See the full list of fashion tech events here: https://tinyurl.com/NYFTW

-------
For the second year running, The Pattern Maker is partnering with the Fashion Institute of Technology to bring together the brands, builders, and innovators shaping fashion tech's next chapter and this year's lineup is not one to miss.

We've put together two panels getting at the most top of mind topics for fashion leaders and fashion tech founders, plus a tech talk to dive deep on how the technology reshaping fashion production actually works. 

Panel 1 — Beyond the Headlines: Why Getting AI Right for Fashion Is Harder Than It Looks and the Founders Fixing It

Founders and AI experts enabling AI across the fashion value chain will dig into what it really takes to build with this technology in a way that actually solves real problems, why the gap between the hype and the reality is wider than most people think, and what the path forward looks like for fashion brands and builders alike.

For full details about each panel and panelist, check out our latest post: https://thepatternmaker.substack.com/p/free-fashion-tech-panels-and-demo

Moderated by Rachel Sterling of The Pattern Maker, featuring:
✦ Franz Tschimben, Co-Founder & CEO, ALLSIDES
✦ Yusan Lin, Founder & CEO, Mirror Mirror AI
✦ Sreya Halder, Founder & CEO, The Mall
✦ Sophia Sterling, Founder & CEO, Paprika (Stealth)

Tech Talk, presented by Make the Dot

Between our two panels, Emilie Ho, Co-Founder & CEO of Make the Dot, takes us behind the scenes on how they've rethought the technology supporting every step of fashion production and beyond. 

Panel 2 — Fit Check: What It Actually Takes for Fashion and Tech to Partner Successfully

Getting to the heart of what every founder and fashion leader wants to know: What should fashion brands look for when choosing a tech partner? How do you stay ahead of constantly shifting technology? And what do fashion tech companies need to understand to land and keep major partnerships?

Moderated by Michael Ferraro, Executive Director of the FIT DTech Lab, featuring:
✦ Corey Moran, CMO, Michael Kors
✦ Diarra Bousso, Founder & Designer, DIARRABLU
✦ Emily Erkel Erkle, Co-Founder, LePrix

Doors open at 3:30 PM · Panels 4–6 PM · Open networking 6–7 PM

--------
This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Causal Foresight with Anthos: Navigate Uncertainty with Better Decision-Making.
description:
​Leaders are regularly asked to make high-stakes decisions: where to invest, which interventions to prioritize, and how to allocate limited resources for the greatest impact. And these decisions only grow harder as systems become more deeply intertwined, consequences less predictable, and the stakes higher. Yet the tools we rely on weren't built for this. Large language models and conventional ML share a critical blind spot: they can't predict how a system will respond to an intervention it has never encountered before. To navigate today's complexity, we need tools that can reason beyond historical precedent and anticipate the downstream effects of the choices we make.

Anthos is a simulation platform that lets investors, philanthropies, and policymakers stress-test decisions before they commit capital or sign policy. It ingests the relevant literature, then applies neuro-symbolic causal inference to model the second- and third-order consequences spreadsheets and standard forecasting tend to miss. Non-technical users can run "what-if" scenarios through conversational AI and quickly see whether an investment thesis or policy proposal holds up under pressure.

Be among the first to experience Anthos. In this intimate, hands-on workshop, founders Devin Young (CEO) and DJ Passey (CTO) — joined by advisor C. Robin Castelli, MSc (former Global Head of Climate Modeling Analytics at Citi) and World Bank design partner Jasmin Sadegh — will run live model demonstrations. Then you'll have the chance to build your own causal models in Anthos, guided by our AI agents.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

description:

The Beauty of Infrastructure is an industry conversation during NYC Tech Week about the guest-and-resident-experience gap — and the operators, investors, developers, and builders who are already closing it.

Beauty, wellness, and personal services used to exist outside the building. They are moving inside it. The properties winning on loyalty, repeat bookings, and revenue per guest have figured out that the experience between check-in and checkout is the product. This panel is about what it takes to build that — and what the business case actually looks like when you do.

We're hosting this conversation where it belongs — on the rooftop terrace at Mr Purple, Hotel Indigo LES. The venue makes the argument before anyone sits down.

The evening includes a panel on the rooftop spanning hospitality, real estate, and technology, followed by a live Beauty Room 888 activation in the indoor lounge — makeup touch-ups, hair styling, and a braid bar available to every guest. title: Design Thinking Meets AI: Book Talk & Live Build
description:
AI can write the code now, but who's designing what it builds? Louise Macfadyen, author of the newly released O'Reilly book, Designing AI Interfaces, joins Bethany Crystal for a conversation that starts with a deceptively simple question: What does it mean to design AI-native systems and interfaces?

​Together they'll explore the philosophy and surprises of designing AI systems, poking at questions like: Who counts as a designer now? What does good taste looks like in a prompt-driven world? What happens when the coding is the easy part? Then they'll put it to the test.

Bethany and Louise will take a design concept from the book and build it live, in real time (using AI), showing what it looks like when design thinking drives the engineering, not the other way around. They'll close with a reflection on what worked, what surprised them, and what it means for anyone building with AI today.

​Key Takeaways:

- ​Understand the core principles of AI interface design from the author who literally wrote the book on it

- ​Watch design thinking applied live, from concept to working prototype in real time

​- Rethink your own role: You don't need to be a "designer" to start designing better AI experiences

​Target Audience:

- ​Product managers, designers, and builders working on AI-powered products who want a sharper design lens

- ​Founders and operators using AI tools daily who want to move from "it works" to "it works well" and understand why the difference matters

- ​Copies of the book, Designing AI Interfaces, will be available for purchase.

​—

​Co-hosts: Bethany Crystal (Founder & CEO, Build First - https://buildfirst.ai/) + Louise Macfadyen (Author, Designing AI Interfaces, O'Reilly Media)

​Hosted at Studio Rodrigo's design studio in DUMBO
​https://www.studiorodrigo.com/

title: The Brick Room - Where the Best Ideas get built Twice
description:
You've pitched your idea a hundred times.

But have you ever built it?

The Brick Room is a one-night-only experience during NYC Tech Week where founders, investors, and builders come together to connect in a way that no rooftop happy hour ever pulled off.
Using LEGO® Serious Play® - a research-backed methodology trusted by teams at Google, Spotify, and NASA - you'll build with your hands, share what you made, and meet the people in the room on a level that actually means something. No slides. No stages. No "so what do you do?"

Just bricks, builders, and conversations worth having.

Here's how the evening flows:

🧱 Walk in. Find your seat at the table. Get your hands on some LEGOs.
🧱 Build. Respond to facilitated prompts that reveal how you think, not just what you do.
🧱 Share. Your build tells your story better than any elevator pitch ever could.
🧱 Connect. Founders meet investors who are actually curious. Investors meet founders who are actually building.

This isn't a networking event with a gimmick. It's a different kind of room — intentionally small, carefully curated, and genuinely unlike anything else happening at Tech Week this June.

title: Many Models, One Workflow — with Bria and Cloudflare Replicate
description:
A year ago, the question was "which model?"

Today it's "which model for which job — and how do they wire together?"

Devs building real AI pipelines have stopped picking favorites. They're routing across dozens of models through Cloudflare Replicate, then composing the output with editing and control layers like Bria — background removal, generative fill, structured JSON prompts (FIBO), video tools with 1:1 audio preservation.

The new craft isn't prompting. It's orchestration.

For one night during NY Tech Week, we're putting the engineers building this layer in the same room. Come for working patterns, not slideware.
Every RSVP gets free Cloudflare Replicate + Bria API credits — leave with enough to wire something real that night.

What you'll take home
— How teams are routing tasks across model providers in production
— Where editing/control APIs fit in the stack (and where they don't)
— Cost, latency, and quality tradeoffs nobody puts in the docs
— What's actually shipping vs. what's still a demo

Schedule
5:00 — Doors, drinks, mingling
5:45 — Fireside chat + audience Q&A
6:15 — Food, drinks, more conversation
7:30 — Wrap

Speakers
Chris MacDonald — Head of Solutions, Bria
Jon Bunting — Senior AI Solutions Architect, Cloudflare Replicate

Bria
The licensed, IP-safe visual AI platform for editing and control — background removal, generative fill, structured prompts, and video tools built for production.

title: Low Latency Club Voice AI Meetup @NYC Tech Week
description:
The Low Latency Club is heading to NY TechWeek. Telnyx and Deepgram are teaming up for an evening of real conversations around voice AI with the people who are building it.

We're bringing together devs, founders, and product teams shipping voice AI in production, and creating space to share what's working, what's not, and what's been harder than expected.

Voice systems are more than speech. They're a chain of moving parts:

– Capturing audio
– Transcribing in real time
– Making decisions
– Responding fast enough to feel natural

Getting that full loop to work reliably is where things get interesting. That's what the evening is about.

What to expect: Cold drinks, great food, and time to connect. No pressure, no pitches. Just a room full of devs, founders, and product teams solving similar problems.

Why come? Most events talk about what voice AI could be. We show you what it actually looks like running.

Oh, and one of you is leaving with a brand new Mac Mini!!

Who should come– CEOs and founders building or scaling voice AI products– Developers building voice agents– Engineers working on real-time systems– Product teams exploring voice interfaces– Anyone curious how voice AI works beyond demos

Meet people solving the same problems. Leave with a clear sense of how to build and what to watch out for.

title: LegalTech in the Age of AI: Opportunities, Challenges, and What's Next
host: Fox Rothschild LLP, GrayHair Venture Partners
description:
Join Us for an Evening of Insight and Connection

Citizens Bank, GrayHair Venture Partners, and Fox Rothschild LLP invite startup founders, investors, and legal innovation enthusiasts to an exclusive NY Tech Week event exploring the rapidly evolving world of LegalTech.

The global LegalTech market is experiencing unprecedented growth and capital inflows (topping $4.5 billion in 2025 and on a trajectory to surpass that amount in 2026), driven in large part by the transformative impact of artificial intelligence. AI-powered tools are reshaping how legal services are delivered, from contract analysis and due diligence to litigation prediction and regulatory compliance. 

Panel Discussion: Recent Developments and Challenges in LegalTech

Our expert panel will examine the forces shaping LegalTech today, including:
•	The rise of AI in legal practice and how it is redefining workflows across the industry.
•	Regulatory and ethical considerations surrounding AI-driven legal tools, including questions of accuracy, bias, privilege and the unauthorized practice of law.
•	Investment trends and market dynamics, including where venture capital is flowing and what differentiates the startups that succeed.
•	The evolving relationship between law firms, in-house legal teams, and technology providers as adoption accelerates.

Panelists will include:

•	Lev Loukhton, Founder and Managing Partner at GrayHair Venture Partners
•	Otto Zastrow, Founder of Midpage.AI
•	Sabih Siddiqi, Founder of Iqidis, Inc. 
•	Nick Baughan, Managing Director at Marks Baughan
 •	Arina Shulga, Partner at Fox Rothschild LLP

Following the panel, join fellow founders, investors, and legal professionals for a relaxed reception. This is your opportunity to make meaningful connections, exchange ideas, and explore potential collaborations in one of tech's most dynamic verticals.

This event is designed for startup founders building in the LegalTech space, venture capital and angel investors evaluating legal technology opportunities, and anyone passionate about the intersection of law and technology.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Totems of Hope: Your Brain on Philanthropy
host: Totems of Hope: Your Brain on Philanthropy
hosts: Totems of Hope: Your Brain on Philanthropy
description:
What if you could feel how giving shapes the nervous system in real time?

Totems of Hope is a neuroaesthetic immersive installation exploring presence, attention, and human connection through sound, spatial design, and non-invasive neurotechnology.

As participants move through the experience, physiological patterns subtly influence an evolving sensory environment — where sound, interaction, and neuroadaptive systems create a living feedback loop between inner state and space.

This is not a talk.  
It is something you step into.

As part of the experience, participants collectively prepare food resources that are redistributed to a local community, linking inner experience with tangible impact. The event gathers artists, technologists, founders, researchers, and cultural innovators around emerging conversations at the intersection of humanity and technology.

The experience will take place at Duca Studio by Duca Sartoria, bringing an additional layer of craftsmanship, elegance, and contemporary Italian design into the experiential environment — creating a dialogue between embodied technology, aesthetics, and human expression.

Presented internationally, including at AWE USA and LA Tech Week / Internet Tech Week @ FYI, and engaging over 1,000 participants internationally, the work sits at the intersection of immersive systems, human perception, and social impact.

A space to slow down, tune in, and experience technology differently — not as distraction, but as a medium for connection.

This event is part of NY Tech Week — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the technology ecosystem.

title: Rooftop Kickoff Cocktails - IBM & Tech:NYC
How To Get Your First 1000 Customers: A Zero To One Hands-On Workshop
description:
​Traction is the only metric that matters. This time, you're paying for it.

​The last time we ran this workshop, it sold out fast and founders walked out with real work done. Not slides. Not a framework to "implement later." Actual outreach sequences. Actual channel strategy. Actual next steps.

Only committed founders in the room. No tourists. No note-takers who disappear. Just people serious enough to show up with a laptop and leave with something that moves the needle this week.

​Building a great product is only half the battle; getting people to actually use it is where most startups die. After going through YC with Humoniq in S25 and driving the early traction needed to secure our $8.5m round, I’m sharing the exact, unscalable playbook we used to get off the ground.

​I’ll share:

​The "Do Things That Don't Scale" Reality: What growth hackers won't tell you about acquiring your first 100 users.

​The Zero-to-One Arsenal: Over 20 battle-tested cold outreach scripts, DM templates, and landing page frameworks.

​Where They Hide: How to find and infiltrate the highly specific communities where your ideal customers are already hanging out.

​The Conversion Playbook: How to turn a cold lead into an early adopter, and an early adopter into a vocal evangelist.

​Bring your computers, landing pages, and outreach drafts. This is a hands-on workshop. At most, I will be speaking for the first 20 minutes to lay out the framework. After that, we are rolling up our sleeves and building your early growth engine together. You will leave this room with a concrete plan to hit 1,000 users.

​What you get when you register:

​The No BS Startup Guide ($179). Free. All of it.

​Over 10,000 founders have downloaded this guide. It covers idea validation, customer discovery, early fundraising, and your first 90 days of growth. It's the foundation everything in this workshop builds on. Yours the moment you sign up.

​You're not paying for a lecture. You're paying for 2 hours of focused work with someone who has done this at the pre-seed stage and helped 500+ founders do the same. The guide alone is worth the price of admission.

​I’m a 3x founder, operator, and executive coach working at the intersection of leadership psychology, AI systems, and company building. I’ve launched four agentic AI products generating over $1M in revenue and most recently served as Fractional COO and first employee at Humoniq (YC S25), helping raise an $8.5M seed. I’ve coached 500+ founders and executives, mentored through programs like Intel Ignite Startup Montreal and Nucleate, and I’m currently writing The Primal Trap, a book about how primitive human psychology quietly shapes leadership and organizational success. Earlier, I built and led Customer Success at Nylas through its $140M Series C and later rebuilt and relaunched the CPG brand Hot Date Kitchen after a shutdown.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Leading HR Through the AI Revolution
description:
Together with Justworks and leaders from companies including Snappy, NICE, Amdocs, Compete, and more, we’ll discuss how AI is reshaping hiring, people management, leadership, and organizational culture.


AI is no longer just another productivity tool, it’s changing how companies hire, manage teams, build culture, and scale.

Join us for an evening bringing together founders, operators, HR leaders, and executives to discuss what leadership looks like in the age of AI. From AI-first organizations and leaner teams to talent strategy, employee trust, and the future role of HR — this conversation is about the real operational changes already happening inside modern companies.

In collaboration with Justworks and Bereisheet Tech.

Agenda

5:30 PM — Networking & Drinks

6:30 PM — Panel Discussion:

AI-First Companies: How Founders Must Rethink Hiring, Management & Culture

AI is fundamentally changing how modern companies are built. Teams are becoming leaner, workflows faster, and expectations from employees and managers are evolving in real time.

This conversation will explore what it actually means to build an “AI-first” organization today — from hiring and productivity to culture, trust, and leadership. The panel will discuss how founders and executives should rethink organizational structures, what skills will matter most in the next few years, and where human leadership becomes even more important as automation expands.

We’ll also discuss practical realities:

How AI is already changing recruiting and talent evaluation

What happens to middle management in AI-driven organizations

Whether startups will hire fewer people — or simply different people

How to maintain culture and employee engagement during rapid transformation

The balance between operational efficiency and human connection

What founders are getting wrong about internal AI adoption

The session will include audience Q&A and open discussion with founders and operators in the room.

Moderator:

Noa Lotan, NICE

Noa Lotan is a senior HR leader at NiCE with more than 20 years of global HR experience across multinational organizations, including Comverse, Teva Pharmaceutical Industries, and NiCE. specialises in talent strategy, organizational effectiveness and leadership development. partnering with executive leadership teams to drive workforce transformation and scalable people initiatives worldwide. Her work focuses on aligning business strategy with talent, leadership and the evolving impact of AI in HR.

Speakers:

Hamutal Oren-Fox, Snappy

Hamutal Oren-Fox is the Chief People Officer at Snappy, where she leads the People and Culture function, focusing on creating an environment where employees feel valued, engaged, and empowered. Before joining Snappy, Hamutal served as a Chief People Officer at Venn City and EVP of People at Cuebiq, where she played a key role in establishing the company’s HR function and thriving initiatives to enhance employee performance and engagement.

Amit Rapaport, Compete

Amit Rapaport is the Co-founder and CEO of Compete, an AI-powered workforce intelligence platform helping HR leaders make smarter, faster decisions with confidence. With over a decade of HR leadership experience at high-growth technology companies including SentinelOne and Palo Alto Networks, Amit founded Compete to transform fragmented people data into actionable insights for HR, Finance, and executive teams. Today, Compete helps organizations uncover workforce risks, identify opportunities, and modernize how they manage compensation, performance, and strategic workforce planning in the age of AI.

Leadership from Justworks


7:00 PM — Fireside Chat:

Building and Leading Teams in the AI Era

Beyond the headlines and tools, AI is forcing leaders to rethink how they personally manage, communicate, prioritize, and execute inside their organizations.

In this fireside chat, we’ll move from theory to real operator experiences: how leadership changes when teams move faster, expectations increase, and employees themselves are adapting to entirely new ways of working.

The conversation will touch on:

How leadership styles are evolving in AI-driven companies

The changing expectations from employees and managers

Decision-making in a world of constant AI acceleration

Managing uncertainty, fear, and resistance internally

How operators personally use AI in their day-to-day work

What remains uniquely human in leadership

What the future workplace may realistically look like in 3–5 years

This will be an open and candid discussion designed for founders, executives, and leaders navigating these changes in real time.


Fireside Chat:

Idit Duvdevany Aronsohn, Amdocs

Idit Duvdevany-Aronsohn heads ESG and People Relations at Amdocs – a global telecommunications software & services company [NASDAQ:DOX] with over 26,000 employees worldwide. In this role, Idit focuses on improving the lives of people in our communities, fostering diversity & inclusion, promoting sustainability with technologies, prioritizing workplace practices & employee wellbeing. Her responsibilities include ensuring that Amdocs’ global ESG, employment strategies, and global people relations create lasting value.

Eran Friendinger Co-founder & Chief Product Officer, Voyantis

Eran is the Co-founder and Chief Product Officer of Voyantis, the AI decisioning platform that helps the world's most ambitious brands not only acquire their highest-value customers, but grow them across every channel, touchpoint, and stage of the customer journey. The platform is trusted by growth and marketing teams at ZipRecruiter, Miro, Opendoor, SonderMind, MoneyLion, Upside, and more. A seasoned entrepreneur with over two decades in the tech space, Eran brings deep technical expertise to his role leading product at Voyantis, where agentic AI turns first-party data into predictive intelligence that trains ad platforms, optimizes lifecycle spend, and routes every offer and action toward real, incremental revenue. Prior to Voyantis, he co-founded Adience — later acquired by MarketTech — a groundbreaking AI platform that pioneered audience profiling on web and mobile using advanced Machine Learning and Deep Learning algorithms.

7:30 PM — Networking


About the Organizers

Justworks￼

Justworks is a technology company that levels the playing field for all small businesses. Through our software and as a partner, we help our customers take care of their teams, streamline their operations, and navigate the complex aspects of managing a workforce with confidence.

Bereisheet Tech￼

Bereisheet Tech is the leading Israeli high-tech community on the U.S. East Coast, connecting founders, executives, investors, and operators through curated events and professional communities.


Important Notes

• This event is intended for founders, executives, operators, HR leaders, and professionals in the tech ecosystem.
​• Space is limited, and registration approval is required.​
• By registering, you agree that your registration information may be shared with the event hosts.​
• Exact location details will be shared with approved guests closer to the event.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Scale Without the Burn in AZ: A Private Founder Dinner
description:
Arizona is aggressively supporting companies that choose to scale there. The Arizona advantage includes access to non-dilutive and dilutive capital, infrastructure and location, high-quality workforce, multiple testing opportunities and business-friendly policies. 

During New York Tech Week, Musa Capital and the Arizona Commerce Authority are hosting a private, invitation-only dinner for revenue-generating founders ready to expand to Arizona. This curated gathering is designed for founders with $1M+ in annual revenue who are evaluating expansion strategies and seriously considering Arizona as a possible location. 

During New York Tech Week, Musa Capital and the Arizona Commerce Authority are hosting a private, invitation-only dinner for revenue-generating founders ready to scale.

The dinner will be held at a restaurant in Manhattan, shared upon confirmation.

This curated gathering is designed for founders with $1M+ in annual revenue who are evaluating expansion strategies and want direct access to capital, incentives, and state-level partnership opportunities.

You’ll gain insight into:

●	Direct investment capital available to Arizona-based and relocating companies

●	Incentive programs designed to reduce operational burn

●	Lower cost-of-operations environments without sacrificing access to talent

●	Favorable regulatory frameworks built to accelerate growth

●	Enterprise and government partnership pathways and cutting-edge testing facilities

This dinner is intentionally small and strategic — built for operators making serious expansion decisions, not casual networking.

Eligibility requirement:
Companies must have a minimum of $1M in revenue and be open to exploring relocation or expansion opportunities.

Attendance is limited and subject to approval

title: Decoding Legal AI’s Talent Market
description:
A quiet migration is happening in legal tech. Attorneys are leaving practice to build, sell, and scale the very tools transforming their profession. New kinds of roles are emerging faster than the market can define them - legal engineers, solutions architects, attorney-operators - but comp, role expectations, and career paths are still opaque. This event changes that.

Join current and former attorneys, legal AI operators, founders, and legal tech builders for a candid evening on what these roles actually look like, what skills transfer, and how lawyers are navigating the shift from traditional legal work into building, deploying, and adopting AI.

🎤 FEATURED PANELISTS

- Emilie McConnachie — Applied Legal Research Manager at Harvey
- Dan Ton-That — Legal Engineer at Legora
- Justin Cooper — Legal Ops Leader
- Avi Brudner — Co-Founder of AI-native law firm Clara Law and ex-COO of Blue J

📋 AGENDA:

6pm - 7pm: Mixer with light bites and drinks

7pm - 7:20pm: The market right now - a quick map of the legal AI talent landscape, presented by Stephanie Nweke

7:20pm - 8:20pm: Panel discussion, moderated by Sally Wong, on making the move from law into legal tech — followed by Q&A

8:20pm - 9pm: More mixing & mingling!

🎯 WHO THIS EVENT IS FOR: 
- Practicing attorneys thinking seriously about what's next
- Lawyers already working in legal tech who want to compare notes
- Legal AI builders, founders, operators, and GTM teams
- JD/MBAs exploring non-traditional paths
- Recruiters, investors or advisors close to the space

🎤 HOSTED BY: Stephanie Nweke (Sandstone, ex-DraftWise, ex- WSGR) and Sally Wong (Crux, ex-Harvey, ex-Latham). 

Questions you’re already curious about? Share in advance here: https://forms.gle/D8PEhX5qcDuPsBsu7

title: Earnings vs. Ethics in the Age of AI
host: Kirkland & Ellis, Fordham University Gabelli School of Business
hosts: Fordham University Gabelli School of Business
description:
The Fordham AI Business Hub presents Earnings vs. Ethics in the Age of AI, a NY Tech Week panel bringing together senior leaders from venture capital, the startup ecosystem, corporate America, law, and academia to explore the defining trade-offs of the AI era: how organizations balance speed and accountability, shareholder returns and societal impact, competitive advantage and the public good.

Networking reception with a selection of appetizers, empanadas and signature cocktail to follow the panel discussion. 

--
Featured speakers include:

Anthony Ferrante, Senior Managing Director, Global Head of Cybersecurity, FTI Consulting | Former Director for Cyber Incident Response at U.S. National Security Council and Chief of Staff for FBI's Cyber Division

Dana Rao, Former General Counsel and Executive Vice President, Adobe | Former IP General Counsel, Microsoft 

Lerzan Aksoy, Dean and George N. Jean Ph.D. Chair, Fordham Gabelli School of Business

--
The Fordham AI Business Hub is New York City’s new home for ethical, applied AI grounded in discipline-specific expertise, academic rigor, and experiential learning. The Hub connects companies, entrepreneurs, policymakers, students, and community partners to harness AI responsibly for growth, innovation, and meaningful impact.

title: Unlocking Cognitive Performance
description:
You have optimized your calendar, your team, your systems. Have you thought about your mind?

Jiali Zhang is a computational neuroscientist from Oxford and the founder of Soma. She spent a decade mapping how the brain processes information, builds memory, and sustains attention. She left the lab with one conviction: the science of peak cognitive performance belongs in the hands of the people actually trying to do hard things.

Join founders, investors, and neuroscientists to learn the latest research in cognitive performance and discuss what it means to perform at your best.

Ang's work spans the frontier of artificial intelligence: AI-powered advertising, autonomous driving, lifelong autonomous agents, video intelligence, computer vision and mapping systems

As CEO & Co-Founder of Simular, the autonomous computer company, Ang is building at the frontier of artificial intelligence. Simular’s AI agent became the first in the world to surpass human-level performance on OSWorld, a major milestone in autonomous computing.

And you won’t just hear about it—you’ll see it live.

Experience Simular's AI product "Sai" (https://sai.work/) which is one of the world’s most advanced AI assistants, performing real work on a real computer in real time. It’s like having an executive assistant who never sleeps, never forgets, and works at the speed of light.

SAI can quickly perform following tasks for your advertising needs:
• Social Media & Content Creation at Scale

• Lead Generation & CRM Management

title: VoiceScale Launch Party
description:
We built something. Now we’re celebrating it with the people who get it.

VoiceScale is the employee advocacy platform built for B2B marketing teams who are done watching their brand page reach disappear. We take one piece of company content and turn it into personalized LinkedIn posts for every person on your team, written in their voice, matched to their role. Your employees show up consistently. Your company surfaces in AI search. Your team does none of the writing.

We’re throwing a launch party during NY Tech Week to bring together the founders, marketing leaders, and operators who know that the next wave of B2B growth isn’t coming from ads. It’s coming from the people already inside the building.

title: The job market in the age of AI
description:
The job market is being rewritten as we speak. Mass layoffs, mass job applicants and mass use of AI in recruitment.  Roles are also being created, collapsed, and redefined as AI changes what companies need and who they hire. The question isn't whether AI will impact your career, it's how fast and how. This is a fireside, interactive chat where we will explore which roles and industries are growing, shrinking, the evolution of recruitment and what professionals at every level should be doing to stay ahead. 

Join Lucy Schott (Founder, AI & Workforce Strategy Consultant, Cornell MILR), Andrea Henderson (Partner, DHR Global; Angel Investor; Columbia MBA) and Max Horton Director at Oakleaf Partnership who all work at the front lines of talent strategy and AI adoption, advising executives and organizations on what comes next. 

Networking and drinks after. 

Lucy spent 10+ years in global HR, Culture & Capability for Fortune 500 companies, but started her career as a games designer. She now is a founder of a self service AI Adoption Platform, advises companies on AI workforce strategy and teaches AI to leaders at General Assembly. 

Andrea is a Partner at DHR Global (top 6 exec search firm), a Perplexity Fellow, and has 25+ years matching talent to value across life sciences, healthcare, and tech.

Max Horton is a Director at Oakleaf Partnership, leading the firm’s New York office, specializing in executive search across the CPG, manufacturing and life-sciences sectors. With over a decade of experience in the talent market, he advises clients on hiring strategy, leadership trends and the impact of AI on workforce structure and capability needs.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Good retrieval = good agents hackathon
description:
Come spend an evening building with Shaped, a retrieval API that sits on top of your data and returns ranked, minimal context. 

Why? Because the best agents in the world are backed by good retrieval. They don't drown in 200 search results for every query, they get the top, ranked ones. 

We'll guide you on setting up your first retrieval engine and wiring it to your agent.  Feel free to bring a project you've been sitting on, or just show up with a rough idea. 

You'll get direct API access and real talk from people running this at scale.

title: Open Source Must Win
description:
The most important software in the world is open source.
The internet runs on it. The cloud runs on it. AI runs on it.
So the question isn’t whether open source matters.
It’s whether open source wins.
We’re bringing together the founders, engineers, and investors who are building in the open — and making the case that the future of the entire stack depends on it.
THE PANEL
A sharp, honest conversation about where open source is winning, where it’s losing, and what it actually takes to beat proprietary.
THE NIGHT
6:00 — Doors + drinks
7:00 — Panel
8:00 — Open networking
THE ROOM
OSS contributors. Builders. Investors backing the open stack.
📍 Manhattan venue revealed to RSVPs

Zo is your intelligent home. We help you kill SaaS you don't actually need and build a system that's truly yours. You get a 24/7 agent in a personal cloud computer. With Zo, you can create simple automations through iMessage or build complex apps to run your business.

title: Trust Issues - A Traitors style game for NYTW
description:
Trust Issues is a live compatibility experiment inspired by the Black Mirror episode "Hang the DJ." 

Here's how it works:
→ You fill out a short questionnaire
→ An algorithm pairs you with someone in the room
→ Then.. like a metal detector for human connection, your devices pull you toward each other. It beeps. It gets faster the closer you get.

When you find your match, you'll move through a few rounds of questions and challenges (think Traitors meets Love Island) designed to reveal how well you actually click. 

Each round receives a compatibility score, and at the end, the system reveals who in the room you're most compatible with, plus a few people you should really go find before you leave.

No name tags or "what do you do" questions. Just a slightly unhinged, very fun way to meet the right people.

We make no promises about what kind of compatible. 👀

🔬 Spots are limited. Come be a test subject. The algorithm needs you.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

description:
Building in fintech isn't like building anywhere else. The stakes are real: money moves, regulations are constant, and downtime is always an on fire sitatuation. You're writing code where a bug can mean a failed payroll run, a frozen account, or a compliance nightmare at 11pm on a Friday.

Come decompress with other engineers who get it. Whether you're at a startup working towards getting licensed or a scaleup with a system processing billions, the problems are hard, the constraints are rigorous, and the stories are great.

title: AI Agents in Finance - The Next Wave of Adoption
description:
2026 is shaping up to be the year AI agents move from concept to workflow. In finance, that shift matters: the work is knowledge-intensive, regulated, and dependent on judgment and trust.

Hosted by Women Leading AI, this NY Tech Week panel will explore how agentic AI is beginning to reshape institutional financial services – from banking, portfolio management, investment research, wealth management, risk, compliance, operations, client service, and capital allocation to the broader governance questions raised by increasingly autonomous workflows.

This will be a dynamic women-led speaker panel featuring startups and leaders building at the intersection of agentic AI and financial services. The conversation will focus on what is real today, where adoption is emerging, and what finance and AI leaders need to understand before agentic systems become more widely implemented.

Drawing on Women Leading AI’s institutional finance & AI lens, the event is designed for investors, finance and AI leaders seeking a practical, high-signal discussion of the next wave of AI adoption in financial services.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek – a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: The Great Lab Exit: From Academia to Funded Ventures
description:
Many aspiring scientists wonder how they can take the leap into building their own company. At the same time, many entrepreneurs are looking for ways to tap into the deep knowledge, IP, and research emerging from academia to build their next venture.

This event brings together ex-Nucleate founders who have successfully made that transition from academia into entrepreneurship. They will share how they started, how they turned research into companies, and what it actually took to raise capital and scale beyond the lab.

The panel will be followed by networking and drinks.

Speakers:
Aykut Aksit (Co-founder and CEO of Haystack Medical) 
Demetri Maxim (Co-founder and CEO of Nephrogen) 
Jonathan Scheiman ( Co-founder, CEO Fitbiomics)

Moderator: 
Scott Alpizar (Managing Partner, Emerging Innovations Consulting)

Doors Open: 6.30PM
Networking:  6.30 - 7.00PM
Panel: 7.00PM - 7.45PM
Networking: 7.45 - 9.00PM

title: True Confessions:AI Hype & Fundraising
description:
What would you say about AI right now if no one could trace it back to you?

TRUE CONFESSIONS is a candid, facilitated conversation about the AI boom's real impact on fundraising.  We'll cut through the hype so founders and investors can make better decisions based on what's actually happening.

Hosted by Flywheel Strategy x Tech/ish during NY Tech Week, this session brings together investors, AI founders, and non-AI founders for a structured discussion grounded in anonymous feedback and sentiment from those in the room and outside experts.

The Problem

AI hype is distorting capital markets — pressuring AI companies to overstate, making non-AI founders feel locked out, and skewing perception for everyone keeping up through headlines.

How It Works

Attendees complete an anonymous pre-event survey where unpopular opinions are welcome. We open by sharing what the room actually believes: where investors feel pressure to deploy, where AI founders feel pressure to overstate, where non-AI founders feel locked out. Then we dig in.
No panels. No pitching. No performative takes.

You'll Leave With

- Clarity on signal vs. hype: a grounded read on what’s actually driving AI fundraising decisions right now (and what’s just noise)
- Unfiltered market insight:  real, anonymous sentiment from founders and investors you won’t hear on stage or in press cycles
- Actionable positioning edge: how to navigate (or leverage) the AI narrative whether you’re building in AI or not, to raise and compete more effectively

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Leap: From Executive to Founder
description:
Are you a senior leader thinking about starting a company?

Do you have deep industry insight and wonder if it could become a venture-backed business?

Are you ready for your next chapter to be something you build, not just something you join?

The Leap: From Executive to Founder is an invite-only intimate dinner hosted by Build Momentum for senior executives, operators, and domain experts exploring the move from leadership into company building.

You have built teams, carried responsibility, solved hard problems, and seen an industry from the inside. At some point, the question shifts from How do I keep climbing? to What do I actually want to build?

This gathering is for people standing at that edge.

Life is short. The best ideas do not wait forever. And the move from executive to founder is rarely simple. It asks for conviction, courage, and a willingness to trade certainty for possibility.

We’ll gather a small group of experienced leaders, founders, investors, and builders for a candid dinner conversation about what it takes to turn expertise into a company, move from idea to execution, and make the leap from executive to founder.

Who this is for:

● Senior executives and operators considering a founder path
● Domain experts with a bold company idea
● Leaders ready to turn hard-earned insight into a company
● Experienced builders looking for their next big chapter
● Investors and partners who support executive-led startups

The evening will include:

● An intimate, invite-only dinner
● A highly curated room of serious leaders and builders
● Honest conversation about ambition, risk, reward, and transition
● Practical perspective on moving from idea to company
● Meaningful relationships with people who understand the leap

For those ready to make a big move, this is the room for the conversation that comes before the company.

Attendance is invite-only. Request an invitation to be considered.

Location shared upon approval.

This event is hosted by Build Momentum as part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: AI, Time, The Future of Computer
description:
#A16Z #Techweek #AI 

AI agents that use computers like humans are here. What does that mean for how we work and live? Join us during NYC Tech Week for an intimate panel with Ang Li (co-founder of Simular, ex-DeepMind), Christopher Fong (founder of the Google alumni network Xoogler), and a special guest investor. Expect sharp insights, real conversation, and networking with the right people in the room.

We’re fortunate to have one of the world’s leading AI innovators — Ang Li, PhD — joining us for this exclusive evening. Ang is the CEO and co-founder of Simular, the autonomous computer company, and his work spans the frontier of artificial intelligence: autonomous driving, lifelong autonomous agents, video intelligence, computer vision, mapping systems, and AI-powered advertising.

Over his career, Ang has held research and engineering roles at some of the most influential AI organizations in the world — Google DeepMind, Baidu Apollo, Facebook AI Research, the Carnegie Mellon Robotics Institute, Apple, Google Street View, and Comcast Labs DC. He holds a PhD in Computer Science, with research focused on spatial perception through visual and linguistic information — pioneering work at the intersection of AI, robotics, and human-machine understanding.

At Simular, that research has become product. Simular’s agent was the first in the world to surpass human-level performance on OSWorld ￼, the leading benchmark for AI that operates real computers — and the company is backed by $21.5M led by Felicis, with participation from NVIDIA’s NVentures and Basis Set Ventures ￼.

And you’ll see it live. Watch Sai (https://sai.work/) — one of the world’s most advanced AI assistants — do real work on a real computer, right in front of you. Unlike chatbots that just hand you answers, Sai operates your computer: browsing the web, filling out forms, creating files, sending emails, and completing multi-step tasks from start to finish. It’s like having an executive assistant who never sleeps, never forgets, and works at the speed of light.

No slides. No buzzwords. Just AI doing actual work.

🔍 Find You a Job — Sai searches openings, matches your skills, tailors your resume, and drafts applications, in minutes
💰 Raise Capital — Sai identifies the right investors, pulls their contact info, drafts personalized outreach, and builds your pipeline
📊 Prepare Any Report — name a topic (market analysis, competitive research, financial modeling) and Sai researches, writes, and delivers a polished document

And much more…

Christopher Fong (Berkeley-Columbia MBA '10) is the founder of Key, an AI startup unlocking professional networks. He founded Xoogler.co, Google's alumni community with 28,000 members. An angel investor in 100+ startups, Fong spent 8+ years at Google on the Project Sand Hill team working with VCs and startups.

Arian Agrawal is a Partner at South Park Commons (SPC), where she leads operations and heads the NYC office. A product of the SPC community herself, she previously co-founded Riya Collective (YC W'20), a mobile shopping app connecting US consumers with boutique South Asian designers, raising a seed round before the pandemic. She has also worked in product management at Fin and business operations at Homejoy, and holds a B.S. in Management Science with a concentration in Finance from MIT.

📍 Location: Somewhere Nowhere NYC - Nightclub, Lounge, & Rooftop Pool, 112 W 25th St 39th Floor, New York, NY
📅 Date & Time: June 1, 6-9pm

Spots are limited—reserve your place asap.

We look forward to seeing you there.

title: SOLO UNICORN HOUR — Day 1 Debrief
host: Solo Unicorn Club, Manhattan Elite Club
description:
Tech Week Day 1 just hit.

You probably spent the whole day hearing about AI agents, agentic workflows, and the future of automation.

Tonight, we’re not just talking about agents.

We’re letting your agent shop.

Join Solo Unicorn Club, Manhattan Elite Club, and Snaplii for a Day 1 debrief, founder social, and live agentic shopping experiment during NYC Tech Week.

We’ll start with a warm founder lounge: meet solo founders, AI builders, indie hackers, fintech operators, and small AI-leveraged teams navigating Tech Week.

Then Snaplii will show how AI agents can move from recommendation to real-world transaction — using a payment layer built for agentic commerce.

Instead of your agent stopping at “here are some options,” imagine your agent actually completing the purchase: coffee, dinner, or drinks.

⸻

Tonight’s Theme

Stop talking about agents.
Let your agent shop.

AI agents can already recommend products, plan trips, compare prices, draft orders, and summarize your choices.

But they usually stop at checkout.

Snaplii is building the payment and merchant layer that lets agents safely complete real-world purchases through tokenized, scoped payment flows.

Tonight, you’ll see the full loop:

Agent → tokenized payment → real purchase → receipt

⸻

Tonight Only: Try Snaplii and Claim Up to $100

Snaplii is giving eligible onsite guests up to $100 to try the experience live.

Use it toward:

☕ Coffee
🍽️ Dinner
🍸 Drinks

Come to the event, register onsite, try the Snaplii flow, and experience what it feels like when an AI agent can actually shop for you.

Limited quantity. One per eligible participant. Onsite verification required. Drinks are 21+ only where legally permitted and subject to venue, merchant, and Snaplii policy.

⸻

What to Expect

Founder Social
Meet the builders, operators, solo founders, AI hackers, and quietly ambitious people Tech Week’s headline events often miss.

Snaplii Live Demo
A short presentation on agentic commerce: why agents get stuck at checkout, why normal cards are risky for AI agents, and how tokenized payment flows unlock the next step.

Agentic Shopping Activation
Try the Snaplii flow onsite. Register, activate, and see how an agent can move from intent to purchase.

$100 Shopping Challenge
Use your Snaplii activation toward coffee, dinner, or drinks — and test the agentic shopping loop in real life.

NY Tech Week Day 1 Debrief
Trade hot takes from Day 1. What was overhyped? What was real? What should AI agents actually be allowed to do next?

Real Conversations
No panel theater. No generic networking. Just founders, builders, and operators talking about what they’re actually building.

⸻

Run of Show

7:00 PM — Arrival + Founder Social
Check in, grab a drink, meet other solo founders and AI builders.

7:30 PM — Welcome from Solo Unicorn Club
Why this room exists, why Day 1 matters, and why agentic commerce is one of the most interesting questions coming out of Tech Week.

7:40 PM — Snaplii Presentation + Live Demo
From AI recommendation to AI transaction. How agents can safely complete purchases using tokenized, scoped payment flows.

8:00 PM — Snaplii Activation Sprint
Register onsite, try the flow, and get help from the Snaplii team.

8:25 PM — Let Your Agent Shop Challenge
Use the experience toward coffee, dinner, or drinks. Builders can also explore the API and think through real product use cases.

8:50 PM — Day 1 Debrief Open Mic
60-second hot takes: best event, worst take, strongest founder, weirdest demo, and what agents should buy next.

9:15 PM — Founder Networking + Snaplii Office Hours
Keep socializing, ask technical questions, find collaborators, and talk with Snaplii about pilots, integrations, and agentic commerce use cases.

10:00 PM — Wrap

⸻

Best For

AI agent builders
Solo founders
Indie hackers
Fintech founders
Payment and commerce operators
Product engineers
Applied AI teams
Investors curious about agentic commerce
Anyone who spent Day 1 hearing about agents and wants to see one actually do something useful

⸻

Bring

Your phone
Your laptop, if you want to try the API
One idea for what your AI agent should be able to buy
Your best Tech Week Day 1 hot take

⸻

Presented By

Solo Unicorn Club × Manhattan Elite Club
Powered by Snaplii

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem.

⸻

Host Note

This is still the Solo Unicorn Hour you RSVP’d for: warm room, high-signal people, light programming, and real conversations.

We’re just adding one twist:

After a full day of talking about AI agents, tonight we let them shop.

Savor three distinct Colombian coffee varieties, each with its own story, flavor notes, and brewing method. Guided by coffee experts, you’ll explore the richness and complexity that make Colombian beans world-renowned.

Enjoy light snacks alongside your coffee and conversations. Whether you’re looking to make new business connections, exchange ideas, or simply enjoy a great cup of coffee, you’ll find your people here.
Join us for an energizing start to your day — where great coffee meets great minds.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Run & Raise: 5K Social Run
description:
The best deals start with a conversation. Some of the best conversations start on a run. Join DealMaker for a morning 5K through New York City before Tech Week gets into full swing. All paces are welcome. Snacks and coffee after.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Tech IPO Summit: The path from private to public
description:
2026 is shaping up to be a defining year for the most anticipated tech IPOs—companies that have stayed private longer, scaled larger, and carry unprecedented expectations into the public markets.

Join us for an intimate morning summit at Nasdaq MarketSite, bringing together investors, advisors, and operators at the intersection of private and public markets to unpack what’s coming—and what it signals for the future of capital markets, investor access, and the next generation of private companies. 

Hear from industry leaders across Morgan Stanley, Nasdaq, EquityZen, Bernstein Wealth, and more for an inside view into this unprecedented time in the industry. 

Agenda 
8-8:50am: Check-In, Refreshments & Networking 
8:50-9:00am: Opening Remarks 
9:00-11:20am: Sessions across Private Markets, The IPO Threshold, and Liquidity
11:30-12:30am: Networking & Light Lunch

More details will be shared closer to the event date. Request to join to get updates!

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Cut through noise & make the news: media panel for founders
description:
You've built something worth talking about. Now you just need journalists to agree. 

Getting press coverage in 2026 is both more important and more difficult than ever. One story in the right outlet can do more than a year of paid ads - but that email you sent to a journalist about your startup is one of hundreds of pitches just like it.

Join us for a breakfast panel with journalists and comms leaders from major media and tech companies including Yahoo and Vox Media who will share what actually lands coverage in the age of AI slop.

Founders, apply for the a limited spot in the workshop to follow and put what you've learned into practice. 

Presented by LaunchLink, Pressto and Smooth. Fast-moving tech brands, get expert PR service at launchlink.co. Early brands that want to DIY their PR can do it all with heypressto.ai. Smoothmedia.co represents the most influential knowledge creators who educate and empower their audiences through smart, insightful content. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: 4th Annual NY Tech Week Soccer Championship
description:
Investors. Founders. Operators. One Pitch.

Back for the 4th year running, the NY Tech Week Soccer Championship brings together the startup ecosystem’s most competitive (and collaborative) investors, founders, and operators for a morning of fast‑paced soccer, real connections, and post‑game community.

This is a fun & casual game that's well‑organized and  high‑energy designed to spark offline relationships that actually last beyond NY Tech Week.

Whether you’re a former college player, weekend league regular, or just competitive enough to lace up boots for good people and a great cause, this is your chance to play with the ecosystem, not just network around it.

👥 Who’s Playing

--> VCs & Angels
--> Startup Founders
--> Operators across Finance, HR, Ops, Product, and GTM
--> All skill levels welcome (we organize accordingly...see below)


🧩 Team & Match Format

Teams: ~6–8 players per team (small‑sided for high engagement)

Organization:

--> Players will be grouped by role (Investor / Founder / Operator)
--> And by self‑reported skill level to keep games competitive and inclusive

This ensures:

--> Balanced teams
--> High‑quality play
--> No one stuck defending a former D1 striker all afternoon (unless they want to)

👟 What to Bring
Please come ready to play:

--> Soccer or turf shoes (no metal cleats)
--> Athletic wear
--> Water (we’ll have limited extras, but bring your own to be safe)
--> Fun energy + good sportsmanship (no jerks, no slide tackling, no hard shots to someone's face)

🤝 After the Whistle: Stay Connected
The game is just the beginning.

All players will be invited to:

--> World Cup Community Days...in the spirit of the tournament coming this summer to the US where we'll have games, co-working, and watch parties...all in the name of the beautiful game
--> Private WhatsApp Group... for follow‑ups, pickup games, intros, and future matches across NYC and beyond

This is where conversations continue, partnerships form, and next year’s rivalries begin.

✅ Why Play

Real relationships > surface‑level networking
Shared experiences beat small talk
You’ll leave with sore legs, new friends, and actual follow‑ups scheduled

Spots are limited to keep teams tight, RSVP early to secure your place on the pitch.
See you out there. ⚽

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Fenwick Fun Run
description:
Start your day on the move with a casual 5K designed to be as welcoming as it is energizing. Whether you’re race-ready, an avid runner, or a light jogger, all paces are encouraged. This is about getting outside and starting the day together, not setting personal records.

Our run will begin and end in the Flatiron District. After the run, we’ll gather for a healthy breakfast for networking and conversation. Connect with fellow founders, investors, and operators over great food and even better conversation in a relaxed, post-run setting. Come solo or increase the fun by bringing a colleague!

The run will be led by professional running coaches Matt and Sammy McClintock!

By confirming your attendance you agree to receive communications and updates from Fenwick.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Founders House - Morning Wellness
description:
Founders House (www.foundershouse.nyc) is a members-only home base in Manahattan where founders work, recover, and build alongside others going through the same thing.

​It's a workspace (phone booths, conference room and hot desks), a wellness club, and a community in one - designed around how founders actually spend their days.

We’re starting the morning with movement and good energy at the Founders House wellness studio. 

Come start your day at Founders House with a guided morning yoga session led by Taylor Fishman (200HR YTC)

A simple way to move your body, meet great people, and feel ready for the full Tech Week ahead.

title: Vanderbilt Founders x Investors Breakfast
description:
Join us for a 2.5 hour breakfast roundtable + anti-conference format.  We look forward to a lively conversation about:

- Marketing strategies that breakthrough the AI slop
- Innovating away from the edge + the strategies of moats vs. bridges
- AI operational excellence
- Talent + scaling strategies in the age of AI
- Seeking alpha in the age of AI / new paradigms for VC + founder collaboration
- What Vanderbilt can provide you / role of the NYC campus in promoting the alumni startup ecosystem

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Raising Capital in Today’s Tech Market - An insider conversation with VCs and family offices
description:
Raising capital looks different in today’s tech market and founders need direct access to how investors are actually making decisions. Join RSM tech leaders, with Michael Loeb and Nicole Enslein for a candid conversation on how investment decisions are really made—from first meeting to diligence and beyond. 

Attendees will gain unfiltered insight from capital allocators actively shaping the market, what they are prioritizing right now, how their investment theses are evolving, and what founders should be prepared for when raising capital. 

FEATURED SPEAKERS: 
Michael Loeb
Founder & CEO, Loeb.nyc
Investor, entrepreneur, and former media executive known for building and scaling iconic digital businesses.

Michael Loeb is a serial entrepreneur and the founder and CEO of Loeb.nyc.

After starting his career at Time, Inc., Michael co-founded the Synapse Group with then-partner, Jay Walker. Under Michael’s direction, Synapse became the largest seller of consumer magazine subscriptions worldwide through its revolutionary, patented Continuous Service Model. Michael and Jay went on to form and incubate Priceline .com together at Synapse, leveraging a shared team of operators and capital.

Nicole Enslein
Principal, Sublime Communications
www.sublimecommunications.com
CEO & Founder, Brand Builder, Growth Accelerator, Connector, Angel Investor- strategic advisor with a focus on capital deployment and long term value creation.

Nicole Enslein is the Founder & CEO of Sublime Communications, an award-winning growth acceleration firm helping high-growth companies shape narrative, build trust, and drive market momentum. She has supported companies through IPOs, billion-dollar exits, and 1,000–10,000% growth trajectories, working with category leaders like GitLab, SentinelOne, and Cylance. A seasoned operator and investor, she brings a real-world lens to how capital decisions are made and what it takes to win in today’s market.

What Attendees Will Walk Away With
•	How VCs and family offices are assessing risk and growth in today’s market
•	What investors are screening out early—and what gets follow up meetings
•	How diligence expectations are shifting (and where founders get caught unprepared)
•	The real decision making dynamics behind capital allocation
•	Direct access to senior investors in a small, curated setting

Space is limited. Attendance is curated to support meaningful dialogue and real investor access.

title: Ironspring Ventures/Eclipse/SVB Industrial Tech Breakfast
description:
Join Ironspring Ventures, Eclipse, and SVB for a special Tech Week edition of our quarterly NYC breakfast series gathering physical AI and industrial tech founders and investors for ecosystem building and startup pitches from some of New York's most exciting entrepreneurs building in digital industrial innovation.

Featured Speakers:
-Adam Gluck, Founder & CEO, Copia
-Benjamin Gibbs, Co-Founder & CEO, Stealth
-Saleh ElHattab, Founder & CEO, Gravity

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: GTM Breakfast: Rooftop, Robots & Revenue
description:
We're giving away a robot dog at the end of breakfast. One lucky attendee will walk out with their own AI quadruped robot (~$3,700 retail).

A rooftop morning gathering for revenue operators, GTM leaders, data nerds, and the companies rethinking how autonomous systems run their pipeline. 

Good food, live robot dogs you can pilot with a controller, and a room full of people who care about the infrastructure and data layer underneath every AI workflow.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Calm-pliance Cafe
description:
Compliance Cafe — Hosted by Vanta & Agency

​Stop in, grab a drink, and have a real conversation about what's slowing you down.

This year we're adding something new: MC'd by Agency CEO Amir Tarighat, we're kicking off the morning with a candid fireside between Vanta's CEO Christina Cacioppo and Anarghya Vardhana. 30 minutes of conversation, 15 minutes of your questions. 

After that, the cafe is yours. ​Whether you're pre-seed, Series A, or just starting to think about what compliance means for your company, stop by. Grab a drink (we have a signature one), say hi, and talk through whatever's on your mind — SOC 2, HIPAA, what investors are asking for, or whether you even need to think about any of it yet.

​We're buying the drinks. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The AI Founder Playbook: Breakfast & Panel @ Microsoft
description:
AI is rewriting the rules for how startups get built, funded, and scaled — and the founders who adapt fastest will define the next decade. Join us at Microsoft's NYC office for a morning with leading investors and operators unpacking what's actually working right now: how founders are winning in an AI-native market, what VCs are betting on, and where the real opportunities are over the next 12–18 months. You'll leave with sharper conviction on where AI is heading, a clearer playbook for building in it, and a room full of people worth knowing.

Hosted by Miguel Martinez (Principal AI Solutions Engineer, Microsoft), the panel features Kelvin Keane (Global Business Development Manager at Microsoft for Startups), Ashley Ryder (Partner, VamosVentures), Laura N. Montoya (Managing Partner, Accel Impact Ventures), and Richard Ortega (AI Applied Research Engineer, stealth AI startup).

This event is for early-stage founders, engineers considering the leap, investors, and operators who want sharp takeaways and real conversations — not recycled AI talking points. 

Doors open at 8:30 AM with bagels and coffee, the panel runs 9:30–10:50, and we'll close with rooftop networking until noon.

Special thank you to our event sponsors:
- Awana. We help founders hire software engineers and technical talent (sales, marketing, operations.
- Silicon Valley Bank (SVB), a division of First Citizens Bank, has been the trusted financial partner of the innovation economy since 1983.  From seed to IPO, SVB offers startups, founders, and investors the expertise, connections, and tailored solutions to fuel their growth.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Fueling Founders: Brex + DoorDash for Business
description:
DoorDash for Business and Brex are hosting a proper founder-fuel up this Techweek: coffee and lunch taken care of, and a room filled with people building what’s next. Come to work, come to eat, come to connect with people who get it.

What to expect 
- Coffee & More: coffee, tea, matcha and specialty drinks  
- Catered lunch from local NYC favorites
- Coworking workspace: a spot to take calls or handle emails without disappearing entirely
- Founder Spotlight: Get filmed, get featured - your edited video, ready to post the next day.
- Networking: a room full of founders with no forced agenda
- Exclusive startup perks: TechWeek offers from Brex and DoorDash for Business (business.doordash.com/en-us/techweek) 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: VC Breakfast [Hosted by Collab Fund x J2 x Rho]
description:

Meet the hosts:
