title: Executive Builders Summit with Lovable
description:
Join us for a day of hands-on building hosted by Paradigm & Lovable.

This is for senior leaders who are ready to experience what's actually possible. Over a few focused hours, you'll go from concept to working solution, guided by expert Lovable experts and AI leaders using today's most powerful AI tools. No coding required.

Whether you're a CEO, Partner, Managing Director, or senior executive exploring how AI fits into your roadmap, you'll leave with something tangible: an app you built yourself and clarity on what's achievable. Expect light bites, real builds, and the rare opportunity to learn by doing. 

Spots are limited. Must be approved to join. 

https://executive.builders/ 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Everywhere Ventures Pre-seed founders breakfast
description:
Join us for a special breakfast event with like-minded pre-seed stage founders in NYC, hosted by Jenny, Scott, Anna and Michael from Everywhere Ventures in partnership with Ayo from Microsoft for Startups. The morning will feature light programming followed by a hearty sit-down breakfast and time for meaningful conversation.

​This event is exclusively for founders of product focused companies at the pre-seed stage and space is limited to keep it intimate. 

​​​Everywhere Ventures is a global founder collective and early-stage venture fund investing across vertical AI. Over the last 8 years, we’ve invested into 300 pre-seed companies, making us one of the most active early stage funds around. Our capital comes from hundreds of limited partners who are all founders themselves and help to support our growing portfolio.

https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/startups

Microsoft for Startups is a global startup program designed to help founders build, scale, and succeed by providing access to technology, cloud infrastructure, and go-to-market support. Through technical enablement, Azure credits, and direct connections to Microsoft’s product teams, partners, and customers, the program supports startups from idea to growth. Microsoft for Startups works with thousands of founders worldwide, helping them accelerate product development and bring innovative solutions to market.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Fintech CFO Office Hours
description:
We are bringing our popular Fintech CFO Office Hours to NYTechWeek! Scalepoint provides fractional CFO and Capital Markets Advisory services to Seed to B VC-backed Fintech startups. Early stage Fintech finance is hard, and sometimes founders just need someone to talk to about it. Book a time slot with us here https://cal.com/scalepoint-cfo/ny-tech-week-cfo-office-hours to bounce your ideas, get some hot takes and sage advice from our experienced Fintech CFOs and Capital Markets experts. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Big IDEAS @ Big SNOW - 2026
description:
Last year was a huge success, so we’re running it back. Big Deals at Big Snow returns for year two during New York Tech Week. Expect more turns, more friends, and a whole lot more stoke.

​We’ll kick things off with buses leaving Manhattan at 9am sharp (~100 spots available, or you can drive yourself), landing us at Big Snow for two solid hours of skiing and riding from 10 to 12. Then we’ll shift into après lunch at noon, with two return buses heading back to the city at 12:30 and 1:30.

​Whether you’re here to connect with other VCs and founders or just want to shred a few laps on North America’s only indoor snow dome, this one’s about community, conversation, and carving it up.

​I’m Mike Ma, GP at Sidecut Ventures and former PSIA-AASI National Team Member. Snowboarding’s been a lifelong passion, and I’ll be out there riding with you—offering tips while listening to pitches, and soaking up the good vibes. Whether you're raising a round, looking to network (not at a bar!), jonesing for hot park laps, or just learning to link your first turns, you're in good company.

​****It's all-inclusive—ski or snowboard, poles, boots, pants, jacket, and helmet are all provided, so no need to bring gear unless you want to. The only thing they don’t include are gloves, so feel free to bring your own or grab a cheap pair there.  So this isn't one of the NYTW events you can ghost. Hope you understand.

Huge thanks to our amazing sponsors—Mercury, Reitler, Vector, Impressit, and Backblaze—for making this possible!

Mercury is a radically different banking* product — engineered from the ground up to hold, move, and help you truly understand your money like never before. Everything you need is built in: cards, invoicing, bill pay, spend management, real-time insights, intelligent categorization. And because it all lives in one place, it gets smarter the more you use it — surfacing what you need, when you need it, in ways a patchwork of tools never could. To join the more than 300K entrepreneurs that trust Mercury, visit www.mercury.com.

​Reitler Kailas & Rosenblatt LLP is a national law firm with offices in New York, Princeton and Los Angeles.  With a strong focus on providing sophisticated legal services across a range of practice areas, including venture capital, mergers and acquisitions, private equity, fund formation, taxation, intellectual property, privacy law, real estate, hospitality, entertainment & IP litigation and general commercial litigation, Reitler has established a reputation for excellence.
​Consistently ranked Top 10 in venture capital deal activity per Pitchbook, Reitler's dedicated attorneys employ an innovative approach and ability to close transactions quickly and efficiently at every stage of an organization’s lifespan, from seed funding through a liquidity event, IPO, or other exit.
https://www.reitlerlaw.com/

https://www.vectorais.com/

Impressit is an Anthropic Services Partner shipping AI-native products with Seed+ founders in weeks, not months. Example – we shipped a SaaS platform quoted at $120K and four months in under four weeks at half the budget. Anthropic-certified architects on every engagement. Your stack. Your IP. No vendor lock-in. Find Roman at the event.
https://impressit.io/

Backblaze is a leading cloud storage platform built for simplicity, scalability, and predictable pricing. Through Flamethrower, its founder-first startup program, Backblaze helps startups building AI, media, and other data-heavy products scale without getting crushed by infrastructure costs by offering credits, support from people who have built and scaled companies, and a simpler path to growth. Find James at the event.
https://www.backblaze.com/flamethrower

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Agentic Commerce: Build Day
description:
Join us for a full-day, hands-on exploration of agentic commerce, where AI agents don’t just assist users, but actively transact, decide, and execute. 

Hosted by Microsoft in collaboration with Tavily and Coinbase.

​We’re bringing together builders, founders, and operators to explore what happens when search, agents, and payments converge.

​You must be registered to attend the Event.

​Morning (9:00–10:00 AM): Lightning Talks

​Quick, high-signal talks from teams building at the frontier of:

​Agentic workflows
​AI-native commerce
​Search + retrieval infrastructure
​Payments & execution layers

​Build Session (10:00 AM – 5:00 PM)

​Roll up your sleeves and build alongside:

​Tavily
​x402
​Lovable

​Whether you’re prototyping an autonomous shopping agent, a research assistant that transacts, or something entirely new—this is your playground.

​🍽️ Food & Fuel

​We’ll keep you energized throughout the day:

​Morning: Coffee + bagels
​Lunch: Sandwiches & salad
​Afternoon: Snacks
​
​👥 Who Should Attend

​Engineers building with LLMs & agents
​Founders exploring AI-native business models
​Product builders experimenting with commerce flows

​Anyone curious about what happens when agents start buying things

​🎟️ RSVP

​Spots are limited. Apply early to secure your place.

title: Stop Scaling Churn: What Founders Get Wrong About Customer Success
description:
You keep acquiring customers. But net revenue isn't moving the way it should.

You're not sure if it's the product, the team, or the process. And you don't have time to figure it out.

This is a small, curated breakfast for founders who are in it - or can see it coming. We'll spend 20 minutes on the framework, then open it up for real conversation.

We'll cover:
- Why retention breaks down as you scale
- The customer engine every growing company needs before it's urgent
- When to hire your first CS leader - and what to build before that

Format:
Coffee + breakfast → 20-min talk → open founder discussion

Capacity: 15 founders
Intentionally small. Come ready to talk.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: J.P. Morgan Payments x Tech:NYC Media Breakfast
description:
Join J.P. Morgan Payments, Tech:NYC, and the top voices in New York’s tech scene for an invite-only breakfast during New York Tech Week featuring an on-the-record fireside chat with Zack Anderson, Chief Data & Analytics Officer at J.P. Morgan Payments, moderated by Bloomberg’s Paige Smith. 

We’ll explore the forces shaping fintech today, from emerging technologies like AI and its impact on both clients and employees to the biggest stories emerging across New York’s broader technology ecosystem, while bringing together the industry’s storytellers for a morning of conversation and connection.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together founders, builders, investors and the media across the tech landscape. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Mercury Vinyl House
description:
Mercury’s Vinyl House at NY Tech Week 
June 1st, 2nd, and 3rd · 9 am–3 pm 
Port Sa'id · 88 King St, NYC

NY Tech Week moves fast. Mercury's Vinyl House is a place to slow down — just long enough to catch your breath, get inspired, and keep moving.

We're taking over Port Sa'id in SoHo for three days: part co-working hub, part vinyl listening lounge, all founder-first. Whether you're between meetings, hosting one, or just need somewhere to land, this is your home base.

What to expect:
☕ Coffee + light bites to keep you going
🧑‍💻 Comfortable workspace to plug in and get things done 
🎧 Live vinyl DJ sets (Monday and Tuesday 12 pm -3 pm)
🎶 Listening booths + curated music moments 
🛍️ Limited edition merch
🤝 Meet members of the Mercury team from 12 pm-3 pm each day

Plus two expert sessions:

Live Pitch Jam — June 1 @ 10:30 am 
Want real, unfiltered feedback as you sharpen your ideas? In this interactive, fast-paced session, founders share their working pitches live with Brandon Bryant (Partner, Harlem Capital Partners) and Jenny Fielding (Co-founder & Managing Partner, Everywhere Ventures). Come ready to think on your feet — they've been known to flip the script just to see how you handle it.

How did you do that? — June 2 @ 10:30 am 
Everyone talks about strategy and outcomes. But what actually happened behind the scenes? Channing Allen (Co-founder, IndieHackers and Hatch) and Brandon Gell (COO, Every) break down the pivotal moments — what they did, what worked, what didn't, and what they'd do differently.

We'll wrap it all up with a Closing Brunch on June 3, 9–11 am, with a live DJ set.

Register now if you're planning to stop by. 


➡️ MERCURY
Mercury is a radically different banking* product — engineered from the ground up to hold, move, and help you truly understand your money like never before. Everything you need is built in: cards, invoicing, bill pay, spend management, real-time insights, intelligent categorization. And because it all lives in one place, it gets smarter the more you use it — surfacing what you need, when you need it, in ways a patchwork of tools never could. title: The AI x Sustainability Paradox: Using Climate AI Responsibly
description:
Join Women in Cleantech & Sustainability NYC and getWITit for a discussion that will explore how sustainability leaders are leveraging AI for climate wins like grid optimization and renewable forecasting, while tackling AI's own challenges, such as data center energy demands and emissions.   

Some questions explored by this discussion include:  
• Where is AI already delivering measurable climate benefits? 
• How do we account for the energy and carbon footprint of AI itself? 
• What governance, standards, or procurement choices can climate‑focused organizations make to ensure they are using “climate‑safe AI”? 

Featured Speakers: 
• Maria João Sousa - Executive Director at Climate Change AI
• Mandy DeRoche - Director of Emerging Issues, Clean Energy Program at Earthjustice
• Porsha Seechung, MS - Director, Academic Relations at Green Project Management

description:

title: Build, Buy or Borrow: Launching your product in the age of AI
description:
A 30-Minute Panel + Networking at Tavern Community Coworking.
As a founder, you're constantly deciding what to do yourself, what to hire for, and what to buy or build a tool for. But AI is changing that equation—fast.
Entire categories of work that used to require a hire, an agency, or a vendor can now be handled by AI tools. At the same time, the need for human judgment is getting higher, not lower. There’s a critical difference between outsourcing a task and outsourcing judgment. 
The risk isn’t just delegating work—it’s losing control of decisions that define your company. Get it wrong and you're either burning cash or burning out. 
This panel is about getting it right.
We'll dig into how to figure out which parts of your business need you, which are better handed off to an external specialist, and which problems already have a tech solution waiting for you. This discussion will help you move faster without losing control of the things that actually matter.
We're bringing together founders who've been in the weeds, operators who've built the systems, vendors who sell the solutions, and investors who've watched it play out to give you insights from experience you can use for your own discernment.  
After the panel, stick around for bagels and Coffee and to connect with fellow founders, potential partners and investors.

Meet the panel🎤

Marc Kaufman
Partner, Potomac Law Group

Eni Maj
CEO and Founder, Travel Spoken

title: Digital Identity: Fighting Consumer Fraud in Financial Services
description:

The discussion will examine emerging fraud patterns, regulatory developments, and the future of identity verification in financial services. We’ll explore the impact of AI on fraud and financial services, with a focus on synthetic identity fraud, account takeover attacks, impersonation, and the growing sophistication of modern fraud networks.

title: What Quantum Actually Means for Computing
description:
For years, quantum computing was seen as a breakthrough perpetually just out of reach. That perception is beginning to shift. Scientists are now combining quantum and classical systems to address problems that become prohibitively complex at scale, especially in molecular modeling, chemistry, materials science, and energy. This session will explore what has enabled recent progress, what quantum computing can deliver today, the importance of building a quantum workforce for today and for the future, and how the gap between long‑term promise and practical impact is starting to narrow.

Moderator: Karmela Padavic-Callahan, Physics Reporter at the New Scientist​

Confirmed panelist:​
- Juan de Pablo, Anne and Joel Ehrenkranz Executive Vice President for Global Science and Technology, New York University & Executive Dean, NYU Tandon School of Engineering​
- Osama Raisuddin, Quantum Algorithms Research Scientist, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute​
- Andrea Goldsmith, President, Stony Brook University
- Jerry Chow, CTO of Quantum-Centric Supercomputing and IBM Fellow

title: GEO & PR Brunch
An A2A World: Who Builds It, Who Wins, and What's Still Missing
description:
99% of the most useful agents, tools, and services haven't been built yet. Not because the models aren't good enough but because most people still can't picture what a real agent-native world looks like. More and more people are building agents and building products that serves agents. But we are still multiple steps a way from a true A2A economy

Dedalus Labs is bringing together a panel of founders working to close that gap and building the missing parts for where the future of AI agents is headed.

In this session, we examine the state of AI agents now, discuss the A2A future, and breakdown what is stopping a world where agents communicate, coordinate, transact, and be evaluated in the real world. Ending with live Q&A segment with the audience.

Light food, drinks, and intentional networking to follow.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: 🚀 From First Check to Exit: The Board Strategy Most Founders Don't Know They're Missing
description:
🚀 Successful exits: what founders should be doing now to get there.

Most early-stage founders focus on product, growth, and fundraising. The companies that consistently outperform are doing something else early, they're building the right people around them and structuring those relationships to actually drive outcomes. 

Join us for a high-impact morning designed for pre-seed to Series B founders who want to build smarter, scale faster, and unlock access to capital.

We will demystify advisory and governing boards and show you how they can accelerate growth, strengthen your investor narrative, and open doors to capital. We'll start with a short fireside on exits; what founders get wrong, how early decisions shape outcomes, and why the people around you matter more than most realize. Then we'll zoom out to the full playbook.

Advisory boards, governing boards, strategic advisors - these are the most underleveraged tools in a founder's arsenal, and experienced entrepreneurs never leave this value on the table.

Why? How to actually build, structure, compensate, and paper these relationships has been nearly impossible to find in one place. Until now.

🎓 You'll walk away knowing:
- the difference between advisory and governing boards and when to build each
- how the right board increases your fundability and investor confidence, practical strategies for structuring, compensating, and managing boards early
- how to identify and recruit high-impact members — and why diversity on your board leads to stronger decisions and better outcomes

💡 Boards should not be scary. They should be your rocket fuel.

Real-world lessons, common pitfalls, actionable frameworks. You'll hear from a VC, an M&A lawyer, and an operator-turned-founder on what the best companies do differently. Whether you're pre-seed or preparing for your next round, you'll leave with the clarity and tools to turn your board into a true strategic advantage, not the other way around.

⏰ Agenda:
10:00 – Arrivals
10:15 – Programming starts​Agenda:

title: Sick Care is Dead. What Comes Next?
host: Bright Signal Consulting, Scalpels & Spreadsheets, VO Health
description:
*Event to be covered by People, Inc, home to People, Health.com, Verywell Health, EatingWell, and Byrdie. 
*Light bites and beverages by Mid-Day Squares, Slate Milk, Smearcase, and WAWA water.

The doctor’s office is no longer the default entry point into healthcare.

GLP-1s are prescribed over text. Blood panels are ordered by consumers before symptoms appear. Gyms are evolving into longevity clinics. AI is becoming a first-line triage tool. A new health stack is forming outside traditional institutions, and clinicians, payers, and patients are all trying to understand where they fit.

This panel explores what that emerging stack actually looks like, who gets to participate in it, and what is lost and gained when health and the human body become part of a consumer-driven system.

Speakers:
Alina Zolotareva, RDN (Co-founder & CMO of VO Health)

title: Startup to IPO Candidate in 3 Years: The Timeline Most Founders Don't know
description:
[2026 Tech Week] Early-stage founders rarely think about IPO.
It feels distant — something for “later.”

But in reality, exit is not a final milestone. It’s a filter applied from day one.
Every early investor is implicitly asking:

*Can this company scale into something public markets will understand?
Will institutional capital show up at exit?
*Is the path structurally clean — or broken before it begins?
*That lens shapes which companies get funded, how they’re valued, and what paths remain open.

And here’s the part most founders don’t realize: NASDAQ’s Capital Market Equity Standard requires only a two-year operating history.Meaning many companies could be IPO-ready within ~36 months — if the right capital and structural decisions are made early.

This Panel Connects the Full Capital Lifecycle. Bringing together perspectives across every stage:

Panelists include:
Annabelle Zhang — Founder & CEO of Allin AI, the intelligence layer for capital formation, and founder of International Elite Capital, an IR advisory firm serving public companies and institutional investors. 12+ years in institutional capital markets, former NYSE-based financial reporter, ESG Investing CFA, and Nasdaq Milestone Circle member.
Chris Van de Voorde — Founder & CIO, JUUNOO Scaled bematrix from $3M to $50M and built JUUNOO in Europe f, with Saint-Gobain as an investor. Holds 10 patents, two-time Red Dot Award recipient, two-time Deloitte Fast 50 honoree, founder of the Circular Value Institute.

Iynna Halilou —a General Partner at the MBA Fund, an early-stage venture capital firm backing exceptional founders from the world's top innovation ecosystems: Harvard, Stanford, UPenn, and Berkeley. The fund has backed over 180 companies at the earliest stages of their journey, with multiple unicorn outcomes across fintech, AI, data, and space tech.

Ron Gregory — Chief Revenue Officer, SiteIQ Technologies CRO at SiteIQ Technologies, a spatial intelligence and digital twin platform that unifies facility layouts, physical assets, and autonomous operations into a single coordinate system. 

Ronald Glenn — Managing Director, Williamsburg Venture Holdings Managing Director at Williamsburg Venture Holdings, a family office deploying across PIPEs and equity lines into public companies.

Together, they map the critical inflection points across the journey —
what to build, what to prove, and what fundamentally changes when the company becomes investable to institutional capital.

What Founders Will Learn
*How VCs actually think about exit potential at the earliest stage
*What makes a company investable across multiple rounds — not just one
*The structural decisions that expand (or limit) your future capital options
*Why most startups become uninvestable long before they realize it


About the Host — Allin AI
The only platform founders will ever need across their capital journey.
From startup to public company, we help companies: be seen → be investible → get funded → stay funded

Not a point solution — an operating system for capital formation across the entire lifecycle.

More: theallin.ai

title: Coffee Chat Manhattan: Faith In Business!
description:
Pastor Poju Oyemade is a Nigerian pastor and thought leader.  He is known for connecting faith with leadership, business, and governance. 

As founder of The Covenant Nation, with church campuses across Africa, Europe, and North America; he has influenced both entrepreneurial and policy conversations across the world.

He is respected for a structured, systems-driven approach, emphasizing discipline, execution, and long-term thinking: equipping founders to build institutions that last.

For this session with Builders, Backers & Believers, he brings:
- Practical integration of faith and leadership
- Insight on building enduring careers & institutions
- A credible voice on Christian integrity and stewardship. 

A BUILDERS, BACKERS & BELIEVERS event!
>>A community for honest conversations at the intersection of faith, ambition, and business: for founders, operators, professionals, and investors of faith to navigate purpose through the lens of the Bible, and pursue meaningful kingdom impact together. 2 Cor 4:6!

title: Agent Era: How Women Founders Are Building AI That Works for Their Business
description:
Description:
AI agents are the biggest unlock for resource-strapped founders — but most of the conversation is happening in rooms that don't look like us. Agent Era flips that. Hosted by Black Women Talk Tech, this half-day experience brings women founders together to cut through the AI noise, hear from operators who are already deploying agents in their businesses, and walk out with a working agent they built themselves. No jargon. No fluff. Just results.

Agenda:
10:00 AM – Check-in & Networking
10:30 AM – Opening: The Agent Opportunity (Why agents, why now, why us)
10:45 AM – Panel: "Agents in Action" — Women founders share how they're using AI agents for sales, ops, customer support, and content. What's working, what flopped, and where the real ROI lives.
11:45 AM – Break
12:00 PM – Workshop: "Build Your First Business Agent" — Guided, laptops-open session. Attendees choose their use case (outreach, scheduling, customer service, content) and build a functioning agent with step-by-step instruction.
1:30 PM – Show & Tell: Attendees demo what they built
1:50 PM – Closing & Next Steps
2:00 PM – End

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: An Engineering Mindset for AI Adoption Beyond Engineering
description:
What does it actually take to make an organization AI-first- not in slogans, but in how every team works day-to-day?

amp is a connected-fitness company building adaptive strength training hardware and software. In this session, CTO Omer Bahari shares how amp drove an AI-first transformation across every department.
AI finally lets the engineering mindset escape R&D- marketing, customer success, and ops can now build systems instead of buying tools. This talk is about the leadership shifts that make that real, especially at companies that weren't built AI-native.

title: Founders & Executives: Move 5x Faster When Your Entire Team Vibe-Codes
description:
Join us for a focused morning of real case studies, investor perspective, and hands-on building — limited to 30 founders and executives. Breakfast included. 

The companies winning in the AI era have changed who builds. Enabling non-technical teams to build the frontend is how the fastest companies are expanding velocity - and it's more practical than it sounds.    
 
What we'll cover?
🔹 How mature companies are restructuring who builds what to accelerate time-to-market
🔹 Intro to frontend vibe-coding and best practices
🔹 In-product building in practice: a hands-on workshop, going from PRD to production-ready component
😀 The Lineup: 
→ Jacob Kitchen (Associate, Radian Capital) — how AI is changing the scaling playbook

→ Keren Fanan (Co-Founder & CEO, Myop — ex CRO at Gett) — the org change behind 5x faster time-to-market: real case studies from teams where product, design, and marketing all ship

→ Hadar Geva (Co-Founder & CTO, Myop — ex Wix) — hands-on session: practice it live, don't just hear about it


Who is this for: 

🔹 Founders, who want to move faster and improve time-to-market
🔹 CPOs, CMOs, and VPs of Product and Marketing, at companies with mature products 

🧑‍💻 We’ll be working hands-on, so make sure to bring your laptop and charger.

Co-hosted with Radian Capital.

title: Building AI-Powered Customer Retention Systems
description:
Sponsored by Wooxy, this hands-on workshop dives into how modern teams are using AI to design, launch, and scale customer retention systems—without massive engineering resources.

If you’re curious how startups turn user data into personalized journeys across email, SMS, and in-product experiences, this session will show you exactly how it’s done.

What to Expect

In this interactive session, we’ll break down how leading companies build retention loops powered by real-time data, segmentation, and AI-driven personalization—and how you can apply the same principles immediately.

You’ll get:

* A clear framework for designing AI-powered retention systems
* A practical checklist to implement omnichannel strategies
* Hands-on exposure to AI agents and marketing automation workflows

Then, you’ll put theory into practice.

Working in small groups, you’ll analyze real use cases, uncover hidden retention opportunities, and define one actionable experiment you can launch right away using existing AI tools.

💡 What You’ll Learn
* How to design retention loops using real-time data and AI personalization
* How to activate users across email, SMS, and product touchpoints
* How to identify and test high-impact retention opportunities quickly
* How startups leverage AI to compete with larger teams

👥 Who Should Attend

This workshop is designed for:
* Students in computer science or AI
* Recent graduates exploring AI startups
* Developers interested in generative or conversational AI
* Entrepreneurs building AI-driven products
* Marketing specialists looking to boost retention with AI

🎁 What You’ll Get

All participants receive 3 months of free access to Wooxy, so you can continue building and experimenting with your own AI-powered marketing systems after the event.

📍 Location will be shared with registered attendees.

title: Future of Flight: From Innovation to Certification
description:
Future of Flight: Aerospace & Dual-Use Investor Forum

Join us during NY Tech Week for an RSVP-only investor forum focused on the future of aerospace, defense technology, aviation modernization, autonomy, UAS, advanced manufacturing, and dual use systems.

Aerospace is entering a new investment cycle. National security priorities, commercial aviation modernization, pilot and workforce constraints, industrial base limitations, autonomous systems, certification complexity, and supply chain resilience are converging to create a new class of hard tech opportunities.

This forum is designed for investors, founders, operators, and technical leaders who want to better understand how aerospace and dual use companies are built, financed, certified, protected, and scaled.

The evening will feature a moderated expert panel with perspectives across aircraft certification, aviation operations, venture capital, intellectual property, and aerospace company building.

Featured speakers include:

Donald Maloy
President & CEO, Maloy Aircraft
Founder of an emerging aircraft OEM developing the MA-1 Blue Jay, a clean-sheet training aircraft and integrated training system with relevance across civilian airline pilot training and military primary flight training.

Mike Borfitz
CEO & Partner, Kilroy Aviation
Aviation certification expert with decades of experience across FAA certification, delegation, production certification, and continued operational safety.

Dagmar Andersch
Co-Founder & CEO, Acelorix
International aviation safety, compliance, certification, and UAS operations expert with deep experience across commercial aviation, safety oversight, and operational risk.

J.P. Keating
Managing Partner & Founder, proChain Ventures
Early-stage investor focused on supply chain, logistics, mobility, autonomy, and critical technologies tied to economic resilience and national security.

Jose J. Jimenez
Partner, Polsinelli
Patent attorney and former Boeing aerospace engineer focused on patent protection for aerospace, AI, machine learning, UAV, rocket, and satellite technologies.

The discussion will translate complex aerospace and regulatory topics into practical investor judgment:

• How to evaluate aerospace and dual use companies
• What separates credible execution from technical hype
• Why certification and operating authority matter
• How customer adoption works in aerospace markets
• Where investors are seeing opportunity across autonomy, logistics, defense technology, and aviation modernization
• How intellectual property and technical defensibility affect company value

This is not a general networking mixer. It is a focused conversation for investors and leaders looking to understand the companies, technologies, and market forces shaping the next generation of flight.

Format
Networking and arrivals
Moderated expert panel
Audience Q&A
Post-panel networking

Attendance is RSVP-only.

title: How to Write a Book on AI in Enterprise SDLC While Patterns Keep Changing
description:
New model drops. New framework. New "this changes everything" thread. By the time you've integrated one tool, three more have made it obsolete.

So we asked ourselves a different question: what doesn't change? Across enterprise teams, across codebases, across the hype cycles. Which engineering practices actually hold up when the underlying models rotate every quarter?

Artur Skowroński and Tomasz Lelek spent a year shipping AI-assisted code across enterprise clients, watching what broke and what survived, then committed the patterns to print in Vibe Engineering: Best practices, mistakes, and tradeoffs, co-published with Manning Publications.

Join Artur and Tomasz for a discussion panel and community mixer, and learn about:
- Where AI earns its place in the delivery cycle, and where it creates new debt 
- Vibe coding beyond the individual contributor: what changes at team and org scale
- Keeping quality and trust intact when half your code has a co-author 
- Staying fast without losing control of what ships

And if you are interested in writing a book yourself, Artur and Tomasz will also talk about:
- Picking the right topic, so that the trend is still relevant when the book is out
- Getting past the writer’s block
- What reputable publishers like Manning are actually looking for right now

After the panel, you are invited over for drinks, networking, and the kind of conversations that don't happen in Slack.

Bonus: We'll be giving away 30 demo copies of Vibe Engineering: Best practices, mistakes, and tradeoffs. Grab one and get it signed by Artur and Tomasz in person.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Culture is evolving into something far more structural than expression alone—it is becoming a system layer for capital, media, and digital coordination.

This invitation-only Lunch & Learn brings together investors, founders, and operators for a focused discussion on how cultural activity is shifting from fragmented creative output into scalable, investable infrastructure.

We’ll explore how new coordination models, incentive structures, and platform architectures are enabling culture to function more like a networked system—capable of compounding value over time rather than relying on isolated, short-term cycles.

As you begin vetting new employees, the Relationality Test provides measurable compatibility insights between candidates and your company’s culture  helping organizations move beyond resumes and instinct-based hiring into intentional, aligned team building.

title: Dear AI, We Need To Talk.
description:
Dear AI, We Need To Talk.

An “Agony Aunt/Uncle” Session for AI Projects Going Sideways…

Your AI project looked promising. Then reality showed up.

This session is for people whose AI projects are flailing in some form or fashion. Or for people who want to start an AI project and avoid issues such as:
* inconsistent answers to the same question, when the answer needs to be precise & repeatable
* outputs that are plausible but wrong
* prototypes that worked in demo, then failed in real workflows
* unclear ROI despite time, tools, and budget already spent
* workflows that still need too much human checking and correction
* no clear decision on whether this should be AI, automation, better data, or simply a process fix

Your panel slash "Agony Aunts/Uncles" include:
1/ Darlene Newman (Ivy Cap Tech)
2/ Anurag Banerjee (Axioned - COO & Head of AI Adoption)
3/ Drew Burchfield (VP Product/AI Delivery)

Three people who have been hands-on working with:
* AI implementation inside real businesses
* operational workflows and automation
* product and engineering delivery
* data, accuracy, and reliability challenges
* scaling AI projects beyond the prototype stage
* rescuing projects that stalled after leadership bought into the hype

title: No legal, no problem? Leveraging Fractional GCs
host: AMBART LAW pllc, The Chief Counsel
description:
You’re building with AI and moving fast. You may not have the budget for a full-time in-house lawyer or a big-firm billable rate, but you still need to make real decisions about data, governance, and corporate structure. In the age of AI, gaps in data governance aren’t just compliance issues anymore—they turn into technical debt that’s harder and more expensive to unwind later. When selling your AI-powered products, there are questions vendors must be prepared to answer and defend.

Join Yelena Ambartsumian (AIGP, CIPP/US), founder of AMBART LAW (https://ambartlaw.com), and Princess Uchekwe, founder of The Chief Counsel (https://thechiefcounsel.com), for a straightforward conversation about how to get real legal help without hiring a full-time GC. A fractional GC is an experienced lawyer who joins your team part-time and on a flexible scope, so you get ongoing, business-savvy legal support at a cost a startup can actually carry.

Yelena and Princess will break down how they work as fractional counsel with growth-stage companies: setting up practical data and AI governance, spotting risks early, helping structure deals and product launches, and acting as your “in-house” legal brain without the headcount or big-firm overhead. They will discuss what diligence questions vendors should be able to answer when selling their AI-powered products and what "good" and "good enough" compliance actually looks like from a data and privacy perspective. 

Designed for founders, operators, and in-house counsel navigating the new AI economy, you’ll learn how startups who do not have the budget for an in-house legal hire can leverage fractional general counsel for senior-level legal guidance on AI governance, data governance, and deals.

No legal, no problem? Come decide what “just enough legal” looks like for your company.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Oasis @ NY Tech Week Day 2 - Future of Work
host: Flowstate, Vibe Space, Boardy, The AI Collective
description:
Tech Week can be chaotic. Running between panels, pitching, networking, and feeling overstimulated. The Oasis is a daily retreat: a place to belong, breathe, and actually co-create.

We're building a persistent home base across the week where builders, founders, and creatives show up every day to flow together, have real conversations around meaningful topics, and leave refreshed and inspired. The series blends daily 'co-flow' drop-in sessions with curated collaborative programming: roundtable discussions, fireside chats, and mentor office hours. Each day seeds Flowstate Sparks, structured artifacts that keep the conversation going long after the week ends.

Day 2: The Future of Work

Today we sit with one of the most consequential questions of this moment: what happens to work as AI and automation accelerate? If technology continues to absorb the things we used to do for a paycheck, what gets freed up, and what do we choose to do with it? We'll explore the shift from working out of need to creating out of passion, the new shapes careers and companies might take, and what it means to build a life that holds up when the old scripts no longer apply. Expect honest conversation about both the opportunity and the uncertainty.

With thanks to our venue partner, BELONG NYC

The Oasis is hosted at BELONG NYC (@belong.nyc), a hospitality-driven workspace and urban sanctuary in the heart of SoHo. Think airport lounge meets coworking: a high-design, high-service environment purpose-built for the in-between moments of life in motion. Beyond hosting our series, BELONG offers Flowstate members on-demand access to private meeting rooms, phone booths, and hot desks, plus showers and changing facilities, secure luggage storage, and rentable lockers throughout Tech Week. If you're juggling flights, workouts, dinners, and back-to-back meetings, it's the place to reset and keep going.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem.

title: Hacking Customer Retention with AI
description:
Sponsored by Wooxy, this fast-paced hackathon challenges you to design and build real AI-powered solutions to customer retention problems—in just a few hours.

Whether you're a developer, founder, or AI enthusiast, this is your chance to go from idea to working prototype using cutting-edge AI tools.

What to Expect

This is not just brainstorming—you’ll actually build.

The session is structured in three focused stages:

1. Methodology
We kick off with Wooxy’s specification-driven coding approach—showing how to rapidly build high-quality MVPs using AI-assisted development.

2. Ideation
Form teams, identify high-impact retention challenges, and validate your ideas with guidance from Wooxy experts.

3. Build & Demo
Bring your idea to life using AI tools and APIs. At the end, present your working MVP and get feedback from engineers and fellow participants.

💡 What You’ll Gain
A hands-on understanding of building with AI under real constraints
Experience applying AI to customer retention challenges
A working MVP you can continue developing after the event
Feedback from experienced builders and peers
Exposure to modern AI workflows and rapid prototyping techniques
👥 Who Should Join

This hackathon is ideal for:

Developers and engineers experimenting with AI tools
Startup founders and builders
Students and recent grads in tech or AI
Product thinkers interested in real-world applications
Anyone excited to build something real, fast
🛠 What You’ll Leave With

By the end of the session, your team will have:

A functional prototype (MVP)
A validated idea targeting a real retention problem
A clear next step to turn your hack into a live experiment or product

🎁 All participants will receive 3 months of free access to Wooxy, so you can keep building after the hackathon.

title: AI Founder Playbook: Stop Building AI Products No One Pays For
description:
NOTE: Due to a large number of RSVPs, this event will be first come, first served. So, come early. I will follow-up to all RSVPs afterwards as well.

--

Most AI products die before they're built. 💀  

Pitch decks pile up. Side projects get half-shipped. The thing that was supposed to change everything quietly becomes a folder of unused Notion docs and a vibe-coded prototype with zero users.

This is the workshop that fixes that.

* * *

In 2 hours, you'll move through the same methodology I've used at work, taught at universities (e.g. Brown University, Hunter College), and in my 6-week cohort to 80+ professionals.

80% of them launched an MVP or advanced their career within 90 days.

You'll work through:

→ Problem Pressure Test: find a problem worth solving (and walk away from three that aren't)

→ Decision Value Stack: pressure-test whether anyone will actually pay for what you're building

→ Build / Skip Matrix: what to ship in v1, what to defer, what to kill.

You'll leave with a 1-page pressure-tested concept brief for your own AI product, and a clear sense of what "build it" actually looks like, whether "it" is a startup, a side project, or an internal tool at your company.

Learn by doing.

* * *

👋 About Alisha Outridge

Former CTPO at TuneCore, led AI projects that drove +25% revenue and +30% engagement across 500K+ creators. CPO at FlyBy (acquired by Apple, work seeded ARKit) and WHIM (acquired by Videri). Brown University & Hunter College CUNY professor.

Currently, CPO/CTO of Byte&Chord, advising the U.S. Department of Education on AI governance. Founder of the AI Founder Playbook: 80+ founders trained, 80% launch-or-advance rate. Co-inventor on AI/ML patent US040728282.

* * *

Want the full 6 weeks or 1:1 product strategy + execution support for yourself or your team? → https://aifounderplaybook.com

title: Land Your First U.S. Tech Opportunity
description:

Join us for an honest, practical conversation with people who live and breathe this world every day: a recruiter who places international talent at U.S. companies, an admissions expert who has helped hundreds of students build their legal foundation in America, and an immigrant founder who turned his own visa journey into a platform serving thousands.
What we're covering:
— What U.S. recruiters actually look for in international candidates (and what's killing your chances without you knowing it)
— How to position yourself for OPT, H-1B sponsorship, and other visa pathways that actually lead to a career
— The immigration side of the equation — what needs to be in order before you can focus on the job hunt
— Real talk, real stories, real Q&A — no fluff
Who's in the room:
→ Sebastian Paredes — Founder, Interlix Staffing. He recruits international professionals for U.S. companies. He knows exactly what hiring managers want.
→ Jerry Tong — Manager of Admissions, DreamIT ESL. He's helped hundreds of international students build a legal, stable foundation in the U.S.
→ Alejandro Calderón — Founder & CEO, EZ Visa. A Colombian Air Force veteran and immigrant himself. 9 years in the U.S. 5,000+ visa cases. He built the platform so the next generation doesn't have to figure this out alone.
What you get just for showing up:
✦ Free access to the EZ Visa platform as a Founding Member — the AI-guided immigration tool being unveiled this week in NYC
✦ Snacks and drinks on us
✦ More surprises on the day
Space is very limited — only 50 seats.
This is not a panel you watch from the back of the room. This is a working session where you leave with clarity, contacts, and a plan.
If you're an international student in NYC trying to build your future here — this room is for you.

title: Rebuilding The Creative Stack
description:
The AI-first creative workflow is no longer theoretical; it’s happening now. Every week, a new tool promises to upend how work is produced, forcing designers, strategists, editors, and marketers to adapt or risk being pushed out.

This panel brings together creative leaders and AI builders to address what should change now, what the next five years will bring, and how to position yourself to lead the next wave of creative production.
 
🤠  Tuesday June 2, 2pm, Midtown
👽  RSVP for location details
🤠  Complimentary snacks and drinks provided.


—

About the Panel
We’re excited to be joined by the following panelists:

Savannah Wolf
DESIGN, PRIMARY VENTURES
Savannah leads design at Primary Ventures, an early-stage venture capital firm, partnering with founders to build brand foundations that support critical moments—from fundraising to securing early customers—as they define what they’re building and who they’re building it for. She has spent over a decade building products and design teams at early-stage startups across a wide range of industries, guided by a central question: how do complex human problems, good design, and early-stage chaos not only coexist but make each other better? She is also deeply curious about how AI will shape the products and experiences we design next, with a critical eye on what we should have learned by now and how creative problem-solving will shape a future worth being part of.

Louis Kishfy
FOUNDER, TRIE
Louis is the founder of Trie (trie.dev), an AI associate for the last mile of deliverables. Trie helps professional service businesses turn meetings and context into structured, production-ready output. Louis believes the best tools solve specific problems without replacing human judgment—reducing the friction between thinking clearly and making something real, while preserving the human texture that makes work meaningful.


Brian White
COFOUNDER, HOWDY

Brian is the cofounder of Howdy, a brand and marketing studio supporting both early stage startups and established household names like the Obama Foundation, Sanrio, and Wonder.

When it comes to AI, Brian is equal parts optimist and cynic. He sees its potential for freeing the workforce from tedious repetitive tasks while staying critical of the bar for quality across solutions.

Howdy created an AI-supported brand and marketing sprint, Leapday.ai. It combines senior-level expertise with AI speeds to meet the needs of startup speeds and budgets.

—


RSVP & Arrival
Please note that RSVPing does not guarantee entry. Capacity is limited, and seating will be available on a first come, first served basis. We strongly encourage you to arrive early and allow enough time for travel and entry, as we may not be able to accommodate additional guests once the room reaches capacity.
—
Hosted by Howdy (www.howdy.nyc), a brand and marketing studio focusing on impactful creative content for startup clients.

title: Ancient Wisdom Meets Modern Fertility: A Sound Bath + Conversation on Cycle Intelligence
description:
Join Lotus Fertility, NJ Fast, and The Rewilding Collective for a special sound reset and conversation on fertility wisdom in the modern age, during NY Tech Week.

This intimate gathering for female investors, founders, and corporate leaders, brings together fertility wisdom, cycle awareness, and the emerging intersection of femtech and data privacy. 

We'll open with a sound bath to settle the nervous system and create space. Then we'll move into a facilitated conversation about cycle intelligence: what it means to understand your body's internal seasons, how to honor where you are in your cycle, and how technology, when we can trust it, can deepen that self-knowledge rather than extract it.

This is a space to rest, reset, and reconnect with a kind of intelligence that isn't always talked about. 

Agenda

2:00 – 2:15pm · Arrive & settle

2:15 – 3:00pm · Sound bath, a guided sound experience to calm the nervous system

About Lotus Fertility 

Lotus bridges the gap between proactive wellness and financial security in reproductive health. By partnering with major life insurance carriers, Lotus embeds comprehensive fertility protection as a living benefit directly into term life insurance policies. This approach replaces unpredictable out-of-pocket medical expenses with a reliable financial safety net, empowering individuals to take control of their fertility and build their families on their own terms

About NJ Fast

NJ FAST is a New Jersey-based fintech accelerator founded by NJEDA, Prudential Financial, and Stevens Institute of Technology in partnership with Plug and Play. Designed to help high-potential startups scale through strategic connections, pilot opportunities, and industry-informed support, this non-dilutive program strengthens New Jersey’s position as a leading hub for financial innovation. Together, NJ FAST creates a powerful platform for collaboration between startups and established industry leaders to accelerate the future of fintech.

The Rewilding Collective designs experiential gatherings that help people reconnect with themselves through movement, play, creativity, reflection, and a deeper connection to nature. Founded by Dina Samra, the Collective creates space for people to pause, reset, and reconnect with who they are beneath the noise of modern life.

title: Building Safe Agents for Business
description:
AI agents are moving from demos to production. They execute payments, write code, access sensitive data, and ship decisions to clients without a human in the loop. 

The safety infrastructure has not kept up, and this workshop is focused on fixing that.

Today's guardrails are assertions, not proofs. They only work most of the time, fail silently in the cases that matter, and can be hijacked by a single line of injected text. Every public agent incident ends with the same question: how do you prove the agent followed the rule?

We'll learn how missing layer is cryptographic guarantees, not better prompts or more filters: mathematical proof that an agent action complies with your policy before it runs.  

This workshop teaches you how to easily build formal verification of agent policy before each action; defenses that survive prompt injection; and portable receipts your security team, counterparty and your auditor can easily verify. 

Hands-on and accessible to all - we hope to see you there!

title: How do you have a conversation with a city?
description:
Join us down on the 6-Train platform for a conversation about civic impact and conversation. We'll host friends of the Data Vandals Newsstand from 3-6pm to collect and analyze data collaboratively + meet new friends from the platform.  

We swear its a unique experience you'll not have anywhere else in the world!

Data Vandals Newsstand
As part of the MTA’s Vacant Unit Activation Program, the Data Vandals created a data-art kiosk and conversation space on the downtown 6-train platform at 51st & Lexington. 

When the space is open, it will be a place for conversation, with weekly surveys, changing exhibitions, in-person events, talks, music, and a free newspaper. Through weekly sticker votes, charts, maps, and quirky drawings, riders can share their experiences with others, opening lines of communication and dialogue. 

When closed, the outside mural details moments from New York’s history and showcases data and facts that the average strap hanger can enjoy.

In 2019, this station had 18.9 million passengers, making it the 10th-busiest station in the subway system. This translates to an average of 52,000 passengers per day.

title: The Great AI Divide: U.S., China vs the rest
host: Rest of World, Access Now, AI Now Institute
description:
AI’s promise is to level the playing field and deliver economic gains to everyone. In reality, it’s shaping a very unequal world. The U.S. and China together command 90% of global computing power, employ 70% of the world’s top AI researchers, and corner a lion’s share of AI investment. Where does this leave everyone else, with countries subject to the whims of Washington and Beijing? How can countries and communities deal with AI’s disruptive effects, including job losses and social upheaval? Is a more equitable future possible?

Speakers include: 
Rina Chandran, Deputy Editor, Rest of World
Peter Micek, General Counsel, Access Now
Amba Kak, Co-Executive Director, AI Now 
Aditya Vashistha, Assistant Professor, Cornell University
Sam Winter-Levy, Fellow, Carnegie Endowment

title: From Idea to Impact: Building Products That Actually Ship
description:
You don’t need more ideas.
You need a way to actually build and ship them.

Most builders get stuck not because they lack creativity, but because they overthink, overbuild, or don’t know what to build first. So nothing ships.

This is a hands-on working session led by AI product leader Nia Joseph.

It’s designed for founders, product operators, and builders who are past the “I have an idea” stage and stuck on:

How do I turn this into something real?

You’ll work through a simple, practical build loop used to go from idea → MVP → real user feedback.

💡 What You’ll Do
• Define a real problem worth solving
• Identify your core product assumption
• Scope the smallest possible version of your idea (MVP)
• Apply a clear build → test → iterate loop
• Turn your idea into something you can actually validate

📌 What You’ll Walk Away With
• A clear path from idea to MVP
• A structured way to stop overthinking and start building
• A working framework you can reuse for future products

🛠 Workshop Format
Not a demo. Not a panel.

A small-group working session (30 people max) focused on execution.
You’ll actively work on your own idea during the session.

Bring your laptop.

👥 Who This Is For
• Founders (pre-seed to $1M raised)
• Product builders
• Operators working on 0 → 1 ideas
• Ideal for: aspiring entrepreneurs, early-stage founders, indie builders, product managers, and operators ready to move from idea to MVP

📍 Hunter College (CUNY) – Room 603
 695 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10065
📅 Tuesday, June 2

title: Recess at Tech Week
description:
Tech Week is here!! Which means a full week of non-stop panels, pitches, and hackathons, oh my! But amidst the marathon, you're gonna need to take a break and PLAY. Here’s a way to touch some grass between panels. 

On Tuesday during Tech Week, the Recess bell will ring in Union Square Park at 3PM, 4PM, and 5PM (Yes, literally. We have an actual bell). 

Show up for classic Recess games: 4-square, kickball, crafts, field day challenges, and whatever inspiration strikes! 

THE GAME PLAN:
1. Show up, touch grass, lose track of time, remember your favorite activities from childhood
2. We'll ring the Recess bell each hour so you know when time is up!
3. Connect with a new friend over a favorite childhood activity (who knows - could turn into a future investor or job or any of those other fun things we won't speak about at Recess)

THE RULES:
1. We don't talk about tech/AI (we’re cosplaying the 90’s here, so AIM acronyms are the only tech talk that make sense)
2. We don't talk about what we do for work (no companies, no titles, no funding numbers. We're kids, remember!?)

title: Profit through Partnerships
description:
Did you know you can access non-dilutive capital in the form of infrastructure cloud credits, co-sell funding, and marketing benefits? Or that you can shorten enterprise sales cycles by selling through platforms and partners your customers already trust?

Most B2B founders think partnerships are a “later” growth lever. In reality, they are one of the fastest ways to unlock revenue, credibility, and enterprise access early.

At Profit through Partnerships, you will hear from B2B SaaS and AI companies that are using cloud ecosystems like Microsoft to accelerate growth in ways that traditional GTM cannot.

This session combines a fireside chat with operators who have done it and a hands-on workshop where you will map your own partnership strategy. You will leave with a clear understanding of where the real leverage is, what most companies miss, and the specific next steps to start unlocking revenue through partnerships immediately.

If you are building or investing in a SaaS or AI company and not actively leveraging cloud partnerships, you are likely leaving both growth and capital on the table. Join us for dessert, drinks and revenue acceleration on June 2, 3-5pm and supercharge your partnerships journey.

title: AI, Capital & CRE: What's Actually Working in the Built Environment
description:
Hosted by Relm, in partnership with Metropolis Group, JuuNoo, and Proptimal.

Commercial real estate is at an inflection point. AI is no longer a concept on a conference slide — it's being deployed across the deal lifecycle, from underwriting and diligence to permitting, financial modeling, and physical buildout. The question isn't whether the industry will change. It's who's leading it, and how.

During NYC Tech Week, join us for an intimate evening of candid conversation and high-signal networking with the operators, founders, and investors who are building the future of CRE — right now.

We'll kick off with a focused panel — AI, Capital & CRE: What's Actually Working in the Built Environment — featuring practitioners from across the real estate stack, followed by open networking in Juunoo's NYC showroom. No fluff. No keynotes. Just real talk on what's working, what's not, and where the industry is headed.

Schedule
3:00 PM — Doors Open
3:30 PM — Welcome & Panel Discussion
4:00 PM — Audience Q&A
4:15 PM — Open Networking
6:00 PM — Event Closes

Speakers:

Moderated by Jackson Reiter, Co-Founder @ Relm

Evan Robinson — CEO & Co-Founder, Relm | Evan Robinson is the CEO and Co-Founder of Relm, an AI platform purpose-built for real estate intelligence and deal analysis. His career began in public policy — working on zoning and housing reform at LA City Hall and on U.S. Congressional campaigns — before moving into growth strategy consulting for Fortune 100 firms at MediaLink. At Relm, he focuses on unifying the real estate ecosystem through automation and data intelligence. Evan holds a B.A. in Political Science from New York University.

Kristen Fortino — VP, Metropolis Group | Kristen Fortino is the VP of Business Strategy at Metropolis Group, a specialized consulting firm that acts as a liaison between real estate developers, architects, and city agencies on permitting, zoning, and code compliance. She also serves as the Founding Head of Sales & Partnerships at Relm and is the Founder of OnePoint Real Estate Group, a woman-owned firm focused on multi-family investment and code consulting. Her work sits at the intersection of real estate operations, investment strategy, and technology adoption — giving her a uniquely cross-functional view of where the industry is heading.

Maxime De Scheemaeker — CEO, Juunoo | Maxime De Scheemaeker is the Co-CEO of Juunoo's USA division, leading the company's North American expansion of its circular, demountable interior wall systems for commercial offices, schools, and healthcare spaces. Originally from Belgium, Maxime joined Juunoo in 2022 as VP of Sales to spearhead the company's US launch — building a local team from the ground up and reaching early sales targets before being elevated to Co-CEO. His background spans sales leadership and product expansion across multiple office and commercial real estate companies in Europe and the US.

Lilian Chen — CEO & Founder, Proptimal | Lilian Chen is the Founder and CEO of Proptimal, a software platform that enables any real estate team to build institutional-quality financial models without a single Excel formula. Drawing on her background in both commercial real estate and the Silicon Valley tech ecosystem, she is on a mission to put frontier AI tools in the hands of everyday operators and investors. She also writes and speaks widely on proptech as the author of The Proptech Girl, with appearances across Bisnow, NAIOP, Thesis Driven, and numerous industry podcasts and keynotes.

Space is extremely limited. RSVPs are capped and will close early.

title: Workflow Delivery in Action: Automate your work with Skywork @ NYCTechWeek
description:

This session will be presented by Skywork’s Head of Operations and will include product presentations and real-world case studies showing how Skywork works in real production environments.

title: J.P. Morgan Innovation Economy Quantum & Photonics Lightning Pitch Reception
description:
Join us for an evening of conversation, connection, and discovery at the frontier of quantum and photonics.

Last year, our J.P. Morgan Innovation Economy team gathered to celebrate 100 years of quantum mechanics — a century that took us from theory to lab and into market. The pace of progress has only accelerated. Photonics and advanced systems engineering are increasingly central to scaling quantum technologies — from interconnects and error correction to networking and sensing. The companies building at this intersection are tackling some of the most complex engineering challenges in frontier technology today.

J.P. Morgan's Innovation Economy team is bringing together the founders, investors, and technologists driving this momentum — for an honest, in-depth look at what it actually takes to build and scale quantum technology today.   


[Approved RSVP required to attend]

title: Change Agents: Tech for Sustainability
description:
What if the next breakthrough sustainability or climate solution started as a simple prototype?

Hosted by Green Project Management (GPM) and MiGSO-PCUBED (MP), this session brings together perspectives across implementation, transformation and sustainable delivery to explore how emerging technologies can move from ideation to real-world impact. 

Participants will explore how AI can support sustainability-focused challenges: from climate intelligence and circular economy systems to operational workflows and supply-chain transparency… through hands-on experimentation, prompting, research exploration, and no/low-code tools.

The session is followed by a panel discussion on what it takes to move ideas beyond the prototype stage.

Participants are encouraged to come with a sustainability-related challenge, question, frustration, or idea they’d like to explore during the session, either independently or collaboratively with others in the room. Please bring your laptop.

The goal is not to build a polished product, but to explore ideas, test concepts, and turn sustainability challenges into rough prototypes.

Spots are limited ✨

title: From Copilot to Control Plane - Visdom
description:
From Copilot to Control Plane: Building an AI-Native SDLC with VISDOM 

AI SDLC with VISDOM 2.0

Many teams start their AI journey the same way: they introduce AI coding assistants, see early excitement… and then watch usage drop and ROI disappear. 

In this live session, together with guest speakers from leading companies, we’ll share real experiences from AI adoption - what works, what breaks, and how teams are pushing through to real results. We’ll walk through how to assess where you stand today and what it takes to make your SDLC actually ready for AI with a practical demo of the VISDOM platform.


Who should join?
CTOs, VPs of Engineering, Heads of Platform, and senior engineers responsible for developer productivity and AI adoption strategy as well as senior engineers frustrated that their AI tools work in demos but not in their CI/CD pipeline.

Why it’s worth your time?
- See how an AI-ready SDLC looks in practice (live demo)
- Understand how to assess your AI maturity step by step
- Learn how to start small, validate quickly, and evolve without big upfront risk.

What to expect?
Visdom live session & platform walkthrough
A hands-on session showing how to evaluate your AI maturity and define next steps without a big-bang transformation.
- Deep repository scanning that builds the semantic context fabric AI agents actually need - not just open files, but the full dependency and intent graph across the codebase
- Blast radius scoring on an AI-generated PR before it touches CI - catching what code review misses when your team merges 3x more than last quarter
- Governed sandbox execution with full audit trails - the missing layer between "the AI suggested it" and "it's running in production"
- A live maturity assessment on a real delivery pipeline - where it stands today and the smallest concrete move that unlocks the next level

A live demo of VISDOM showing how it removes friction across context, execution, and validation, turning your SDLC into a system AI agents can reliably operate on

Speakers
Practitioners working hands-on with AI-driven delivery, developer tooling, and real-world implementation.

Bonus
We’ll be giving away signed copies of “Vibe Engineering: Best Practices, Mistakes, and Tradeoffs.”

Hosted by VirtusLab

We work with organizations from global brands (including top-tier investment banks, Databricks, Airbnb, DHL) to fast-growing startups helping build data platforms and AI systems that actually scale. At our core, we’re engineers working with engineers actively building and supporting the community through meetups, events, and shared learning.
If you're working on AI in software delivery, this is where it starts to actually work.

title: Operating MCP
description:
The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is rapidly becoming the foundational interface for agentic AI. By providing a standardized way to connect large language models to enterprise data sources and tools, it enables a shift from isolated experimentation to secure production-grade automation. This gathering is designed for practitioners to address the technical friction of operationalizing this control plane at scale. 

4:00 PM – 4:30 PM: Arrival & Networking

4:30 PM – 4:35 PM: Opening Remarks: Defining the Enterprise AI Control Plane

4:35 PM – 5:15 PM: Session 1: The Interoperability Layer

Themes:
API Authentication & Service-to-Service Trust • Cross-Cloud Connectivity • Secure Tool Access for AI Agents • Moving from Read-Only to Autonomous Enterprise Actions

4:35 – 4:45: Open Table: The Integration Tax (Audience Huddle)

Discussion around the operational friction of connecting AI systems into enterprise environments: fragmented APIs, inconsistent permissions, workflow orchestration, and secure connectivity between models and enterprise tooling.

4:45 – 5:15: Practitioner Pitches: Tetrate, Cequence

Tetrate: Service connectivity, governance, and policy enforcement for AI-enabled distributed systems

Cequence: Securing API traffic and protecting the expanding machine-to-machine attack surface created by AI agents and MCP-enabled workflows

5:15 PM – 5:55 PM: Session 2: The Operational Intelligence Layer

Themes:
Operationalizing AI Workflows • AI Decision Visibility • Agentic Process Orchestration • Behavioral Monitoring & Enterprise Oversight

5:15 – 5:25: Open Table: Can Enterprises Actually Trust Autonomous AI Workflows? (Audience Huddle)

Discussion around where organizations are struggling to operationalize AI systems safely: workflow reliability, visibility into agent behavior, orchestration across enterprise systems, and measuring business outcomes from AI deployments.

5:25 – 5:55: Practitioner Pitches: Distyl, Singulr, Terragrit

Distyl: Designing and deploying production-grade AI workflows and enterprise copilots integrated into real operational systems

Singulr: Delivering visibility, behavioral intelligence, and operational oversight across complex AI-driven enterprise environments

Terragrit: Applying operational AI and intelligent automation to industrial and field-based environments, helping enterprises bridge AI decisioning and real-world execution and operational resilience.

5:55 PM – 6:00 PM: Closing Thoughts & Transition to Happy Hour

6:00 PM – 7:00 PM: Happy Hour & Peer-to-Peer Technical Sync

Audience
Senior enterprise leaders across AI, engineering, and security (CIOs, CTOs, Heads of AI, and platform/security leaders), along with investors and builders.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Investment Game: In Pursuit of Series A [LIVE with a16z!]
description:

Across multiple rounds, founders will respond to hot-button investment questions as Kenan evaluates first impressions, compatibility, and potential dealbreakers. After each round, he'll break down what’s sparking interest and what's raising red flags - all leading up to his final choice.

Along the way, the audience will weigh in on who’s making the strongest impression and who’s headed for a missed connection.

title: Gender HealthTech: The Next Investment Frontier (Pre-Seed/Seed Founders & Active Angels/VCs)
host: Trans Health HQ, Visible Hands, VCFamilia
description:
Technology built to improve access, delivery, financing, and outcomes across women's and LGBTQ+ health is one of the most undercapitalized and highest-potential sectors in healthcare today.  This invitation-only salon brings together the founders building it, the investors funding it, and the health system and healthcare leaders who are deciding where it gets adopted next.

We'll skip the awareness conversation and get into the work: where the 2026 market opportunity actually is, where capital is moving and where it's stuck, and how founders, angels, VCs, payers, and healthcare leaders can stop operating in silos and start coordinating. Then we'll keep the conversation going, over structured networking and a salsa class.

Our afternoon together will look like this:
• Panel (30 min): CEOs and founders of leading gender healthtech companies in conversation with active investors, sharing what it actually takes to build and fund category-defining companies & hearing about investors' scars and triumphs in this space 
• Structured networking (30 min): A curated room of partner-level VCs, active angels with recent deployment history, and executives from ecosystem partners designed to make introductions that matter

RSVP now to redefine healthcare for all. Hosted by Trans Health HQ, Visible Hands, and La Familia. Space is intentionally limited.

title: Rebuilding Health Benefits for a Modern Workforce
description:
For decades, the systems that underpin work — benefits, healthcare, and financial stability — were built around a single assumption: one job, one employer, one steady income.

That assumption is breaking.

Today, millions of Americans earn across multiple roles, platforms, and income streams — a structural shift often referred to as the "microshifting workforce." But the systems designed to support them haven't kept up.

As this shift accelerates, the gaps are becoming harder to ignore: lost coverage, fragmented care, and systems that weren't designed for movement.

At the same time, new technologies, particularly AI, are beginning to reshape how individuals navigate fragmented systems, manage care, and coordinate across multiple sources of income.

Join us for a conversation with leaders Ellyn Shook, former CHRO of Accenture; Susan Podlogar, former CHRO of MetLife; and Tim Massa, EVP and CHRO of Kroger. Moderated by Bradley Tusk, founder and CEO of Tusk Ventures, with Felix W. Ortiz III, founder and CEO of Smirk Health.

This panel brings together leaders across tech, healthcare, and the future of work to explore what happens when foundational systems fall out of sync—and what's being built to replace them.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Why Corporate × Startup Usually Fail -And How They Succeed?
description:
This is a curated evening for early-stage AI founders, investors, and corporate innovation leaders exploring the future of AI collaboration.

The evening will feature a fireside chat on what it actually takes to make corporate × startup partnerships work — not the press release version, but the real one. Why do so many collaborations stall after the MOU? What does a startup actually get out of working with a large enterprise — and what should they be trying to get? How do investors evaluate corporate partnerships, and when do they become a liability?

Our speakers bring the full picture — a founder living a corporate partnership in real time, a VC who evaluates them from the outside, a researcher who builds them from inside a global enterprise, and an ecosystem builder who has seen the same patterns play out across factory floors, investment portfolios, and communities on both sides of the Pacific.

🎤 Fireside Chat Speakers

・Jason Shuman — General Partner, Primary Venture Partners. Vertical AI investor and operator-turned-VC with 10+ years building companies before joining the investment side. Lead investor in Multitude Insights and host of Change Order, a podcast on the tactics behind breakout companies.
・Sabrina Sasaki — Investment Partner, Monozukuri Ventures & Co-Founder, Deep Tech Forum. Has worked at the intersection of hardware manufacturing, startup investment, and Japan-North America ecosystem building.
・Akihiko Izu — Co-Founder, Multitude Insights. Building an AI-powered platform for public safety, backed by NEC X and Primary Venture Partners.
・Kay Seo — Senior Director & Head of Strategic Pursuits and Global Partnerships, NEC Laboratories America. 25+ years of global experience in high-tech, with a track record of building cross-sector partnerships across corporations, universities, and public institutions.
・Moderated by Tak Kihara, NEC X

Come ready to share what you're building, exchange ideas, and connect with founders, investors, and ecosystem builders over sushi and drinks. Appetizers and beverages will be available while supplies last.
A select group of startups will also take the stage to pitch and connect directly with potential investors, partners, and collaborators.
This event is hosted by NEC X, the Silicon Valley innovation arm of NEC, and home to Elev X! — a venture studio program that has helped launch and scale AI startups in the U.S. and Canada.
