title: Innovate, protect, monetize: Navigating IP rights and infringement risks in the age of AI
description:
Join Norton Rose Fulbright during New York Tech Week for an intimate happy hour at the intersection of innovation, intellectual property and artificial intelligence.

As AI reshapes how companies create, compete and commercialize, a strong IP strategy is no longer just about protection. It's about turning your intellectual assets into meaningful revenue while navigating an increasingly complex legal landscape. This evening will explore how founders, technologists and legal leaders can move beyond defensive IP strategies to actively monetize innovation in the AI age, without stepping on the rights of others.

Partner Johanna Schmitt and Counsel Sam Eichner from Norton Rose Fulbright's Brands Group, along with Stephanie DeBrow from the firm's Patent Disputes practice, will kick off the evening with a brief presentation covering timely topics, including:

Monetizing your IP
•	Copyright in the age of AI: Who owns AI-generated content? How are courts and regulators approaching authorship, fair use and training data liability?
•	Trademark strategy for AI-driven brands: Protecting and monetizing brand identity in a world of AI-generated names, logos and marketing assets
•	Patent monetization: Structuring licensing programs, identifying monetization opportunities and navigating patent disputes in emerging tech
•	Building a holistic IP portfolio: Integrating patents, trademarks, copyrights and trade secrets into a coordinated commercial strategy
•	Licensing and commercialization: How companies can structure IP licensing deals to generate revenue and strategic advantage in AI-driven markets

Avoiding infringement: What tech businesses need to know
•	Copyright clearance & AI training data: Understanding the risks of using third-party content to train AI models and how to implement responsible data sourcing practices
•	Trademark clearance and brand adoption: Why conducting thorough clearance searches before launching a new brand, product name or logo is essential, and what the consequences of skipping this step can be
•	Patent freedom to operate: How to assess whether a new product or technology infringes existing patents before going to market, and strategies for mitigating exposure
•	Open source compliance: The often overlooked IP obligations that come with incorporating open source software into commercial products
•	Third-party content and  licensing: Best practices for securing rights to images, music, text and software to avoid unintentional infringement across your platforms and products
•	Employee and contractor IP assignments: Ensuring your business actually owns the IP created by those working for it, and avoiding disputes over ownership down the line

Following the presentation, join us for drinks, conversation and networking.
 
This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Doxxing & Drinks 3.0

title: The Internet of Building Things
description:
HardTech Forum hosted by MistyWest (www.mistywest.com) and presented by Intent (https://www.withintent.com/)

---

Since Kevin Ashton coined "Internet of Things" in 1999, the promise of real-time tracking has finally become practical, built on RFID, BLE, cellular telematics, and satellite connectivity. Devices now run 10 years on a single battery, and new systems are experimenting with Bluetooth signals detected directly by satellites.

After our April 9th Vancouver HardTech Forum on The Internet of Moving Things, MistyWest and Intent are bringing the conversation to NYC with The Internet of Building Things. Construction is one of the hardest environments to digitize—chaotic, temporary, and GPS-denied—but autonomous drones, LiDAR, and adapted fleet sensor stacks are changing that fast. This HardTech Forum asks: now that we can see the physical world with unprecedented resolution, where does the real value get created, in the hardware, the data, or the platforms that turn sensor feeds into decisions?

---
SPEAKERS

Ben Williams - COO at Exyn Technologies (www.exyn.com/)

Ben is the COO/CPO of Exyn Technologies, pioneering GPS-denied autonomy in multi-modal robots. Previously, he co-founded and led Reelio (acquired by Fullscreen Media/AT&T), helped commercialize deep tech at Lockheed Martin, and earned his MBA from Wharton — where he was later named to their 40 Under 40. A former US Naval Officer and recovering engineer, he also mentors early-stage startups and serves on various boards, all while keeping his two young sons out of the snack cabinet.

Niran Shrestha - CEO at Kwant (https://www.kwant.ai/)

Niran Shrestha is the CEO of Kwant, an AI-powered industrial workforce management platform using wearable technology to improve safety, productivity, and compliance on job sites. Deployed by ENR 500 contractors like Kiewit, Turner, and Walbridge, and Fortune 200 companies including Ford, Microsoft, and General Motors, Kwant's flagship projects include Ford's Blue Oval City (24K workers), major Microsoft data centers, and Nucor's largest steel plant — with case studies showing a 50% reduction in safety incidents and an 11% productivity gain.

Conley Oster - COO & Co-Founder at Raise Robotics (www.raiserobotics.ai/)

Conley is COO and Co-Founder of Raise Robotics, deploying mobile robotic systems across construction, shipbuilding, and heavy industry to automate high-risk, labor-intensive field work. He leads commercialization, partnerships, and deployment strategy — and brings a grounded perspective from a career in construction equipment, field operations, and heavy industrial sales that informs how Raise bridges the gap from promising technology to scalable adoption on real jobsites.
---

​The HardTech Forum (https://mistywest.com/hardtech-forum) series would not be possible without gold sponsors Intent, and silver sponsors Nordic Semiconductor, and Boast. We extend to them endless thanks for enabling us to host such incredible events!

Intent (https://withintent.com/) is a Polish software design and engineering firm specializing in connected devices, with clients like Oura, Bose, and Nike.

Nordic (https://nordicsemi.com/) makes the wireless chips powering the world's most advanced connected devices.

Boast (https://boast.ai/) is a Vancouver-based platform that automates SR&ED and R&D tax credit claims, helping North American hardware and deep tech companies recover up to 64% of eligible R&D expenses with less paperwork and audit risk.

title: Best Meta Ads Campaign Structure for DTC in 2026
description:
This isn’t another boring panel. It’s actually a fun one. Join us for an insightful conversation followed by drinks and bites on a rooftop with the DTC ecom crowd.

AI is changing how Meta ads work faster than most brands can keep up.

What used to be best practice just a year ago already feels outdated. This panel brings together operators and founders working hands-on with Meta ads to talk about what’s actually working right now.

We’ll cover:
• How campaign structures are changing in 2026
• What still matters and what doesn’t anymore
• How to think about testing, scaling, and consolidation
• The role of creative, data, and attribution in performance
• Real examples from brands actively spending on Meta

Our panelists:
• Yiqi Wu - Founder & CEO @ Aimerce
• Charles Tichenor - Founder @ Disrupter School
• Lee Bissonnette - CEO @ Brand X Commerce

Agenda:
5:00 pm – Doors open
5:45 – 6:30 pm – Panel at the Innovation Hub
6:30 – 8:00 pm – Rooftop hangs with drinks, snacks, and the DTC crowd

This is an invite-only event with a curated group of founders and operators, so expect a higher signal room and more brands.

About Aimerce:
Did you know that third-party cookie deprecation and iOS changes have reduced audience tracking by 40%? As a brand founder and marketer, you need to focus on your campaigns. Let Aimerce handle your pixel headaches. Meta and Google ads rely on a strong data feedback loop to train algorithms and reach the right audiences. Join the Unique AI team for an evening of Old World versus New World wines, paired with bites and great conversation. We'll be tasting our way through a curated selection of European and American wines side by side, same grapes and different worlds, in a nod to Unique AI's own journey: founded in Zurich and now making our mark in the US market.

This event brings together two kinds of people: those who invest in companies like ours and those who would use what we build at financial institutions. Expect a lively mix of structured tasting and open discussion on the trends, challenges, and opportunities shaping AI adoption across the industry.

title: The Retro
description:
Step into The Retro: a cocktail experience surrounded by artifacts from companies that didn't make it. Each piece tells a story about what was built, what mattered, and what comes next.  

This isn't a cautionary tale or a roast. It's a room that brings order to an ending most of the industry avoids talking about. Because what was built still matters—even when it ends. 

Enjoy custom cocktails and appetizers, explore curated displays throughout Pearl Box's first floor, and join the conversation about the full lifecycle of building, including closure.

Raise a glass to what didn't make it—and what comes next.

About SimpleClosure: 
SimpleClosure is the shutdown partner for founders and startups navigating the less-talked-about side of entrepreneurship. When it’s time to properly close a company, SimpleClosure handles the paperwork, legal filings, compliance requirements, and final steps founders are often left to figure out on their own. What is traditionally a confusing, months-long process becomes a clearer path forward, so founders can close cleanly, and get back to building. To learn more, visit SimpleClosure.com.

About Miro

Miro is the AI-native operating system for startups. It makes AI multiplayer, helping founders, teams, and agents work together in one shared space. • What changes between Series A → B → Exit

• How to think about personal wealth while still building
• What operators wish they did earlier

– A current Series B founder in the trenches

title: MVP to Launch: Content Credentials for Creators, Builders, and Brands #Adobe
description:
MVP to Launch: Content Credentials for Creators, Builders, and Brands 

Join Adobe for a special gathering that goes beyond the AI conversation to explore what meaningful transparency looks like across the creative ecosystem: who made something, how it was made, and why that matters, whether you're an illustrator, a builder, or a startup founder scaling to enterprise. 

Content Credentials is the global standard for digital provenance, securely attaching verifiable information to content, including photos, videos, audio, and generative AI, proving the origins of media and how it was created, composited, and edited. This information travels with the content and can be surfaced across apps, platforms, and websites. 

Drinks and heavy appetizers are on us! Given limited capacity, we'll follow up to confirm your registration.

title: SafeStops Global, Inc. Launch Party
description:
We're inviting you to witness the first public demonstration of our Law Enforcement UX prototype a mobile platform purpose-built to improve safety, clarity, and trust on both sides of a traffic stop. Drivers get a secure, organized way to share documents and send SOS alerts. Officers get context before the window rolls down. Everyone gets home safe.

This isn't a concept. This is a working prototype. And tonight, Westchester sees it first.

Join us for a live demo, a candid conversation with law enforcement leadership and elected officials about the road to implementation, and a reception celebrating Westchester County's arrival on the national public safety tech map.

This launch marks more than a product reveal  it's proof that the most important technology being built right now isn't always coming from Silicon Valley. Sometimes it's coming from right here.

title:
Consumer Crashout: The Shift Nobody's Talking About 
Founders + Funders @ iHeartRadio HQ
description:
iHeartMedia Ventures, Alumni Ventures, and Coefficient Capital invite you to pull back the curtain at iHeartRadio HQ. We're bringing together sharp minds in tech, media, and venture for an evening of honest conversation moderated by Oz Woloshyn — Peabody and Emmy Award-winning journalist and host of iHeart's TechStuff.
 
We'll get into:
•	The attention economy is broken — so what comes next? Algorithms flattened culture. Feeds got saturated. Digital burnout is real. Oz covers this weekly on TechStuff — and our investors are living it in their portfolio companies. What does it mean for consumer brands when people are simultaneously more online and more checked out than ever?
•	The consumer story nobody's telling Every week on TechStuff, Oz finds the angle everyone's missing. Tonight, we ask our panel the same question: what's the most important consumer shift happening right now that isn't getting nearly enough airtime?
 
Then John Stessel, NYC magician and mentalist, takes the stage — a fitting close to an evening spent separating signal from noise.
 
Stay after for cocktails, networking, and music. The conversations that matter most usually start after the mics go off.


About Alumni Ventures: AV is a venture co-investor managing over $1.5B AUM and backing 1,600+ portfolio companies from Pre-Seed through Series D. Consumer investments include Oura, Quince, Goodles, Sleeper, Gametime, Bluesky, and more.

About iHeartMedia Ventures: iHeartMedia Corporate Development & Ventures invests in companies across all consumer industries and companies strategic to iHeart’s business and growth.

About Coefficient Capital: Coefficient Capital leads venture and growth stage investments into businesses powering modern transformational consumer shifts.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

​​​Whether you're looking to network, learn about AgeTech in NYC, or make new friends, you'll be in great company as we all work together to make NYC and beyond the best place to grow old!

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Hackathon & Career Fair
description:
It's Tuesday. You could be at just another networking mixer. Or — you could build the project that changes your entire career trajectory. We vote for the second one.

The Career Hack is one electric evening built around a deceptively simple idea: stop building stuff for everyone else and build the thing that gets you where you're going. That side project you keep "meaning to start." The wild idea you pitch at parties but never ship. The portfolio piece that screams "hire me / fund me / partner with me." Tonight's the night it gets real.

No client specs. No boring prize categories. Just you, your laptop, good snacks, and a room full of people building toward their next big thing.

The vibe:

⚡ Pitch the project that represents your next move (60 seconds, make it spicy)
🛠️ Build a working prototype before the night's out
🧠 Trade feedback with mentors and fellow builders who get it
🏆 Walk away with something real — and bragging rights

Who's invited:
Builders, designers, engineers, founders, career-changers, and the chronically curious. Whether you're plotting a pivot or scratching a long-ignored itch, pull up a chair.

The best careers turn on something you actually made — not a resume line. So bring the idea. Bring the caffeine. Leave with proof.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Synthetic Audiences, Real Consequences
description:
Synthetic audiences and digital twins are changing how companies understand their customers. 

Used well, they close the gap between what people say and what they actually do. Used poorly, they make that gap invisible — and the decisions built on them more confident and more wrong.

Join Panoplai for an evening with founders, marketers and innovators who’ve been humbled by that gap — and who are using AI to close it. Honest conversation about what actually works, what’s overhyped, and what it looks like when you finally stop guessing about your audience.

Why come:
- Hear from practitioners who’ve built AI audience intelligence inside real companies — and hit the limits
- Get an honest take on where AI and digital twins create real value and where they produce confident nonsense
- Walk away with a clearer picture of how the best teams are making faster, better decisions about their customers

For: 
Insight professionals, marketers, innovators and strategists who make decisions about people they’re not always sure they understand.

The panel:
Neil Dixit — CEO, Panoplai
Josh Adams - Maple Leaf Foods. Brand-operator POV on making AI audience work usable inside a big consumer business.
Phil Ahad — Managing Director of Data, Cint. The data-infrastructure POV on what synthetic audiences and digital twins need to actually hold up.

Moderated by Josh Ingram, TheMostWanted.

Panel discussion followed by happy hour at Pianos NYC.

www.panoplai.com

title: Fin x Clay: Scaling CX in the AI Era
description:
When adopting customer-facing AI, companies often start with service. But now, the most forward-thinking teams are taking a more holistic approach. They're not just using an AI Agent for service – they're collapsing the walls between support, success, and sales, to build a more a connected customer experience.

For Clay, the transformation is already underway. They've embedded GTM engineering into Ops teams to drive support efficiency at scale. Support Engineering is placed at the intersection of CX teams to reduce volume by fixing issues and building internal tools. Support and Success are becoming more collaborative— moving from focusing volume management to building durable customer relationships.

Fin is building toward the same future — a single Customer Agent that delivers a seamless customer experience — from sales to service to success.

Join Declan Ivory, VP of Customer Support at Fin / Intercom, and George Dilthey, Head of Support at Clay, for an honest conversation about what scaling CX with AI actually looks like in practice — the org changes, the use cases you haven't tried yet, and where this is all heading.

This one's for teams who are past the starting line and ready to talk about what comes next.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The AI-Ready Creative: An Evening with Luma & Silverside
AI, Algorithms & Liability: What Tech Leaders & Creators Need to Know
description:
AI governance has moved from the IT department to the boardroom with mounting legal exposure. 

This session unpacks the enterprise liability landscape for tech companies, founders, and creative professionals navigating algorithm-driven products, data privacy mandates, and evolving regulatory enforcement. We'll examine what boards are demanding on AI risk and where compliance gaps are creating real legal vulnerability.

For business owners and founders operating in spaces that intersect with the creator economy, we'll dive into how AI use for creative is adding a new layer of exposure to product, content, and partnership decisions. 

Panel led by Co-Managing Partner Shaliz Romano and Entertainment Partner & Chair Novika Ishar, moderated by Founder & Managing Partner Domenic Romano. Learn more about our panelists and moderator here: https://www.romanolaw.com/the-firm/team/

If you’ve ever thought, “there’s no way that company should exist”… this is your chance to build it anyway.

You’ll get a crash course in rapidly spinning up products using tools like Lovable, Replit, and other AI platforms, then you’ll create your own startup and pitch it live like it deserves a $10M seed round.

title: The Future of Wealth: AI Insight and People
description:
The Future of Wealth: Powered by Insight, Guided by People

Human judgment in an age of intelligent systems

In a landscape shaped by both human expertise and intelligent systems, this session explores how today’s leaders are making smarter decisions around income, protection, and long-term wealth.

Hosted by Mothers to Daughters (M2D), a global platform advancing intergenerational mentorship and economic empowerment, this conversation reflects a broader mission:
to support the launch and growth of 100,000 women entrepreneurs by 2035.

In collaboration with New York Life, a trusted financial educational and community empowerment partner. 

At M2D, we view generational intelligence as a strategic advantage, where access to relationships, lived experience, and decision-making frameworks is intentionally transferred across generations to accelerate outcomes.

This session brings that philosophy into practice, offering a space where capital, technology, and human judgment converge, and where proximity leads to possibility.

This is not a traditional panel. It’s a high-signal room designed for thoughtful exchange, meaningful connections, and a deeper look into how decisions are actually made.

What to Expect:
- A curated panel of experts and founders across HR, consulting, and AI shaping how wealth is built and protected today
- Real stories behind wealth decisions — from income strategies to protection and long-term asset building
- High-signal conversations that go beyond theory into how decisions are actually made
- A thoughtfully curated room of founders, operators, and investors across capital, culture, and technology
- Light bites and drinks in an intimate, connection-driven setting
- Meaningful introductions and organic networking designed to spark long-term opportunities

title: from PMF to Scale! If Your GTM doesn't scale, let's build an agentic GTM system
description:
You closed your first customers on grit. The problem is grit doesn't compound.

Most founders hit the same wall: product-market fit is real, revenue is coming in, but growth feels like pushing a boulder uphill every single morning. Your GTM motion is a patchwork of one-off plays, manual outreach, and tribal knowledge locked in someone's head. You know it won't survive the next stage. You just haven't had time to fix it.

This workshop transforms that in real time. You will walk out with 1) clarity on the systems mindset you need to scale, 2) a working piece, and 3) a downloaded DragonflySkill for you to use during the workshop with Claude.

Where should you start? RSVP to receive via email a free GTM assessment that takes 15 minutes. This diagnostic assessment will help you determine your GTM stage.

Come ready to work!

title: Why Are Women Spending $100K+ on IVF Without Real Answers?
description:
Women’s healthcare is a trillion-dollar market still running on 1990s trial-and-error. Nowhere is this more broken—or more lucrative—than IVF, where patients routinely spend $60k+ out-of-pocket on guesswork.

Why are women blindly taking 79 different supplements when their actual biomarkers only require 18?

Join VitalHelix Happy Hour during NY Tech Week for the exclusive unveiling of our predictive AI platform. We are replacing the guesswork with data-driven accuracy to deliver fewer cycles, optimized protocols, and faster pregnancies.

🧬 Who is in the room:
• Investors & VCs: See the data, meet the founders, and look under the hood of a high-growth FemTech platform disrupting a massive private-pay market.

• Our Pilot Cohort: We are hosting a select group of women (currently in IVF, post-failed cycles, or successfully pregnant) who are validating our platform in real-time.
Come for the cocktails, stay for the paradigm shift. Let’s talk scalability, data moats, and changing the future of fertility.

FUTURE TITANS LP/GP Movie Night: The Big Short (with Greg Lippmann live commentary)
description:
Rock Yard Ventures' FUTURE TITANS presents the first-of-its-kind, exclusive private screening of The Big Short, featuring live commentary from Greg Lippmann — the Deutsche Bank trader portrayed by Ryan Gosling in the film.

The Big Short tells the story of those who saw what others missed and had the conviction to act when the world thought they were crazy.

During the film, for the first time ever, Greg will provide live commentary on how events actually unfolded, offering firsthand insight into a trade that defined an era. Following the screening, he will share lessons from building LibreMax Capital, which now manages over $13 billion in assets, and discuss conviction, risk, and navigating asymmetric opportunities.

And to make it even cooler, each attendee gets a signed copy of the book to take home!

Event Details:

Limited to 40 attendees (NOTE: we will be accepting attendees on a rolling basis. Please do not reach out after applying if you do not hear anything. If not accepted, you will automatically be added to a waitlist.) 

NOTE: incomplete registration applications will automatically be rejected.

Food and drinks provided

description:
Join Databricks Ventures and VC Friends for a founders and investors meetup!

Amidst the chaos of NY Tech Week, we’re carving out a focused, curated space for people building and backing data and AI startups. No panels, no pitches on stage, no sitting around—just sharp conversations, real connections, and space to talk honestly about what’s working (and what isn’t).

Databricks Ventures invests in innovative companies that share our view of the future for data, analytics and AI. Our focus is on supporting early- and growth-stage companies that are empowering AI in innovative ways on top of or alongside the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform.

title: Building with AI: What Founders Need to Know
description:
A curated speaking panel and high-quality networking event at Tech Week for founders who are actively building with AI and want to compare notes with peers who are in the arena -- shipping, learning, and adjusting in real time.

The best conversations between builders are still happening in private. We are putting them on stage.
Every panelist comes with something specific: a pricing model they got wrong, a technical decision that set them back months, a sales motion they scrapped and rebuilt. No slides. No safe takes. Just the decisions that actually shaped what they are building today.

If you are an early-stage founder or GTM operator working on an AI product right now, this room will be full of people solving the same problems you are.

One question on every RSVP: "What are you building with AI right now?" That is how we keep it worth your time.

No panels for the sake of panels. No badge-scanning. Just 80+ founders, operators, investors, media professionals, and ecosystem builders, all there to connect, exchange, and build.

The event is produced by the team behind Global Connect Show (GCS), a series of global events that connects hundreds of innovative brands to business stakeholders in North America, Europe, MENA, and Southeast Asia.

title: AI Builders x Founders: Matchmaking
description:
Pioneering Minds AI: AI Founder Matchmaking Night
​Meet your co-founder. Join a founding team. Start building.

​Team is what makes or breaks a startup.

​Most great startups don’t fail because of ideas. They fail because the team wasn’t right. The tema lacks chemistry, conviction, loyalty, and time is too costly for startups to mess around and find out.

​This night is about one thing: finding the right people to build with. Explore the tech talents that New York has to offer

​This is not another networking event. This is a structured, high-signal room designed to help founders and builders actually find the people they can build with.

​Whether you’re:

​A founder looking for your first engineer, operator, or early team

​Looking for a co-founder

​A builder looking to join something meaningful early

​Or someone exploring ideas but ready to commit when it clicks

​What makes this different
​We’ve designed the format to remove randomness and maximize real connection.

​Curated room: ~40 high-intent participants (founders + builders)

​Structured matching rounds: No awkward mingling but focused conversations

​Signal over noise: You’ll meet multiple potential collaborators in under an hour

​Designed prompts: Conversations that go beyond “what do you do?”

​You won’t leave with a stack of LinkedIn connections that you may or may not stay in touch with. You’ll leave knowing who you’d actually build with.

About Cooley:
Cooley is the go-to advisor for innovators and disruptors, helping to turn great ideas into great companies. As one of the most active global firms for early- and late-stage financings, IPOs and M&A, we combine our multidisciplinary platform with efficient, tech-enabled resources to provide clients with premium counsel through each stage as they scale. Cooley is deeply connected in the venture ecosystem, working with startups, boards, leadership teams and investors to support 7,000+ high-growth private companies reshaping the global economy. Our distinctive approach to client relationships, proactive problem-solving and team collaboration ensures clients have a legal partner to take their business to the next level. 

​About Pioneering Minds AI:
At pioneeringminds.ai, we grow AI native pioneers who build, deploy, and govern AI responsibly and put humans at the front seat. We help YOU - our AI pioneers - to truly understand AI and create real value out of AI.

Our mission is straightforward:

​​​Accelerate Innovation: Equip participants with cutting-edge AI tools to deliver rapid prototypes.

​​​Foster Collaboration: Unite diverse teams—students, engineers, and civic leaders—to tackle urban challenges head-on.

title: The Principal Club [Hosted by Rho x Draper]
description:
Join us for an intimate evening hosted by Draper Associates and Rho, bringing together a highly curated group of VC Principals and Senior Associates across New York. And this time, for tech week, please bring 1-2 of your coolest founders!

​We start every Principal Club gathering the same way, with a collection of questions designed to break the ice, drop the work voice, and help everyone get to know each other as people first. No pitching, no performative networking, just genuine connection from the start.

The rumors are true... a magican will be on site... and yes there will be drinks and bites, so you won't be thirsty or hungry! (Hopefully)

​More about the hosts:

​Draper Associates is an early-stage venture capital firm, funding the future first. We invest from pre-seed to Series A and remain industry-agnostic and opportunistic, while gravitating toward transformative ideas in AI, robotics, biotech, Biocures, defense, crypto, space, and deep tech. We're especially excited by the wild, the weird, and the unconventional — world-changing ideas that seem impossible today but will be inevitable tomorrow. The firm's founding partner is Tim Draper. Our mission is simple: Fund the people crazy enough to change the world.

​Rho is the all-in-one banking platform for startups and the accountants supporting them. With banking setup in minutes, cashback cards, and powerful tools for managing spend, paying bills, and closing the books, Rho helps high-growth startups move fast and stay focused, backed by support that goes to the end of the earth to help you win. Rho is a fintech company, not a bank. Checking and card services are provided by Webster Bank, N.A., member FDIC; savings account services are provided by American Deposit Management Co. and its partner banks.

Join us for the BGDE x STEMXUS™ Open House in Brooklyn, hosted during #NYCTechWeek—a citywide series of events uniting founders, innovators, and the broader tech ecosystem.

description:

Serena Ainslie - CoFounder of Girl Math Capital, Angel Investor

title: Lessons from People Who Have Sat on Both Sides of the Table.
description:
An intimate conversation with founders, operators, and investors who have sat on multiple sides of the table. We’ll share the real lessons behind fundraising, execution, and scaling—what actually works, what breaks, and what both sides wish they understood earlier.

Expect honest stories, tactical insights, and meaningful connections between builders and capital.

Speakers:

Toan Huynh is a Partner at Baylane Capital/Momentum Partners, where she invests in and advises early-stage B2B technology and IT services companies at the intersection of AI, data, cloud, and financial services. A tech-native operator and ex-founder whose cloud consultancy was acquired by Accenture, she brings over two decades of experience helping founders scale go-to-market, partnerships, and M&A in highly regulated industries.  She is also an EIR at Morgan Stanley's venture labs.

Elisabeth Bykoff is the Founder & CEO of Boxsy.io, an AI-powered COO platform helping startups streamline fundraising and operations. A former GTM executive at Conga, YayPay by Quadient, and Agiloft, she previously built PwC’s U.S. Salesforce practice from $0 to over $200M globally. Elisabeth was also an Entrepreneur in Residence at the Morgan Stanley Inclusive & Sustainable Ventures Lab and the author of French Landing, a memoir about immigrating to the U.S.

Oni Kabir brings 30+ years of experience across enterprise architecture, cybersecurity, risk management, and business strategy. He previously served as Chief Risk Officer for New York City’s financial systems, overseeing the security of more than $115 billion in annual transactions. Oni has also led organizations including GSC Assets, Argon Holdings, and Sora Defense, spanning hedge funds, private equity, and cyber intelligence. As both a founder and enterprise operator, he advises early-stage companies on scaling, enterprise strategy, and operational execution.

Stefan Lehmann-Karp is an Executive Director with Morgan Stanley Inclusive & Sustainable Ventures. Stefan brings over 20 years across venture capital, investment banking, and startup operations to supports early-stage founders through fundraising, growth, governance, and strategic scaling. Previously, he held leadership roles at Bridgewater Associates, Morgan Stanley, and Lehman Brothers, as well as CFO and COO positions at venture-backed startups. Stefan holds an MBA from Columbia Business School and a BA from Cornell University.

description:
You already know the truth. Longevity isn't built in a clinic. It's built in your kitchen, your calendar, your friendships, and yes, your bank account. 

For one evening during NY Tech Week, ionava, Yunara Life, and The Maze are teaming up to ask what it looks like when the way you fund your health, the way you navigate care, and the way you live every day finally line up. 

Three perspectives. One evening. One model.

- How you fund your health (ionava - https://ionava.com)
- How you navigate care and self-care (Yunara - https://yunara.life)
- How you live day-to-day (The Maze - https://www.themazenyc.com/)

The night includes a fireside chat, an open house at The Maze, and showcases from partners working across wellness and health innovation. Food will be provided.

Come for the conversation. Stay for the community.

About the Hosts:

Ionava is a financial platform designed around health and wellness, enabling individuals to direct resources toward everyday well-being while reserving liquidity for major life events. (Not health insurance.)

Yunara is rebuilding the first layer of the healthcare by transforming prevention into a lifestyle, while helping people cut through the noise of the longevity industry. 

The Maze is New York City's first alcohol-free members club, built around something rarer than access - it's built around belonging. Every element of the community is designed to foster genuine, meaningful connection among members who show up with intention.

By signing up for our event, you agree to join Club ionava and receive emails with info, news, updates, and perks about the ionava way.

Please kindly note that photography and videography will take place throughout the event. By attending, you consent to the use of your likeness, voice, and appearance in event recap, promotional, and marketing materials by ionava, Yunara Life, The Maze, and their partners.

description:
#A16Z #techweek #AI #Travel

Jetzy collaborates with A16Z techweek to present : The Future of Travel - Meet Investors and Founders 

The lifestyle travel network Jetzy invites you to join us for an inspiring evening exploring the Future of Travel—where technology, culture, and experience come together.

Connect with investors, founders, travel leaders, and innovators as we discuss:
🌍 How AI and technology are transforming travel
🌍 The rise of personalized and experiential journeys
🌍 New opportunities in global mobility and hospitality
🌍 What the next decade of travel will look like

Speakers:

Shama Zehra
Shama Zehra is the Founder & CEO of JETZY which is the world’s first social platform to connect and reward people with passion for travel, adventure and unique lifestyle experiences.  On Jetzy you can connect with like-minded people and access VIP perks and up to 70% discounts for hotels, restaurants, car rentals, yachts, private jet and many other lifestyle brands and experiences. Jetzy is transforming travel by replacing fragmented search with a personalized, conversational AI experience. By combining AI, social discovery, and seamless booking, Jetzy will turn travel planning into an intuitive, end-to-end experience designed around each individual. 

Shama is a serial entrepreneur and has built several successful businesses in technology, financial services and fashion space. Shama began her career as one of the youngest female entrepreneurs in Pakistan when she built and sold a women apparel firm 12x at age 19. Shama later moved to financial services industry where she worked at Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley before starting her own investment advisory firm Aligned where she raised more than $2 billion. Shama’s entrepreneurial vision and success has been featured on the cover of Forbes Entrepreneurs and in Crains New York, Huffington Post, Nasdaq Entrepreneurs, Columbia University’s Core Magazine and several other publications. Shama holds two MBA degrees: one in Finance & Strategy from Michigan Ross School, and second in International Business from IBA, Karachi (formed under affiliation with Wharton, University of Pennsylvania). Shama lives in New York City and enjoys travel, skiing, yoga and spending time with her family (esp her niece and nephews).

Zain Jeewanji
Zain is a globally recognized pioneer in the Insuretech space, a visionary entrepreneur, investor, speaker, humanitarian, and an insurance industry veteran. Zain created the first true travel medical insurance product in the USA and online travel insurance in 1996. Zain is a mentor, investor and advisor to numerous startups, entrepreneurs all over Silicon Valley and sits on the board of advisory of several companies. He is also on the Board of advisory of Rand Corp’s Center for Asia Pacific Policy.

An avid cricket fan, you will find him on stadiums across the globe. When not watching cricket, he is busy helping startups in marketing and advising young CEO’s, founders, & entrepreneurs on challenges they are facing. Zain lives in Silicon Valley, California


Rob Mihalko
Rob is a tech executive, investor, and business educator with 20+ years of experience building and scaling software businesses. Rob is founder and managing director of Spectus Ventures. His background spans SAP/Ariba, Microsoft, and United Airlines, and he has a particular interest in travel technology. Rob developed and teaches a course on digital marketplaces through Stanford Continuing Studies. Rob is an avid traveler and resides in Silicon Valley, California

title: Enterprise AI Happy Hour
description:
Building AI at the enterprise level? Come meeting your people.

Credal AI and the AI Platform Collective are hosting a happy hour for the practitioners actually shipping agents into production: the people navigating agent sprawl, designing governance frameworks, securing data pipelines, and building the internal platforms that enterprises run on.

There exists a large gap between the capabilities of AI and harnessing it at enterprise scale. This is a room for the people who know that gap intimately.

Come ready to talk about what's working, what's not, and what you're thinking about next.

Space is limited and the guest list is curated. RSVP to secure your spot!
______

About Credal AI

Credal.ai is the control plane for enterprise AI: a platform for building, deploying, and governing AI agents at scale. Credal is built around a security-first philosophy: agents that automatically mirror existing access controls, full audit trails, and org-wide governance policies that enforce across every team, every agent, and every chat surface. Customers like MongoDB, Wise, Checkr, and Comcast use Credal to move past proof-of-concept and into production, all without sacrificing compliance or control.

About AI Platform Collective
AI Platform Collective is a community for the people building the AI infrastructure layer inside enterprises, the platform leads, architects, and technology decision-makers shaping how their organizations adopt and scale AI.  Apply here: https://aiplatformcollective.com/

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Growth Mixer
AI in Finance: From Reporting to Real-Time Decision Making
Prompting Prejudice: How AI Learns — and Unlearns — Hate
description:
As AI systems become the new gatekeepers of information, they're also learning our oldest hatreds. How can we make sure the Large Language Models we have transcend Antisemitism and other hatreds from the darkest corners of the Internet?

Take part in panel unpacks the bias “under the hood" of the Gen AI and how we can address it. Hosted in partnership with Vine Ventures and US Alumni Association Reichman University. 

About the Speakers:
Daniel Kelley is the Senior Director of the Anti-Defamation League (ADL) Center for Technology and Society (CTS). 

Raquel Saxe leads strategy and program management at Jigsaw, a Google incubator that builds technology to increase human agency in the AI era. 

Benjamin Dynkin is Head of AI Governance for Global Markets at Barclays, where he drives the firm's strategy for deploying advanced AI responsibly across the business.

Margarita Lyadova is the Host & Creator, People Jew Wanna Know' 

Rabbi Mordechai is the founder of Tech Tribe, a community for Jews in tech and digital media.

description:

title: NYC RegTech and Compliance Happy Hour
description:
Join us during #NYTechWeek for a lively gathering of technology, compliance, legal, and financial services professionals exploring the next wave of RegTech innovation.

This is your chance to connect with peers, exchange insights, and discuss how AI is reshaping compliance operations, marketing review, risk oversight, and audit readiness across regulated industries. Whether you’re navigating evolving regulatory expectations, modernizing manual compliance workflows, or simply looking to meet like-minded operators, founders, and compliance leaders, this event is for you.

We’ll dive into emerging trends, real-world use cases, and the future of compliance technology—from AI-powered review and monitoring to human-led automation, governance, and scalable oversight. Expect meaningful conversations, fresh perspectives, and opportunities to expand your professional network in a relaxed, welcoming environment.

Discover Surveill:

Surveill is an AI-driven, agentic compliance platform built for legal, compliance, and financial services teams operating in highly regulated environments. Surveill’s custom-trained AI Compliance Agents reflect each firm’s unique policies, workflows, risk profile, and regulatory reality—helping teams make faster, more consistent, and more defensible compliance decisions.

From marketing compliance reviews and digital content oversight to audit-ready workflows and traceable decision-making, Surveill helps firms move beyond manual review bottlenecks and toward real-time, human-led AI compliance operations. The platform is designed to improve transparency, accelerate first-level review, support regulatory readiness, and give compliance teams the visibility they need across complex marketing, sales, and communications footprints.

Come see how Surveill is helping firms stay ahead of regulatory risk while unlocking greater speed, scale, and confidence in their compliance programs.

Don’t miss out—RSVP and be part of the RegTech conversation at NY Tech Week.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Next-Gen Social Content: AI Video Screening & Deep Dive
description:
LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/martiniart/
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/martiniart_sf/
X: https://x.com/MartiniArt_
MartiniArt: https://martini.art/

Join us for an exclusive AI video showcase and deep dive into the next generation of creator tools. Hosted by StoryFlow AI, this event is built specifically for social media creators and creative teams ready to move beyond short, disconnected AI clips.

We will showcase the Martini platform, demonstrating our node-based, canvas-centric workflow designed for crafting consistent, longer-form video narratives. During the live demo, we'll explore the technical nuances of directing "intent"—focusing on the emotional performance and specific creative goals of a scene, rather than just tweaking general metadata. Stick around after the showcase for a creator panel discussing the latest in Social Media Content Creation and AI influencers, followed by networking.

(Plus, complimentary drinks and popcorn will be provided throughout the entire event!) 🍿🥂

🗓️ Event Schedule:
6:00 PM – 6:30 PM | Doors Open & Welcome

Settle in, grab a drink and some popcorn, and meet fellow forward-thinking creators.

6:30 PM – 7:00 PM | AI Short Film Screening

Sit back and enjoy a curated selection of AI-generated short films, showcasing the cutting-edge of synthetic media storytelling.

7:00 PM – 7:45 PM | The Martini Showcase & Live Demo

Discover the Martini platform's canvas-centric workflow. We will walk through a live demonstration on how to direct "intent" for cohesive, long-form narratives.

7:45 PM – 8:30 PM | Guest Panel Discussion: The Future of Content & AI Influencers

A deep dive into social media content creation, featuring insights from creative partners like Elena Savlokhova and Cat Jin. We'll discuss actionable playbooks for building an audience with AI.

8:30 PM – 9:30 PM | Networking & Happy Hour

Connect with the StoryFlow AI team, discuss the screening, and brainstorm your next project over drinks.

title: Moving Fast, Building to Last
Building Biotech in NYC
host: Fish & Richardson PC, C10 Labs, Biolabs @ NYU Langone
description:

title: Light Dao Salon: Who Wins in the Age of AI? Labor Markets and Capital in Transition (NYC)
description:
AI is not just a technological shift - it’s a restructuring of the labor market.

White-collar roles are compressing. Entry-level pathways are breaking. Entire categories of work are being reorganized faster than new ones are being created.

At the same time, demand is rising for real, task-based capability - often in areas the market has historically overlooked or misunderstood.

We’re entering a moment where:
> Output is becoming abundant
> But signal on human capability is increasingly scarce

This salon brings together a curated group of founders, investors, and operators to explore how labor markets are evolving - and where capital is likely to flow next.

We’ll discuss:
> Where displaced talent goes - and where new demand is actually emerging
> What replaces traditional entry pathways - and who controls access to opportunity
> Which sectors expand vs. contract - and why the market may be mispricing both
> Where talent is being undervalued - and how that creates asymmetric opportunity
> What new infrastructure is needed - and which companies could define it

Join Light Dao, and conscious investors and entrepreneurs for an evening filled with connection and deep conversations with Jenny Fielding, Co-Founder & Managing Partner of Everywhere Ventures, and Shrinidhi Rao, Director of Workforce Innovation at The Burning Glass Institute.

Guest Speaker: Jenny Fielding
Jenny Fielding is one of the most active global pre-seed investors, having been the first money into 300+ startups across a portfolio worth over $10B. As Co-Founder & Managing Partner of Everywhere Ventures, she leads a thriving community of 500+ founders and operators investing across money, health, and work. Formerly Managing Director at Techstars, Jenny is a 2x founder, lawyer by training, and an Adjunct Professor at Columbia University and Cornell Tech. Her forthcoming book, Venture Everywhere, distills her global perspective on building purpose-driven companies. More info: https://www.jennyfielding.com/venture-everywhere

Guest Speaker: Shrinidhi Rao
Shrinidhi Rao is Director of Workforce Innovation at The Burning Glass Institute - a leading research organization focused on the future of work, skills-based hiring, and labor market transformation. His work sits at the intersection of workforce strategy, labor market intelligence, and economic mobility, helping organizations understand how AI, shifting workforce demands, and evolving hiring systems are reshaping opportunity and talent allocation across industries.

---

#AIandLaborforce #LightDAO #NYTechWeek #Workforce #FutureOfWork #ResponsibleTech #ImpactInvesting

title: LOCK UP AI?
host: Unmute, Healthcare Club of New York
description:
In partnership with the Healthcare Club of NY and Caseflow AI (usecaseflow.ai) for NY TECH WEEK.

New York is about to decide something the rest of the country will be watching: whether chatbot companies should be on the hook when their AI gives medical, legal, or mental health advice that causes harm.

Senate Bill S7263, sponsored by State Senator Kristen Gonzalez, would make chatbot operators civilly liable when their AI provides "substantive" advice in domains covered by professional licensing law. That's 14+ licensed professions plus the practice of law. Disclaimers don't save you. A "you're talking to a bot" notice doesn't save you. And the private right of action means anyone harmed can sue.

Supporters say it's common sense: if a human pretending to be a doctor can be prosecuted, a chatbot that hallucinates a medical license shouldn't get a free pass. Critics say the bill is vague about what counts as "substantive" advice, puts nonprofit and government chatbots in the same legal crosshairs as OpenAI, and ends up taxing the people who most need low-cost information (tenants, patients, small business owners) on behalf of licensed incumbents who don't love the competition.
Both sides have a point. That's why we're putting them in a room.

Against S7263:
Sateesh Nori: attorney, legal tech founder (LawDroid), and one of the more thoughtful voices arguing that restricting AI legal guidance hurts the people the legal system already fails.

For S7263:
Dr. Cecilia Dones: data and analytics practitioner-academic, founder of 3 Standard Deviations, and a faculty member at Columbia's School of Professional Studies. Her work sits at the intersection of AI, data, and trust: translating what these systems actually do for organizations, consumers, and the people who get caught in between.

UNMUTED: Where talking points go to die.

title: AI for HR: Confidence vs Fluency
description:
You're using AI. Your team is using AI. But do you actually understand what you're doing? We assessed 577 HR leaders and found something that should worry every CHRO: 45% think they're further along than they really are. They're confident. They're not fluent. There's a 43-point gap between how HR applies AI practically and how well they understand the people and culture implications. In this working session, we'll surface where that gap lives in your organization, why it matters, and what to do about it. This isn't a lecture. We'll dig into real scenarios, map your team's actual readiness, and walk out with a clear picture of what fluency actually looks like for your function. Bring your questions. Bring your friction points. Leave with a path forward.

title: Paid Wildfire Game: Invest in Resilience
description:
​Event Description

​The interactive wildfire workshop simulates real-world wildfire challenges with game-based learning. In the workshop, participants will collaborate and make resilience investment decisions to explore ways to mitigate and adapt to the growing wildfire risks.

​In most cases, investment will be easy if we have unlimited resources and complete information. Unfortunately, most of our decisions will have trade-offs and complex uncertainties.

​If we would love to mitigate the wildfire damages, what are the decision blind spots we should consider? And what are the climate actions we should first prioritize?

​Join our workshop and shape our future with wildfire-resilience practices.

​

​Recommended Audience

​Aspiring Climate Fellows: Advance the knowledge for wildfire mitigation and adaptation

​Social Climate Influencers: Promote wildfire knowledge and action to local communities.

​Corporate Investment: Revisit the location vulnerability with mitigation and adaptation investment.

​The workshop's main value will come from discussion and reflection. No climate background is required—just curiosity to explore more with others.

​

​Expected Outcomes

​✅ Understand the complexity and the trade-off for wildfire prevention

​✅ Explore wildfire resilience investment strategy with various actions

​✅ Strengthen cross-sector collaboration with workshop stakeholders

​Trauma-Informed Practices

​If you and/or your family members or friends have recently experienced a wildfire, please kindly self-evaluate your current condition for participation. During the workshop, if you feel uncomfortable, please notify the speaker and stop the engagement. If needed, we recommend seeking professional support.

​

​About Optivide

​Optivide is a B2C social enterprise that supports people in solving the climate problem with financial confidence. The event information is shared for wider climate stakeholders with the support of Optivide.

​As a climate stakeholder, if you would love to solve multiple climate problems, at scale, in time, with limited resources, at a highest possible success rate…

​Explore potential decision blind spots with our paid climate workshop here: https://optivide.notion.site/2f994d0420f880afa95cccf68be5bf0b?pvs=105

title: From Prototype to Production for Non-Technical Founders
description:
Your AI prototype works on your laptop. Now what?

Most non-technical founders hit a wall when their demo needs to become a real product. Auth, costs, latency, observability, evals, deploys, rollback. The things engineers take for granted are the things that scare you most.

In this 90-minute working session, we'll take a small group of non-technical founders through the same checklist Tequity uses to ship our clients' products from prototype to production:

- Audit what's actually production-blocking vs. what feels scary but isn't
- Map your real costs at 100, 1,000, and 10,000 users
- Identify the 5 things that will break first when you launch
- Write a one-page production readiness brief you can hand to a dev team (or use to hire one)

This is hands-on. Bring your prototype (or a screenshot of it). You'll leave with a checklist you can act on this week.

Who this is for:
- Non-technical founders who have a working prototype and don't know what "production" actually requires
- Founders who built something with Claude, Cursor, Lovable, or Bolt and are stuck on what comes next
- Anyone who's been told "you can't ship that" but doesn't know which parts are real

Limited to 10 people so everyone gets individual attention.

Hosted by Applied AI at Tequity, the engineering team that ships AI products for early-stage founders. Qualifying startups can also apply to your grant program.

Coffee provided.

This event is a part of NYC Tech Week, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Seats are limited to keep the conversation candid and the room peer-level.

About Concourse:

Concourse is the platform for finance teams to build AI agents. Teams connect their core data systems, automate the work that slows them down, and deploy agents into production they can trust — turning finance into a faster, sharper decision engine.

Concourse is deployed in production at companies like Palo Alto Networks, Front, Tecovas. Concourse has raised over $17M from Standard Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, CRV, Y Combinator and leading operators from OpenAI, Vercel, and Cursor.

title: 3rd Annual Partner2Partner VC Bfast
Build Your Team for the Agentic Era
description:
Build your team for the AI Era: A working session for founders and operators

Not a panel. Not a presentation. A hands-on session where you leave with something real.

Most teams are letting people go, handing work to agents, and then realizing no one knows how to manage, debug, or course-correct when things break. The org chart isn't just a people problem anymore and most teams have no framework for thinking about it.

What we'll do:

Part 1: Design Getting everyone on the same page, literally. We'll start with a shared vocabulary (what is an AI agent vs. AI, what is an AI Orchestrator, where do agents sit in a reporting structure) and then work through how to reimagine an org chart with both human and agent roles mapped explicitly. Where does discretion live? Where does transactional work get handed off? Who owns the system when it breaks?

Part 2: Implementation Once the design is clear, the harder question: how do you actually build toward it? This is where workflow architecture, change management, and the culture of how humans and agents operate together come into focus.

What you'll walk away with:

1) A reimagined version of your org structure and new roles identified, handoffs mapped. 
2) A "Before & After" org chart visualization showing where agents plug in and where humans move up to Orchestrator. 
3) An AI usage diagnostic: where you are vs. where you could be

***Bring your laptop to be able to participate in the session.

Why Britt & Mina: Britt has spent four years deploying fractional operators onto early and growth-stage teams, a model that's essentially a preview of where all teams are heading. Mina brings the AI implementation layer, including a sharp framework for thinking about agents the way we once thought about org hierarchy. Together: here's how to design it, and here's how to build it.

title: Sweat Equity: Industrials and DeepTech Founders
From Detection to Deployment: A New Era for Diabetic Foot Care
host: Solecooler, Maison de la région Occitanie à New York
hosts: Maison de la région Occitanie à New York
description:
Most diabetic foot amputations are preventable.
So why are 150,000 Americans still losing a limb every year?

The science has been settled for over a decade. The technology exists. The patients are waiting.
What's missing is the deployment layer and that's exactly what's about to break open.

Battery-free wearables. Mass-market manufacturing. New regulation pushing the industry past disposable, battery-dependent devices. 
For the first time, the economics of diabetic foot prevention work at the scale of the problem. 

This is one of the biggest unsolved categories in medtech:  tens of billions in annual US healthcare spend, millions of underserved patients, and a 24-month window before the category winners are picked.

Join us for a NY Tech Week 2026 breakfast roundtable on what it actually takes to make prevention work at scale.

Around the table: clinicians on the front lines, payers tired of paying for what shouldn't happen, investors scanning for the next medtech category, founders building in wearables and chronic care, plus the regulators and patient advocates shaping what gets deployed. 

[REGISTER NOW →]

title: The Future of Entertainment: Content, Creators and Capital
host: Manatt, The Ember Company, Animal Capital
description:
As startups and investors rethink how technology reaches consumers — and how value is created at the intersection of hardware, AI, and media — the lines between distribution, creativity, and commerce are being rapidly redrawn.

Against that backdrop, the event will open with a 20-minute “fireside chat” with Manatt Partner Eric Bergner and Dan Glassman, Senior Director and Head of New Business Development for Samsung. The conversation will focus on the intersection of hardware, AI, consumer and immersive media — and how global tech companies are shaping the distribution layer of the future as well as the future of commerce and brand building. 

The “fireside chat” will be followed by a panel with leading voices across talent, venture capital, and emerging media to explore: 

- AI as creative collaborator vs. infrastructure layer
- The economics of the modern creators, the power of AI as a tool and its risks
- The role the new AI and creator economy plays in commerce and brand building
- How to leverage or stagnate investment theses in entertainment-tech compliance, content, consumer, regulatory and related challenges

Panelists for this event include: 
- Vejay Lalla, Manatt
- Avani Patel, The Ember Company
- Marshall Sandman, Animal Capital
- Sören Wrenn, TITLES
- Po Yi, Manatt

Expect a candid, off the record conversation with time for audience Q&A, followed by informal networking with fellow founders, operators, and investors across entertainment, advertising, hardware, and consumer tech. Refreshments will be provided.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Age of Influence in B2B Marketing
description:
Traditional marketing and communications are undergoing a seismic shift as the line between B2B and B2C strategies continues to blur. Modern B2B buyers no longer exist in a vacuum of dry sales data and technical white papers; they move fluidly between expert voices in media, social influence, independent analysts, and industry peer communities.  B2B buying decisions now demand the same high-quality, personalized content experienced by consumers.

Our panel is made up of:
• Lisa Kay Davis, Associate Director at Kyndryl
• Gordy Hao, Reddit B2B
• Hope King, Founder at MacroTalk
• Tom Telford, Chief Digital Officer at Clarity

Hosted by:
• Kristen Ingraham, Managing Partner at Clarity

This panel of enterprise B2B brand experts will explore how "Clarity through Influence" has become the new standard for B2B success, focusing on the evolution of marketing, content, and communications into a human-centric ecosystem. Key themes will include:

• Social & Influencer Extensions: Leveraging executive visibility and online influencer advocacy networks to expand brand reputation beyond traditional earned media.
• The AI Gatekeeper: Understanding how AI search, GEO and LLMs increasingly act as the first interpreters of information, requiring content that is authoritative, structured, and "human-led" to be surfaced.
• Themed Content Ecosystems: Moving away from siloed content toward interconnected storytelling that addresses the "rest, motion, and work" lifestyles of C-suite decision-makers.

With marketing budgets reaching five-year lows and "shrinkflation" putting pressure on decision-makers to deliver more for less, the ability to build trust and authority as a B2B tech brand is paramount. This discussion will provide actionable insights into how to bridge the gap between high-level awareness and product-driven conversion by utilizing a mix of digital marketing, communications, and web content expertise.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Next Gen Innovation Summit - Future Builders: From Vision to Impact
description:

Convening voices from industry, academia, and government, the program will highlight the power of cross-sector collaboration in shaping the future of cities and global innovation ecosystems.

title: AI in the Warehouse: Real Results From the Floor, Not the Boardroom
Who Will Defend the Internet? The Youth Cybersecurity Crisis
description:
Light refreshments will be served ☕ 🥐

As cyber threats grow more sophisticated and widespread, the demand for the next generation of cybersecurity leaders has never been greater. Yet, young people remain significantly underrepresented and often disconnected from the field.

Moderated by Girls Who Code CEO Dr. Tarika Barrett, this panel convenes leading voices in cybersecurity education to examine the emerging gap between the scale of digital risk and the pipeline of young talent prepared to defend against it. Panelists Bianca Lewis of Girls Who Hack, Keely Quinn of DoSomething, and Vivian Yu of Girl Scouts of Greater New York will explore where cybersecurity is headed, why youth engagement in the field remains limited, and what it will take to inspire, train, and empower the next generation to help secure our digital future. title: Fast Growth, Real Pressure: Leading AI-Driven Organizations from fragmentation to alignment
description:
Fast Growth, Real Pressure: Leading AI-Driven Organizations to Alignment and Performance

Veritas Quest is excited to partner with Cure to host this #NYTechWeek workshop built for founders, product leaders, and VCs navigating high-velocity environments where performance must hold, even without clear answers.

AI is accelerating everything: product cycles, expectations, growth and competition. The pressure to grow fast and deliver results is constant.

But here’s the reality:
Most companies don’t break because of bad strategy. They break because their teams can’t stay aligned as they scale up.

As organizations grow, execution slows, priorities clash, and leaders become bottlenecks. What looks like a technical problem is often a leadership one.

In this interactive workshop, Ashveena Gajeelee (Founder, The Adaptive Leader; Harvard-trained leadership educator) will help you see what’s really happening inside your organization and what to do about it.

Through real-world tech scenarios, you’ll learn how to:
- Identify the unseen obstacles that emerge with fast growth
- Navigate resistance, misalignment, and competing priorities
- Lead under pressure: when authority, data, or AI alone aren’t enough

@veritasquestllc
Built for founders, product leaders, and VCs navigating high-velocity environments where performance must hold, even without clear answers.

If your organization is growing but execution is getting harder, this session will show you why and how to fix it.

description:
Capital to Customers: Why smart funding, clean ownership, and retentive CX matter.

How do founders turn capital into customer demand and drive scalable growth?

This session brings together leading voices across venture capital, fintech, and customer experience to break down what actually drives efficient growth. Speakers from FJ Labs, TrueBridge, Airbus Ventures, Hustle Fund, Zendesk, Efficient Capital Labs, and Bright Ventures will discuss powerful strategies for capital sourcing and allocation as well as customer acquisition, retention, and experience.

title: The Oasis @ NY Tech Week Day 3 - Finding Ikigai
host: Flowstate, Vibe Space, Boardy, The AI Collective
description:
Tech Week can be chaotic. Running between panels, pitching, networking, and feeling overstimulated. The Oasis is a daily retreat: a place to belong, breathe, and actually co-create.

We're building a persistent home base across the week where builders, founders, and creatives show up every day to flow together, have real conversations around meaningful topics, and leave refreshed and inspired. The series blends daily 'co-flow' drop-in sessions with curated collaborative programming: roundtable discussions, fireside chats, and mentor office hours. Each day seeds Flowstate Sparks, structured artifacts that keep the conversation going long after the week ends.

Day 3: Ikigai, Finding Purpose and Meaning

Today we slow down and turn inward. Using the Japanese concept of ikigai, the intersection of what you love, what you're good at, what the world needs, and what you can be paid for, we'll explore the questions that often get drowned out in the noise of building. Expect honest conversation, guided reflection, and the kind of company that helps you hear your own thinking more clearly. Whether you're between chapters, deep in one, or just sensing it's time to recalibrate, this is a space to sit with the bigger why.

With thanks to our venue partner, BELONG NYC

The Oasis is hosted at BELONG NYC (@belong.nyc), a hospitality-driven workspace and urban sanctuary in the heart of SoHo. Think airport lounge meets coworking: a high-design, high-service environment purpose-built for the in-between moments of life in motion. Beyond hosting our series, BELONG offers Flowstate members on-demand access to private meeting rooms, phone booths, and hot desks, plus showers and changing facilities, secure luggage storage, and rentable lockers throughout Tech Week. If you're juggling flights, workouts, dinners, and back-to-back meetings, it's the place to reset and keep going.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem.

title: Achieving Operational  Excellence
The (Ethical) Guide To (AI) Hacking
description:
Last week, we tried breaking into 30 startups.

20 of them had severe vulnerabilities.

AI is writing code faster than ever - and nobody is securing it. Founders are shipping new features, instead of building a digital fortress. 

The result? 

A generation of products that look polished on the surface and are wide open underneath.

This isn't a theory talk. We're doing live hacks.

Bring your laptop. Bring your ego. Leave with a clearer picture of where your product actually stands.

What to expect:
- How hackers are approaching AI-generated codebases differently
- The most common (and embarrassing) vulnerabilities we keep finding
- Live breakins - on volunteer products in the room, and public startups
- What a real security baseline looks like for a startup that's already shipped

Who this is for:
Founders - technical or not - who have a live product and genuinely don't know if it's secure. Especially if you've leaned heavily on AI to build it.

What this is not:
A vendor pitch. 
A beginner's intro to cybersecurity. 
A safe space for your tech team.

---

Seats are limited. We need volunteers willing to let us attempt to break into their product live. Sign up in the RSVP notes if you're brave enough to hear your weakness exposed, but courageous enough to take action on them.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Unlocking Defense Contracts: OTAs & Non-Dilutive Funding for Deep Tech
Ugly Talk: Can Your Brand Thrive in Agentic Commerce?
description:
💡 What’s the Ugly Truth
People are not going to shop the way they do now forever. The shift is already starting. Consumers will increasingly ask AI agents to search, compare, recommend, and in some cases buy for them.
That means brands cannot rely on the old playbook alone. If discovery moves from browsers and marketplaces to AI-led interfaces, brands risk losing attention, pricing power, customer data, and margin.
The real question is not whether agentic commerce is coming. It is whether your brand will know how to compete when the buyer is no longer a person clicking through your storefront, but an AI making the shortlist.

🎯 Why You Should Attend
-Learn what agentic commerce actually looks like right now, not just the hype.
-Understand how shopping behavior may shift over the next 5 to 10 years.
-Get clear on what parts of the old ecommerce playbook still matter, and what is starting to break.
-Learn where brands should invest now to stay discoverable, defensible, and profitable.
-Avoid expensive mistakes around channels, data, pricing, and customer acquisition.
-Meet the founders, operators, and researchers shaping this shift in real time.

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room
-Brand owners
-Ecommerce tech founders
-Retailers
-Growth and marketing leaders
-Researchers studying consumer behavior and digital commerce
-Builders working on AI shopping, search, recommendation, and agent infrastructure
-Investors focused on AI and digital commerce

🎤 Who’s Taking the Stage
Danny Smith - Agentic Commerce Solution Architect Lead at Stripe

Ali Mirzaei - Founder & CEO of Dimension Studios

Ali Mirzaei is the founder and CEO of Dimension Studios, the social commerce company behind Seller OS, an agentic AI platform that replaces TikTok's native Seller Center with a unified operational command center for brands and agencies. Ali built the #1 shop on TikTok within six months of the platform's US launch, and Dimension has since operated four #1 shops for clients including Liquid Death, Final Boss Sour, T3 Micro, SquidHaus, Bones Coffee, and more. The company is profitable at roughly $2.5M ARR with multiple product launches planned for 2026.


Joshua Cohen - Co-Founder & CEO of Pinpoint

Joshua Cohen is the founder of Pinpoint, the AI layer between social and revenue. Used by brands like Cozy Earth, Gopuff, Represent Clothing, and hundreds of others.

Moderator: 
Alan Zong - Co-Founder & Partner at UpScaleX

Alan Zong is Co-Founder and Partner at UpScaleX, a San Francisco-based seed fund investing in AI-native applications and commercial innovations. He brings 10+ years of experience across VC and CVC and has been an early backer of multiple unicorns and decacorns. Alan holds an MBA from Harvard Business School and previously led innovation investment at Alibaba International.

💬 What We Do Not Sugarcoat
-Brand loyalty may get weaker when AI optimizes for convenience, price, and speed.
-Paid media may become less effective if discovery shifts upstream into AI interfaces.
-Consumer behavior is changing faster than most brands are planning for.
-Brands may lose access to the clickstream signals that used to reveal intent, hesitation, and purchase readiness.
-Marketplaces and retailers may lose power if agents can bypass them and go more directly to sellers or alternative paths.
-Margin pressure gets worse if AI agents consistently surface the lowest-priced option.
If your brand is invisible to AI systems, it may become invisible to future buyers.
-A direct conversation for people trying to understand what happens when commerce stops being click-first and starts becoming agent-first.

📋 What Attendees Will Walk Away With
By the end of the session, attendees should leave with a clearer understanding of:
-How agentic commerce is beginning to show up in the market already
which risks and opportunities matter most for brands and commerce platforms
-Where to invest attention now, before the shift becomes obvious to everyone
-How to think about defensibility in a world where AI increasingly mediates buyer behavior
-Who else in the room may be worth building with, learning from, or partnering with 

🤝 Who's Backing This
UpScaleX - A Palo Alto–based seed fund and founders’ community investing in AI-native businesses and supporting the builders redefining how commerce is discovered, operated, and scaled.

Stripe builds programmable financial services. title: The Architecture of Failure: A 401-Level Masterclass on AI Agents
description:
UPDATE: We received an overwhelming amount of interest and due to capacity limitations, we are unable to offer any more spots for this session. All other Google for Startups NY Tech Week events will stop taking applications on Thursday, May 21st or until we reach capacity.

However, we still have space in a few other sessions listed in the Google for Startups Tech Week calendar. If you are interested in attending any of those sessions, please check out the Google for Startups Tech Week calendar and apply for entry to the session(s). We will evaluate your application(s) and you will receive a confirmation directly from Partiful.

------------------------------

Everywhere you look, people claim to be deploying AI agents successfully and yet despite following all the “best practices,” agentic workflows keep failing. Why? Because when it comes to AI architecture, there is no silver bullet.

In this advanced, 401-level session, Google Founder Advocate and AI researcher John Alioto strips away the hype to reveal why smart teams hit a wall when building and deploying agents. This deep-dive 90 minute session unpacks the foundational principles that dictate how AI systems actually work and why they. We will explore foundational and advanced concepts in large language models and software architecture. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Founder's Guide to AI Transformation: Workflows, Culture, and Scaling
description:
Join us for an interactive workshop on how Google scales with AI—and how you can apply those same frameworks to your startup architecture.

As the startup landscape in 2026 shifts toward hyper-efficiency, the goal is no longer just "integrating AI"—it's about becoming an AI-native organization. In this session, we’re opening the hood on Google’s internal operations. We’ll explore how we use our own AI tools to accelerate code generation, automate complex sales triage, and drive market intelligence, and how these internal wins serve as the roadmap for your growth.

This is a workshop-style session designed for high-potential AI founders. We will move beyond the slides to discuss real-world implementation, agentic workflows, and the specific technical hurdles of scaling in 2026.
