title: The Overlooked Alpha: Backing the Next Frontier of Faith-Driven Diaspora Entrepreneurs
description:
Institutional capital is built on access to differentiated opportunities—and the most compelling ones are often hiding in plain sight.

Join EMTIC Basileia for a private NY Tech Week gathering designed exclusively for fund of funds, LPs, and institutional allocators seeking exposure to an untapped and structurally overlooked segment: faith-driven diaspora entrepreneurs.

While traditional venture continues to crowd into known ecosystems, a new class of high-conviction founders is emerging at the intersection of global diaspora networks, values-driven leadership, and underserved markets. These founders bring asymmetric insight, built-in distribution, and a bias toward resilient, capital-efficient growth—yet remain significantly underfunded.

EMTIC Basileia is positioning itself as a first-mover in this category through a hybrid accelerator + venture model that systematically sources, develops, and backs these entrepreneurs from pre-seed through Series A.

This event will explore:

- Why diaspora founders represent a structurally mispriced asset class
- How faith-driven leadership translates into performance, discipline, and long-term value creation
- The EMTIC Basileia model as a scalable pipeline for proprietary deal flow
- Opportunities for fund of funds to gain early exposure to a differentiated venture strategy

This is not an impact story—it is an access story. One where cultural insight, conviction, and community-driven distribution create the conditions for both outsized returns and durable global impact.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Primary x Supermomos x Gunderson: Founder and Investor Lounge
host: Gunderson Dettmer, Supermomos, Primary Venture Partners
description:
NY Tech Week is nonstop. Between panels, pitches, and meetups, it can be hard to find a moment to breathe—let alone actually recharge.

Our lounge is your low-key escape in the middle of it all. Join Primary Venture Partners, Supermomos, and Catalyze, Gunderson Dettmer’s client platform for founders and investors, for a come-and-go space designed to give you a quick break between a packed schedule of events.

Drop in anytime between 11 am and 2 pm to:

- Relax: Unwind with a short chair massage between events
- Recharge: Refuel with coffee, drinks, and light snacks
- Reconnect: Work (and network!) in a Wi‑Fi‑enabled space

There’s no stage, no panels, no formal programming—just a comfortable place to land, catch up on email, trade stories with other attendees, and reset between your Tech Week events.

---

Gunderson Dettmer is the preeminent international law firm exclusively focused on the innovation economy, partnering with visionary founders, disruptive startups, and pioneering investors to build companies that reshape how we work, live, and connect. Building on this deep commitment to the startup ecosystem, Catalyze is Gunderson Dettmer’s client platform that enhances the startup journey through thoughtfully curated events, deeper connectivity with our legal team and your peers, and expanded access to resources.

Supermomos is NYC's premier platform for connecting with professional communities in technology, business, and finance. We're dedicated to bringing people together in real life to foster meaningful connections and knowledge exchange, all while facilitating the participation of female and underrepresented minority groups.

Primary is the largest dedicated seed firm in the country, with $1.6B in AUM, a 60-person team, and sector-specialized partners across Compute, Healthcare, GTM, Enterprise AI, Fintech, Cybersecurity, Vertical AI, Industrials, and Consumer. The Impact team outnumbers investors 2:1 and runs at full operating intensity year-round: 2,500 hours of embedded fractional leadership, 3,000 candidate interviews, a live pool of 5,000+ job seekers, and 250 events for the NYC tech community.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Tech for Safer Cities: Tackling Gender Based Violence for Women & Youth
host: Red Dot Foundation Global, Mobile Pathways, The CUNY Public Interest Tech Lab
hosts: The CUNY Public Interest Tech Lab
description:
Gender-based violence (GBV) remains one of the most underreported human rights violations globally, disproportionately affecting women, youth, and vulnerable communities who often face barriers to reporting, limited access to support systems, and a lack of trust in institutions.

This session brings together Red Dot Foundation Global, Catcalls of NYC, Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team and Integrated Fundraising, in collaboration with The NYC PIT Pop Up, an activation of CUNY Public Interest Technology Lab, to explore how technology can make invisible harms visible, amplify survivor voices, and strengthen public accountability.

To make the experience more participatory and hands-on, the session will be delivered as an immersive solution showcase and rotating demo lab rather than a traditional panel.

Participants will explore practical, community-driven technologies and creative approaches that address gender-based violence, strengthen civic participation, improve public decision-making, and support safer, more inclusive communities.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: The Heartland Venture Summit
description:
Why the Next Great Venture Opportunity Is in the Midwest

​During New York Tech Week, the Ohio Startup Network will host a private gathering of venture capitalists, founders, and ecosystem leaders aboard the Hornblower Lexington to explore one of the most important shifts happening in the startup landscape.

​Across the American Midwest and broader Heartland, a powerful innovation corridor is emerging. Cities like Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, Detroit, Pittsburgh, Chicago, Indianapolis, and St. Louis are producing world-class research, ambitious founders, and globally competitive startups—often operating with exceptional capital efficiency and deep technical talent. As infrastructure investment, manufacturing resurgence, and frontier technologies converge, the region is becoming one of the most compelling new frontiers for venture capital in the United States.

​The question facing investors is no longer if venture will expand beyond the coasts.

​The question is who will recognize the opportunity first.

​This curated event brings together national investors and Heartland ecosystem leaders to discuss the structural advantages of the region, highlight emerging venture opportunities, and build new relationships between coastal capital and Midwest innovation.

​Set against the backdrop of the New York skyline, this gathering offers a rare chance to meet the people building—and funding—the next generation of companies across America’s fastest-rising tech corridor.

​What to Expect

​🚀 High-signal networking with venture investors and founders
🌎 Insight into the Midwest’s emerging innovation corridor
💡 Conversations on AI, frontier tech, and capital-efficient startups
🛥️ A private New York harbor experience during Tech Week

​Come celebrate and explore the incredible venture opportunities coming to the heartland of the United States.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Leadership Bottleneck — and How to Break Through It
description:
High-growth companies rarely stall because of lack of talent or opportunity. More often, momentum slows because decisions stay psychologically open—creating hidden drag across teams, priorities, and execution. This session reveals the often-invisible leadership bottleneck that causes capable organizations to loop, delay, or split responsibility at critical moments.

In this talk, executive decision strategist Kim Groshek introduces the concept of UN-DECISION and the Decision Containment framework—showing how leaders restore clarity, reclaim authority, and create calm momentum across their organizations. You’ll leave with a practical lens for identifying decision drag in real time and a clear method for closing loops so your team can move forward with confidence.

Perfect for founders, executives, and operators navigating growth, transition, or complex decision environments. Expect sharp insight, practical frameworks, and a new way to think about leadership momentum.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

description:
You’ve built your initial product or MVP, launched your website, and maybe landed your first few customers through personal outreach or a handful of experiments.  How do you know if you’ve achieved product-market fit (PMF)?

In this panel discussion, we’ll hear from various points of views on how to get to the elusive product-market fit. We cover questions such as:

- What data and insights should you pull to understand your customers?
- What are investors like Mucker looking for from their founders when sharing that insight and data?
- What do we see as strong signals from other Seed/Series A startups who are validating PMF?

SOC Transformation: AI, Automation, and the Hard Choices Ahead- #NY TechWeek
description:
A ground-level reality check on SecOps modernization: when to rebuild, when to replace, and when to rethink entirely. Security leaders share what it actually takes to move past legacy SIEM — the tradeoffs, the blind spots, and the lessons learned.

Session Type: Panel, 45 minutes

Description: Your SIEM bill keeps growing. Visibility across your environment is still a problem. Your stack has more tools than your team can manage. And somewhere in the building, someone is asking whether there's a better way.

This session is a frank discussion about what SOC modernization actually looks like — not the pitch deck version. Three panelists, over 75 years of combined experience across enterprise security leadership and company building, will share what worked, what didn't, and what they'd do differently. Expect an honest look at the move away from legacy SIEM: the budget pressures, the operational complexity, and the real cost of getting it wrong.

title: Why Great Startups Build Communities
description:
As part of NYC Tech Week, Pollen8 and Die-Hard are bringing together founders, operators, and community builders for a focused conversation on one of the biggest shifts in startup growth: community as a core strategy.

Startups don’t win on ads the way they used to. The companies breaking through today are building engaged communities that drive acquisition, retention, and long-term loyalty from the ground up.

This session explores how early-stage and growth-stage startups are turning users into advocates, designing experiences that create real belonging, and building products that feel more like movements than tools. We’ll go beyond surface-level advice and get into what’s actually working, what’s overhyped, and where founders get it wrong.


Expect a candid panel discussion with operators actively building community-led companies, followed by intentional networking with other founders, builders, and community leaders.

Light refreshments will be provided.

Turning users into advocates
Building belonging vs. building an audience

Featured Speakers: Myles Scolnick (marimo Co-Founder, CTO) and Trevor Manz (marimo Founding Engineer)

About marimo: marimo is an open-source reactive Python notebook designed specifically for reproducible computation, rapid data manipulation, and interactive science communication. 

Unlike Jupyter, marimo notebooks are reactive, stored as pure .py (Git-friendly), executable as scripts, and easily deployable as web apps. 

marimo has been downloaded over 12 million times and is used for research and AI/ML development at companies like BlackRock, Shopify, OpenAI, Cloudflare and more.

title: Pull Up a Chair: A Coffee Salon on What It Would Take to Actually Fix Tech
host: The Make Sense Podcast w/ Lindsay T., Lady Engineer®, Leap_year, elsuss
hosts: The Make Sense Podcast w/ Lindsay T., Lady Engineer®
description:
The media hysteria frames a fight between Robot and Human, and the industry is happy to oblige.

​It's easier to have a conversation on "right vs wrong" than to admit the fundamentals are broken: skills, accountability, and the will to hold the industry to a higher standard.

​We don't have a technology problem. We have a discipline problem.

​At this Coffee Salon, join an intimate (max: 12 people) & facilitated discussion around what it takes to move past the bizarre and intrusive to achieve the economic and social outcomes it touts.

​In this session, we will look at:
• Which values we're really affirming when we turn founders into prophets — and what gets erased from the story of every company that actually lasted

• How the discipline that would have kept the focus on real problems and human interaction missed its chance to mandate a seat at the table

• Why the obsession with speed is the industry's own kind of substance problem, and which tradeoffs are being made, intentionally or not.

If you're an experienced practitioner or industry stakeholder who enjoys honest conversation and despises charlatans, please join.

​About Your Moderator: Lindsay Tabas first called out the tragedy of siloed enterprise software systems while getting her Masters at UC-Berkeley in 2006. She was told to earn her stripes before she could speak louder. She has.

​About Your Co-Hosts:

​Evan Sargent spent years as a designer, finding UX precious and slow. She then wondered if she'd just internalized speed culture. She founded Leap Year to help startups build smarter and faster. Together, if possible.

​Lori Sussle Bonanni, founder of elssus, has spent her career helping tech and healthcare companies say what they mean. She has a theory: most technology breakdowns aren't technical. They are communication problems nobody named.

​Pull up a chair.

--
This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: How To Raise VC Like A Pro: A Zero To One Hands-On Workshop
description:
After going through YC with Humoniq during the summer of 2025 and raising $8.5m, I’ll share:

​​​What VCs don’t want you to know about raising money

​​​Over 50 guides, checklists and templates for your slides

​​​How to reach out cold

​​​How to find VCs

​​​How to improve your slide deck, pitch, and outreach

​​Bring your computers, pitch decks, and emails. This is a hands-on workshop. At most, I will be speaking for the first 20 minutes, then we will get into specifics.

​

​I’m a 3x founder, operator, and executive coach working at the intersection of leadership psychology, AI systems, and company building. I’ve launched four agentic AI products generating over $1M in revenue and most recently served as Fractional COO and first employee at Humoniq (YC S25), helping raise an $8.5M seed. I’ve coached 500+ founders and executives, mentored through programs like Intel Ignite Startup Montreal and Nucleate, and I’m currently writing The Primal Trap, a book about how primitive human psychology quietly shapes leadership and organizational success. Earlier, I built and led Customer Success at Nylas through its $140M Series C and later rebuilt and relaunched the CPG brand Hot Date Kitchen after a shutdown.

title: NYTW Panel: VC + PE's Role in Women's Health
description:
The New Math: How VC and PE Are Rethinking Capital in Women's Health

Presented during New York Tech Week

Women's health is having a moment—but the capital backing it remains uneven. Investors are paying unprecedented attention to the category, driven by its scale, its underserved patient base, and a wave of clinical and commercial validation. Yet beneath the surface, the financing landscape is anything but straightforward. Capital remains concentrated in a small number of high-profile deals, early- and growth-stage companies face persistent cliffs, and founders often find themselves navigating a mismatch between the businesses they're building and the investment models available to them.

Meanwhile, the investors themselves are evolving. Venture capital is recalibrating its timelines, its risk tolerance, and its assumptions about what constitutes a durable women's health business. Private equity is playing an increasingly central role, building platforms and infrastructure with a fundamentally different approach to value creation and scale. These two worlds—once distinct—are beginning to converge in ways that could redefine how women's health companies are financed, operated, and brought to market. The question is no longer whether this sector matters. It's what kind of capital will shape its next chapter.

This panel brings together venture and private equity investors active in or adjacent to women's health to explore:

👉🏼 Where the capital is really going—and where it isn't

👉🏼 How investors assess opportunity across stages, sectors, and business models

👉🏼 The trade-offs between speed, scale, and sustainability in today's approaches

👉🏼 Whether hybrid VC/PE models are emerging—and how founders should prepare

👉🏼 What the current dynamics mean for the next generation of women's health companies

This is a candid, strategic conversation for investors, founders, and ecosystem builders who want a clear-eyed view of where women's health investing stands today—and where it's headed next.

🔅 Who Should Attend: Investors across venture, growth equity, and private equity; founders and operators in women's health and healthcare services; limited partners with healthcare mandates; and ecosystem builders interested in the intersection of capital strategy and care delivery.

title: From Wall Street to Web4: Tokenization Forum
description:
Description

As capital markets evolve, the lines between traditional finance, blockchain infrastructure, and artificial intelligence are beginning to blur. Tokenization, stablecoins, and programmable financial systems are transforming how assets are issued, traded, and managed — laying the foundation for what many are beginning to call Web4.

During NY Tech Week, CoinBridge will host an evening forum exploring the transition from Wall Street to Web4, and what it means for the future of global capital markets.

The discussion will bring together investors, founders, and financial professionals to examine how tokenized assets, on-chain infrastructure, and emerging AI-driven systems could reshape the next generation of financial networks.

As a platform focused on bridging traditional finance with on-chain economies, CoinBridge works with institutions and asset issuers to bring real-world assets and capital flows onto blockchain infrastructure, helping build the foundations of next-generation financial markets.

The evening will feature a short panel discussion followed by networking with professionals across finance, Web3, and AI ecosystems.

Space is limited. RSVP required.

title: Building agentic experiences on the frontier: Learnings from Google Antigravity
description:
Every month brings new frontier model capabilities, agent extensibility points, standards, and more. These can often be overwhelming - what will last the test of time vs just momentary fads? what should you build that will scale with future improvements at the model level? what moves the needle the most for the end user experience?

In this deep-dive presentation straddling technical and product, we will share the tenets that we’ve anchored around while we have developed AI products for the last few years, most latest being Google Antigravity, Google’s agent-first platform. We will share the decisions that we made while designing and shipping Antigravity, grounded in real examples and demonstrations of today’s frontier capabilities, and how the thinking could be applied to agentic products more generally.

The frontier constantly changes, and you’ll learn how to innovate on this constant change, not just how to maximize today’s fleeting frontier.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: How to Scale Business with People & AI
description:
Join NYC founders and GTM leaders for a practical NY Tech Week workshop on building a revenue engine that can scale with AI while keeping people, strategy, and execution at the center.

AI is changing how teams sell, market, hire, and work with outside partners. It can make strong systems stronger, but it will not fix unclear positioning, weak ICPs, disconnected sales and marketing, or teams that are not set up to execute well.

This session is for leaders who want to pressure-test their GTM approach, understand where AI can create real leverage, and think more clearly about the people and partners needed to support growth.

We’ll work through questions like:

• Is your GTM motion ready to scale, or is it creating more activity than pipeline?
• Where can AI improve the quality of sales and marketing work?
• What should stay human-led?
• What kind of team do you need for the next stage of growth?
• How should the agency/client relationship evolve as AI becomes part of execution?

✦ Workshop Circles

Kate Vasylenko, 42DM
Kate will lead a GTM checklist workshop on what to validate before launching or scaling a GTM motion, including ICP clarity, messaging, demand signals, pipeline quality, and sales/marketing alignment.

Ioanna Onasi, Dextego
Ioanna will lead an AI coaching exercise on how sales teams can use AI to improve discovery, buyer understanding, coaching, and the quality of sales conversations.

Tijana Buric, Career Alchemist
Tijana will lead a workshop on human skills and team compatibility, with a focus on the people dynamics that shape GTM execution and help teams work better together.

✦ Format

The afternoon starts with networking, food, and drinks, followed by short host introductions and rotating workshop circles of around 10 people.

After the workshop rounds, each group will share key takeaways with the room, and we’ll close with more time to connect.

3:00 PM — Networking, food, and drinks
3:30 PM — Host introductions
3:45 PM — Rotating workshop circles
5:15 PM — Group takeaways and share-back
5:45 PM — Closing networking

✦ Food Included

We’re planning summer-style catering with charcuterie, healthy lunch boards, fresh vegetables, seasonal fruit, cheeses, crackers, and light bites. The food will be easy to enjoy while networking and moving between workshop circles. No pizza.

✦ Location
Fabrik, Tribeca. Full address shared with approved guests.

✦ Arrival
Doors open at 3:00 PM. Programming starts at 3:30 PM.

✦ Parking & Transit
Street parking is limited in Tribeca. Public transit or rideshare is recommended.

✦ Who Should Join

This event is designed for B2B founders, CEOs, CROs, CMOs, VP GTM, VP Sales, VP Marketing, revenue leaders, and startup operators building or scaling B2B GTM teams.

If you are thinking seriously about how AI changes GTM, sales, hiring, and execution, come.

This event is part of NY Tech Week. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: From Content to Course Build World Class Learning Experiences with AI
description:
Learn how to turn product knowledge into structured sales enablement and build world-class learning experiences that drive customer acquisition, improve ramp time, and increase win rates.

Most teams already have product docs, call transcripts, demos, and internal knowledge. The challenge is turning all of that into scalable, sales-ready enablement systems.

title: Fast Track to the Future: Building AI-Native Apps with Firebase and Google Cloud
description:
The leap from simple client-server apps to intelligent, automated workflows is the next frontier for developers. Building these high-performance, AI-driven applications requires more than just a clever prompt—it demands seamless access to enterprise-grade infrastructure that bridges the gap between frontends and the full power of Google Cloud.

In this deep-dive technical session, we explore how Firebase has evolved into the ultimate platform for "vibe coding" and agentic development. Discover how to move beyond basic LLM integrations to build full-stack, production-ready apps that leverage relational data, instant identity verification, and collaborative AI agents.

title: Humans, Robots & The Factory Floor
host: New York Robotics, American Manufacturing Futures Institute
description:
As robotics and automation accelerate across industries, most conversations remain abstract and focused on technology capabilities rather than real world deployment. 

Robotics adoption in U.S. manufacturing is accelerating. Nearly 400,000 industrial robots are now operating in U.S. factories, with tens of thousands added each year. At the same time, manufacturing continues to face a labor gap measured in the hundreds of thousands of open roles, with millions more projected this decade. But adoption is not the same as success. Most automation challenges do not fail at the level of technology. They fail on the factory floor, where people, process, training, safety, and operating constraints
determine whether systems work or stall.

Humans + Robots & The Factory Floor brings together operators, robotics companies, and systems leaders to explore what happens when automation meets the real world of production.

This session moves to examine the lived experience of integrating robotics into active manufacturing environments.

Why This Matters Now
Robotics adoption is increasing, but deployment challenges remain widespread. Many automation efforts fail not because of the technology itself, but because of misalignment with human systems.

Workforce, training, and operational realities are too often excluded from innovation conversations.

There is a growing need to connect startups, manufacturers, and workforce leaders in a shared dialogue.

This event addresses a critical gap by focusing on the intersection of technology an

About the Hosts
New York Robotics (NYR) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing the robotics and embodied AI industry across the regional, national, and global landscape — becoming a driving force for innovation, investment, and industry growth, positioning New York as a premier global hub for robotics

The American Manufacturing Futures Institute (AMFI) is a national 501(c)(3) advancing U.S. manufacturing through the intersection of policy, people, and place.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Recess at Tech Week: Throwback Field Day
description:
Tech Week is here! Which means a full week of non-stop panels, pitches, and hackathons, oh my! But amidst the marathon, you're gonna need to take a real break — not just a drink in hand at a "networking happy hour," but an actual, moving-around, playing-with-friends kind of break.

On Wednesday during Tech Week, we're teaming up with Throwback Fitness to bring you a real Field Day. Not a pickup game. Not unstructured hang time. A produced, coached, team-vs-team field day with rotating game stations, bandanas, and a live leaderboard tracking every point.

Here's how it works: show up, get assigned a team, and compete across a rotation of field day games — think egg & spoon race, logic puzzles, and a Family Feud-style trivia mashup. Throwback coaches run everything. You just show up and play. Games are designed for all fitness levels — think brainpower and teamwork, not wind sprints and pushups.

Throwback has been running field days like this for companies like Google, Netflix, Uber, and many others since 2014. Tech Week attendees get a taste for free.

See what a Throwback experience looks like here: https://youtu.be/X5JFLSZzPlQ

THE GAME PLAN:
1. Show up, get your team bandana, meet your teammates
2. Compete across rotating game stations — Throwback coaches run the clock
3. Live leaderboard tracks every point in real time
Winning team gets eternal glory (and bragging rights through the rest of Tech Week)
4. Winning team gets eternal glory (and bragging rights through the rest of Tech Week)

title: The 10x Moat: Valuation, Competitors, and IP Rights
description:
An NY Tech Week panel + Q&A hosted by ZeroToIP
Most deep tech and AI founders treat IP as paperwork — something to get to before the next round, handled by outside counsel, forgotten until diligence. Meanwhile, the companies that treat their patent portfolio as a financial instrument are raising at multiples their competitors can't touch.
What actually changed: AI has rewritten what's patentable, what should stay a trade secret, and how investors diligence a moat. Post-Thaler, post-2024 USPTO guidance on AI-assisted inventions, the rules your lawyer learned in 2019 aren't the rules anymore. The founders who understand this are repricing their companies. The ones who don't are leaving a multiple on the table.
Join us for a candid panel with VCs who actually diligence IP, GCs from later-stage deep tech companies, and AI-native founders who've navigated the patent-or-trade-secret decision — moderated for honesty, not PR.
You'll leave with:

A concrete sense of how IP moves valuation at Series A through C
A decision framework for patent vs. trade secret in an AI stack
What to file now, before your next round prices
An IP health check you can run against your own portfolio Wednesday morning

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Agent Harnesses: What It Takes to Make AI Agents Work in the Enterprise
description:
Agent Harnesses in Practice

AI agents are advancing quickly, but turning them into dependable business systems requires more than model access. It requires a harness: the specifications, controls, validation layers, tooling, and operating patterns that make agent behavior useful, repeatable, and safe.
This New York Tech Week panel brings together practitioners working at the intersection of AI, software delivery, and enterprise operations to discuss how organizations are approaching this new engineering discipline. We’ll break the space into clear categories, examine where current tools fit, and explore practical patterns for moving from prototype to production.
The conversation will focus on real-world implementation questions: how teams define agent scope, structure workflows, validate outputs, introduce human oversight, and apply these systems inside complex existing environments. 
We are inviting engineering leaders, founders, product and platform teams, and anyone trying to understand where agent systems create real value today.

title: Liquidity for RWA Private Credit Asset Managers
description:
This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. 

Private credit is moving on-chain. The question is no longer if, it's how fast and who captures the liquidity.

Join Protofire for an evening of sharp conversation and direct networking at the intersection of private credit and decentralized finance. We're bringing together the two sides that need to be in the same room: asset managers tokenizing private credit, and the DeFi protocols and liquidity providers deploying capital into real-world yield.

What we'll cover:

The panel will cut into the mechanics that actually matter — how tokenized credit instruments access deep, persistent liquidity on-chain; what infrastructure you need to make these assets tradeable, composable, and attractive to institutional capital; and where the bottlenecks sit between origination and on-chain distribution.

This is not a surface-level overview. Expect candid discussion on the engineering, economic design, and protocol architecture that separates functioning RWA markets from static token wrappers collecting dust. Who should attend:

- Private credit fund managers and allocators exploring on-chain distribution

- DeFi protocol teams building lending, trading, or structured product infrastructure

- Liquidity providers and institutional DeFi participants seeking real-world yield exposure

- L1/L2 ecosystem teams looking to attract RWA activity and TVL

This event is hosted in partnership with Adelphi University's Manhattan Center, providing personal, professional, powerful graduate degree programs and educational opportunities for New Yorkers who never stop moving forward.

About Protofire:

Protofire is a Web3 engineering partner to the world's leading blockchain ecosystems. We architect and deploy the financial infrastructure (staking, DEXs, liquid staking, lending markets) that transforms static tokens into productive, yield-generating assets. Our work is the engine behind TVL growth and capital efficiency for protocols that refuse to be ghost chains.

Seats are limited. If you're building the liquidity layer for real-world assets, or you're the capital looking for it, this is your room.

title: When AI meets the patient
description:
Clinical AI is starting to change how care gets delivered. The harder questions are who builds it responsibly, who gets to set the bar, and who patients will actually trust.

Amigo is hosting a panel for healthcare operators, clinicians, founders, and investors who are already doing this work. The panel brings together five people who have made real decisions about AI in healthcare from very different vantage points. 

Komal Bajaj ran quality and safety across NYC Health + Hospitals and has a documented framework for the red flags she sees in AI pilots. 

Kabir Daya is Chief Digital Officer at Thriveworks, one of the country's largest behavioral health platforms, bringing the perspective on an operator deploying AI at scale.

Kathleen Leger is a triple board-certified physician in Internal Medicine, Nephrology, and Critical Care Medicine at Extension Health, specializing in personalized longevity, women's health, and hormone optimization.

Claire Siegel leads product at Humanaut Health, a longevity and preventive medicine platform building AI-enabled care, bringing the perspective of a builder who makes daily decisions about what AI touches in a patient relationship. 

Jay Shah, Chief of the Medical Staff at Stanford and Chief Medical Officer at Amigo, moderates from the seat of someone who builds and practices.

We are shaping this to be a candid conversation. Expect a room curated for honest disagreement, hot takes from people in the audience as well as on stage, and a working answer to what it takes to deploy AI in healthcare responsibly. When you walk in, we will ask you where you stand on AI in healthcare today. Where the room lands by the end is part of the conversation. Stay for bites and drinks after.

Part of NY Tech Week. Learn more at tech-week.com.

title: Building the Responsible AI City
description:
Building the Responsible AI City brings together New York's public-sector leaders, employers, workforce, and education institutions, AI builders, civic organizations, investors, and funders to examine what responsible AI adoption requires at the city scale.

Hosted at Celonis, 70th floor, in 1 World Trade Center, Building the Responsible AI City brings this conversation into one of New York's most iconic business landmarks, a fitting setting for a discussion about the infrastructure, workforce, and trust needed to shape AI adoption at the city scale.

AI adoption is already moving through New York's companies, agencies, schools, and communities. The next question is whether the city has the workforce, infrastructure, and governance capacity to make that adoption useful, trustworthy, and broadly beneficial.

The conversation will focus on strengthening AI-ready talent pipelines, modernizing digital and civic infrastructure, supporting practical AI governance, and ensuring AI-driven innovation creates economic and community benefit across New York.

title: Hard-Won Lessons from Deploying AI Agents in Regulated Environments
description:
AI agents are already running inside some of the world's largest banks, asset managers, and insurers. But deployment without governance creates real exposure: regulatory violations, failed audits, and gaps nobody saw until it was too late.

Valliappa Lakshmanan, Cofounder & CTO of Obin AI, will moderate an expert panel with: 
– Andrew Lang, Global Chief Technology Officer at JP Morgan Chase
– Virender Bedi, Managing Director, Client & Product Solutions Technology at Apollo 
– Toby Kovacs, Financial Services Account Director, at Google

Questions we'll tackle:

– Why do most enterprise AI agent deployments fail, and what separates the ones that survive?
– How do you build agents your board can defend and your regulators can audit?
– What governance frameworks are institutions actually putting in place right now?
– How do you prove ROI on AI investments when most of the value is hard to measure and the costs are easy to see?
– Who owns AI agent failures when something goes wrong: the vendor, the internal team, or the exec who signed off?

title: Agents in Production: The Memory Problem
description:
Agent memory is a mess. 

Most teams are stitching together a vector store, a graph database, and some middleware - three systems to manage, three potential failure points, and no guarantees any of it stays consistent. It works until it doesn't.

This meetup is about solving it properly. Not a quick fix, not another abstraction layer - memory that's transactional, structured, and built into the database itself. We'll show what that looks like in practice with Spectron, SurrealDB's brand new agent memory system.

We'll be joined by engineering teams from Pydantic and Tavily - covering how to structure and validate the data your agents work with, and how to handle retrieval at runtime without losing the plot mid-task.

Hard questions welcome. Drinks on us.

--Talks--

Tobie Mogan Hitchcock - Knowledge, Memory, Cognition: The Journey Towards Agent Consciousness

Jameson Lee - The Agent Harness: Memory, Observability, and Runtime Control for Production AI

Evan Rimer - Agentic Search: Context Management in Research Agents

title: Become a Superhuman: Turn Hybrid-AI into Practical Success Cases
description:
Become a Superhuman: Turn Hybrid-AI into Practical Success Cases

Join AxonJay and BelCham for an evening at the New York Tech Week exploring how to responsibly implement AI in B2B contexts. This event is designed for business decision-makers across industries, featuring real-world case studies, expert panels, and actionable insights — all centered around supercharging human capability with AI, not replacing it.

The Evening
5:00 PM – 5:10 PM · Welcome & Introductions by Jean-Philippe M.L. Schepens van Thiel

5:10 PM – 5:45 PM · Hybrid AI: Become a Superhuman in the B2B Market — How AxonJay's AI-Signal Agents augment human decision-making and what "hybrid AI" means in practice for B2B organizations.

5:50 PM – 6:20 PM · Responsible Usage of AI by Uvika Sharma — Lessons from real-world failures, governance frameworks, and what responsible adoption actually looks like beyond the buzzwords.

6:20 PM – 7:20 PM · AxonJay's Practical B2B Use Cases: Become a Superhuman — Real-world case studies showcasing how AxonJay's technology applies to organizational growth and human resource management.

· Werner Vergult (Remote): HR use case with AxonJay — how AI-driven signal intelligence transforms executive search and recruitment workflows.

· Yvan De Munck: Panel Discussion — Opening perspective on AI in capital markets and financial services, drawing on 10+ years of experience across two companies applying AI and ML to trading, asset management, and talent acquisition. 

Moderated by Jean-Philippe: Structured Q&A exploring hybrid AI in HR, competitive advantage through AI-powered recruiting, and real-world lessons from financial services.

7:20 PM – 8:00 PM · Drinks, Small Bites & Networking

8:00 PM · Optional Group Dinner for Continued Conversation


About the Speakers
🎙 Uvika Sharma — Visionary Data & AI strategist with 20+ years of experience guiding Fortune 500 companies. Co-founder of Asian Women Advancing AI (AWAAI). She focuses on responsible AI adoption, governance, and avoiding costly automation blind spots. At this event, she'll share insights on AI responsibility, decision-maker awareness, and case studies of AI failures and successes.

🎙 Werner Vergult (Founder and Managing Partner, Q4Talent) — Bridges the gap between talent and companies in technology, logistics, and retail, specializing in innovative executive search and interim management. At this event, he will share a practical AI use case in HR, offering firsthand insights into how AI is reshaping HR processes and decision-making.

🎙 Yvan De Munck (Director European Desk, YER USA) — Connects the best European companies with America's top talent, specializing in executive and professional search, international recruitment, and cross-cultural expertise. Benelux350 Ambassador. At this event, Yvan will share market insights on AI's role in HR and banking, and why AxonJay's approach is uniquely effective for global decision-makers.

🎙 Jean-Philippe (Founder & CEO of AxonJay) — Creator and entrepreneur with three successful exits in Deep-Tech, and a five-time Davos speaker. Not just a predictive AI pioneer — he's also an economist, statistician, and ornithologist, blending AI-driven precision with a deep appreciation for nature's patterns to inspire AxonJay's human-centric, sustainable AI solutions.

Why Attend?
✅ Actionable Insights: Walk away with actionable frameworks, not buzzwords. See live B2B use cases in HR and financial services.
✅ Expert Panel: Hear from AI strategists, founders & advisors.

title: From Thesis to Fund: The AI-Native Hedge Fund Competition
description:
Wednesday, Jun 3

5:00pm – 9:30pm

Hosted at Studio 260
260 W 36th St, New York, NY

For decades, the path to managing institutional capital has been gated not solely by skill, insight, or discipline — but by access to infrastructure, networks, and institutional machinery.

The world’s leading investment firms did not simply identify talented investors. They built operating systems around them: research workflows, risk frameworks, execution infrastructure, portfolio controls, and organizational discipline that transformed differentiated theses into real investment operations.

Podium exists to make that operating layer accessible to a new generation.

We believe AI, software, and modern infrastructure are fundamentally reshaping who can build, validate, and scale investment strategies. The next wave of exceptional portfolio managers may emerge not only from traditional hedge funds or investment banks, but from high-agency founders, systematic thinkers, domain experts, and technical builders who possess real edge — but have historically lacked the institutional environment to prove it.

This program is designed to identify and cultivate those individuals.

title: Robotics and LLM: LP–GP Roundtable (Amino Capital, Pillsbury)
description:
As AI enters its next phase—from copilots to autonomous systems—the convergence of Robotics (Embodied AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) is reshaping how work gets done in the physical and digital world.

In this new paradigm, capital is not just funding software—it is underwriting end-to-end intelligent systems that integrate perception, reasoning, and action. From industrial automation and service robotics to agentic workflows and real-world deployment, a new category is emerging:
👉 “Service-as-Software” powered by Robotics + LLMs

This private roundtable convenes leading LPs, GPs, family offices, and frontier founders to explore how global capital can align with this shift—and where the most asymmetric opportunities lie.

1️⃣ Capital Flows into Robotics & Embodied AI
Where is smart money moving? From hardware-enabled AI to full-stack robotics platforms, we examine capital allocation strategies across the stack.

2️⃣ LLMs as the Control Plane for the Physical World
How LLMs are evolving from copilots to decision-making engines—powering robotics, automation, and real-world workflows.

3️⃣ LP–GP Alignment in Frontier AI Investing
New expectations on fund construction, timelines, and risk in deep tech and embodied AI.

4️⃣ From Labs to Deployment: Building the Robotics & LLM University Ecosystem
Bridging hardware, developers, and capital—how to accelerate real-world adoption beyond demos.

5️⃣ Long-Term Partnerships & Global Collaboration
Cross-border challenges, trust, and structuring durable partnerships in a geopolitically complex environment.

(Please register with full name for building access.  ID is required at check-in.)

Speakers: 

Larry Li, Managing Partner at Amino Capital — 2025 Forbes Midas List honoree, 2-time Forbes Midas Seed List recipient (2023–2024); early investor in Zoom (2011); Wells Fargo AAPI Alumni of the Year (2024); inducted into the Asian Hall of Fame (2024), Board Member of Palo Alto Hills Golf and Country Club

​David T. Holland | Partner, Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman LLP

​​​Huican Zhu, PhD, Managing Partner, Amino Capital; Joined Google in 2000, and creator of Google Image Search. Board member of UC Santa Barbara Board of Trustees; Board member of Harker School.

Evan Tao is the Co-Founder and CEO of Chestnut Robotics, with background including designing the Tesla Bot (Optimus) hand and serving as a Lead Product Design Engineer for the Apple Vision Pro. He holds a Master’s in Mechanical Engineering from Carnegie Mellon University, where he specialized in soft robotics and sensor integration.

Neal Jean, Ph.D., is the Co-founder and CEO of Beacons, an AI-driven platform for over 2 million creators, who leverages his Stanford machine learning background to build advanced tools for the creator economy.

Kazuki Tanida, Sumitomo Heavy Industries USA

​Boyang Song, Portfolio Manager and Venture Investor, NataRock Partners (LP allocation perspective)

Holly Wu, Director, North America Tech M&A, Venture and Partnerships at Mercedes-Benz

​Simon Kim, Principal, SK Telecom Ventures

Jerry Wang, Executive Chairman, Faraday Future, Faraday X Robotics

Joanne Chien, Co-founder and CEO, Richoo

Sue Xu, Ph.D., Managing Partner at Amino Capital (2012-present)

​This time around, we have a restricted number of openings for the upcoming intimate event. Feel free to submit your application, and if additional space becomes accessible, we will certainly accommodate more attendees.

​We bring together an exceptional and carefully curated group of notable individuals from tech and venture. Each gathering features ample time to meet and get to know one another.

Previous events were phenomenal success, often with 120 attendees chosen from 800 applicants. We’re excited to recreate that experience with you! The feedback was glowing, and participants didn’t want the night to end. https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7122787964404830208/


🤝 Sponsorship & Partnerships (Open)
We welcome sponsorship and partnership applications to support this event and our broader community initiatives.
Benefits may include: speaking opportunities, brand visibility, panel naming rights, VIP networking, and company showcase. For details, please contact the event team to receive the sponsorship package.
​

​​About Amino Capital (https://www.aminocapital.com/)

​​AMINO Capital is a venture capital firm based in Palo Alto, with AI investment theme of data moat and network effect. With over $1 billion in capital under management, Amino has funded hundreds of companies in seed-to-growth stages across Consumer, PLG SaaS, Frontier Tech, and Blockchain, including 35 successful exits, around 20 unicorns and over 35 companies which are valued over $100M, such as Chime, Webflow, Rippling, Grail, Weee, Replit, Turing, Dfinity, OmiseGo, Oasis Labs, Instawork, Wetravel, Equityzen (acquired by Morgan Stanley, , AIFI.io, Wyze, Writesonic, D-Matrix and Beacons.ai. Amino Capital is continuously redefining the infrastructure of VC by building an AI-powered ARM (Amino Resource Management system) and LP Expert System (LPM) — real-time, transparent LP-GP collaboration.

​About Pillsbury

Pillsbury is a leading international law firm advising innovative companies, investors, and financial institutions on their most complex transactions and strategic matters. With deep expertise in venture capital, private equity, and fund formation, the firm partners closely with LPs and GPs across the lifecycle—from structuring and fundraising to cross-border investments and exits. Pillsbury is particularly known for its strong presence in technology, AI, and emerging industries, supporting clients at the forefront of innovation.


​This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Golden Handcuffs are still Handcuffs: A Financial Strategy Session for Women in Tech
description:
A candid conversation for high-earning women in tech who are doing all the “right” things with money - but still don’t feel strategic.

You’re earning well. You’re saving. You’ve opened the accounts.
But if you’re honest… it doesn’t feel like a system. And it’s not clear what it’s all building toward.

In this session, we’ll walk through a practical framework to:
	•	take your finances from scattered to structured
	•	align your money with your actual life goals
	•	and start using your income to create real flexibility - not just long-term savings

So when you think about your next chapter - whether that’s a break, a pivot, or something new - you’re actually in a position to choose it.

Includes a 45-minute session followed by Q&A and networking over wine and light bites.

title: Grow Everything LIVE: Can AI Engineer the Future of Manufacturing?
description:
Join Roebling for a Grow Everything Live: AI-Powered Engineering from Scratch podcast event.

How much capital does it take to build and operate at scale?

That question drives every major decision in deep tech (fundraising, site selection, go/no-go) and most teams are still answering it with spreadsheets, consultants, and months of waiting.

In this live recording of the Grow Everything podcast, hosts Erum Azeez Khan and Karl Schmieder will bring an industrial scale product idea to Roebling's CTO Brentan Alexander, Ph.D., who will use Roebling's AI-enabled platform to design, simulate, and cost it out on stage in real time from equipment sizing through capital costs and financial projections. Then they'll pressure-test the results live. The event will also be livestreamed and recorded for a future Grow Everything episode.

Speakers: Brentan Alexander (CTO, Roebling), Erum Azeez Khan and Karl Schmieder (Grow Everything podcast)

title: Physical AI: A Reality Check on AI’s Next Frontier by ALLSIDES & NVIDIA
description:
Artificial Intelligence is moving beyond the digital world and into the physical one. This shift, often referred to as Physical AI, is quickly becoming one of the most important frontiers in the field.

Leading AI labs, global technology companies, and a new generation of startups are building the foundations for this transition. But while progress is rapid, one question remains central: what does it actually take to make Physical AI work in practice?

NVIDIA is building the compute platforms and simulation tools. ALLSIDES is building the 3D data layer and infrastructure.

Join Franz Tschimben (CEO, ALLSIDES) and Adam Brasic (Senior Solution Architect, NVIDIA) for a fireside chat and reality check on where Physical AI stands today, what is already working, and where real value is emerging.

The conversation will explore how simulation, infrastructure, and AI come together in real-world applications, and what it takes to turn Physical AI into a viable business. It will touch on platforms such as Omniverse and Isaac Sim, the role of high-quality 3D data, and why data is increasingly becoming the bottleneck in this space.

This is a curated gathering of founders, investors, developers, and researchers working at the intersection of AI and the physical world.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Shabbat State of Mind: A Wednesday Gathering for Israeli Founders, Executives & Investors
description:
Shabbat State of Mind: A Wednesday Gathering for Israeli Founders, Executives & Investors

As Tech Week NYC kicks into high gear, join us for an evening that brings the best of Shabbat — community, reflection, and rest — to a Wednesday night in Manhattan. Step away from the noise of the conference circuit, settle in with good wine and great food, and make space for the kind of honest conversation that built the Startup Nation.
Fox Rothschild invites Israeli founders, company leaders, and investors building, scaling and backing the next generation of technology companies to come together for a night that trades the pace of the work week for something deeper — real community and genuine connection. Whether you're forming your first U.S. entity, scaling a team through hypergrowth, navigating a growth round, or deploying capital, this is a room built for you. Expect Israeli wines, Mediterranean-inspired bites, and a room full of people who understand what it takes to build something from scratch — no panels, no pitches — just the kind of unhurried, meaningful conversation that Shabbat was made for, shared with fellow founders, executives and investors shaping the Israeli tech ecosystem in New York.

Time: 5:30 PM – 7:30 PM

Location: Manhattan. Venue will be shared with confirmed attendees only 

Hosted by: Tamar Gubins and Sarah Biser at Fox Rothschild

Tamar is a business-oriented attorney who advises emerging and technology companies on formation, financing, corporate governance, and M&A throughout the full lifecycle of a technology company. Sarah Biser is co-chair of the firm’s Israel Practice Group, International Arbitration Practice Group, and Construction Law Practice Group. With more than 25 years of experience in construction and infrastructure, Sarah drafted and negotiated Technion University’s joint venture agreement with Cornell University to develop and construct a premier, state-of-the-art applied science graduate school and research facility in New York. Fox Rothschild's dedicated Israel Practice Group — recognized for six consecutive years on the Legally Israel 100 list — helps Israeli companies from startup to market leader navigate expansion into the US market. 

This is an intimate, curated gathering. Space is limited and registration is required. Priority is given to founders, executives and investors active in the Israeli and Israeli-American technology ecosystem.

title: The Future of Work: AI for People Operations
description:
AI is no longer just a technology issue, it’s a workforce and operating model challenge. 

This session gives people leaders a practical framework to define what AI capability looks like in real roles and workflows, breaking it into clear, assessable skill areas that can be developed and embedded into talent systems. It shows how to apply this framework across the employee lifecycle, linking AI capability to workforce planning, hiring, performance, learning, succession, governance, and compensation. 

The focus is on turning AI ambition into practical infrastructure: clearer roles, better assessments, targeted development, and stronger organizational readiness.

Who Should Attend:
Founders, HR Leaders & VC Firms Head of Talent

Featured Speakers:
Kenneth Matos, Ph.D., is Director, Insights Lab at HiBob.  He leads HiBob’s research on global trends in employee experience and HR best practices. Dr. Matos uses that research to educate the HiBob and media communities on the strategic impact of emerging trends in human capital management and people analytics. A former consultant to global organizations, he has published on inclusion, wellbeing, leadership, and culture. He holds degrees from Amherst, Cornell, and The George Washington University, and is a frequent speaker on engagement, flexibility, and workplace culture.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: From Pitch to Check: Preparing for Your Seed Raise
description:
Join us for Pitch to Check: Preparing for Your Seed Raise at New York Tech Week, hosted by Brown Girl Angels, Powered by Brown Girl Magazine and NineF. 

This is an intimate, community-driven evening designed to unpack how investors actually make decisions and assess investments. 
We’re bringing together a candid conversation between operators and investors to explore what happens behind the check:

• How conviction is built — beyond the pitch
• The balance between instinct and financial analysis
• What makes a founder (and a market) feel investable
• How investors think about risk, loss, and long-term potential

This is a behind-the-scenes look at how capital moves and how to position yourself when you’re in the room.

Learn From: 
Raeva Sayed — Founder & CEO, Tavia Health
Maya Bakhai — Founder & General Partner, Spice Capital
Bhargavi Varma -Founder, Brown Girl Angels

Moderated by Sravani Kallepalli & Nikhila Kulukuru - Cofounders, NineF

Following the conversation, join us for an intimate reception to continue the dialogue with founders and investors over food and drinks.

Event Flow
5:30 – 6:00 PM — Arrival & Registration
6:00 – 6:45 PM — Panel Conversation
6:45 – 8:30 PM — Reception & Networking

Tickets
Limited seating available
To secure your spot, please purchase your ticket here: https://shorturl.at/EjvCa

Please RSVP by June 1st.

About Your Host:
Brown Girl Angels is a global collective of South Asian women investors and founders, building wealth and community on our own terms. What started as a group chat became a movement to close the funding gap for South Asian female founders — through capital, connection, and access.

About Your Co-Hosts: 
Brown Girl Magazine is a mission-driven media and events platform operating at the intersection of content, community, and commerce—centering the voices, stories, and cultural influence of the South Asian diaspora. Brown Girl leads a growing ecosystem of 250+ contributors, a thriving digital platform, and its NYC-based BG Clubhouse—an in-house studio and events space designed to bring community to life through curated, high-impact experiences

NineF is an angel investing community for millennial and gen Z professionals.  Nine F is closing the gap by addressing the knowledge barriers and the imposter syndrome that keeps young investors on the sidelines.

Dress code: Business Casual

Location: BG Clubhouse 
37 E 28th St
201

For any questions, please reach out to angella@browngirlangels.com

By RSVPing, you agree to be added to the Brown Girls, Money Moves newsletter. Connecting the dots between angel investing, funding, founder stories, and community wins — delivered straight to your inbox.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: When is AI Good Enough? A conversation with builders and operators
description:
AI is moving out of the sandbox. As organizations shift from experimentation to deployment, leaders are facing the ultimate judgement call: “Should we ship it?”

Join us for a happy hour and panel conversation with leaders working across journalism, legal services, and civic technology. We’ll explore how teams set quality bars, manage risk, and decide when AI is ready for the real world.

Panelists:
- Justin Bleuel, OpenAI
- Teresa Mondría Terol, NPR
- Sanket Karuri, Code for America
- Zoë Egelman, Elego Law

Come for the conversation; stay to meet other people thinking seriously about what it takes to deploy AI responsibly.

- - - 
This event is hosted by Collected Company as part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com and collected.co

title: Coding Agents Write Code.  Remy Builds Products.
description:
The internet is full of almost-finished things.

- Half-built apps. 
- Abandoned Notion docs. 
- Figma files nobody has opened in 2 years. 
- Notes app ramblings that were definitely going to be a startup.

We're putting them on a wall and bringing them back to life.

Tiny Internet Museum is a gallery of "almosts" — an exhibit of abandoned ideas, forgotten prototypes, and digital artifacts from builders and creatives. Wander the space museum-style, leave reactions, vote on your favorites, and connect with founders, almost-founders, operators, product managers, and investors who all share the same graveyard of ideas.

Just the honest, human side of building things.

Featuring a Q&A session from Dmitry Shapiro, CEO of MindStudio, who is also the ex-CTO of MySpace, ex-PM Lead at Google, 3x founder with $140M+ raised, on what he's learned about product lifecycle across decades of building, scaling, and starting over.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/dmitry-shapiro-a2b1/

Building a product used to require a team, a budget, and months of runway. AI has changed that and the gap between idea and shipped product has never been smaller. Remy is proof. 

The world's first product agent, Remy turns your abandoned spec into a real, full-stack application: backend, frontend, database, auth, integrations, and deployment. Your idea didn't fail because it was bad. It failed because building was hard. That excuse is gone.

🎁 Submit your most abandoned ideas for a chance to win raffle prizes! Prizes include: Meta Ray-Bans, free credits, and access to Remy:
https://forms.gle/7KcJDVafUTtcZQhD7

Come wander. Come remember. Come build.

Co-hosted by LvlUp Ventures and Sip & Scale.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: How Great Teams Win in the Age of AI
description:
AI is accelerating but but team performance is not.

As organizations adopt new tools and technologies, many are running into the same challenges: unclear ownership, slower decision-making, and growing disconnects between strategy and execution.

This live webinar brings together experienced leaders to unpack what it actually takes for teams to perform in the age of AI.

This is not a passive panel, it is a guided, interactive conversation.

In this session, we will explore:

Why teams still matter in an AI-driven world
The core attributes of high-performing teams
The most common breakdowns teams experience today
How leaders can improve decision-making, alignment, and trust

The discussion will go beyond surface-level insights. Panelists will challenge assumptions, share real experiences, and work through practical scenarios that leaders are facing today.

Attendees will leave with clearer thinking, sharper questions, and a better understanding of how to strengthen their teams in an AI-driven environment.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Freeze Your Timeline: Egg Freezing Meets Big Data
description:
Join us in New York City to discuss one of the hottest topics in healthcare + tech: egg freezing, at the Sunfish office (near Grand Central Station).  

Presented by AI startup Sunfish and the Cooley Healthcare Team, at the Sunfish offices, this event will bring together medical expertise, patient testimonials, and analytical leaders for a conversation about how new data can help provide more clarity. 

Learn how your fertility intelligence (through Sunfish) can help define a target number of eggs to freeze, inform the right timeline, and support more personalized guidance, along with new financial protection models like Sunfish’s “second cycle free” package.

Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of what fertility data can (and can’t) tell you, and how AI is being used in real life for egg freezing. 

What You'll Get:
- Meet others in the New York health tech / fertility community 
- Connect with a leading reproductive endocrinologist and fertility specialist, Dr. Canon of RMA of New York
- Hear a real patient story about Egg Freezing
- Learn about Sunfish's new model and how a second cycle is included if you don't freeze your target number of eggs from Sunfish's CEO Angela Rastegar
- Leave with discounts and swag 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem in New York. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: She Ships: A Guided Build Session for Women with Ideas
description:
She Ships is a 2-hour guided build session for women who have an idea and are ready to do something about it, in the room, live. (All attendees will receive 100 complimentary Lovable credits!)

NO coding background required.
NO competition.
Just creation, community, and the moment you realize you can actually build this.

HOSTS
- Marian Bacol, Founder of Somarae + Lovable Global Ambassador
- Brooke Gramer, Host of How I AI Podcast + AI Strategist

She Ships walks you from raw idea to working product in a single session using Lovable and other AI tools. At the end of the event, attendees can demo what you built to everyone and network with others in the tech community.

WHAT YOU’LL WALK THROUGH
- Clarify and structure your idea before you touch a single tool
- A live, guided build using Lovable & Claude, the AI-powered no-code platform making software creation accessible to everyone
- A real product, shipped by the end of the session
- A celebration showcase where every builder presents what they made

THIS IS FOR YOU IF YOU’RE A...

- Founder or aspiring founder with an idea you haven’t launched yet
- Non-technical woman who’s been curious about vibe coding and AI tools
- Team member at a tech company who wants to build something for your department
- Creator, side-hustler, or anyone who just wants to make something for themselves

OUR MISSION
She Ships isn’t a hackathon. There’s no competition, no winners, no gatekeeping. Just women building in real time, being seen, and walking out knowing they can do this. (All attendees will receive 100 complimentary Lovable credits!)

Come with an idea. Leave with a product.

ABOUT YOUR CO-HOSTS

MARIAN BACOL
Seasoned community builder, TEDx speaker, 3x founder, and founder of Somarae, with 20+ years of experience spanning global corporations, tech startups, and entrepreneurship. Lovable and Connectd Global Ambassador. Certified somatic coach with formal training in breathwork and meditation, integrating neuroscience-backed, body-based practices into her work with founders and operators.
Marian’s career bridges both sides of the ecosystem, having worked inside large organizations and startups, while also building and leading her own ventures.

BROOKE GRAMER
Creator and speaker who makes emerging tech feel human and useful. Career spans New York, Los Angeles, and Miami, with 15 years leading marketing, client service, and partnerships across entertainment, SaaS, wellness, and hospitality. Certified life coach and facilitator, bringing human connection to every project and making innovation feel grounded and accessible.
Brooke hosts How I AI, an internationally recorded podcast that spotlights founders, creators, and innovators bringing AI into life. Alongside the show, Brooke serves as a marketing and AI advisor, collaborating with small teams to shape strategy.

ABOUT ANTLER
This event is being hosted at Antler NYC. We are founder's first believer at their inception stage, investing $600K as their first check, we back the most obsessed builders, pitch for funding: www.antler.co/us  

NY TECH WEEK
This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

NOTE
We value and appreciate our male allies. However, this particular workshop is designed as a space for women and those who identify as women.

TICKETING
This is a free event. Registration does not guarantee entry. Seats are limited based on venue capacity and desks are on a first come, first served basis.

title: Forward Deployed Social
description:
Your AI demo works perfectly. Your production deployment is on fire. Sound familiar?

This is the gap that Forward Deployed teams live in — and during NY Tech Week, Cresta is bringing together the builders closing it.

🏆 Cresta’s FDE organization was recently recognized as “FDE Team of the Year” at Propel26 — Huge recognition for the incredible work the org has been doing around AI agents, customer impact, and innovation.

Join Cresta’s Head of FDE, Jove Zhong, alongside our Forward Deployed Engineers and Product Managers deploying AI agents into some of the most demanding enterprise environments in the world, for an evening of technical discussion, live demos, and networking with operators working at the frontier of AI deployment.

We’ll explore topics including:
• Why vibe coding gets you to a demo — but not production
• What it actually takes to deploy reliable AI agents at scale
• Designing AI systems that know when to escalate to humans
• How Forward Deployed teams are shaping the future of enterprise AI

This is an invite-only event with limited capacity. If you're building, deploying, or obsessing over AI in production — request your spot now.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Putting the "AI" in Learning
host: Zo Computer, The Future of Higher Education
description:
Everyone is suddenly putting AI into everything. The more important question is what that actually means for learning.

During New York Tech Week, the Future of Higher Education and Zo Computer are bringing together educators, founders, operators, and investors for a focused conversation on how AI is changing learning, teaching, and the role of higher education itself.

We’ll discuss questions that are becoming impossible to ignore: What should students actually be learning now? How should teaching evolve when AI is part of the classroom and workflow? What happens to
instructional design, credentials, and the value of the institution itself?

The evening will begin with a short panel featuring leaders across education and technology, followed by a mixer for deeper conversation with people building the future of learning.

If you care about the future of how we’re going to be teaching,
learning, and growing in the decades to come, this is the panel for
you.

Zo is your intelligent home. We help you kill SaaS you don't actually need and build a system that's truly yours. You get a 24/7 agent in a personal cloud computer. With Zo, you can create simple automations through iMessage or build complex apps to run your business.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

AI in Go-to-Market Masterclass: From Single-Player GTM to Multiplayer GTM

AI has made it easier than ever to hack GTM alone with Claude Code, Clay, Cursor, agents, and automations. But real go-to-market is still a team sport - inside the company and across customers, partners, creators, communities, and investors.

This hands-on Masterclass will focus on the shift from single-player GTM to multiplayer GTM: how teams and networks can use AI to learn faster, coordinate better, and build growth systems that compound.

Join us on Wednesday, June 3, 2026 at Insight Partners for the next AI in Go-to-Market Master Class, hosted by the NYC GTM community and supported by Swan.

This event's special lineup will feature world-class GTM experts Eric Nowoslawski, Sangram Vajre, Nimo Shkedy and more - who will share their thoughts, strategies, and methods with the community. It's an event you won't want to miss!

title: Switchcraft — Build Your Own Keyboard (Anterior)
description:
Swap keys. Talk shop. Leave with a keyboard. Join us for a hands-on build night where every attendee assembles their own premium mechanical keyboard and takes it home.

Every attendee will receive a keyboard builder kit and choose between two custom keyboard designs before building their board. You’ll also get to pick from three switch types: linear, tactile, or clicky, and take home a custom switch keychain. 

Once the keyboards are built, attendees can optionally join our engineers for Type Racer battles or LAN games. (Just bring your own device if you want to participate in the games.) 

We’ll have a fully stocked fridge with every drink you can dream of, pizza for dinner, and merch bags for everyone who attends.

We’ll also be raffling off a high-value gift for one lucky attendee who applies for our Member of Technical Staff role at www.anterior.com/careers

title: AI Search: Off the Record
description:
Billions of people are asking ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini before before making a buying decision. The brands showing up in those answers aren't there by accident, and the work it takes to get there looks very different from traditional SEO.

Join Goodie for an exclusive evening on what's actually working in AI Search right now, broken into three focused sessions:
- Agentic Commerce: what brand visibility looks like when the buyer is an AI agent, not a human, and what determines who gets picked.
- Social as Infrastructure for AI Search: why Reddit, LinkedIn, and YouTube have quietly become some of the most-cited sources in LLM answers, and how to show up there.
- How to Win AEO/GEO Today: the signals, content moves, and technical fundamentals driving citations across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude and Google's AI Overviews.

We're also bringing in a curated lineup of mystery guests: operators driving AI Search visibility at some of the most recognizable brands in tech, retail, and media. Names revealed closer to the event.

Complimentary food and drinks courtesy of Carbone and Levain Bakery.

Designed for founders, search nerds, senior marketing, comms, PR, and growth leaders who want to leave with a clear, current view of how AI Search works, and what to do Monday morning. Space is limited; registrations will be reviewed to ensure a relevant peer group.

6:00 PM - Keynote session, followed by an audience Q&A
7:30 PM - Refreshments + networking

title: From Classroom to Civic Life: How Innovative Tech Is Reshaping Education and Democracy
description:
Join Retro Report, New Media Ventures and New Rising Ventures during New York Tech Week to explore how artificial intelligence and innovative technology are revolutionizing education and shaping civic participation. This event examines how educators and innovators are leveraging AI and tech tools to transform learning, foster civic awareness, and promote equity. Together, we’ll discuss the future of AI in education and its broader societal impact.

Moderated by Liz Willen, editor-in-chief of The Hechinger Report, the event features conversations with educators, entrepreneurs, and leaders at the forefront of AI, civic education, and public-interest technology. Panel discussions will explore how AI can spark civic action, nurture responsible tech practices, and prepare the next generation to tackle social challenges.

The event will include a networking reception with drinks and light bites, offering a unique chance to connect with professionals, foundations, investors and changemakers. Discover how education technology is shaping a more equitable and engaging future for all.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: AI in Construction
description:
AI is everywhere in construction right now. But where is it actually working — and where is the hype still running ahead of reality?

Join founders from the companies building vertical AI across construction's most critical workflows for an honest, unscripted conversation about what's real, what's broken, and where this is all going.

We'll cover pre-construction and estimating, field operations, and construction finance — and we won't be pulling punches on where the industry is still getting this wrong.

Joining us for the panel:

Lisa Kelly, CEO & Founder of Radical Results (moderator)

description:
Modus Salons are intimate, invite-only dinner events designed to gather senior leaders to network, learn about industry trends, and build community in this era of rapid technological change.

As AI evolves from chat interfaces to agentic systems that can reason, recommend, and act on our behalf, the role of digital experience is fundamentally changing. Instead of simply helping people find information, intelligent systems are increasingly helping them choose.

Join leaders from Amazon and Google DeepMind for a conversation on the emerging discipline of agentic design—how AI advisors are reshaping commerce, decision-making, and the relationship between brands and customers.
