title: Knowledge Systems for Chiefs of Staff
host: Ask a Chief of Staff, Notion
description:
How do the best Chiefs of Staff build systems that actually scale — not just for themselves, but for their entire org?

As a Chief of Staff, you’re often the connective tissue across teams — responsible for turning scattered information into something structured, accessible, and actionable. But what does it look like to build systems that don’t break as your company grows?

In this panel, we’ll explore how Chiefs of Staff design and manage knowledge systems that support fast-moving teams — from documentation and decision-making to cross-functional alignment. Expect practical insights, real examples, and a behind-the-scenes look at how operators are using tools like Notion to keep everything running smoothly. 

Join Notion and Ask a Chief of Staff during NY Tech Week as we dive into how high-performing operators design knowledge systems that keep teams aligned, informed, and moving fast. 

Our panel will feature...

🌟 Moderator:
Clara Ma — Founder & CEO, Ask a Chief of Staff

🌟 Panelists:

✨ Kyle Hagge — Chief of Staff @ Morning Brew Inc.
Kyle is currently Chief of Staff at Morning Brew Inc. He also co-hosts Morning Brew's career & work-life podcast Per My Last Email. Previously he worked at Marquette University, the Housing Authority of the City of Milwaukee, Ballot Ready, and AmeriCorps.

✨ Ty Rosa — Chief of Staff @ Heartshare
Ty is currently a Chief of Staff at Heartshare, a healthcare human services organization in NYC. Her work centers on the intersection of business strategy, operations, and people. She has previously worked in management consulting and venture capital.

✨ Timothy Bristow — Chief of Staff @ Edge
Tim is Chief of Staff and Head of Commercial at Edge, where he's building the AI-native workspace for commodity markets. Tim charted his career to work across functions, including Ops, Growth, Strategy, Transformation, Marketing, and Change & Culture, to forge an end-to-end toolkit that draws the best from each discipline. Leading teams for organizations that range from <10 employees to the Fortune 10, he excels at building and executing strategies that transform ideas into tangible results. Combining entrepreneurial tenacity with the rigor of a seasoned management consultant, Tim is a systems thinker with a proven track record of designing and executing complex, high-impact programs.

✨ Zsuzsanna Gedeon — Chief of Staff @ Spritz Finance

Come for the insights and connect with other Chiefs of Staff shaping how modern teams operate 🚀

title: Is Software the New Fast Fashion? Happy Hour Roundtables at #NYTechWeek
description:
Fashion's been doing this for 20 years. Tech, welcome to the chat.

This is the direction tech is headed: AI writes the code, nobody maintains it, everyone rebuilds from scratch every six months. Custom tools nobody documented. Codebases that sprawl like a Shein cart. 
(Scott's take: https://worksonmymachine.ai/p/entering-technical-debts-zirp-era)

This is what fashion has been doing for twenty years: make it cheap, make it fast, make it disposable, make it again. A closet full of things you don't remember buying and a trillion dollars in returns and waste. (Nancy's take: https://repairera.substack.com/p/earth-month-is-a-trap)

The question is whether tech learns from fashion's mistake, or repeats it at a new scale.

This Tech Week, we're hosting a happy hour of guided roundtables across tech, fashion, ops, & sustainability. 

Expert hosts leading conversations on different angles of the theme. Everyone has a chance to bring their perspective. Topics like what fast fashion does right, what tech can learn from fashion (and vice versa), infrastructure nobody sees, and what "buy it for life" means in 2026.

Expect cross-industry collaboration, a few drinks, a few laughs, and maybe some insight on how to move forward.

Tech bros, fashion girlies, investors looking for the moat, and sustainability folks who saw this coming. 
All are welcome. 

Co-hosted by 
Nancy Rhodes (Alternew, The Repair Era) and 
Scott Werner (Artificial Ruby, Sublayer).

By registering, you'll be subscribed to Works on My Machine and The Repair Era. Consider it homework for the roundtable. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Approval required.

Plug and Play is a global innovation platform that connects startups, corporations, and investors. With programs across major industries and offices around the world, they help founders scale through partnerships, pilot opportunities, and access to capital.

Plug and Play has accelerated thousands of startups and built one of the largest innovation ecosystems globally, bringing together early-stage companies and some of the most influential enterprises and investors.

---
About Raisi

Raisi is a fundraising operating system built to help founders run their raise end to end. From identifying the right investors to executing outreach and managing conversations, it replaces fragmented fundraising workflows with one system.

Since 2024, once every 50.1 minutes, a founder has met with an investor interested in their company through Raisi. The platform has facilitated 21,000+ positive connections between founders and investors and is powered by up-to-date signals from millions of live interactions across the private markets.

---
About OWM

OWM is a creator equity platform for brands and creators looking for long-term, invested partnerships. 

We help founders find the creators who actually influence their customers, and provide the equity infrastructure like a standard Influence for Equity agreement and the CSOP (Creator Stock Option Pool) with performance tracking for "earned equity”. With over 10,000+ creators looking to invest their social capital, creator ownership is our wedge in, but we’re building ownership management for all.

---

title: Unclaimed: AI, Creator Wealth, and the Inventory the Industry Never Priced
description:
The creator economy made a lot of people rich. Just not always the creators.

Join us during NY Tech Week 2026 for an evening that takes that problem seriously. Unclaimed is a live demonstration and conversation built around a simple premise: the most valuable advertising inventory in digital media has been sitting inside creator content for years, unpriced and unclaimed, and the creators who built it have never had a mechanism to access what it was worth.

FullScale brings its AI-powered product placement technology into the room and runs it live, on real content, in front of the audience. You will watch it surface placement opportunities and attach dollar values to inventory that existing ad infrastructure has never been able to see. title: AI x The Future Of Work: Social & Human Capital
description:
What is HuCapital?

​​​​​​HuCapital is an AI social platform uniting entrepreneurial creatives and professionals through 1:1, peer group and prompted matches; powered by our Al Engine Huey. Learn more at hucapital.co.

About This Event

HuCapital is teaming up with New York Tech Week to bring together founders, builders, and tech professionals for a hands-on experience exploring AI and the Future of Work. This event blends insight and execution, starting with a fireside chat featuring investors and operators shaping the future of work, followed by a hands-on vibe coding session where attendees will actively build AI-powered solutions.

Attendees will collaborate in real time using tools like Lovable to prototype solutions that address real challenges across hiring, productivity, and access to opportunity in an evolving workforce.

This experience is designed to bring together people who are not only thinking about the future of work, but actively building it!

Key Takeaways

Understand What’s Fundable:
Learn what VCs are actually looking for in the future of work space and how to position AI-driven ideas to be investable and scalable.

See How AI Is Being Applied:
Gain real insight into how founders are using AI to solve challenges across hiring, productivity, and skill development.

Navigate the Shift in Hiring & Talent:
Understand how Talent and People leaders are adapting to AI-driven changes in workforce strategy, recruiting, and evaluation.

Leverage Community as a Competitive Advantage:
Explore how social capital, networks, and ecosystems are becoming key drivers of opportunity and career mobility.

Go From Idea to Prototype:
Learn how to quickly build and test ideas using vibe coding and AI tools, and walk away with something tangible.

Meet Our Speakers

Ash Straughn | Co-Founder & CEO, SAM

Ash is the Co-Founder & CEO of Sam, an AI-powered platform that places US-based, startup-experienced talent through flexible, subscription-based engagements. With 10+ years spanning operations, marketplace scaling, and startup infrastructure, including experience at Postmates and JPMorgan Chase, Ash brings a deep understanding of what companies need at every stage. At Sam, she drives company strategy, AI product development, GTM, and partnerships, with a clear mission: make Sam the default talent infrastructure built for how startups hire in an AI-first world.

Juju Fontaine | Founder & CEO, HuCapital

Jerlisa “Juju” Fontaine is the Founder & CEO of HuCapital, an AI-powered community platform uniting entrepreneurial creatives and professionals via 1:1, peer group, and prompted matches, all powered by AI superconnector Huey. Through HuCapital, Fontaine is on a mission to transform online connections into lasting IRL relationships that spark fruitful career, entrepreneurial, and collaborative opportunities for the next generation of leaders. Fontaine is also a seasoned product manager and tech leader with over a decade of experience in digital health and professional development, having worked on AI-powered career tools, health insurance engagement systems, SMS-based programs, chatbots, and optimized EHR software.

Jonaed Iqbal | Talent Acquisition Partner, Parola

Jonaed Iqbal is a Talent Acquisition Partner at Parola, Founder of NoDegree.com, and host of The NoDegree Podcast, where he interviews people without college degrees and shares their stories to help others follow similar paths to success. Jonaed has helped over 800 candidates, with clients landing jobs and interviews at companies including Amazon, Google, Microsoft, Meta, Spotify, Tesla, TikTok, and Disney. He is committed to shattering the stigma that college is the only path to success.

Yanique Elliott | Founder & Venture Investor, Ambitious Global

Yanique is a Technologist, Entrepreneur, and Venture Investor with expertise in data privacy, risk management, operational strategy, VC due diligence, and career development. She has been recognized by Yahoo Finance as a Top 100 Future Leader and named among NYC’s Top 65 Inspiring Women in FinTech. Yanique is the Founder of Young Ambitious One (YAO), a career advancement platform helping job seekers, students, and professionals land their next roles. She is also the Founder & Managing Partner of Ambitious Global, an advisory services platform connecting businesses, entrepreneurs, and startups with leading advisors, capital, and resources for growth.

Boaz Descalo | Founder & CEO, Node8.ai

Boaz Descalo is an AI strategist, technologist, and Founder of Node8.ai, an AI advisory and implementation firm that helps companies design, build, and deploy production-ready AI systems. He also serves as an AI expert with Primary Venture Partners, is an Industry Advisor at Cornell Tech, and is a super-connector in the NYC AI startup ecosystem, building AI-first communities that connect founders, investors, and operators across the US.

description:
Are you building a startup outside the U.S. and considering the American market?

Do you have traction abroad and want to pilot, sell, fundraise, hire, or scale in the U.S.?

Are you looking for the right strategy, network, and partners to enter the U.S. well?

The U.S. can be a major growth opportunity, but it gets complicated quickly. Product positioning, go-to-market strategy, cultural context, partnerships, banking, legal setup, fundraising, and the right local network all matter early.

We’ll kick off with a short fireside conversation between Build Momentum and Holland & Knight, covering tactical lessons and best practices for entering the U.S. market, including product, GTM, partnerships, legal considerations, and common mistakes international founders should avoid.

title: The convo the workplace is too polite to have
description:
Most high-performing women have learned to work around it. The two weeks of brain fog, mood dysregulation, fatigue, and physical symptoms that arrive every month like clockwork, and disappear just as quietly. They push through. They reschedule. They apologize for things that aren't their fault.

What they haven't been told is that this has a name, a mechanism, and a cost.

PMS and PMDD affect an estimated 3 to 8 percent of women of reproductive age at the clinical level, and up to 13 to 18 percent experience symptoms severe enough to impair function without meeting the full diagnostic threshold. The economic impact is estimated at $4,333 per woman per year in lost workplace productivity. And the last meaningful clinical innovation for this condition was the birth control pill in 1960.

This panel brings together founders, clinicians, and women with lived experience to have the conversation that workplaces, investors, and the healthcare system have been slow to have. We'll explore the emerging science, including the gut-hormone-brain axis now being studied as a key driver of premenstrual symptoms, what it means for women's performance and careers, and why this is one of the most underfunded and overlooked opportunities in women's health today.

This is not a wellness panel. It is a business, science, and human conversation about a condition that affects half the workforce.

PANELISTS:
— Roma, Founder & CEO, Pinkmatter — biotech scientist, PMDD advocate, and builder of the first gut microbiome based platform for PMS and PMDD
— Kyrie Roger’s, AI Tech Founder — bringing the lived experience and consumer perspective in a fast-paced work envonment
— Dr. Sanya Anand — practising physician and Pinkmatter practitioner partner, bringing the clinical lens on how PMS and PMDD are (and aren't) being addressed in practice

FORMAT: Panel discussion followed by Q&A and networking reception
DURATION: 90 minutes
ATTENDANCE: ~50
AUDIENCE: Femtech founders, investors, operators, women's health professionals, press, and high-performing women navigating these conditions personally

---

WHY THIS EVENT FOR TECH WEEK:
The economic cost of untreated PMS and PMDD is measurable, significant, and almost entirely absent from the workplace conversation. As women's health becomes one of the fastest-growing investment categories in tech, this panel puts the science, the founder building solutions, and the clinical expertise in the same room as the investors and operators who can accelerate change. The performance tax framing makes this a business conversation as much as a health one, and that's exactly what Tech Week audiences are here for.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

description:

title: The Death of the MVP: How AI Changed What “Launching” Means - Bubble x
description:

The old startup playbook is dead. Join Emmanuel Straschnov, co-founder and co-CEO of Bubble, Ezra Gershanok, co-founder of Ohana, and a curated group of founders, operators, and insider voices for an unfiltered conversation on what replaces the MVP era of the startup playbook.

A panel of expert judges provides fast, honest feedback

​​Ilias Anwar is a New York based entrepreneur, content creator, and event producer behind 2.5 billion content views and 500+ events produced, with a community of 200K+ followers and 50K+ newsletter subscribers across the creator, founder, and investor space known as "Anwar Events." We design and produce full event programs for our partners, from intimate VC dinners and pitch competitions to large scale tech week activations, campus creator workshops, and launch parties, handling everything from audience curation and venue sourcing to programming, partnerships, and post-event reporting. With over 100 events produced for clients like rednote, SeedLegals, Red Bull, Udio, and Cliqk, and a built-in community of 60,000+ engaged founders, investors, and creators across NYC and major university campuses, we give tech and consumer brands a faster path to the audiences that move markets.

Recording Notice: If you attend (as a pitcher, judge, or audience member), you will be recorded! By registering, you consent to being recorded, with your image & likeness used on our media channels. We're building the Founder Idol brand starting with this NY Techweek event—and hopefully many more.

title: No Wine Left Behind: Blind Tasting Meets Consumer Data
host: No Wine Left Behind, Le Dû’s Wines
description:
What if consumers actually decided what gets stocked?

Join No Wine Left Behind for an experiential blind tasting + live data demo featuring the winning wines from our Women in Wine Festival. You’ll taste a curated lineup of wines completely blind, submit your feedback in real time, and watch as your palate becomes data — revealing collective preferences, patterns, and top performers.

Then, we reveal everything.

After the reveal, you get to revisit the wines with full context, experiencing firsthand how perception shifts once labels, stories, and pricing are introduced.

This isn’t just a wine tasting — it’s a live demonstration of how consumer data can directly determine retail decisions. By turning individual opinions into actionable insights, we’re building a more transparent, democratized approach to wine buying — where the crowd, not gatekeepers, decides what earns shelf space.

Perks:
– Store credit for all attendees
– First access to winning wines
– A chance to help decide what gets stocked at Le Dû’s

Join us for an evening of drinks, light bites, and an interactive panel where we unpack how agentic commerce is reshaping visibility, preference, and ultimately revenue and what you can do today to stay ahead.

title: Tech Week: Networking Event by Ascent Valley
description:
Most of us are in the same loop. You have an idea. You workshop it with ChatGPT, read another newsletter, tell three friends who don't quite get it. The idea stays in your head. Or worse — it never gets the kind of pushback that would have made it sharper.
Research on creative work is clear: people who articulate their idea to like-minded peers begin to understand it deeper. Quality feedback isn't validation — it's the thing that changes how you see your own work.
We mix the room on purpose. Different industries see the same process from different angles — that's where value compounds. We ask for your LinkedIn in registration so we can group you with the right people, not random tables.
How the evening runs (3 stages)
Stage 1 — Themed Networking
You vote on the night's themes through the registration form (Fundraising, HR, GTM, Product, Pricing, Hiring — whichever wins the room). We group you by background. Each group's representative shares the conversation's main takeaway on stage. Featured contributors get a Featured Founder spotlight on ascentvalley.com — that's how the conversation scales across the ecosystem.
Stage 2 — Pitches
5 curated startups present their idea for live feedback and investor introductions. After the event, we add them to our private founder database and route their application to the right investors inside the Ascent Valley network.
Stage 3 — Chill & Network
The structured part wraps. The bar stays open. The connections you started solidify here — most of our repeat attendees say this is where the real value lands.
Who's in the room
Accelerator representatives — talent and dealflow scouts from New York-based and EU-wide programs
Investors — angels, fund representatives, scouts from syndicates
Founders, Pre-seed to Series A — building across SaaS, AI, ClimateTech, FinTech, ConsumerTech
Operators — PMs, engineering leads, GTM leaders from scaling startups
Entrepreneurs across industries — fresh perspective is the point
Founders traveling through New York — from Tel Aviv, Berlin, London, NYC, Lisbon nodes
Hiring leads from companies actively scaling teams
About Ascent Valley
Ascent Valley is a 3-layer ecosystem: Community, DealFlow, Events. Our mission is to break local boundaries and enable cross-region growth through real connection between founders, operators, and investors.
Philosophy: IDEA → PROCESS → RESULT.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: a16z x Moment: Cash, Activated — Putting Your Treasury to Work
description:
Today, cash management is a board-level conversation and the stakes are high. With rates where they are, every basis point on idle cash matters, and so does every assumption about counterparty risk, liquidity, and duration. The finance leaders getting this right are turning treasury from a cost center into a quiet source of competitive advantage.

Join a16z and Moment — the AI operating system for fixed income and treasury management — for an intimate evening with a small group of CFOs, controllers, and finance leaders during NY Tech Week. We’ll share a dinner together and go deep on the practical questions every modern finance team is wrestling with:
- What are finance teams actually getting from their legacy bank relationships and where are fintechs filling the gaps? 
- Is yield optimization still the priority, or are efficiency and visibility becoming the bigger unlock?
- How are AI and modern infrastructure changing how finance teams manage and optimize their cash balances?

Expect candid discussion, peer learning, and the kind of conversations that don’t happen at larger conferences. Limited seats — finance leaders and controllers only.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: THE INTELLIGENT ATELIER
description:
Inside the Intelligent Atelier: How Fashion Leaders Are Using AI in Their Workflows

 AI in fashion is often discussed in extremes—either as a futuristic disruption or a threat to creativity. This event reframes the conversation as we unpack how AI is actually being used today to enhance creative output, streamline workflows, and drive more effective marketing. 


Panelists will share firsthand insights into where AI accelerates processes, where it still falls short, and how to select the tools to support your unique workflows. The goal is to provide attendees with a transparent, actionable view of AI as a tool—not a replacement—for creative leadership. 

With...

Clare Tattersall, founder of Digital Fashion Week Networking

​Astrid Pilla, founder Your Own Edge Institute, and Astrid's AI Tips


In partnership with DOORS NYC, Digital Fashion Week, 99 Yards, Fashion Week Brooklyn

Part I of a 3-part series: AI for Faster Design, Smarter Workflows, and Stronger Fashion Brands

Summer Series Description

Creativity is no longer limited by time, resources, or traditional production cycles. The Intelligent Atelier brings together leading voices in the fashion industry to explore how AI is being integrated into real-world fashion workflows today.

From concept development and material exploration to campaign creation and customer engagement, AI-supported tools are enabling designers to move faster, iterate more freely, and make smarter decisions across the entire value chain. But beyond efficiency, the real opportunity lies in how these technologies can amplify creativity and unlock access to customers.

we open with a conversation that sets the stage for a hands-on learning series designed specifically for fashion professionals. Attendees will gain a clear, grounded understanding of where AI delivers immediate value, how leading teams are already using it, and what it means for the future of design, production, and brand storytelling.

Whether you are an independent designer or part of an established brand, this session will help you cut through the noise and identify practical ways to integrate AI into your creative and business processes—without losing what makes your work distinctive

description:

title: The Future of PropTech
description:
Real estate is changing faster than at any point in a generation. The buildings winning right now aren't just the ones in the best locations — they're the ones running the smartest technology.

We'd like to show you what that looks like.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
YOU'RE INVITED
SKLUT & JETZY Present:
The Future of PropTech
An Exclusive Evening of Innovation & Investment

Tuesday, June 3rd | Manhattan, NYC
By Invitation Only | a16z TechWeek
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Join a curated room of New York's most forward-thinking founders, investors, and real estate leaders for an invite-only evening redefining what the built world looks like — and how the smartest operators are staying ahead of it.

This isn't a conference. This isn't a panel.

This is a live experience inside the next era of PropTech — and you'll feel the difference the moment you walk through the door.

No tickets. No check-in. Just your face.

WHAT'S INSIDE:
→ Live demos of the technology reshaping how buildings manage access, identity, and security
→ Featured speakers — visionary founders, active investors, and industry leaders redefining the built world
→ Biometric entry powered by SKLUT — a seamless entrance experience that is itself the first demonstration
→ Curated networking with the people actively deploying capital and innovation into real estate right now

Featured Speakers

Joshua Sklüt  
Founder & CEO, SKLUT  
With 20+ years at the intersection of people and technology, Joshua built SKLUT after losing real business opportunities while stuck waiting in a lobby for a manual check-in. That frustration became a global facial recognition-powered access and visitor management platform now operating across New York, Latin America, the Middle East, Europe, and Africa. On June 3rd, Joshua shares what it takes to identify a broken system — and build the future instead.

Sherry Li is Founder & CEO of PaiBox, an AI infrastructure and intelligence layer for property repair operations, autonomously executing real-world workflows with continuous operational coordination. PaiBox reduces up to 80% of manual execution time while improving transparency, operational efficiency, and repair cost control. She brings over 16 years of real estate investment and operational experience, including roles at a $1.1 trillion sovereign wealth fund. In 2018, she founded C-STAR, a $120 million real estate investment platform. 

Sherry Li holds degrees from the University of Michigan and Peking University, and is currently enrolled in the Harvard Business School OPM program. She was named one of GlobeSt.’s 2025 ELITE Women of Influence for her leadership and innovation in PropTech.

Sipho Simela  
Founder & CEO, Matrix Rental Solutions  
A first-generation American from a Zimbabwean immigrant family, Sipho built Matrix Rental Solutions from lived experience — knowing that access to housing is foundational to economic opportunity. Matrix is the first Universal Residential Rental Application for Affordable Housing, serving 460+ customers and 14,000+ users while reducing time-to-lease by over 50%. Sipho currently advises the FHFA, Fannie Mae, and Freddie Mac on making housing access faster, fairer, and more human.

Rowland Hobbs 
CEO and Co-Founder of Stake, a FinTech-powered real estate platform on a mission to make renting financially rewarding through innovative Cash Back rewards. With a career spanning real estate, financial services, architecture, consumer goods, retail, and the arts, Rowland brings a rare breadth of cross-industry expertise to everything he builds.
He previously led design and innovation at Teneo, the global CEO advisory firm, and served as Head of Product Design for Accenture North America. Rowland is also the founder of Post+Beam, an innovation design firm, and Linea, a computer vision–driven photo sharing app. Earlier in his career, he founded the digital marketing division at Ruder Finn.
Rowland holds a Master of Arts in Philosophy from KU Leuven, Belgium, and a Bachelor of Arts from Sarah Lawrence College.

Deekron Krikorian, MBA, is an executive transformation coach, bestselling author, and keynote speaker who helps founders, investors, and business leaders sustain high performance under pressure in fast-changing industries such as real estate technology.

Drawing from his background as an Accenture strategy consultant, tech startup founder, and team performance strategist, Deekron specializes in helping leaders remove the internal blocks that quietly sabotage clarity, confidence, and consistency in high-stakes environments driven by innovation and rapid change.

WHY THIS MATTERS FOR YOUR PORTFOLIO:
Whether you manage residential towers, commercial office space, or mixed-use developments — your residents and tenants expect more. Manual check-ins, outdated access systems, and zero audit trails aren't just operational headaches. They're competitive liabilities.

The operators in this room are already solving for that. Come see how.

The Future of PropTech isn't coming — it's already here.

Seating is strictly limited and this event is by invitation only.

title: Stop Guessing: How Top Teams Make Hiring Decisions From Gut Feel to Structured Decisions
description:
Hiring isn’t the problem — making the final decision is.

As teams scale, signals start conflicting across interviewers. The challenge is no longer collecting information — it’s structuring decisions under pressure.

This session explores how high-performing teams surface tradeoffs, align on priorities, and make clearer high-stakes hiring decisions.

We’ll cover:
• Why disagreement is often a sign of hidden tradeoffs
• How strong teams structure decisions under pressure
• A live breakdown of competing candidates and decision alignment
• The emerging category of “decision infrastructure”

~45 min session + optional Q&A

For founders, operators, and hiring managers scaling teams.

title: THE AUTHORITY EDGE. Rooftop Experience for Founders & Tech Leaders
description:
People decide how seriously to take you before you finish your first sentence. Your posture, your pace, and your voice are already shaping that impression before people have even heard your message. Most people never fully realize what they're signaling to others.

Set on the Starchild Rooftop, 27 floors above Midtown, this interactive experience is designed for founders and leaders who want to understand how they come across and learn how to shift it with intention.

You'll receive The Authority Edge Guide: a research-backed set of 8 precise signals that shape how authority and credibility are perceived, developed by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, executive communication expert and founder of Speech & Mind. You'll apply them in live conversations, experimenting with different techniques and noticing how small shifts in delivery and presence change the way people respond to you.

No lectures, no awkward spotlight. Just practical tools that help you project greater confidence, authority, and credibility in every room you walk into after this.

What to Expect
✦ A curated crowd of founders and tech leaders
✦ Practical tools you can start using immediately
✦ The Authority Edge Guide to take home
✦ Rooftop views, real conversations, and a relaxed atmosphere

Run of Show
🕕 6:30–6:45 PM: Arrival & Check-In
🎤 6:45–7:45 PM: Practices
🥂 7:45–8:30 PM: Open Networking

A rooftop experience for founders and tech leaders who want to be taken seriously the moment they walk in. 

​📸 Photos and videos may be taken for promotional use.

About the Host | Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk helps executives and founders communicate with clarity, authority, and presence when the stakes are high. She holds a PhD in Psychology specializing in nonverbal communication and completed executive coaching training at Harvard. Her approach combines performance psychology and neuroscience to help leaders think clearly, communicate with precision, and stay composed under pressure. 
🌐 speechandmind.com

Thank you to our venue sponsor:
Perched 27 stories high atop the Civilian Hotel, Starchild Rooftop offers an elevated lounge experience with exceptional views. title: Allometric Dining 2026
description:
Loop is an invite-only supper on the recursive patterns behind the physical economy — control loops, feedback loops, scaling laws.

It’s for founders and investors working in manufacturing, field services, construction, energy, logistics, defense, and humanoids — the companies where revenue is earned at an address, not a URL.

Most tech dinners happen in the world of bits — SaaS metrics, model benchmarks, ARR curves. Loop is the other table. It’s for the people building and backing the companies where revenue is earned at a job site, a factory floor, a warehouse, or an address on a map.

The conversation has one spine: the recursive patterns that govern physical revenue. Control loops. Feedback loops. Scaling laws. The physical world doesn’t compound like software — but it does compound. Understanding how is the whole game.

Twelve seats, one long table, a pop-up chef, and natural wine from growers around the globe. Five courses built on a single scaling rule. An Escher print on the menu, a Duchamp quote chosen for each guest, and a quiet personalization on each plate. No decks, no panels, no pitches.

Loop is hosted by Allometry — the agentic operating substrate for physical revenue. Modern industrial operators run every commercial decision through Allometry: every quote, every contract, every dispatch, every renewal, scored against margin and EV at the address level. Each decision is signed at write-time and accumulates in the operator’s vault — a hash-chained, time-locked ledger of operational truth. That vault becomes the interchange standard between operators and institutional capital: continuous quality of earnings, four lender tiers, one ledger underneath. The same control layer that prices a quote today underwrites working capital tomorrow, infrastructure debt next, and makes labor, machines, and humanoids programmable next after that.

If you want to scan the thesis ahead of the table, allometry.com/thesis is the five-minute version — six atoms, with a toggle for the anti-thesis on the reverse. The architecture lives one layer deeper at  allometry.com/the-vault.

The dinner isn’t about Allometry. It’s about the people at the table.

Greenpoint. $295/seat + service. Covers chef, wine pairing, and room. Waived for Allometry design partners. Every seat earns 1 Loop — a free Vault Audit on a real operation, redeemable whenever, or giftable to a portfolio company.


Twelve seats, four kinds of guests:

— Operators building revenue at the address — manufacturing, field services, construction, energy, logistics, defense, humanoids.

— Lenders and capital allocators underwriting the physical economy — banks, infrastructure debt, insurance-linked, pension, rated paper.

— Venture investors in industrial, infrastructure, and agentic AI.

— Partners — channel, implementation, and capital, building alongside.

If you sit at one of those tables, write to hello@allometry.com.


This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at tech-week.com.

title: AI Agents: From Prototype to Production
description:
📋 Event Description

Everyone's building AI demos. This is where you build AI agents that actually work.

AI Agents: From Prototype to Production is a one-evening, in-person sprint for builders who want to go beyond the ChatGPT wrapper and ship something real. Powered by Phinite and GMI, this is your chance to build a production-ready multi-agent application — with real infrastructure, real guardrails, and real stakes.

The top three teams get to pitch their project to a VC.

The Challenge
Build a real-world solution using AI agents. Not a demo. Not a proof-of-concept vibe. Something that could actually run in production — hitting real APIs, talking to databases, handling real workflows.

You'll have access to:

Phinite is building the Multi Agent OS — the infrastructure layer for the multi-agent era. Every enterprise deploying AI agents needs identity, observability, and governance underneath them. That's what Phinite ships, out of the box.

GMI — an AI-native inference cloud built for production AI, combining serverless scaling and dedicated GPU infrastructure with predictable performance and cost.

What to Expect

- Sponsor intros + tool walkthroughs at kickoff so you hit the ground running

title: Ugly Talk: The Future of Fashion & Beauty Tech
description:
💡 What’s the Ugly Truth

Beauty tech is booming, but growth has made the category louder, not clearer. AI diagnostics, AR try-ons, personalization, and smart devices are pulling more founders into the space, but more innovation also means more hype, more copycats, and more brands fighting for the same customer attention.

For founders, a strong product is just the entry fee. The real edge comes from knowing what actually drives trust, repeat purchase, and loyalty in a market where branding and novelty can fake momentum. This room is for people who want to separate signal from noise and figure out which trends are truly shaping the future of beauty tech.

🎯 Why You Should Attend

- Hear from a pioneer in beauty tech and understand what they are building, how they got there, and what they are paying attention to now.
- Connect with other beauty tech founders who are relentlessly innovating and building through the noise.
- Understand shifting consumer behavior, changing demand, and what customers are looking for now.

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room

- Beauty tech founders
- Beauty business owners
- Consumer and brand experts
- Investors interested in beauty tech and consumer companies

🎤  Who’s Taking the Stage
Zehra Soysal - Founder & CEO of Savanah.ai

Zehra's career journey began at Google in business strategy, followed by impactful roles at BCG, where she worked across diverse sectors in Europe, the Middle East, and Asia. She played a key role in establishing a tech joint venture with Turkey's largest conglomerate, igniting her passion for innovative business technologies. She earned her MBA from Harvard Business School in 2022. Post-MBA, she joined Metaphysic.ai in New York, where she was instrumental in scaling operations and driving strategic growth through its $1.4B acquisition. She is now the founder and CEO of Savanah.ai, a New York-based visual AI platform helping brands create e-commerce imagery at scale and optimize conversion.


Yarden Horwitz - Co-Founder of Spate

Yarden Horwitz is the Co-founder of Spate, an AI-driven platform used by 250+ beauty brands like Estée Lauder, L’Oréal, and P&G to make data-driven decisions. Before founding Spate in 2018, Yarden worked at Google, launching the first Google Trends Report and co-founding the trendspotting division. She’s been named a top female leader by Entrepreneur Magazine and sits on the boards of CEW and NewBeauty Brain Trust. Yarden is a frequent speaker at beauty events like CEW’s State of the Beauty Industry and her work has been featured in publications including WWD, Vogue Business, and the New York Times.


Bonnie Young - Founder & Chief Creative at BY. Bonnie Young

Bonnie Young is founder of the luxury brand BY. Bonnie Young, known for its timeless, artful, and purposeful design that merge modern couture with effortless ease. She began her career at Ralph Lauren and Donna Karan before launching her own label. Her work has appeared in leading publications including Vogue, Forbes, and The New York Times, and has been worn by celebrities and Middle Eastern royalty. Young has collaborated with Adidas, Swarovski, and The Hunger Games: Mockingjay Part 2. She is also an author, photographer, philanthropist, CFDA member, and recipient of the 2025 FGI Ready-to-Wear Award for excellence.


Moderator:
Jessica Sophia Wong - Founder & CEO of Yorkseed.co

Jessica Sophia Wong is the founder of Yorkseed — a venture intelligence and relationship platform built for the people who move capital and companies across borders. Through Yorkseed Connect, she has built one of the most active cross-border founder and investor ecosystems in the world, operating across 50+ city hubs and running alongside SXSW, Web Summit, Davos, CES, VivaTech, and New York Tech Week. She also founded Valois, a private invitation-only network for family offices, HNWI, and fund managers at the highest level of global capital. Venture Partner at VNTR Capital. Based in NYC and Paris.


💬 What We Do Not Sugarcoat

- In an industry full of noise and competition, how do you separate real signal from hype?
- How do you fight copycats in a market where products move fast and differentiation can disappear overnight?
- What trends actually reflect customer demand, and what are customers burning for now that they were not asking for before?
- How does AI actually matter here, from diagnostics and personalization to discovery, conversion, and retention?

🤝 Who's Hosting It

Yorkseed - Yorkseed is a global venture platform and community connecting founders, investors, and operators. https://yorkseedhouse.com/nytw2026

Belong NYC- We offer our members a unique blend of luxury and convenience, including access to showers, locker space, luggage storage, private phone booths, conference rooms, nap pods, and co-working space.

title: From Legislation to Action: Shaping the Future of Privacy in New York
description:
Light refreshments will be served! 

Privacy isn’t abstract for LGBTQ+ people. It’s often the difference between safety and exposure. In a world where our lives are increasingly lived online and subject to surveillance, the ability to control our personal information, relationships, and identities is essential to protecting our freedom and well-being. Right now, a coalition of civil rights groups is working to strengthen privacy protections in the New York State Constitution, and the stakes for LGBTQ+ communities couldn’t be higher. Join a panel of advocates and experts to learn what’s driving this proposed amendment, how it could safeguard LGBTQ+ people across the state, and what it means for the future of our rights. You’ll also have the opportunity to ask questions and be part of the movement to secure stronger protections for all New Yorkers.

Moderating our panel will be Sam Cole of 404 Media. 

Our expert panel includes: 
Jared Trujillo, CUNY Law & Equality New York  
Mandy Salley, Woodhull Freedom Foundation  
Corinne Worthington, Surveillance Technology Oversight Project (STOP)  
Lou Rambou, Carbon Arc 
David Siffert, NYU Law

title: Debugging the NYC Rental: Using Data to Escape "The Zero Data Decision"
description:
In a city built on data, why do we still sign leases based on a 5-minute walkthrough and a "gut feeling"? We call this "The Zero Data Decision," and it’s the most expensive mistake a New Yorker can make.

Houstory is hosting a tactical workshop designed to bridge the gap between physical reality and digital history. We’ll teach you how to "debug" an apartment before you sign the lease. This isn't just about aesthetics; it's about infrastructure, accountability, and your right to a safe home.

What We’ll Cover:

The "Hardware" Audit: A deep dive into the Houstory Visitor Checklist. We’ll show you how to spot "Landlord Specials," identify hidden pest mitigation (the "Steel Wool" check), and test the vitals of a unit (water, heat, and signal strength).

The Digital Trace: A live demo of the Houstory app. We will take real NYC addresses from the audience and pull their "Hidden History"—including open violations, past complaints, and owner transparency.

HR to Housing: Why vetting your next landlord should look exactly like vetting your next employer.

Goal: To equip the next wave of NYC residents with the tools to demand transparency and move with confidence.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Climate Tech Night Mixer
description:
​Event Description

Climate change is a global, dynamic, complex problem. To solve the problem at scale, in time, with limited resources, we need stakeholder engagement with different responsibilities.

The networking mixer is designed to bring together climate tech founders, investors, builders, and operators in a friendly gathering place. So that people are able to listen, question, and learn from different perspectives.

To maximize your networking experience, we highly recommend that each participant prepare 3-5 small lead magnets(resources, jobs, people, tools, etc) to others who are looking for support in their climate endeavors or personal life(if shared). The number of people you supported and the quality of your lead magnets will enhance the surface of luck of your climate pursuits. We wish that all attendees could find the gathering fruitful with 1 new learning and 1 new climate stakeholder.

Join us for an evening of meaningful connections that shape the future of climate innovation.

**Recommended Audience**

- Climate Founders: Work on something new to address/mitigate/adapt the climate problems that others cannot
- Climate Investors: Look for potential new climate perspectives on future climate innovation
- Climate Builders: Create simple measurement and visualization tools to raise public climate awareness
- Climate Operators: Support the climate transition implemented in the daily activities.

**Expected Outcomes**

✅ Receive small support from other attendees in the climate ecosystem

✅ Listen to different perspectives with a humble mindset on various climate topics

✅ Maintain the relationship with climate stakeholders for potential future cooperation

**About Optivide**

Optivide is a B2C social enterprise that supports people in solving the climate problem with financial confidence. The event information is shared for wider climate stakeholders with the support of Optivide.

As a climate stakeholder, if you would love to solve multiple climate problems, at scale, in time, with limited resources, at a highest possible success rate…

Explore potential decision blind spots with our paid climate workshop here: https://www.notion.so/2f994d0420f880afa95cccf68be5bf0b?pvs=21


About Tech Week

title: NYC’S FIRST EVER SKY-HIGH PITCH COMPETITION with up to $1M  in funding on the line
description:
It all starts here, folks.  

On June 4, we’re bringing something truly different to NY Tech Week. Rocket Mixer® and Apollo Society are hosting the second edition of a one-of-a-kind event that redefines the tech experience—where wellness meets innovation in one of the most stunning venue in NYC: Edge observation deck.

The life of a founder is anything but linear. It’s lived at the edge—of pressure, uncertainty, sharp turns, and constant intensity. Instead of ignoring that reality, we’ve built an experience around it.

The day begins with guided breath-work led by a professional instructor, helping founders reset and find clarity. 

From there, the energy shifts. At the edge of the observation deck, eight top early-stage startups will step forward for a high-stakes elevator pitch competition, presenting to a leading VC fund for the opportunity to secure significant funding.

​The morning continues with an exclusive private breakfast alongside a curated group of founders, VCs, and operators.

​
​We’re bringing a single, high-impact experience built as a three-part package: immersive breathwork, a pitch competition, and an exclusive private breakfast with founders, VCs, and operators. A can’t-miss gathering.

​Startups will compete for the opportunity to secure up to $1M in funding for late-stage startups, or a first check and accelerator access for early-stage founders, led by LVLUP Ventures.

Priority Access to Event Bags

​For guests attending only the breakfast, we unfortunately can’t guarantee there will be enough bags available as well.

​Thank you to our partners for providing the goodies —

Katya Fuentes | Head of Growth, Comp AI

​ABOUT SOMARAE:
​Somarae is a B2B2C somatic wellness platform helping individuals regulate stress and organizations reduce burnout, retain top talent, and increase leadership + team effectiveness. We help people reconnect with their bodies through science-backed tools, integrate with women's health cycles and foster community-centered support. Visit "somarae.co" for more info

​ABOUT TASK FORCE VA:
Task Force VA is a veteran-built B2C and B2B SaaS platform that simplifies the VA disability claims journey through secure, Al-driven guidance. It brings together key regulations, manuals, and required forms into one intelligent workflow, giving Veterans and Active-Duty personnel instant access to clear rating criteria, structured claim preparation, and trusted support by a vetted ecosystem of VA-accredited advocates, medical providers, and Veteran Service Organizations. https://taskforceva.ai/
https://taskforceva.app
https://reconforce.org


​ABOUT STRIPE:
​Stripe is a global financial infrastructure platform that helps businesses accept payments, manage revenue, and scale worldwide. Stripe’s software and APIs power payments, subscriptions, billing, fraud prevention, and global commerce, helping businesses grow faster and operate more efficiently around the world.

ABOUT CONNECTD:
​Connectd empowers startups and senior fractional leaders to thrive in the era of flexible, high-impact work. We are a people-first platform connecting startups and scaleups with experienced fractional executives, advisors and directors. Powered by AI insights and smart matching technology, Connectd ensures the right leaders are placed in the right businesses, driving growth, clarity, and scale when it matters most.

ABOUT GRAND FOUNDERS:
Grand Founders is a New York–based 501(c)(3) public foundation operating through partnerships with institutional investors, family offices, corporations, and impact-driven leaders.

ABOUT COMP AI:
Comp AI gets you SOC 2 or ISO 27001 audit-ready in just a few days, so your team can get back to shipping. Evidence, controls, vendor management, and audit prep run on autopilot, so you stay audit-ready 24/7. Trusted by fast-growing teams like Dub, Better Auth, and Ramp.

title: Agentic Commerce: What It Is, What It Isn’t – and Who It Should Serve
description:
As AI rapidly reshapes the commerce landscape, “agentic commerce” has emerged as a hot topic, yet an often misunderstood concept. This panel, hosted by Juan Pellerano-Rendón, CMO of Swap, will cut through the noise to define what agentic commerce actually means in practice, and just as importantly, what it does not.

Bringing together perspectives from brand, technology, and commerce infrastructure, this conversation will focus on how AI agents can empower merchants and strengthen the direct connection between brands and their customers, rather than replacing it with big tech models designed only to operate within their own infrastructure. 

Moderated by the host and creator of Fashion & Founders, Victoria Smith, the panel will include Justin Felizzari, Founder of Cueva, and Gina Lombardo, VP of E-Commerce at Retrofête, alongside Juan, to dive deeper into how leading brands are transforming traditional e-commerce sites into conversational shopping experiences and leaning into the world of agentic commerce. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: From unicorn to public: Executive forum on IPO readiness
description:
PwC is hosting a business focused executive forum for CEOs, CFOs, and senior leaders from high growth unicorn startups preparing for the transition to public markets.

The program will focus on the critical decisions, strategic inflection points, and common pitfalls that can shape IPO readiness, public company operating discipline, governance readiness, forecast credibility, disclosure readiness, institutional demand, revenue quality, and related capital markets considerations.

The morning will include a business focused breakfast discussion with perspectives from experienced public company operators, governance specialists, PwC leaders, capital markets participants, and an exchange representative. Attendees may also observe the New York Stock Exchange opening bell ceremony as part of the venue’s morning program.

Through candid discussion and practical perspectives, attendees will gain insights to help position their companies for the next stage of maturity as they evaluate the path to becoming public companies.

Location: New York Stock Exchange, 11 Wall Street, New York, NY

This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: a16z + Sequence + Thomson Group Ride: Watts & Multiples
description:
A morning ride for startup finance leaders — the CFOs, controllers, and operators turning burn into growth. Draft with your peers, trade notes on the climbs, and regroup at the top. The paceline is free; the alpha is earned.

Your hosts for the day form a well-balanced portfolio in their own right. a16z brings the peloton together, the investors backing the founders building what's next, and the community where operators compare notes off the record and on the road. Sequence, an a16z portfolio company, powers the back-office engine behind modern finance teams, the kind of infrastructure that turns month-end from a climb into a descent. And Thomson Bike Tours sets the route, the support, and the pace — seasoned guides who know every switchback, sag stop, and secret coffee shop along the way.

Coffee at the start. Pastries at the finish. Bring a road bike and a helmet — we'll handle the tailwind.

Our plan is to split into two groups with laps around the Central Park (before signing up, please make sure you can ride at this pace): 

- Ride 1: Training ride with Thomson / pace 18+ mph average - 3 laps 
- Ride 2: Social Ride with Thomson / Pace 15+ mph average - 2 laps

title: Law in the Age of AI
description:
Join SimpleClosure and Carta for an exclusive panel discussion on the future of law in the age of AI. Learn how artificial intelligence is reshaping the legal industry and redefining what's possible for startups and established firms alike. 

Featured speakers:
- Steven Lerner, Senior Reporter, Law360
- Dori Yona – CEO, SimpleClosure
- Jesse Carey – Product Strategy & Ops, Stripe
- Sam Garcia – Partner, Amplo
-Jeff Laretto – Partner, Cooley

Speaker bios:
Steven Lerner
Steven Lerner has been a senior reporter at Law360 since 2020, focusing on legal technology and alternative legal service providers. He writes a monthly column called "Technically Speaking" for Law360 Pulse, where he explores various aspects of legal technology and its impact on the legal industry. Through his coverage, Lerner shares insights, trends, and analysis related to legal tech innovations. In addition to his writing, Lerner has participated in several panels and webinars about legal tech.

Dori Yona
Dori started SimpleClosure after facing a tough moment in his own business when he needed help closing down a company but couldn't find good advice anywhere. After running an app called Earny that millions of people used, he knew there had to be a better way to help business owners close their companies. His new company, SimpleClosure, now helps hundreds of other business owners shut down their companies faster and cheaper than traditional methods. Based in Santa Monica with his family, Dori works to show that closing a business doesn't have to be scary or hard, and that learning from tough experiences can lead to helping others.

Jesse Carey
Jesse Carey leads growth for Stripe Atlas, Stripe’s startup incorporation platform. He previously served as Business Operations Lead at Headway, a mental health startup, where he launched the company’s government business. Prior, he was a Strategy & Planning Manager at Uber Eats and a management consultant focused on payments. He holds an MBA from Harvard Business School after attending Sydney Law School, having moved to the US from Australia in 2017.

Sam Garcia
Sam Garcia is a Partner with the Amplo platform of Collective Global. Sam focuses on the Seed/Series A practice at Amplo and serves on the boards of Lightyear, Flume, Kredi, Tradespace, Wizehire, Steno, Axion Ray, Doxel, and previously served on the board of Legalpad until they were acquired by Deel. Sam is a Lecturer at Harvard Law School where he teaches a course called Applying Legal Skills to VC Business Diligence. He also sits on the board of LatinxVC and serves as the organization’s Vice President. Sam is a graduate of the Business Honors Program at the University of Texas at Austin and holds a JD from Harvard Law School.

Jeff Laretto
Jeff Laretto represents high-growth technology companies, predominately those located in and around New York City and Silicon Valley, at all stages of development. He frequently advises on incorporation and organizational matters, angel, seed and venture capital investments, general corporate strategy, venture debt transactions, strategic investments, complex commercial transactions, acquisitions, employment matters, mergers and other sale transactions. He also counsels venture capital funds, private equity funds and strategic investors in connection with investments, acquisitions and divestitures.

Who should attend:
- ECVC Lawyers looking to understand emerging trends
- Startup advisors and investors focused on legal services and compliance
- Anyone curious about how law is evolving with technology

Schedule:
Doors open at 8:30 AM for breakfast and coffee, followed by the panel discussion from 9:00 – 10:00. AM. Light networking to follow. 

About SimpleClosure: SimpleClosure is the shutdown partner for founders and startups navigating the less-talked-about side of entrepreneurship. When it’s time to properly close a company, SimpleClosure handles the paperwork, legal filings, compliance requirements, and final steps founders are often left to figure out on their own. What is traditionally a confusing, months-long process becomes a clearer path forward, so founders can close cleanly and move forward with confidence. To learn more, visit SimpleClosure.com.

About Carta: Carta is the leading cap table management platform trusted by startups and investors to manage equity, valuations, and cap table administration.

Entrepreneurs Run - 5 km
description:

An intentionally small, selected group of founders and investors running together during NYC Tech Week. Hosted by Swiss-based founders. The rule: if you can explain and defend your idea while running, you can do it anywhere. With a pitch deck, it's easy, holding a clear thought and pitching at kilometre five is the real test.

Who this is for:
- Founders in AI, AI robotics, defence tech, deep tech, and infrastructure.
- Investors looking at any of the above.

👩‍💻Founders are reviewed and selected in advance by profile - the list of attendees stays small and relevant on purpose.

Format: 5 km run in Central Park. Exact meeting point and route shared with confirmed participants only.

✍️ About the organiser: LatentWorlds AI is building the data infrastructure layer for physical AI focused on robotics and real-world systems. At its core, the company develops a backend that captures, organises, and processes complex multi-sensor data from robot fleets, turning raw operational data into structured, usable datasets for training and debugging AI systems. The bigger vision: give robotics and physical AI teams the same level of data infrastructure maturity that web and software teams already have, so they can focus on building intelligence, not pipelines.

title: AI x Longevity Summit
host: Longevity Global, Dovetail Biopartners, Yale University
description:
NOTE: You MUST buy a ticket through Eventbrite to attend. Your registration on Partiful does not give you event access.

Purchase your ticket here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/ai-x-longevity-nyc-tickets-1987706522717

--

Longevity science is at an inflection point. Foundation models are learning the language of biology, aging clocks are quantifying human health with new precision, and a new generation of therapeutics, diagnostics, and clinical platforms is emerging to act on those signals.

AI x Longevity Summit, the inaugural flagship summit from the NYC Chapter of Longevity Global, explores how artificial intelligence is compressing the distance between discovery and real-world intervention. This year’s theme, Signal to Intervention, focuses on the systems, infrastructure, and translational strategies shaping the future of longevity science and healthcare.

Hosted at BioLabs @ NYU Langone in the heart of New York City’s biotech corridor, the Summit convenes researchers, clinicians, founders, investors, and engineers building the next generation of longevity infrastructure.

Find this year's agenda, speaker lineup, and pitch competition information here: https://longevitygl.org/nyc-ai-x-longevity-summit/

title: How Humans Experience AI (Design Workshop)
description:
Your AI & Product works. But does your user trust it?

Most founders optimize for capability. The best founders optimize for human experience. The difference is whether someone uses your product once or makes it part of their life.

In this 90-minute working session, we'll take a small group of product founders through the same process Tequity uses with its clients:
- Map the user's world — who they are, what they're doing when they encounter your product, and what they're feeling
- Identify the trust gaps — where your product asks users to make a leap of faith, and whether you've earned it
- Build a design brief — a one-page document that captures the decisions your product needs to make about how it presents itself to humans

This is hands-on. Bring your product (or your idea). You'll work on your own company with guidance from our team.

You'll leave with a design brief you can hand to your designer (or Claude), your dev team, or yourself — and start using immediately.

Who this is for: 
— Founders at the zero-to-one stage building AI products 
— Technical founders who know their product works but aren't sure it communicates well 
— Anyone who's gotten the feedback "the product is powerful but confusing"

Limited to 10 people so everyone gets individual attention.

Led by Design Labs at Tequity, a design and engineering studio that partners with early-stage startups. Design Labs has worked with 25+ 0-1 companies in 2025 alone that were born out of Harvard, Stanford and MIT - helping them find PMF. We also run a grant program where qualifying startups get credits covering 50% of their monthly design and engineering costs with us.

Coffee provided.

This event is a part of NYC Tech Week - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Venture Studio Playbook
description:
Join us during NY Tech Week for a casual fireside chat exploring the rise of venture studios and how they’re reshaping company creation, early-stage investing, and founder support. Featuring Lauren Cooks Levitan of Root alongside Byron Dailey and Jonathan Sagot of Fenwick, the conversation will dive into what makes the venture studio model work, how studios identify and build winning companies, and what the future of startup formation looks like in today’s market.

Expect candid insights, practical lessons for founders and investors, and the opportunity to connect with NYC’s startup ecosystem over networking and drinks following the discussion.

Root is a venture studio and company-building platform focused on creating and scaling next-generation consumer businesses. Founded by longtime consumer and marketplace operator Lauren Cooks Levitan, Root partners with founders, creators, and operators to help turn emerging ideas into enduring brands through hands-on operational support, strategic guidance, and company-building expertise.

By confirming your attendance you agree to receive communications and updates from Fenwick.

title: Exec Briefing on AI Transformation
description:
Three years ago, we bet our entire company on AI, and by going all in on Fin, we turned a 13 year-old SaaS company (Intercom) into one of the hottest AI-native companies around. We are the canary in the coal mine for what must be done by everyone.

Today, our business is over $400m in revenue, our Fin AI Agent is over $100m and growing exponentially. We have nearly tripled product velocity, and over 1100 of our 1300 employees now use Claude Code. How we operate, and how we are organised, is unrecognisable from any point in our history. Through April and May 2026, we shipped eight Tier 1 product launches, one every single week.

Many of my peers ask me, how is this possible?

The transition to get to this place was brutal but necessary. The biggest mistake I see Execs and Leaders make is not making the truly hard decisions required to succeed. I personally learned to throw away many things I thought were very important, and I significantly changed how I lead.

This invite-only Exec Briefing will cover our journey, what we learned, and what we're still learning. It is an open, direct, off-the-record discussion for Leaders to understand how to drive the changes required in their company.

Join me and other leaders, and get practical advice from people in the same shoes as you.

Paul Adams

​Chief Product Officer and Marketing Leader, Fin


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: From Idea to Live MVP in 6 Hours with GitHub Copilot — $25K Azure Prize
description:
Walk in with an idea. Walk out with a live, deployed MVP — and a shot at $25,000 in Azure credits.

This is a hands-on, full-day hackathon for founders, PMs, and builders who want to ship real software using GitHub Copilot and agentic AI workflows — no deep engineering background required. 

We'll start with a masterclass from Miguel Martinez, Principal AI Solutions Engineer at Microsoft, covering the GitHub Copilot fundamentals, prompt patterns, and agentic workflows that turn ideas into working code. 
Then you'll team up (teams of 3–4) and spend the afternoon in a focused build sprint, with Microsoft mentors roaming the room to unblock you.
By 4pm, you'll demo a working product to our judging panel. One team walks away with $25,000 in Azure credits from Microsoft for Startups.

Who should come: Non-technical founders and PMs who want to build the MVP they've been putting off. Engineers who want to ship fast and meet founders. Anyone curious about what's actually possible with AI-assisted coding in 2026.

What you'll leave with: A deployed MVP, a new team of builders, and hands-on experience with the Copilot workflows top teams are using to ship 10x faster.

Agenda: 
9:30 am - Check-in & breakfast
10:00 am - Opening + GitHub Copilot Masterclass
11:00 am - Lunch, team formation, idea pitches
12:00 pm - Build sprint begins
3:30 pm - Demos and judging 
4:30 pm - Winner announcement & close

Hosted at the Microsoft office in Midtown Manhattan. Space is limited. Come hungry to build.

Includes:
- Food

- Everything you need to create

title: Just Build It: Vibe Coding Lab for Founders
description:
You don't need to know how to code. You just need an idea and a laptop.  

Join Female Founder Collective and Vibe Code Mama for a hands-on morning exploring how founders can use AI to rapidly prototype tools, creative ideas, and products - live, together, in the room. No technical background required. Just curiosity, experimentation, and a willingness to build.

Vibe coding is the practice of building apps, tools, and products using AI conversationally - describing what you want in natural language and collaborating with AI systems to help bring ideas to life. It’s changing who gets to build and expanding what's possible for founders at every stage.

In this interactive lab, you'll get a crash course in vibe coding fundamentals, then open your laptop and start building - with real-time feedback from those who do this every day. We'll close with live demos where volunteers share what they built and get feedback from the room.  

The format: 
- Vibe Coding 101 
- Live build session with pro vibe coders 
- Founder demos + feedback 
- Networking  

For the event:
- Bring your laptop
- Sign up for a Claude account

During this session you will be hosted by CEO of Female Founder Collective, Alison Wyatt, along with the Founder of Vibe Code Mama, Jenna Cao, and  Head of Operations and Partnerships of Vibe Code Mama, Kendall Crocker - two builders who believe female founders have the opportunity to shape the next era of AI adoption and growth.  

Capacity is limited. Come ready to build.  

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Co-located with NY Tech Week, Nareit's REITWeek and hosted by the US Proptech Council, the Summit features a curated startup showcase, two executive panels on AI adoption and responsible AI governance, and an open networking reception. This is not a general technology conference. It is a peer-level conversation among the decision-makers who are actively deploying AI across portfolios, operations, and underwriting — and who are accountable for doing it responsibly.

title: B2B Marketing Workshop: Strategic Foundations & AI Visibility Growth
description:
Growth oriented B2B tech start-ups and scale-ups require a shift from intuitive guesswork to a go-to-market approach built on strategic marketing rigor and AI-driven visibility. In this joint workshop, Boon Auditing and BeonAI provide a comprehensive framework to accelerate both brand awareness and lead generation.


The session begins by grounding attendees in the core strategic pillars of Boon’s B2B marketing framework, including branding, targeting, messaging, positioning, understanding your buyers and more. BeonAI then introduces the emerging discipline of AI visibility, showing how brands are discovered, evaluated, and recommended across generative platforms (i.e. ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.) rather than traditional search and showcase their agentic solution. 


Participants will walk away with a blueprint to transform their marketing into a predictable, scalable, and investor-ready revenue engine.

title: Morning Brew: How Founders Win When Government Sets the Rules
description:
In 2026, the relationship between startups and government has fundamentally changed. Regulation is no longer a side issue: it shapes markets, creates winners, and determines how quickly new technologies can scale. For founders building in education, healthcare, fintech, AI, and beyond, understanding policy has become as important as understanding product.

Join Joseph Connor, CEO of Odyssey and Bradley Tusk, CEO of Tusk Ventures for a candid conversation on what it takes to build and grow in this new environment. 

Drawing from Odyssey’s experience helping families access education funding through state-led ESA programs, they’ll unpack the real strategies founders need to navigate regulation, unlock new markets, and turn policy shifts into competitive advantage.

This discussion will explore:

- Why the best startup opportunities increasingly sit inside regulated industries
- How founders can treat government as a customer, partner, or distribution channel
- What policy signals smart companies should be tracking in 2026
- How to build trust while moving fast in high-stakes markets
- Why the next generation of winning companies may come from sectors others avoid

For founders, investors, and operators, this is a conversation about where innovation is headed next, and how to stay ahead of it.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Bring Your Product. We'll Break It.
description:
Bring Your Product. We'll Break It.

Live adversarial testing for 10 AI-native founders. 60 minutes. Real vulnerabilities. Coffee provided.

Your product ships fast. Your attackers move faster.

Most early-stage companies think security is something to solve later, after scale, after growth, after fundraising. But modern products are being probed from day one by bots, automated scripts, AI-assisted attackers, and increasingly sophisticated threat actors.

In this 60-minute live session, we’ll simulate how real attackers evaluate a product and show you where your system breaks before the internet does.

Using controlled offensive security methods, the Tequity Security Ops team will:

— Map your exposed attack surface across product, infrastructure, workflows, and user behavior
— Demonstrate how seemingly small vulnerabilities compound into major security risks
— Identify trust and security gaps across authentication, permissions, APIs, AI systems, internal tooling, and operational workflows
— Show how attackers think, move, escalate access, and exploit assumptions inside modern products
— Help you understand what security maturity actually looks like for a zero-to-one company

This is not a theoretical webinar. It is a live working session.

Bring your product, staging environment, prototype, or architecture. We’ll walk through real-world attack scenarios and show you where your company is most vulnerable today.

You’ll leave with:

* A high-level risk assessment of your current product ecosystem
* A practical understanding of your most critical vulnerabilities
* A security prioritization framework for your current stage
* Recommendations on what to fix immediately vs later
* A clearer picture of what modern Security Ops should look like inside a growing startup

Who this is for:
— Founders building AI-native or SaaS products
— Engineering teams moving quickly without dedicated security leadership
— Startups handling sensitive user, healthcare, fintech, or enterprise data
— Teams preparing for enterprise sales, compliance reviews, or due diligence
— Companies that have never undergone adversarial security testing before

Limited to 10 companies so each team gets hands-on attention.

Led by Tequity, an applied AI company, working with early-stage startups across AI, health-tech, fintech, and enterprise software. Our teams work closely with zero-to-one founders to build products that are not only usable and scalable, but resilient under real-world conditions.

title: The Founder's Exit Hackathon: From Identity to Asset
description:
Most exit readiness programs focus on financials. This one focuses on you.

The number one barrier women cite to exiting their business is not valuation. It is identity. Who am I without this company? Do I deserve this exit?

The founders who exit on their own terms, who convert what they have built into lasting wealth, started thinking about it long before they were ready to sell. This workshop is for established women founders who are scaling, not selling. Yet.

Guided by three experts who have lived and worked every dimension of the exit, this workshop moves established women founders through a structured framework addressing the psychosocial, emotional, identity, financial, and operational work that determines whether a woman actually exits or just keeps building past the point she should have stopped.

Alisha Pennington, multi-seven figure exited founder and creator of Exette, leads the identity and psychosocial framework. Alexis Allen, CEPA of the Teixeira Loughran Group, brings the financial planning lens, helping founders understand what their business is worth and what it takes to convert it into lasting wealth. Adrienne Dorison of Level 11 Leaders, covers the operational side, helping founders build a business that runs without them so the exit is actually possible.

You leave with a personal exit identity roadmap and a clear picture of what it means to build your business as a wealth asset, not an extension of your worth.

https://www.linkedin.com/in/alisha-m-pennington
