title: How Growth-Stage Companies Are Scaling Marketing
description:
ABOUT THE WEBINAR

Growth-stage companies face the same problem over and over. The board approves an aggressive pipeline target. The headcount budget does not move. The CMO is expected to close that gap with the same team, the same spend, and a stack of tools that were supposed to make everything easier, but mostly created more coordination work.

The reflex most marketing companies reach for is another tool. Another platform. Another dashboard. And six months later, the team is managing tooling instead of driving growth, output has not materially improved, and the gap between what leadership expects and what marketing delivers has quietly gotten wider.

The companies that are actually closing that gap right now are not doing it by adding people or buying more software. They are doing it by building infrastructure: AI-powered marketing systems where research, campaign execution, paid media optimization, lead nurturing, and performance reporting operate as a connected sequence rather than as disconnected tasks scattered across a team that is already stretched.

That is what this session is about.

On June 4, as part of Tech Week New York 2026, Hamlet Azarian, Founder and CEO of Azarian Growth Agency, will demonstrate exactly how those systems work: live, starting from a blank screen. Not a recorded demo. Not a slide deck with hypothetical outputs. 

A working system, built and run in real time, across the functions that matter most to a growth-stage marketing operation: demand generation, paid media, pipeline development, and performance analytics.

Azarian Growth Agency works directly with CMOs and marketing leaders at growth-stage companies, navigating precisely this pressure: boards demanding 2x results with flat resources, marketing teams that are capable but over-extended, and AI tools that have produced volume without strategic direction. 

The systems we will demonstrate in this session are the same ones built and operated for those companies. You will leave this session with a clear picture of what AI marketing systems can realistically handle across the full funnel, where human strategic judgment remains non-negotiable, and how to evaluate whether your team should build, buy, or partner to get there. 

You will also leave with a practical diagnostic framework you can run against your own marketing operation the same week, not a theoretical exercise, but a structured way to identify where operational drag is costing you the most and where AI systems would create the fastest lift.

This session is designed for CMOs and VPs of Marketing who are past the AI curiosity phase. If you have already tried the tools and found that they produce activity without outcomes, this is the session that shows you why, and what the alternative looks like in practice.

WHAT YOU WILL LEARN

Everything in this session is built around one question: how does a growth-stage marketing team do more, with the team it already has?

1. Why your current AI tool stack is making your team less productive, not more 

The problem is not the tools. The problem is that tools without a system layer create fragmentation: more inputs, more handoffs, more editing cycles, and more time spent managing software instead of driving growth. The webinar will show exactly where that breakdown happens and what the structural fix looks like across a real marketing operation.

2. What an AI-powered marketing system actually is, and how it differs from what most teams have built 

There is a meaningful difference between a collection of AI tools and a marketing system. A system connects strategy, execution, optimization, and reporting in sequence, so output moves without constant manual intervention. We show you what that architecture looks like across demand generation, paid media, pipeline nurturing, and analytics, and where each function fits.

3. A live demonstration: an AI marketing system built and run in real time 

No prerecorded demo. No best-case scenario constructed in advance. We will build and run a working AI marketing system live during the session, showing exactly which components connect, how decisions get made at each stage, what the output looks like across channels, and where human oversight is required. If something does not run cleanly in the moment, you will see that too.

4. How to scale paid media performance without scaling the team managing it 

Paid media is one of the highest-leverage places to apply AI infrastructure, and one of the most commonly mismanaged. The session will cover how AI systems handle campaign monitoring, bid strategy signals, audience segmentation updates, and performance reporting in a way that reduces the manual workload on your team without removing the strategic judgment that actually moves ROAS and CAC.

5. Pipeline development and lead nurturing at scale without proportional headcount

Building and maintaining a pipeline is operationally expensive. We will show you how AI systems handle lead scoring, nurture sequence logic, timing optimization, and handoff signals to sales so your team is focused on high-value conversations instead of the mechanics of keeping the pipeline moving.

6. How to connect your marketing system to the metrics your board actually cares about

The board is not asking about impressions or publish frequency. They are asking about CAC, LTV, pipeline contribution, and payback period. You will learn how AI marketing systems get built around revenue metrics from the start, not retrofitted after the fact, and what that connection looks like in a reporting layer your leadership team can actually read.

7. Where human strategic judgment is non-negotiable in an AI marketing system 

The marketing organizations that fail with AI do so because they automate the wrong layer. Positioning, ICP targeting, messaging hierarchy, and offer strategy are not tasks for a system to own. We will show you where humans must remain in the loop and how to structure a system that handles execution without losing the strategic coherence that makes execution matter.

8. How growth-stage companies are running more campaigns with smaller teams 

Real examples from companies operating in the same conditions your team is in right now: flat headcount, growing pipeline targets, boards that want board-ready attribution. We walk through what the setup looked like, what changed operationally when the system went live, and what the output difference was across paid, organic, and lifecycle channels.

MEET THE SPEAKER

Hamlet Azarian has spent 20+ years doing one thing: figuring out why growth-stage companies plateau, and building the systems that move them forward. He founded Azarian Growth Agency as a full-funnel, data-driven growth firm, and has since helped companies across SaaS, e-commerce, fintech, and B2B tech raise over $300M and build marketing engines that scale without proportional headcount growth.

He is not a theorist. The AI marketing systems he will demonstrate in this session are the same ones his team builds and runs for clients. His work sits at the intersection of performance marketing, AI-native workflow design, and revenue operations, and his standard for a useful session is simple: you should be able to use what you learn the same week you learn it.

Hamlet also works directly with portfolio company marketing and leadership teams, which gives him a specific vantage point on what growth-stage CMOs are actually facing at the board level right now.
He has led 17 previous sessions in [A] Growth Lab webinar series, covering advanced Google Ads, Meta strategy, email infrastructure, server-side tracking, GEO, vibe marketing, and generative AI, consistently drawing senior marketing leaders who want operational depth, not surface-level overviews.

title: Rewiring Humans for AI Era
host: RobinTests, Consulate General of Argentina in New York
hosts: Consulate General of Argentina in New York
description:
Rewiring Humans for the AI Era

The Rise of the $26T Brain Economy: What Founders, Corporates, and Investors Need to Understand to Build the Next Unicorn

AI is scaling faster than human biology can adapt to it.

While companies race to deploy models, agents, and automation, a new invisible frontier is emerging: the neurobiological infrastructure behind human performance.

This session explores how:

* cognitive overload,
* attention fragmentation,
* chronic stress,
* motivation neurochemistry,
* and nervous system regulation

are becoming critical economic variables in the AI era.

In a world where productivity, creativity, decision-making, and adaptability define competitive advantage, the next generation of billion-dollar companies may not be built on software alone — but on human cognitive optimization.

A frontier conversation at the intersection of:

* neurobiology,
* AI readiness,
* brain capital,
* cognitive performance,
* attention economics,
* nervous system regulation,
* and the future of human adaptation.

Presented by Argentine neurotechnology company RobinTests, with the support of the Argentine Consulate in New York.

Join founder Noelia Aguirre for an exploration into why the AI economy may also become the Brain Economy — and what humans will need to become truly AI-ready.

title: Preparing Today for a Strong Exit Tomorrow
description:
Selling a company is not just about the deal. It is about decisions and preparations that occur long before you go to market.

Founders often focus on buyers, purchase price and closing mechanics. However, the most successful exits are built well in advance. The right metrics, clean financials, strong legal foundations and thoughtful planning can significantly increase your exit value and leverage when it matters most.

The panel will include two M&A-focused investment bankers, a capital markets accountant and an M&A lawyer. The discussion will cover:

🚀 What buyers really care about today
The metrics, signals and trends driving valuations in tech and tech-enabled businesses

💰 How to increase your company’s value before a sale
Positioning, preparation and strategic decisions that move the needle on price and terms

🧠 Building for an exit from day one
Operational, financial and legal steps that reduce risk and improve attractiveness

📊 Reading the market
How timing, industry trends and competitive activity shape exit opportunities

⚠️ Common founder mistakes
What often gets overlooked and how it can impact derail a successful sale

🎯 Real-world insights
Lessons from deals, including what worked, what did not and what founders should do differently

You built something great. Now learn how to exit on your own terms.

title: Collective Action for AI Safety
description:
AI APOCALYPSE OR SALVATION? THE CLOCK IS TICKING.

You might be feeling a knot in your stomach lately; the same feeling you get thinking about climate change, watching democratic norms fall like so many dominoes, or seeing kids glued to their screens, frying their attention spans, having algorithms permanently impact their brain development—a feeling of helplessness. 

We post. We protest. We panic. But nothing changes. 

What if there were a solution? 

This goes beyond a presentation - it's an invitation to reclaim power.

Spartacus.app is a weapon that ordinary people like us have been waiting for.  Drawing on insights from Game Theory, it combines conditional pledges and temporary anonymity to create something revolutionary: a means for the powerless to gain power. 

Think about it: Why don't people speak up? Because it's not "worth it." The risk is too high, the reward too uncertain. 

But what if you knew you weren't alone? What if you knew exactly how many others would stand with you? 

That's what Spartacus does. It builds critical mass. And numbers equal leverage, protection, and impact. 

Whether you're building communities, organizing unions, gathering signatures, exposing corruption, fighting bullies, or starting a revolution within your organization, Spartacus makes taking action worth it. 

Join us in NYC on June 4.  Bring your frustrations, your dreams, and your most provocative ideas about politics, activism, and tech. 

We'll play a coordination game that will blow your mind, demo the platform that could change everything, and talk about the collective action problems facing humanity, including the ones YOU care about. 

The powerful don't want you to have leverage. The status quo is betting you'll stay home. Your future self is begging you to be there. 

We’re in, if you’re in.

title: Two Live Builds: AI Trading Agents with Alpaca + Live Trading Systems with MachineTrader
description:
Join Alpaca and MachineTrader for two live, hands-on workshops, exploring AI-powered trading and automation - from AI agents to fully deployed live trading systems.

12:00 PM – Doors Open.  Come enjoy some coffee and refreshments and get to know the Alpaca and MachineTrader teams.

12:30 PM – Session 1: Build an AI Trading Agent with Alpaca

2:30 PM – Break (Water & Snacks)

3:00 PM – Session 2: Build a Live Trading System with MachineTrader

Session 1: 
Alpaca will guide you through building an AI-powered trading agent connected to real market data and paper trading workflows. You’ll learn how AI agents can analyze markets, generate trade ideas, query market data using natural language, and execute automated workflows. We’ll kick things off with a live demo, then help you build your own working AI trading agent in a paper trading environment. Prepare for the session and sign up for an account - https://app.alpaca.markets/signup

Session 2:
MachineTrader will show how to take a trading idea and turn it into a live automated system running on your own private server. Using AI-generated logic and MachineTrader’s visual drag-and-drop interface, we’ll walk through building and deploying a bearish options strategy (a bear put spread) end-to-end, from identifying the setup and structuring the trade to broker execution and live position monitoring. 

Bring your laptop. 
You’ll leave with working systems you built yourself.

Perfect for developers, traders, and builders exploring AI, automation, and real-world trading infrastructure.

Bridging Together Communities & AI
description:

Co-hosting with Youth Civic Service, a nonprofit dedicated to empowering young people through community engagement, and the Association of Chinese American Physicians--this event brings together roughly 50 attendees in Chinatown, NYC for a live demonstration of the HealthBridge platform, a formal presentation by the founding team, and an open conversation about what it actually takes to build health technology for the communities most often left behind. Refreshments provided. HealthBridge was invited to present at the U.S. Congress on April 21st & 22nd, 2026.

title: Q2 2026 State of AI + AE (Autonomous Economy) & Investment Opportunities
description:
We're at an interesting moment in AI.

The hype cycle has matured. The infrastructure bets are playing out. Agentic systems are moving from demos to production. And a new layer - what we call the Autonomous Economy - is starting to take shape in ways that matter for how companies get built and how capital gets deployed.

Most of the coverage still hasn't caught up. So George is going to share his read on it directly.

What we'll cover:
→ The Autonomous Economy - a working definition. Not a buzzword. A framework for understanding what's actually shifting and why it matters in 2026.
→ State of AI in Q2 2026: signal vs. noise. Where adoption is real, where infrastructure is consolidating, and where the application layer is opening up.
→ Investment themes Untapped is watching. The 3-5 verticals and theses we're most excited about right now - with real context, not generic categories.
→ What this means for founders and operators. Practical implications: what to build, what to avoid, and how to position for what's coming.
→ Live Q&A. Bring your questions. George will answer them directly.

Who should come:
Founders building AI-native or AE-adjacent companies. Investors and allocators looking for a fund-level read on where the market is going. Operators and leaders navigating AI inside their organizations. Anyone who's tired of surface-level takes and wants real signal.

About George:
George Bandarian is an AI investor, keynote speaker, and the Founder and General Partner of Untapped Ventures, an early-stage AI venture fund backing exceptional technical founders building the systems, infrastructure, and applications powering the Autonomous Economy (AE).

Through Untapped Ventures, he has backed 35+ AI-native companies, including xAI, You.com, Extropic, Liquid AI, and Harper, with a thesis spanning agentic systems, vertical AI, and next-generation compute.

George works closely with founders at the pre-seed and seed stages on company building, positioning, and go-to-market, and is also the host of the Agentic podcast (www.agenticai.vc).

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

This session dives into how global organizations and world leaders are embracing digital sovereignty (or aren’t) to drive smarter strategy, stronger resilience, and lasting growth amidst a period of uncertainty shaped by geopolitical tension. Expect a dynamic conversation on how today’s executives are turning complexity into competitive advantage—and redefining what it means to lead in a rapidly changing world.

title: Inside the Investor Mindset: What It Really Takes
description:
Raising venture capital is one of the most misunderstood processes in the startup ecosystem.

Many founders build strong products but struggle to understand how investors actually evaluate opportunities, what signals create conviction, and how to structure a fundraising process that leads to real outcomes.

In this session, Angel León, will break down how venture investors think, how early-stage companies are evaluated, and what founders should do to prepare before raising capital.

The session will feature a curated panel of investors from Angeles Investors, Ghost Angels, Remedy Product Studio, and NEXT powered by Shulman Rogers, creating a dynamic and interactive discussion around how investment decisions are really made. We’ll move beyond theory into real perspectives from active investors, including how they assess opportunities, what drives conviction, and what founders consistently get wrong.

Throughout the session, we’ll open the conversation into a live discussion with the panel—creating space for deeper insights, real-time perspectives, and direct engagement around fundraising strategy, narrative, and positioning.

This event is designed for founders planning to raise capital in the next 6–18 months and looking to gain a clearer, more practical understanding of the venture fundraising process.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Secondary is Primary Panel & Happy Hour
description:
Private markets have become the dominant venue for innovation and value creation. With companies staying private longer and IPO timelines shifting toward mega-offerings, liquidity has evolved from an occasional event into essential financial infrastructure.

Join us for a heavyweight panel featuring the two dominant platform CEOs in the secondary market, alongside an institutional GP perspective, moderated by PitchBook’s lead voice in venture research.

The Perspectives:
•  Moderator: Kyle Stanford — Director of VC Research, PitchBook
•  Tom Callahan — CEO, Nasdaq Private Market
•  Kelly Rodriques — CEO, Forge Global
•  Jared Carmel — Managing Partner, Manhattan Venture Partners

Sponsor: Sichenzia Ross Ference Carmel LLP

The Discussion: Secondary markets are no longer viewed as opportunistic transactions or end-of-fund solutions. They are increasingly strategic tools for navigating extended private company life cycles.We will explore how private market infrastructure is evolving in real time and why “Secondary is Primary” has shifted from a contrarian thesis to a market reality.

Key Focal Points:
•  Strategic Liquidity: How CFOs and Founders are utilizing structured secondaries to manage RSU cliffs and ISO deadlines.
•  The DPI Mandate: Solving the distribution crunch for LPs and GPs in a shifting exit landscape.
•  Institutionalization: The transition from one-off deals to systematic, platform-driven private market value creation.

The Room: To ensure a high-density environment for peer-to-peer connection, we are curating an audience of:
•  Founders & Executives: Leading late-stage or scaling companies.
• Strategic Operators: VP-level and above with a focus on equity and growth.
•  Institutional Allocators: GPs, LPs, and family offices navigating the secondary landscape.
•  Ecosystem Leaders: Partners in banking, secondaries, and private wealth.

Attendance is curated and space is limited. We encourage you to arrive on time.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek – a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the tech ecosystem.

title: Health AI from Research to Production
description:
*Light bites and beverages will be served thanks to our friends at R1!*

Most health AI events follow the same formula: researchers talk about what they built. Founders talk about what they're selling. The audience leaves wondering how the two connect.

We want to break that. We're bringing together scientist-founders to show how the worlds of AI research and healthcare product converge. How do you take frontier science—whether that's foundation models, behavioral insights, or clinical validation—and turn it into a product that clinicians and patients actually use? What do you keep? What do you cut? Where does rigor meet velocity?

For founders scaling health AI, for investors evaluating science vs. hype, for researchers building for impact: this is the hour where the real work gets named.

We're kicking off with a fire-side chat with Martin Seneviratne (R1) for a fireside chat on moving healthcare AI from theory to operation. What does it take to deploy AI in environments where accuracy, compliance, and scale all matter at once? We’ll unpack how R1 is turning messy clinical and financial data into working systems—and what that means for builders who want to take AI beyond demos and into tools people depend on every day.

Next we'll dig into a panel with early-stage founders, living these challenges on a daily basis.

Speakers: 
Fireside: Martin Seneviratne, Co-CEO of R37 @ R1

Panel:
Vitalay Fomin, Co-Founder/CEO @ Numenos AI
Nyamitse-Calvin Mahinda, Founder/CEO @ Vital Audio
Kerry Weinberg, Co-Founder & CEO @ PhenoML
Tim Martens, Co-Founder @ Vaark Ventures, CMO @ uCTRL, heart surgeon

title: Proof of Build -An agent building hackathon by Lyzr
description:
PROOF OF BUILD
->An agent hackathon by Lyzr, during NY Tech Week.
->Six hours to build. One yard party to close it out.

Tech Week is full of panels about the future of AI. This isn't one of them.

Proof of Build is a working afternoon for people who'd rather spend six hours writing code than sitting through another keynote about code.

We're bringing together ~150 founding engineers, ML builders, and product engineers to build agents that solve something real - not a demo, not a slideware prototype, something you'd actually use on Monday. 

Lyzr is hosting, our infra is available if you want to use it, and our team will be in the room building alongside you.

Then we close the day properly. Once the builds land and the demos wrap, the yard opens - DJ, drinks, food, and the kind of crowd that'll still be talking about your agent at midnight.

Come with a laptop, a teammate (or find one here), and a problem you've been meaning to solve. Leave with a working agent, a small crowd that saw you ship it, and - if your build clears the bar - a prize.

Prize to be announced soon.

Who this is for: founding engineers, ML/AI engineers, senior software engineers, and technical founders currently building with agents or planning to. This is a hands-on build day, not a meetup - please only RSVP if you plan to actually code.

Format: Arrive 2 PM. Team up or fly solo. Build until 8 PM. Five-minute demos. Yard party runs until late. Light judging, real prizes, zero pitch decks.

Lyzr Events will confirm your spot within a few days of RSVP. Address shared with approved guests closer to the date.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Community Jam: NYC
description:

title: AI at Scale: From Deployment to Business Impact
description:
Most organizations have deployed AI in some way, but far fewer have translated it into measurable business impact. 

Our curated panel of AI leaders and practitioners from Anthropic, Akamai, Cisco, and Jobbrex will explore what happens after deployment, using real-world use cases and on-the-ground insights to examine where AI underperforms, where costs rise, and what it takes to drive business impact and achieve ROI.

Designed for team leaders, founders, and operators moving past the experimentation phase, this session offers practical guidance on AI transformation, adoption, workflow integration, and how to apply AI where it creates the most value. 

PANELISTS:
- Kyra Atekwana, Anthropic
   AI Education & Transformation

- Lena Hall, Akamai
   Senior Director, Developers & AI

- Tyrone Ross Jr, Cisco
   AI Infrastructure & Product Strategy

- Maureen Erokwu, Jobbrex

title: GenAi: Acquire Properties Swiftly
description:
Project REDS, "Real Estate Development Streamliner."

Without Project REDS, the current predevelopment timeline is:
1) Site Selection & Feasibility: 6 months to 2 years (includes market analysis and locating land).
2) Due Diligence & Land Acquisition: 6–9 months (includes soil testing and environmental checks).
3) Investment Scoring: 1 to 6+ months (varies by NOI, property value, and cap rate).

With Project REDS, the current predevelopment timeline is:
1) Site Selection & Feasibility: 10 minutes (includes market analysis and locating land).
2) Due Diligence & Land Acquisition: 10 minutes (includes soil testing and environmental checks).
3) Investment Scoring: 10 minutes (varies by NOI, property value, and cap rate).

This workshop will allow the audience to gain hands-on experience on how to use generative AI to acquire properties more swiftly than before! This workshop will enable developers to acquire land, multifamily, and commercial properties swiftly, test scenarios, and visualize community impact before breaking ground!

Learn more about us: https://www.projectreds.info/

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Screens That See: How AI Is Turning Signage Into Revenue.
host: WillDom, Consulate General of Argentina in New York, CieloVision
hosts: Consulate General of Argentina in New York
description:
Monetizing the unseen moments.

Every day, billions of moments pass in front of screens that aren't paying attention. A customer walks in. A family browses the menu. A guest waits in the lobby. The screen plays the same loop it played an hour ago, a day ago, a week ago — regardless of who's standing there. That's wasted inventory, and most of the industry has accepted it.

Join CieloVision and WillDom for a fireside conversation at NY Tech Week on how a US founder and an Argentine engineering partner built a new category of intelligent signage — and what it took to get there.

In 18 months, CieloVision went from a services company to a platform redefining how physical screens generate revenue. WillDom, the Argentine engineering and brand partner behind the build, was the reason it held together. Together, they've built a system that turns the screens retailers, restaurants, hotels, gyms, and entertainment venues already own into responsive environments — with a measured 25.7% lift over static content in active deployments.

We'll cover:

• The pivot. What it takes to rebuild a company, a product, a brand, and a team — all at once — and survive it.
• The market timing. Why screens that have been deployed for years are suddenly the most valuable real estate nobody is monetizing.
• The category shift. Why digital signage as we know it is broken, and what changes when a display understands who's in front of it — in real time, without storing a single piece of personal data.
• The partnership model. What US–Argentina engineering partnerships look like when they actually work, and why the next wave of AI products will be built this way.

Fireside chat 

Imre Szenttornyay, CEO, CieloVision 
Beatriz Burgos, SVP of Marketing & Managing Director, WillDom

Moderated by 

Peter Czimback, CieloVision Advisory Board

Who this is for 

Founders, operators, and investors interested in where intelligent signage is heading, how AI products actually get built and scaled, and what cross-border engineering partnerships look like when they produce category-defining work.

Hosted as part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Wear Your Coach: A Live Wearable Movement Demo
description:
Name: Wear Your Coach: A Live Wearable Movement Demo
description:
A live wearable scoliosis tech demo + networking event at Tech Week 2026
Ever finish a workout, a yoga flow, or a stretch routine and wonder if you actually did it right? Most of us have no idea when our bodies are compensating, tilting, or drifting off course. LumbarGuide makes the invisible visible — and tonight, you get to feel it yourself.
This hands-on demo event puts a working wearable biofeedback system directly on attendees. No slides. No theory. Just real-time haptic feedback the moment your body moves wrong.
The evening features:
🏋️ Plank Station — "How Long Can You Actually Hold It Right?" Think you know how to plank? Strap on the device and find out. The wearable detects the moment your lower back starts to sag, tracks your hold time, and buzzes when the clock runs out. No more guessing. No more cheating yourself.
🧍 Scoliosis Simulation — "Now Imagine Doing This Alone, Every Day" Watch a short clinical rehab video — as many times as you want, just like a real patient would after a clinic visit. Then try to replicate the movement from memory. The sensor will tell you if your correction went the right direction, if your pelvis compensated, and whether you held it correctly. Most people are humbled. All people get it.
🤝 Networking — Connect with founders, healthtech investors, engineers, and wellness enthusiasts in a casual, participatory setting.

Hear from the founder behind LumbarGuide — originally built to help adolescents with lumbar scoliosis practice rehabilitation exercises accurately at home using dual IMU sensors, it revealed something much more universal: most of us move through life with zero feedback on whether we're doing it right when the professionals aren’t around.
Whether you're into wearables, healthtech, fitness, or just curious what it feels like when a device knows your body better than you do — you'll leave this event moving differently.
No medical background needed. Just bring your body.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Your Next Exec Won't Be Human
host: Masinov Company / Claw Mart, Build Momentum
description:
The startup org chart is breaking.

Founders are under pressure to move faster, stay leaner, and build more with less. But the old answer (hire a full executive team, add more headcount, and hope the org chart catches up) no longer works for most early-stage companies.

At the same time, two new forms of leverage are changing how startups operate: AI agents and senior fractional.

AI agents open up a nearly infinite set of opportunities to automate tasks, redesign workflows, monitor execution, and support day-to-day operations.

Fractional leaders give founders access to experienced operators across go-to-market, product, operations, finance, engineering, design, people, and strategy without the cost or commitment of premature full-time hires. 

But for early-stage founders, the hard part is knowing how to put those pieces together.

Which roles need senior human judgment? Which functions should be fractional before they become full-time? Which tasks are ready for agents? What should stay founder-led, what should be delegated to expert operators, and what should become an AI-enabled workflow?

This NY Tech Week session helps founders answer those questions.

Felix, the AI CEO of The Masinov Company, Build Momentum, and Founders & Fractionals will guide founders through a practical, hands-on session for redesigning the early-stage operating team around founder ownership, fractional talent, future full-time hires, and AI agents.

The program includes:

A quick demystification of AI agents for early-stage founders
What agents are, what they can realistically do today, where they are useful, and where the hype gets ahead of reality.

A focused discussion on building with fractional talent
How founders can access senior operators across key functions without overhiring too early, and how to scope fractional support around outcomes instead of vague advisory work.

An interactive operating team mapping exercise
Participants will identify the tasks, roles, workflows, and bottlenecks inside their business, then draft the ideal mix of founder ownership, fractional leaders, full-time hires, and AI agents.

A real-world AI executive example
Felix will show how an AI executive can support prioritization, execution, monitoring, follow-up, and day-to-day company management inside a real operating business.

Founders will leave with a first draft of their hybrid operating roadmap: what to own, what to fractionalize, what to hire for later, and what to turn into AI-agent workflows.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Session Title: 
IEEE and the Future of AI - NYC Tech Week

Abstract: 
As artificial intelligence transitions from experimental hype to foundational infrastructure, the path forward requires a multidisciplinary approach that balances rapid innovation with global responsibility. This panel brings together a distinguished group of leaders to explore the next decade of the AI trajectory. We will examine the tech industry roadmaps and entrepreneurship strategies necessary to scale AI ventures, alongside the cutting-edge research and architectural breakthroughs emerging from world-renowned labs.

The discussion will bridge the gap between "Big Tech" implementation and the complex logistics and governance required for public sector integration. Crucially, the session will highlight the humanitarian aspects of technology, focusing on how IEEE standards and AI applications can be leveraged to solve the world’s most pressing social challenges. Join us for a comprehensive look at how these diverse sectors are intersecting to build an AI future that is as ethical and inclusive as it is technically advanced.

Moderator: 
Abhimanyu Gupta, Director of InfoTech and Services, IEEE ComSoc

Panelist Breakdown
Baw Chng: Industry Roadmap perspective (BAWMAN)

Matthew Andrews: Research & Technical Infrastructure perspective (Nokia Bell Labs)

Mariela Machado: Humanitarian Impact & Social Responsibility perspective (IEEE Humanitarian Technologies)

Jeff Lontoc: Implementation & Entrepreneurship perspective (Guidehouse)

Gautami Nadkarni: Cloud & Industry Trends perspective (Google)

title: An Executive Harbor Dinner Experience
description:
AI Adoption vs. Risk Tolerance
An Executive Harbor Dinner Experience | NYC Tech Week

Join an exclusive gathering of C-level executives, board advisors, cybersecurity leaders, and AI decision-makers for a private executive experience during NYC Tech Week.

This curated evening is designed for leaders navigating the growing tension between innovation, governance, operational efficiency, and enterprise risk in the age of AI.

Featured executive voices include Teri Green alongside senior leaders across technology, cybersecurity, infrastructure, and enterprise operations.

Together, attendees will explore how organizations are balancing AI adoption with board expectations, security realities, and evolving risk tolerance models.

Executive Discussion Themes

◆ AI & Machine Learning Governance
◆ Operational Efficiency Through AI
◆ Data Management & Enterprise Trust
◆ Legacy Systems vs. AI-Integrated Technologies
◆ Cybersecurity & Critical Infrastructure Risk
◆ Boardroom Strategy, Risk Appetite & AI Adoption

Special discussion will examine how emerging threats, including the Mythos Vulnerability, are influencing executive and board-level conversations surrounding AI governance, resilience, and critical infrastructure protection.

This is intentionally not a traditional panel.

The evening is structured as a high-trust executive roundtable focused on authentic conversation, strategic insight sharing, and meaningful relationship building among leaders shaping the future of AI adoption.

Agenda

2:45 PM — Marina Arrival & Executive Check-In

3:00 PM — Private Harbor Departure
Executive yacht experience through New York Harbor featuring views of the Statue of Liberty and Manhattan skyline

5:00 PM — Return to Dock & Transfer to The Skylark
Private transportation provided

Networking overlooking the Manhattan skyline

6:30 PM — Executive Dinner Conversation
Risk Appetite, Governance & the Business Impact of AI

7:00 PM — Curated Executive Roundtable Dinner
3-course dining experience with open bar

8:00 PM — Rooftop Networking Experience
Executive networking, cocktails, and continued discussion above New York City

Attendance is intentionally limited to preserve the quality of conversation and executive experience.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

description:

Agenda:
3:30 PM — Networking + Check-In

4:00 PM — Opening Discussion | Rob Metz + Anita
Why construction teams still operate reactively — and why AI-native infrastructure is changing how projects are managed in the field.

4:20 PM — Product & Operator Perspectives | Jobotics AI + Bowser AI
How new systems are capturing on-site reality, connecting fragmented construction data, and enabling real-time operational visibility.

Topics include:
- field-first workflows

5:00 PM — Audience Q&A + Open Discussion

title: AI for Finance: Building with Claude + Excel, MCP, and more!
description:
Finance leaders know they need to adopt AI but most don’t know where to start.

The space is filled with either hype or overly technical deep dives. This is neither.

Join us for a practical, hands-on working session where finance teams will learn how to actually apply AI to their day-to-day workflows. From forecasting to reporting to decision support, we will break down real use cases and show how to implement them using modern AI tools, agents, and workflows.

Bring your laptop. Bring your real problems.
We will build solutions together.

This is an interactive session, not a panel, where you will work alongside other operators to identify opportunities, test ideas, and walk away with workflows you can apply immediately.

Hosted by Pluvo & BMO, this session is designed to help finance teams cut manual work, move faster, and start operating in an AI-first way.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

About BMO: BMO has strong roots in and a deep understanding of the technology space and its many subsectors. Our seamless, integrated approach provides technology companies with full lifecycle coverage from whether you’re a growth-stage company in need of recurring revenue lending, a mature-stage company forming a bank syndicate or tapping the capital markets for the first time, or a well-established large multinational with the most complex financial needs, we’re here as trusted expert advisers to support you in your growth journey. Thine is a personal AI that passively listens to your real-life conversations and builds a living memory of your life. It remembers the people you meet, decisions you make, and ideas you discuss - so you never have to. It syncs with your calendar and email, connects the dots, and sends you smart nudges exactly when you need them. Think of it as a second brain that actually listens. iOS only for now.

Notable investments include OpenAI, Anthropic, Xai, SpaceX, World Labs, Mercor, Crusoe, Scale, Deel, Figure Technologies, Netskope, Digital Ocean.

​About AGI House:

​At AGI House, our mission is to support humanity's journey toward AGI by fostering collaboration among the brightest minds in the AI community. Through a diverse range of events, including hackathons, dinners, salons, and mini-conferences, we create opportunities for insightful dialogues and meaningful partnerships. title: VC Connect
description:
VC Connect is an exclusive and carefully curated gathering designed to break away from the traditional pitch format.

The evening will begin with a reverse pitch session, where selected VCs will share their fund focus, investment thesis, and what they look for in founders — offering a unique opportunity to understand the ecosystem from the investor’s perspective.

Following the conversation, attendees will have the opportunity to connect directly with participating investors in a more relaxed setting designed to encourage meaningful conversations, strategic networking, and potential opportunities.

The participating funds invest in early-stage startups across Latin America and the United States, from pre-seed to Series A.

This edition will feature investors from leading funds across the region, including:
• Ashley Ryder - Vamos Ventures 
• Jeffrey Reitman - Entropy Ventures 
• Richard Odior - Zeal Capital Partners
• Jess Schram - Remedy
• Neal Mintz - Wischoff Ventures
• Claire Biernacki - BBG Ventures 
• Lauren Reeves - MGV VC
• Maurice Russo - Lerer Hippeau 
• Dorothy Chang - Flybridge
• Kody Amburgey - Alumni Ventures
• Lauren Roberts - Forum VC

​📍 Limited spots. By invitation only.

About us 
​Lazo
Lazo provides incorporation, tax, bookkeeping, fractional CFO and fundraising solutions for startups, powered by AI Agents and Human Experts. Through our Lazo One platform, we streamline essential operations, enabling founders to focus on growth while we manage the complexities. We help startups scale with confidence.

Vamos Ventures
VamosVentures is an LA-based VC fund backing diverse founders building scalable, tech-driven companies across Health & Wellness, Future of Work, FinTech, and Sustainability. They believe innovation can come from anyone — and that diverse teams create stronger outcomes and lasting impact.

Your Cohort
Your Cohort is a hands-on finance partner providing fractional CFO leadership and execution across FP&A. We turn finance into an operating system, with financial forecasting, cash flow management, and investor-ready reporting, so you can manage cash, reduce risk, and scale with clarity.

AlleyCorp
AlleyCorp is a New York-based venture capital firm and venture studio that incubates and invests in transformative technology companies. AlleyCorp focuses on backing founders building in sectors such as AI, digital infrastructure, healthcare, enterprise software, and marketplaces. Beyond capital, the firm partners closely with early-stage teams to help launch, scale, and build enduring businesses with global ambition.

Otis 
Otis is the AI digital advertiser for businesses and agencies to manage digital ads across all major platforms. Otis does the work of a professional performance marketing team, with better results.

Somnio Software
Somnio is a digital product agency helping companies build, scale, and evolve software products,  from early-stage MVPs to full product evolution. Recognized by Google as one of the top 5 Flutter experts worldwide, Somnio brings deep technical expertise across mobile, web, AI, and backend to deliver end-to-end product experiences that drive real business impact.

title: From First Principles: Physics AI for the Next Era of Industrial Innovation
description:
The way complex physical systems are designed and built is changing. AI-driven simulation is compressing development cycles, unlocking design spaces that were previously out of reach, and shifting engineering from iteration-heavy processes to system-level optimization. This panel brings together engineering leaders, founders, and practitioners at the frontier of physics AI to explore what's actually being deployed — across advanced manufacturing, industrial systems, and beyond. Hosted by PhysicsX with NVIDIA.

description:

Hosted by Siva Surendira, founder of Lyzr.ai, who has spent the past year sitting across from leadership teams at Pepsi, WTW, Prophet, and 200+ enterprises deploying AI agents. 

This is a small, curated gathering for candid conversation about what is actually working in enterprise AI — what is moving beyond pilots, where agents are creating value, what is still breaking, and where the market is heading next.

A deliberate mid-week reset away from the crowded streets and panel rooms. No slides. No pitches. One boat, a small room of peers, and the skyline at golden hour.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Future of The Store Floor
Shipping Faster with AI Coding Agents: What's Working and What's Not
description:
Join Driver AI for an exclusive evening panel during NY Tech Week, co-hosted by Materialize and Liberty Ventures.  We're bringing together engineering leaders, investors, and operators navigating the shift to agentic development — and the real decisions that come with it.

Panelists include:
- Adam Tilton, CEO and Co-founder of Driver AI
- Matt Nassr, VP of AI & Global Data Engineering at Optiver
- Jacob Radkiewicz, CTO of ShipBob
- James Musson, VP Engineering at Lucanet

The way software gets built is changing faster than most engineering organizations can keep up with.  There's no shortage of one-off opinions on the topic, but few honest conversations about the actual journey to make agentic coding work. 

Adam and the panelists will share what it looks like to redesign your development process around agentic coding — the workflow changes, inevitable context chasm, common mistakes and challenging cultural shifts. And lastly the productivity gains that ultimately make it worth it.

About Your Host
Backed by YC and Google Ventures, Driver AI is the rigorous, pre-compiled context layer that brings deterministic performance to agentic coding at scale. Learn more at driver.ai.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Deel&a16z Masterclass: Founder's guide to building one of the world's fastest growing companies
description:
A rare, fireside conversation with two architects of hypergrowth: Shuo Wang (Deel Co-Founder & CRO—who helped build the world's fastest growing company, surpassing $1B+ revenue in under six years) and Anish Acharya (a16z GP and Deel board member who backed Deel from the start). 

Shuo, a two time founder (with one successful exit to iRobot) will talk about how she founded two companies to solve problems from her own life. She'll discuss how she saw obstacles as opportunities and built solutions to real-world problems. 

Topics: global scaling, building for solutions instead of TAM, hard decisions founders face. 

One of a16z's flagship NY Tech Week events. 
Drinks & networking to follow.

title: Bodega and Small Business Innovation Summit - Digitizing the Corner Store
host: Bodega and Small Business Group, Venture Outfitters, Inc. DBA Tech Ranch
hosts: Venture Outfitters, Inc. DBA Tech Ranch
description:
The Bodega and Small Business Association, representing neighborhood businesses across New York City, invites you to join a conversation on how we can better support and strengthen small businesses in our communities.

There are 15,000+ bodegas in New York City. They move billions in commerce—and almost none of them run on modern infrastructure.

That’s not a problem. It’s a massive opportunity.

The Bodega and Small Business Innovation Summit brings together founders, operators, investors, and technologists to explore how media tech, AI, fintech, and security can transform the future of small business in urban communities.

Now featuring partners including Fiado, Ensamble Etereo, and World Mobile, expanding the conversation around technology, financial tools, creative infrastructure, and connectivity for independent operators.

This isn’t about adding gadgets—it’s about building integrated systems that allow small businesses to unlock new revenue streams, compete with larger players, and scale on their own terms. Modernization without displacement. Tech without erasure.

This will be a free event, with refreshments provided and ample opportunity to network, connect, and engage with fellow founders, operators, and community leaders throughout the evening.

Hosted by the Bodega and Small Business Association, Tech Ranch, Bodegas TV, and Angeles Investors as part of #NYTechWeek, this is where community-rooted commerce meets institutional capital.

If you're building, investing in, or supporting the future of local commerce—this is a room you want to be in.

📍 10 Centre St, Bronx, NYC
Stuyvesant Yacht Club
🗓 June 4 | 4:00 PM

Limited capacity — curated room.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: RE:WEALTH #NYCTECH WEEK
description:
Innovation builds companies. RE: WEALTH explores how more of us can participate in ownership, investing, and the future of wealth.

​Join founders, investors, artists, and corporate leaders for a cross-sector conversation on reclaiming wealth and expanding participation in the tech ecosystem.

​Overview
​While much of the tech ecosystem focuses on building companies, RE: WEALTH explores how more of us can meaningfully participate in building and moving wealth.

​At its core, RE: WEALTH invites us to reconsider our relationship to wealth and resources — to reclaim, rethink, and remember that participation in shaping wealth is not reserved for a select few.

​This is a space for all of us — including those who may not traditionally see themselves as part of the “tech economy.”

​Our perspectives, resources, and participation are essential to shaping our future of wealth.

Check the RE:WEALTH event page for updates on the agenda and speakers.


This event is a part of #NYCTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

description:

We’ll dive into what makes NYC uniquely positioned for the next wave of AI innovation, the realities of building in today’s market, lessons from scaling early-stage companies, and where the future of AI is headed.

title: The New 0→1 GTM Playbook for AI-Native Founders
description:
Join Digitalista during NYTechWeek for a curated founder conversation on what 0→1 go-to-market looks like now that AI is changing how fast companies can build, test, sell, and learn.

This is designed as a high-signal founder room, not another generic GTM panel.

We’re bringing together early-stage investors, AI-native GTM operators, and founders/operators building in fast-moving markets to unpack how teams move from scattered customer calls, vague ICPs, founder-led sales, and disconnected experiments into a repeatable system for learning, messaging, growth, and revenue.

4:30–5:00pm - Arrival & networking
5:00–5:45pm - Panel
5:45–6:00pm - Q&A
6:00–6:30pm - Networking

Potential discussion themes:

– how founders find signal before scaling the wrong GTM motion

– what 0→1 GTM looks like when AI compresses timelines

– how AI changes customer research, outbound, positioning, and sales

– what investors now expect from early traction

– how to build a GTM system before hiring a full GTM team

– how to move from scattered experiments to a repeatable GTM workflow

Speakers include:

– Jay Bhandari, GTM at Clay
– Nikolai Kirpichikov, Partner at AltaIR Capital
– Alexander Zemlyak, Partner at Geek Ventures
– Additional AI-native founder / operator speaker TBA

What’s the setup?
We’ve got a dedicated space, and name tags at the door
Flash a business card or a LinkedIn QR—whatever works
Dress code? Business‑casual. Jeans totally fine.

A quick word on Startup+
We kicked this off in 2021, in the middle of the pandemic, because Zoom mixers just weren’t cutting it. Fast‑forward: 25k members, 75+ mixers, 12 pitch nights, and 6000 folks through the doors. We keep it simple—connect good people and let the magic happen.

host: Los Angeles Cleantech Incubator (LACI), Urban Future Lab (UFL) at NYU Tandon

description:
Calling all startups! 

Join LACI climate programs @ BATWorks, Urban Future Lab (UFL) at NYU Tandon, and the ClimateTech Expertise Network (CTEN) for a Climate Scaling Summit: Financing the Full Capital Stack on June 4, 2026. 

Hear reverse pitches from investors, capital allocators, and lenders about the role their work plays in the overarching development and commercialization of climate technology, covering everything from working capital, equipment leasing, infrastructure financing, grants, venture capital and insurance. Then meet 1-1 with these organizations to learn how your startup can plug into the resources and opportunities these organizations provide. 

Organizations include: LACI Cleantech Debt Fund, Brooklyn Alliance Capital, Closed Loop, Wells Fargo, NY Green Bank, Enduring Planet, National Grid Ventures, Am Fam Institute, GreenieRe, Prime Coalition/Trellis, Community Development Venture Capital, US Green Bank 50, NYSERDA, Montauk Capital, The Clean Fight, and more. 

This Climate Scaling Summit is one of 4 convenings LACI is hosting at BATWorks in 2026, all under the theme of accelerating commercialization of clean energy innovation in NY by focusing on common problems faced by startups after the pilot stage. LACI has deployed $25M in cleantech pilots over the past four years—from first commercial deployments to the nation's first zero-emissions delivery zone and is now supercharging the Pilots at BAT program (https://www.batworks.nyc/pilots-at-bat) and deeper deployment of the LACI Cleantech Debt Fund (https://laincubator.org/laci-debt-fund/). 

This event will be followed by a happy hour. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

This event is sponsored by NYSERDA and Wells Fargo.

title: CREWASIS Presents: Human in the Loop: Marketing in the Age of Agentic AI for Enterprise
description:
CREWASIS presents: Human in the Loop in the age of Agentic AI
https://crewasis.ai/

Cindy Cook, Chair of the New York Angels will be providing an interesting perspective on how Agentic AI investments are being looked at from a preseed/seed standpoint.  

Sharon Joseph, CEO of CREWASIS, will provide a perspective in building an AI native company and adding in agentic AI. CREWASIS is a Techstars company.

Agentic AI has made it trivial to build.
It has made it effortless to generate.
It has made it faster than ever to launch and optimize.

It has not made it easier to stand out.
It has not made it easier to earn trust.

Enterprise teams are now operating in a sea of Agentic tools, each promising automation, each claiming intelligence.

But most are producing more noise, not better decisions.

So the real question is not how to use AI Agents and test them for success.

It’s how to use Agentic AI to actually improve marketing outcomes inside enterprise environments.

This session brings together operators and investors at the front lines of that shift to unpack what’s working, what’s not, and where human judgment still creates disproportionate advantage. What's hot in the space and working? What's not?

If you’re a B2B SaaS founder, investor, or enterprise operator navigating Agentic AI in your go-to-market, this conversation is designed for you.

We will be discussing how marketers, researchers, digital execs, and digital agencies are using Agentic AI in Enterprise companies. We'll also dig into how investors are looking at Martech AI companies and what differentiates them.

Our panel:

Cindy Cook, Chair of NYC Angels, Agentic AI investor
https://www.linkedin.com/in/cindy-cook

Tova Feldheim, Co-Founder, York Effect, Techstars 2024
https://www.linkedin.com/in/tovafeldheim/

Moderator: Sharon Joseph, CEO & Founder, CREWASIS, Agentic AI Platform, Techstars 2024, HBS, m+a Accelerator 
https://www.linkedin.com/in/sharonemilyjoseph/

Follow us on instagram: @crewasis.ai

title: AI Velocity Trading: Official Launch & Strategy Discussion
description:
MachineTrader.io is excited to launch its new dedicated algorithmic trading site as part of Tech Week - AI Velocity Trading.  While MachineTrader offers its users a platform for writing proprietary trading algorithms using the visual programming environment Node-RED, AI Velocity offers pre-built, backtested strategies running in live and paper trading accounts.

AI Velocity Trading is a high-performance execution platform offering retail traders the infrastructure,  sophisticated, and pre-configured algorithms reserved for hedge funds.  Users have Immediate access to professional-grade, multi-leg options strategies—including Iron Condors, Iron Butterflies, and Bull Call Spreads—alongside momentum, mean reversion, and statistical arbitrage models.

AI Velocity’s scale is our speed. Unlike legacy institutions, we iterate on market signals in seconds, not weeks, maintaining a market-neutral posture through pure algorithmic rigor.

Evidence-Based Trading
Every strategy is supported by exhaustive backtesting data and transparent daily performance reports, ensuring your edge is backed by numbers, not intuition.

Seamless Alpaca Integration
AI Velocity is not a broker-dealer.  Your trades are executed throughout the top-rated, commission-free Alpaca Trading API. Their state-of-the-art trading architecture handles the heavy lifting of execution so you can focus on the signals.

Risk-Free Iteration
In this session, we will show you validate your approach with the ability to run simulations in up to three concurrent paper trading accounts before putting real capital at risk.  Move from sandbox to live trading with a single toggle when your data confirms the edge.

What You’ll Do
Bring your laptop and leave with running strategies deployed to your Alpaca paper account —without writing code.  We’ll take you through some of the strategies and explore the thinking behind them. 

Who Should Attend
Those curious about how trading systems work end-to-end.
People interested in automation who don't want to build from scratch.
Anyone exploring AI workflows for real-world use cases.

https://www.aivelocitytrading.com

title: Innovation at the Regulatory Frontier: A Digital Assets Cocktail Evening
description:
Innovation at the Regulatory Frontier: A Digital Assets Cocktail Evening

The digital assets space moves fast — and the regulatory landscape is evolving to keep pace. Whether you're launching infrastructure, deploying capital, or navigating compliance as new frameworks take shape, the next chapter belongs to the builders and investors who move boldly and stay ahead of the curve.

Join Fox Rothschild and EisnerAmper for an evening of conversation and connection with the founders, executives, senior industry leaders, investors, and operators shaping the future of digital assets, fintech and blockchain. The evening will feature a brief fireside chat on the forces reshaping crypto regulation — but mostly open networking over cocktails with the builders, thought leaders and backers driving the space forward.

Leave the pitch decks at home — this one's about good drinks, sharp minds, and the kind of conversation that actually moves deals and ideas forward.

Date: Thursday, June 4th 

Time: 5:00 PM – 7:00 PM

Location: Manhattan, New York, NY. The venue address will be shared with confirmed attendees only.

Hosted by: Tamar Gubins and Stephen A. Aschettino, Partners at Fox Rothschild LLP and John Delalio and Chris Brodersen from EisnerAmper. 

This is a curated gathering for founders, executives, senior industry leaders, and investors in the digital assets ecosystem. Space is limited and registration is required.

Fox Rothschild is a national law firm that delivers strategic and practical solutions for clients. Our Fintech & Digital Assets attorneys advise clients navigating the rapidly evolving landscape of financial technology, providing guidance on regulatory compliance, transactions, and emerging legal frameworks.

Tamar Gubins is a business-oriented attorney who advises emerging and technology companies on formation, financing, corporate governance, and M&A throughout the full lifecycle of a technology company. 

Stephen Aschettino is the Chair of Fox Rothschild's Fintech & Digital Assets practice, with over 30 years of experience at the intersection of law, finance, and technology. A former Chief Legal Officer of a leading payment processor, he is a frequent speaker and author on payments, blockchain, and cybersecurity issues. 

EisnerAmper is one of the largest business consulting firms in the world, with a dedicated Blockchain and Digital Assets Group providing tax, accounting, assurance, advisory, forensics, and outsourcing services to the bitcoin, blockchain, and Web3 community. Our clients rely on us to help them navigate regulatory requirements and emerging risks in a space at the forefront of innovation. 

John Delalio is a Partner at EisnerAmper and member of the firm's Blockchain & Digital Asset Services Group, with over 25 years of experience modernizing financial operations through cloud-based technologies. He serves on the Board of Directors of NY Tech Alliance, New York's leading tech nonprofit.

Chris Brodersen is a Managing Director at EisnerAmper specializing in blockchain and digital asset solutions, with nearly 40 years of cross-industry experience. Formerly a Blockchain Lab Leader at a Big 4 firm, he now leads the firm's blockchain and digital asset practice.

title: Stakeholder Risk: The Hidden Driver of Value Creation & Loss
description:
50–70% of private equity deals fail to create value. The reason is almost never financial. 

It's Stakeholder Risk .

From regulators and employees to local communities and strategic partners, mismanaging stakeholder dynamics can erode value just as quickly as strong alignment can accelerate it. 

As AI rapidly reshapes industries, a new layer of risk is emerging making stakeholder alignment more critical than ever. 

This session explores how leading firms identify, quantify, and proactively manage stakeholder risk, including AI-related exposures, as a core lever of value creation.

Featuring: 

Jennifer G. Bates, founder & CEO of HumanFactor Enterprises, a stakeholder risk Intelligence platform.
 
Known for her ability to see what others cannot, Jennifer Baytes transforms Stakeholder Risk and decision-making into structured, data-driven frameworks. Her work has contributed to outcomes including valuation increases of up to 800%, $140M+ in M&A readiness and transaction positioning, multi-million-dollar exits, and over $600M in enterprise value creation.

Key discussion points:

Why stakeholder risk is often overlooked in traditional PE due diligence

How misalignment with regulators, employees, or communities can destroy deal value

The rise of AI-related stakeholder risks: bias, transparency, data privacy, and regulatory backlash

Frameworks for identifying and mapping key stakeholders across the investment lifecycle

Real-world case studies: where stakeholder dynamics drove unexpected outcomes

Integrating stakeholder and AI risk into value creation plans and exit strategies

Preparing portfolio companies for AI governance

ORGANIZED BY: 

HumanFactor Enterprises, the world leader in stakeholder risk intelligentce. 

The Vertical, an online publication covering the international venture ecosystem.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: The Messy Middle: From AI Promise to Clinical Reality
description:
We are convening a highly curated group of healthcare leaders, pharma executives, clinical innovation leaders, provider organizations, investors, operators, founders, and ecosystem stakeholders for an honest and practical conversation on the realities of scaling AI from pilot projects to organization-wide implementation across healthcare.

The discussion will focus far less on hype and far more on the operational realities behind adoption — including validation, workflow integration, governance, organizational buy-in, change management, patient impact, and what it actually takes to move beyond experimentation toward meaningful implementation and scale.

The event is designed to surface real-world lessons from organizations actively deploying AI across healthcare environments — including providers, pharma, clinical development, and broader care delivery systems. Together, we will explore what healthcare organizations actually need from AI today, where implementation friction exists, and what separates successful enterprise adoption from pilots that never scale.

The conversation will address practical challenges such as integration into clinical and operational workflows, cross-functional alignment, evidence generation, responsible governance, and scaling AI initiatives across complex healthcare organizations.

We will also feature a select group of companies working directly with healthcare organizations, sharing implementation lessons and insights from real-world deployments.

title: Infinite Impact Lab: From Invisible Work to Undeniable Proof
description:
Sponsored by Havas

Infinite Impact Lab is a hands-on workshop for professionals, operators, and team leaders who are doing meaningful work but struggling to clearly explain the value they create.

This workshop is for you at any stage of your career.

Whether you are getting back into the job market after a layoff, preparing for a promotion, trying to get stronger in your current role, pivoting into a new role or industry, returning after a career break, or getting ready for performance reviews and salary conversations, one thing is true:

Being capable is not enough if your impact is hard to see.

Many professionals are doing the work, solving problems, supporting teams, improving processes, driving outcomes, and creating value every day. But when it is time to talk about that work, they default to tasks:

“I manage projects.”
“I support customers.”
“I run reports.”
“I lead meetings.”
“I handle operations.”

The problem is, tasks do not always tell the full story.

Infinite Impact Lab helps attendees turn everyday execution into clear, measurable business value.

In this session, attendees will use GTM’s framework — Task → Skill → Impact → Proof → Transfer — alongside AI to uncover hidden skills, sharpen outcome language, identify stronger KPIs, and build proof around the work they already do.

Attendees will learn how to:

Translate daily work into strategic skill language
Use AI to strengthen impact statements and surface measurable proof
Connect execution to outcomes leaders, hiring teams, managers, and clients actually recognize
Identify transferable skills for promotions, pivots, interviews, and internal growth
Build an Impact Portfolio they can use for interviews, performance reviews, promotion conversations, salary conversations, career pivots, and leadership opportunities

This is not another “build your personal brand” talk.

It is a practical working session for people who need better language, stronger proof, and a clearer way to explain why their work matters.

Attendees leave with tangible outputs, stronger positioning, and a repeatable system for making their contribution undeniable in an economy where AI is changing how work is evaluated, communicated, and rewarded.
--

title: The New Beauty Counter: A Curated NYC Tech Week Experience
description:
The beauty counter used to be where you went to feel understood.
You sat down, someone looked at you, asked the right questions, and helped you make sense of it all. You left with more than products. You left with clarity.

That experience still matters — but the way people access it has changed.

Today we have more access than ever — and people feel more overwhelmed and less seen than ever.

The New Beauty Counter is a curated experience during NYC Tech Week exploring how that feeling is evolving.
How are people actually discovering and trusting beauty now?
What role does retail still play — and how is it being reshaped by community, creators, and more intentional customer experiences?
And what happens when AI finally gives that experience the infrastructure it’s always deserved?

This is not a traditional panel. It is a room designed to feel like a community, with a real conversation, a live AI experience, and a Signature Glam pop-up counter where you can build your look in real time.

The evening includes the soft launch of Char, T8C’s AI beauty consultant, making its public debut. Guests take the Signature Glam Test at check-in and receive their personalized beauty identity. The room becomes a community of identities, not a group of random attendees.

title: The Case for Open Generative Media: Bria X fal X LTX
description:
​Generative Media wants to be free!

The best generative media models will be open. Open weights mean faster iteration, deeper customization, and real transparency about what's inside. Closed APIs can't offer that — and the market is starting to figure it out.

​Bria, fal, and LTX are building on this bet. Bria ships creative control and rights-clear models designed for commercial production. LTX builds open-source foundational video and image models pushing the frontier on quality and efficiency. fal makes them all run fast at scale. Together, we think this is what the generative media stack looks like going forward — open, fast, and commercially safe.

​Join us during NYC Tech Week for an evening of talks, a panel, and honest conversation about where open generative media is headed.

​🗓 Schedule

​5:00 PM — Doors open + drinks

​6:00 PM — Talks + panel + drinks

​6:45 PM — Networking + drinks

​7:30 PM — Wrap + drinks 

​💬 Speakers

Michael Loiacano Bria.AI — VP of Global Sales

​Blendi Bylygbashi, fal.ai — Senior Creative Engineer 

Noa Sella, LTX — Partnerships

Moderator: Gil Shefler, Bria -- Partnerships

​Perks 🧆 Food 🍺 Beer 🎁 Swag 🚀 Free API credits for attendees

​Spots limited — RSVP to secure yours.

​Bria is the trusted Visual Generative AI platform, purpose-built for commercial ROI. We combine creative control, builder-grade flexibility, and rights-clear models — so every output is on-brand, commercially cleared, and production-ready from day one.

​fal.ai is the fastest inference platform for generative AI, purpose-built for speed, scale, and reliability.

​LTX is an open-source foundational multi-modal model built for precision, control, and efficiency — from production-ready media to content at scale.

title: After Hours: Fashion x Tech Rooftop Mixer
description:
Throughout Tech Week, it’s been nonstop panels, fireside chats, networking events, and workshops — now it’s time to unwind.
​Join 99 YARDS and Breakline Society for After Hours: FashionTech X FinTech Rooftop Mixer, an exclusive indoor rooftop gathering where fashion, technology, finance, and culture collide. Step away from the formal conversations, grab a drink, enjoy the DJ vibes, and connect with an incredible mix of FashionTech founders, FinTech innovators, investors, creatives, models, and industry leaders in a more relaxed setting.

​About 99 YARDS & Breakline Society:
​99 YARDS is an AI-powered fashion ecosystem connecting brands, creatives, agencies, manufacturers, and fashion professionals into one streamlined network. We’re building the future of fashion industry collaboration, talent discovery, service booking, and idea execution — all in one place.

​Breakline Society, a community bringing together forward-thinking founders, operators, creatives, and innovators across emerging industries, has designed this mixer to spark authentic conversations and meaningful connections across both FashionTech and FinTech.

​With confirmed models, startup founders, investors, fashion executives, and creatives attending, this is the perfect opportunity to network organically while enjoying an elevated rooftop atmosphere.

​Whether you're in fashion, tech, finance, investing, or simply curious about where these industries are headed next — come hang out, connect, and enjoy a night of good people, great energy, and unforgettable conversations.

​Hosed by Tony Henry, Founder, & CEO of 99 YARDS

​is a fashion producer and entrepreneur with over 10 years of experience in the fashion industry, producing fashion shows and creative experiences across major platforms, including New York Fashion Week (NYFW) and Miami Swim Week. Throughout his career, he has worked closely with designers, models, production teams, and creative talent to bring fashion concepts to life on the runway.

​

​Lucia Lu, Founder & CEO Of Breakline Society


​Is a Wall Street quant, fashion entrepreneur, and founder of Pink Lady Nail Studio. In 2025, she founded Breakline Society, a curated network connecting Chinese founders, investors, and creatives across AI, FinTech, and fashion. Within its first year, Breakline hosted 10+ private salons and founder roundtables across New York, bringing together hundreds of entrepreneurs, operators, and capital partners.

title: Creators x Marketers Margarita Mixer
description:
For NYC Tech Week, get transported to paradise. We’re bringing together a curated group of tech marketers and knowledge creators into Smooth HQ and transforming our space into…Margaritaville.

To the average human, this theme may seem incredibly random (fair!), but this is inspired by our conference rooms, which are all named after beloved chain restaurants. 

We’ll have a margarita bar, some appetizers, and have “It’s Five O’Clock Somewhere” by Jimmy Buffett on repeat.

Smooth’s own Ben Smith, Senior Creator Manager, will kick things off with a facilitated open forum where everyone gets a seat at the table. Expect real conversation around questions like:
- How are tech companies actually building creator programs right now, and what's working? What do creators want to see from tech brands, that they aren’t currently seeing?
- Knowledge creators have niche, highly trusted audiences. How do they think about understanding their audience and serving their needs?
- How do marketers find the right creator fit and build partnerships that don't feel like ads?
- What have been some of your (creators) favorite campaigns/what made them impactful?
- Creator marketing is bleeding into every function, from PR to paid to events. How are teams structuring this, and who owns it?

Then we open it up. Mingle with the creators, swap notes with the marketers, down some finger foods, and enjoy a margarita or two.

This event is intentionally curated — tech marketers and creators. Come for the conversation, leave with a connection worth way more than the couple hours you spend with us.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Don't Call It That — Naming, Brand & GTM with Eli Altman
description:
Hosted by .tech domains in partnership with A Hundred Monkeys.

Your startup's name is doing more work than your pitch deck — and most founders get it wrong. . Drinks, good people, and a take-home naming kit. Eli Altman, the mind behind Okta, Waymo, and Codex, breaks down the framework.

---
Eli has named some of the most recognizable companies & product in tech — Okta, Codex, and Waymo. His view: the biggest mistake isn't picking something embarrassing, it's picking something safe. And the naming process itself is really just forced clarity on what your company is, who it's for, and how it needs to show up in the market.

At this event he's breaking down what actually makes a name do GTM work: why most founders try to make a name say too much, what real differentiation looks like, and how to build something the market can actually hold onto.

Every attendee leaves with the Go Name Yourself Kit — A Hundred Monkeys' 90-card deck so you can put Eli's process to work the moment you walk out. 

All attendee's can also claim a .tech domain of their choice - on us, valued up to $1M. Companies like Neon (acquired by Databricks for ~$1B), qdrant.tech, and escape.tech have built on .tech from day one. It's a credible home for startups, and the best time to claim your name is before someone else does.

Come for the talk. Stay for the drinks and the room — founders, accelerators, and early-stage VCs all figuring out brand, GTM, and how to build something that actually sticks.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Data + UX in the Reality of AI agents, synthetic users
description:
Data + UX in the New Reality of AI agents and synthetic users
- AI agents as users, and what all of this means for teams, products, and people.
- Data amalgamation and Synthetic users - where we are now and long-term prognosis.

Stellans.io is hosting a panel focusing on the ways UX professionals work together with data engineers and data scientists in an era of large-scale data extraction, data amalgamation, synthetic users, and a new kind of user: AI agents. We explore what those shifts mean for how teams understand behavior, define users, make decisions, and design experiences that still feel trustworthy and useful. Speakers will bring perspectives from data, UX, and applied technology to discuss what is already changing, what remains unresolved, and what deserves much closer scrutiny. The panel will be followed by networking and informal discussions.

​​
About Us

​Nappr is a marketplace for 2 to 12-hour hotel stays and built for people who need rest in the middle of a hectic day.

With 5,300+ properties across 35 countries, you can book same-day stays at brands like Marriott, Hilton and Hyatt for up to 75% off overnight rates with full access to amenities.

We help people recharge on demand and hotels monetize their empty rooms during the day. Check us out today!

Our LinkTree:
https://linktr.ee/nappr
