Flex-N-Fly is a travel wellness company dedicated to supporting the mental and physical well-being of anxious travelers within airports and aviation environments. We bring wellness studios into airports.

​We provide guided stretching, yoga, and relaxation classes designed specifically for travelers, pilots, flight attendants, and flight crews who experience stress and anxiety before, during, and after flight. Our goal is to make mental health a priority in travel while creating a sense of calm and community in spaces built for speed.

​We created The Flex 35, our signature airport-based class offering designed to fit effortlessly into the travel journey. Available in 15 and 30-minute sessions, these classes include stretching, yoga, mobility, tai chi, and other restorative practices that are accessible, inclusive, and thoughtfully crafted for travelers. In addition to our on-site experiences, we offer in-flight wellness programs that help travelers regulate their nervous system and stay grounded while in the air.

title: Doing More With Less (On Purpose): Scaling the AI-Native Startup
description:
Most startups hire their way to scale. We're trying something different. At Cogent Security, every function is being built with AI at the core, not bolted on after the fact. Our designer doesn't touch Figma. Our GTM team runs on custom-built tooling instead of off-the-shelf platforms. Our engineers built their own agent development platform from scratch. Our forward deployed engineers build custom models for each customer's environment. Join us at Cogent NYC for a cross-functional panel on what happens when a well-funded team bets on AI to scale every function with a fraction of the headcount. Hear from GTM, R&D, and FDE on how they're doing the work of teams three times their size, not because they have to, but because they believe this is how companies should be built now. Panel + Q&A, followed by drinks. We're hiring people who want to work this way.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: WORKSHOP: Learn how to pitch press
description:

At this session, you will work through:

About the hosts:

Sutherland Gold is a communications agency that has spent 20 years shaping the narratives of companies like Cloudflare, Circle, Brex, and Superhuman. They specialize in helping venture-backed startups move past the noise to secure top-tier media and own their category.

Founded in 2014 and based in New York, San Francisco, and Toronto, we’re on a mission to make the zero-to-one journey easier, more accessible, and successful for founders. Forum is investing in 65+ companies this year.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Build Smarter Debaters: AI Debating Demo —
description:
AI is everywhere — in your phone, your job, your feed, maybe your love life. But do we actually know how we feel about it?

Come find out at our Tech Week AI Debate — a casual, opinionated night where we hash out the big questions over food, drinks, and a healthy amount of chaos.

How it works
We'll split into two sides and debate a few spicy AI topics — no expertise required, just a take. Rounds are short, arguments are spirited, and you're allowed to switch sides if someone makes a good point. The goal isn't to win. It's to actually think and learn.

Potential topics on the table
• AI is making us less creative — not more
• You should always disclose when you use AI at work
• AI companions will be a net positive for society
• Within 10 years, AI will make college degrees irrelevant
• Should we be polite to ChatGPT?

You'll be judged by Kevin Chen (peaked #40 nationally in competitive debate out of 3,800 teams) and by an AI that doesn't exist yet. We're building it. After the event, it'll listen back through recordings of your arguments and decide who won, and you’ll receive a detailed report of what went well or wrong.

Food and drinks provided. Come hungry, come curious, come ready to be wrong.

title: The New CS Skill Set: Build the System, Become the Strategist
description:
Join Women of Customer Success and Notion for one of NYC Tech Week's must-attend evenings for women working in post-sales.

The CS role is evolving fast. New titles like "Customer Success Engineer" and "Forward Deployed Engineer" signal a shift toward more technically fluent, strategic professionals who operate closer to the product and the business. AI is accelerating that shift - and the teams pulling ahead aren't just using it, they've built a system around it.

This event tackles both sides of what that looks like in practice.

First, an "Under the Hood" session: 3 CS leaders will demo how they are using AI to build their own Chiefs of Staff - the workflows and tools that handle the cognitive overhead of the role. 

Meet your "show & tell" presenters: 

- Jamie Wernet, Director Enterprise Success & Services, Greenhouse
- Melina Giakas, GTM Enablement, Clay 
- Denise King, CSM, and Nida Noorani, AI Solutions, Notion 

Then we'll move to a fireside chat with CS leaders from across the industry on the skills that matter most once AI handles the routine: consulting instincts, technical fluency, and the strategic thinking that separates CSMs who manage accounts from those who drive outcomes. 

Meet your panel: 

- Christina Parra, Head of CS, Notion
- Sam Medina, Snr Director Customer Success & Growth, Bitly 
- Sarah Benussi, CS Leader, Anthropic
- Cassie Vaughn, Global Head of Growth Strategy, Clay.

75 seats. Notion's NYC office. Drinks, food, and a room full of CS professionals serious about what comes next.

Please note this event is for women only.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Frontiers in Computing: Quantum, Chips, Semiconductors
description:
Spots limited!

The global semiconductor market is projected to surpass $1 trillion by 2030, driven by surging demand from AI, cloud computing, and next-generation technologies. At the same time, breakthroughs in quantum computing and novel architectures are pushing the limits of what computation can achieve.

This panel brings together founders, researchers, and investors at the frontier of computing—from chips and semiconductor infrastructure to quantum systems and emerging architectures. We’ll explore how the foundations of computation are being rebuilt—and what it will take to power the next era of technological progress.

H*QUOTIENT is a new-age deep tech community with 10,000+ engineers, scientists, founders, investors, and students across 55+ countries. We aim to empower the next generation of founders in IP-intensive industries, connect over niche STEM-adjacent events, and make deep tech sexy again.

Join our community here: https://tally.so/r/nWjGMe and let us know how we can help you!

description:

AI vs. Human Debate Night is an interactive experience that explores the evolving relationship between artificial intelligence and human expertise. Participants will engage in a live debate on timely topics such as the future of work, cybersecurity, and innovation. One side will rely on AI use in professional settings, while the other opposes its use in cybersecurity. title: Shape What's Next with Justworks - A Networking Happy Hour!
description:
Justworks is bringing together some of NYC's most thoughtful managers in Tech for an evening of meaningful conversation at London & Martin in the Financial District.

Our panel will explore how high-growth companies can flatten hierarchy, unlock accountability, and build cultures where engineering, data, design and product builders think and act like founders!

Happy Hour will immediately follow - 

This is an opportunity to connect directly with Justworks leaders — the people who shape how we build, how we ship, and how we think about the future of our product. Expect real talk about tech culture, product philosophy, AI and what it means to do your best work.

We're actively hiring! Justworks is looking for our next great technologists across engineering, product, data and design— and we expect to meet them at events like this one. If you're open to exploring what's possible, come ready for great conversations with leaders who are ready to hire!

title: Health2Tech
description:
On the Digital Health Inside Out YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@DigitalHealthInsideOut), there are monthly series called Digital Health Vitals — where experts talk through the latest digital health news, grade recent fundraises and public company earnings, and share an honest take on what’s working and what’s not.
For this event, we’re bringing that same format to Health2Tech — live.
We’ll be recording an episode on stage with the lineup of experts, reacting to breaking news, debating a bit, playing a couple of games, and opening it up for Q&A with the audience.
If you want to hear real perspectives and get a feel for what’s happening in healthtech right now, this is the must-attend event. 

​🕕 Event Schedule:

​5:30 PM - 6:00 Doors Open. Begin the evening with a warm welcome and a chance to settle in.

​6:00 - 6:45 Digital Health Vitals, Live Recording!

6:45 - 8:30 Networking. Connect, converse, and collaborate with peers driven by a common passion for advancing healthcare through technology.

🤩 About our Guest Speaker:

​​Host/Moderator: ​​  

Alex Koshykov (https://www.linkedin.com/in/alex-koshykov/) is a co-founder of BeKey (https://bekey.io/) and a digital health expert who constantly shares news, introduces promising technologies, and promotes secure software & hardware for the healthcare industry on his YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/c/BeKey-Hub). Additionally, he is a co-founder of YODD (https://igg.me/at/yodd), a medical device startup.  

Experts:  

Sergei Polevikov (https://www.linkedin.com/in/sergeiai/) has decades of deep experience in math, statistics, and AI, with four graduate degrees spanning mathematics, economics, finance, and machine learning. He has authored over 30 peer-reviewed publications and manuscripts. His work is driven by a relentless pursuit of brutal truth in healthcare. That mission has led him to become not just a founder of successful healthcare AI and digital health startups, but also a healthcare fraud investigator. Today, Sergei is focused on health AI education and truth-telling. He runs the podcast “Digital Health Inside Out” (https://www.youtube.com/@DigitalHealthInsideOut) (with his co-host Alex Koshykov) and the Substack “AI Health Uncut“ (http://sergeiai.substack.com/), which has grown rapidly to over 8,000 subscribers in just a few short months.  

Stephanie Davis (https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephjdavis/) is a Managing Director and senior research analyst covering Healthcare Technology and Distribution. She joined Barclays in 2023 following over a decade of experience covering the Healthcare Technology and IT Services sectors. Prior to joining Barclays, Ms. Davis led coverage of the Healthcare Technology sector at SVB Securities, Citibank, and J.P. Morgan. Prior to Healthcare Technology, Ms. Davis was an Equity Research Analyst at J.P. Morgan on the #1 ranked Payments, Processors, and IT Services team. Ms. Davis ranked #3 in the Institutional Investor 2023 All America Research Team for Healthcare Technology and Distribution, and was recognized as a Runner-Up in both 2019 and 2022. She was included in Business Insider’s “Rising Stars of Equity Research” in 2017. Ms. Davis earned a B.A. in Economics and Mathematics from Columbia University and is a CFA charterholder. She is a member of the Board of Trustees for The New York Foundling.

​Guy Milhalter (https://www.linkedin.com/in/guymilhalter/)  is a business-minded technology transactions attorney who advises technology companies, startups, and health IT companies on complex, strategic transactions. He regularly counsels clients on legal risk, governance, and the commercial deployment of AI technologies. ​Guy frequently supports legal and business teams in fast-paced environments where deal execution, regulatory compliance, and business strategy intersect.

🌟 Why Attend?

​Meet like-minded individuals who are actively shaping the health tech landscape.

​Share experiences, discuss challenges, and explore potential solutions with fellow founders.

​Learn from the journeys of other entrepreneurs, discover something new, seek advice, and perhaps find the mentor or partner you've been looking for.

​🤝 Who Should Attend?

​Digital Health Founders at various stages of their startup journey.

​Healthcare Professionals who are passionate about health innovation.

​Investors looking to make impactful contributions to the digital health and medtech spaces.

​🚀 Why This Event Is A Must?

​Hear from thought leaders and innovators in the digital health domain (at every event, we'll have a new set of speakers).

​Connect with peers, potential collaborators, and mentors.

​Participate in engaging Q&As and discussions that can spark new ideas and opportunities.

​🏙️ Venue Details:

​Great location in the heart of New York, equipped to foster conversations and connections. Venue details will be shared with registered attendees. Because of limited capacity, we won't be able to accommodate everyone, so please register whenever you are sure you can attend.

​Don’t miss this chance to be part of a community that’s driving the future of health tech. Reserve your spot now and join us in shaping the landscape of digital health for 2026 and beyond.

Partners

​​Pearl Cohen (https://www.pearlcohen.com) is an international law firm with offices in New York, Boston, San Francisco, Tel Aviv, London, and Munich. Representing innovation-driven companies, inventors, and their investors, we serve clients worldwide, from Fortune 500 companies to leading academic institutions and promising start-ups. With professionals coming from a wide variety of academic and professional backgrounds, our team is your partner in the business of innovation.

About the organizer

​Alex Koshykov is a co-founder of BeKey and a digital health expert who constantly shares news, introduces promising technologies, and promotes secure software & hardware for the healthcare industry on his YouTube channel. Additionally to that, he is a co-founder of YODD, a medical device startup.

Bailey Morgan, Based in Astoria, NYC, is a versatile healthtech leader who merges clinical expertise with business acumen to accelerate healthcare innovation and community impact. Having started her career as a medical speech language pathologist working within inpatient facilities throughout the boroughs of NYC, she saw firsthand how behind the healthcare tech ecosystem was compared to industries such as tech or finance.

After leaving healthcare to move into the healthtech startup space, Bailey has since raised over $10M in capital, led several successful sales and marketing teams through go-to-market stages in both SaaS and CPG, and has closed over $2M in technology sales herself. She currently serves as Chief of Staff at Groth Pain & Spine, a pain management practice with 10+ locations and 40+ providers, where she leads the change management and technology integrations for the practices acquisitions.

In addition to consulting with various healthcare technology companies, Bailey is a Director and the Treasurer for an NYC-based dementia-focused nonprofit that employs individuals living with dementia and supports families and caregivers.

Additional Information

By signing up, your email will be automatically added to the Health2Tech newsletter with news and upcoming events — but don’t worry, we won’t flood your inbox! ;) 

Also, just a note that this event might be photographed or filmed. If we do capture some moments, Health2Tech may use the photos or videos for marketing and communication purposes. By registering, you're giving us the thumbs up for using your image for these reasons.

title: NYC TechWeek x CHIEF: Building AI for an Intentional Life
description:
A Female Tech Founder’s Story 

Join us for an inspiring evening during NYC Tech Week as we explore a question many of us are quietly asking:
•What if AI could help us live more intentionally — not just work more efficiently?
This event is part founder story, part product demo, and part conversation about the future of human-centered AI.

You’ll hear the journey behind Banjo, an AI-powered platform designed to help people align their time, energy, and daily choices with the life they actually want to live. While much of the AI world is focused on productivity and automation, Banjo was built around a different idea:
Can technology help people feel more fulfilled, connected, and intentional in everyday life?

The evening will include:
•The personal story behind building Banjo
•Lessons learned from building an AI startup 
• A live product demo of Banjo
• Reflections on AI, longevity, and intentional living
• Networking with founders, creatives, technologists, and curious minds across NYC Tech Week

Event Schedule
5:30 PM – 6:30 PM
✨ Arrival, networking, lite bites & conversations
Meet fellow founders, technologists, creatives, and members of the NYC Tech Week community in a relaxed and welcoming environment.
6:30 PM – 7:00 PM
🎤 Behind Banjo: The Founder’s Story
Hear the personal story behind Banjo, the inspiration for building the platform, and the journey of starting an AI company focused on intentional living.
7:00 PM – 7:30 PM
🚀 The AI Journey
A conversation about building with AI, lessons learned along the way, and reflections on creating technology that supports fulfillment, connection, and human well-being.
7:30 PM – 8:00 PM
📱 Live Product Demo
Experience Banjo in action through a live walkthrough of the platform and see how AI can help people better align their daily lives with what matters most to them.

We hope this evening leaves you inspired not only about technology — but about the possibility of designing a more intentional future for ourselves and the people around us.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Vertical AI in Accounting with CPA.com, Adaptive, and Tabs
description:
Join us for an evening of drinks, networking, and a conversation the accounting profession is overdue for.

General-purpose AI is impressive. It can summarize, draft, and reason across an enormous range of tasks. But accounting and tax aren't general problems. They're governed by rules that change, edge cases that carry liability, and judgment calls that no model gets right just by being large. That gap, between what AI can do and what accounting actually demands, is where the most consequential work in the profession is happening right now.

This panel brings together thought leaders, firm leaders from the profession, and the builders working at that frontier to share what's working in practice, where the hype still outpaces reality, and what separates AI that holds up in an accounting environment from AI that doesn't.

If you're leading a firm or advising one, this is a conversation worth being in the room for.

Joining us for the panel:

Seema Amble, Partner at a16z (moderator)
Richard Kopelman, CEO of Aprio
Erik Asgeirsson, President & CEO of CPA.com
Matt Calvano, CEO & Co-Founder of Adaptive
Ali Hussain, CEO & Co-Founder of Tabs

Hosted at CPA.com headquarters, with views overlooking Central Park.

Schedule:
5:45 — Arrival
6:15 - 7:30 PM — Panel + live Q&A
7:30–8:30 PM — Drinks & bites

title: Women’s Physiology in AI, Performance & Longevity
description:
The Hidden Variable: Women’s Physiology in AI, Performance & Longevity

For decades, health research, fitness programming, and even AI systems have been built on male baselines.

As personalized health, wearables, and data-driven optimization become mainstream, one critical variable remains under-modeled: women’s physiology.

This panel brings together founders, researchers, operators, and investors to explore:

• How hormonal fluctuation impacts performance, cognition, and recovery
• Why women are often misrepresented in health and training data
• The implications for AI, product design, and workplace performance
• Where the next wave of opportunity in women’s health innovation lives

Expect a sharp, science-forward conversation — not a wellness panel.

45-minute moderated discussion
15-minute audience Q&A
Curated networking to follow

Open to founders, engineers, clinicians, investors, and operators interested in the future of personalized health.

Space is limited.

description:

This will be a highly curated group of female founders, operators, and VCs, and there will be flowing 🥂, 🍣, and conversation starters. ​About J.P. Morgan
​We are committed to being the leading bank of the innovation economy — bringing together founders, investors, startups, and high-growth companies. With decades of global experience, a robust professional and venture capital network, and scalable money-management solutions, we're the only bank you'll ever need.

​is a global venture capital firm focused on helping entrepreneurs build transformational businesses across multiple stages, sectors and geographies. About the Hosts:
​​Reitler is a top-ranked venture capital law firm with offices in New York, Los Angeles, and Princeton. The firm represents emerging companies, venture investors and limited partners across all stages of investment, in seed financings, growth rounds, mergers and acquisitions and exit transactions. title: Dinner & Insights: FoodTech Innovation Shaping healthier communities
description:
This private dinner experience brings together high-caliber founders, investors, strategic partners, and professionals shaping the future with food technology innovations, artificial intelligence, consumer innovation, and social impact initiatives.

The goal is simple: create the kind of environment where meaningful relationships, strategic conversations, investment opportunities, and long-term collaborations naturally emerge. This is because the best conversations during Tech Week rarely happen on stage.
They happen in the right room, with the right people, over dinner.

A Curated Room of Builders & Decision-Makers

This event is intentionally curated to ensure strong representation across: 
- Venture-backed and emerging founders
- Angel investors and venture capital professionals
- Corporate innovation and strategy leaders
- Professionals interested in the future of innovation and impact

Whether you are actively deploying capital, building transformative technology, exploring partnerships, or looking to connect with exceptional people working on meaningful problems, this evening is designed for high-quality engagement.

Featured Dinner Conversation
Non-Dilutive Funding Strategies for High-Impact Companies
As dinner is served, attendees will join an expert-led discussion exploring one of the most underleveraged growth opportunities available to innovative companies today:
How mission-driven startups are accessing grants, research funding, government-backed programs, strategic partnerships, and other non-dilutive capital opportunities to accelerate growth without immediately sacrificing equity.

While the conversation will include examples from FoodTech, HealthTech, sustainability, and community impact innovation, the insights are highly relevant to founders and innovators across sectors. 

Why Attend?

- Meet Exceptional People
- Connect with founders, investors, and industry leaders in a setting designed for genuine interaction—not transactional networking.


The evening begins with attendee introductions, allowing guests to briefly share what they are building, investing in, or exploring.

Expand Your Strategic Network
Meet people outside your immediate circle who may become future collaborators, investors, customers, advisors, or partners.

End NYC Tech Week the Right Way
A more relaxed, elevated environment to decompress, exchange ideas, and have conversations that are difficult to create in large conference settings.

Tentative Agenda 
Time                                                      Experience
6:00 PM:      Arrival, welcome reception & curated networking
6:15 PM:      Founder, investor & professional introductions
6:35 PM:     Dinner service & featured conversation: Non-dilutive capital strategies for impact-driven innovation
7:10 PM:      Open discussion, audience insights & strategic conversations
7:25 PM:      Closing remarks


The next generation of transformative companies will not emerge from isolated conversations.
They will emerge from connected ecosystems—where innovators, capital, expertise, and opportunity intersect.
This evening was created to bring those people into the same room.

Complete this form before May 30th, 2026 to receive your invite: https://forms.gle/mnHZkMkfjJAvPU3N8


Join us for dinner, networking, and insightful conversations. This event is part of NY Tech Week — a week of events hosted by venture capital firms, startups, and technology leaders designed to bring together the broader innovation ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Agents at Scale
description:
Demo agents are easy. Production agents are a different problem.

Join Modal, Braintrust, Deskree and Tavily for an evening of honest conversation about what it actually takes to ship AI agents that work reliably, at scale, and in real enterprise environments. No keynotes, no product pitches. Just practitioners from leading developer tools companies talking through the problems they've actually solved.

The centerpiece is a panel featuring engineers and builders from across the agent stack. They'll dig into the parts nobody talks about in blog posts: context management, orchestration failures, eval frameworks that actually work, observability at scale, and what "done" looks like when an agent is customer-facing.

This event is for:
Engineers building or maintaining agent-based systems
Technical leads evaluating agent infrastructure
Technical founders navigating the same production challenges

title: PMAI: AI GTM Workshop
description:
Scaling Intelligence: The AI-Native GTM Workshop
Moving Beyond "Spray and Pray": Paid Acquisition for Seed to Series A Founders.

​The traditional GTM playbook needs updating. In 2026, winning the attention of enterprise buyers and consumers requires more than a standard ad account. An autonomous growth engine perhaps?

​This workshop is designed for the founders or growth leads who views marketing as a data-driven problem. Join us for an intensive, in-person GTM deep dive specifically for AI startups. We’re getting into the weeds of how to leverage AI-powered paid acquisition to scale from initial traction to Series A.

​What we’re covering:

​The AI-Native Stack: Practical setup for automated creative testing and real-time bid optimization.

​Measurement that Matters: Moving from vanity metrics to "Cost Per Inference" and predictive LTV.

​The Trapdoor: Common pitfalls that bleed Seed-stage budgets (and how to avoid them).

​Live Audit: Bring your current CAC data, we’ll break down 2-3 accounts live in the room.

​Who Should Attend

​Seed to Series A Founders: Who are ready to move from founder-led sales to a scalable, automated engine.

​Growth Leads & Operators: Those responsible for hitting CAC targets and scaling performance budgets.

​Technical Product Managers: Who want to understand how growth loops should be integrated directly into the AI product architecture.

​VC Platform Leads: Looking for tactical playbooks to bring back to their portfolio companies.

About LiberNovo:
LiberNovo is the pioneer of the world’s first dynamic ergonomic chair. We empower modern professionals and enterprises to work more efficiently by delivering high-performance ergonomic office chairs that optimize sitting posture, ease prolonged sitting fatigue and unlock continuous creative productivity.

About Otis AI:
Otis AI is an AI media buyer for growing businesses. The platform helps founders and lean teams launch, manage, and optimize paid acquisition across channels like Meta, Google, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTube. Otis turns business goals and campaign data into media strategy, creative direction, budget allocation, and always-on optimization, helping teams scale paid growth without hiring a full media buying team or agency.
​
About Pioneering Minds AI:
At pioneeringminds.ai, we grow AI native pioneers who build, deploy, and govern AI responsibly and put humans at the front seat. We help YOU - our AI pioneers - to truly understand AI and create real value out of AI.

Our mission is straightforward:

​​​Accelerate Innovation: Equip participants with cutting-edge AI tools to deliver rapid prototypes.

​​​Foster Collaboration: Unite diverse teams—students, engineers, and civic leaders—to tackle urban challenges head-on.

​​​Create Lasting Impact: Offer continued mentorship and post-event partnerships so promising concepts can scale beyond the weekend.

​​This event is part of #NYTechWeek — Techweek creates and curates engaging media, dynamic events and disruptive content on web, digital and interactive technology, bringing together diverse communities of entrepreneurs, corporate professionals and thought leaders. The ultimate goal of Techweek is to spotlight and enable innovation ecosystems.

title: AEO + PR = Your Winning Comms Strategy
description:
Search has fundamentally changed, and if your communications strategy has not changed with it, your brand is already losing ground.

In this joint session, Tiffany Guarnaccia (Kite Hill) and Cara Corbett (SUSO Digital) bring together the worlds of earned media and Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) to show NYC’s agency and tech marketing community what a winning, future-proof communications strategy looks like in 2026 and beyond.

AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google’s AI Overviews now answer questions directly, often without a click. The brands that show up in those answers are not there by chance. They have built the right mix of owned content, technical visibility, and earned credibility.

This session will break down how modern search works, why traditional PR and SEO can no longer operate in silos, and what brands need to do now to improve their visibility in AI-generated answers.

Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of how to align PR, content, and search strategy to strengthen discoverability, credibility, and brand presence in the new era of search.

This session is designed for senior PR, communications, and marketing leaders at brands and agencies. Space is limited and registrations will be reviewed to ensure a relevant peer group.

Time of Events
6-8 p.m. ET
6 p.m. networking  
6:30 p.m. - 7:30 p.m. keynote session, followed by an audience Q&A

Elevator and Accessibility - Please be advised that the event space is on the 2nd floor and does not have an elevator. Guests must utilize stairs to reach the event floor. If you require mobility assistance, please get in touch with ops@kitehillpr.com so that we can coordinate alternative entry, if available.

description:
Join us for 'The New Arena: How Sports Are Driving Culture, Commerce, and Connection' for an engaging panel of industry leaders discussing the way sports & media, commerce, and modern consumer culture are deeply intertwined.

The creator economy and commerce run on cultural currency — and sports and athletes are minting it at a record pace. Caitlin Clark’s rookie season shattered WNBA viewership records. Angel Reese became a brand empire before her first professional dunk. Simone Biles’ return to competition generated more social engagement than the Super Bowl halftime show. From NIL deals reshaping college sports to athletes who've built media companies to the creator-versus-athlete moment that's defining a new entertainment category, this session goes beyond the highlights reel. 

Expect a direct, unscripted conversation about how sports are shaping cultural moments that are driving commerce and connection.

description:

This NY Tech Week panel unpacks what it means when AI can finally see and understand video the way humans do, and what that transformation means for publishers, broadcasters, creators, brands, and the technologies connecting them.

You’ll hear from leaders across media and AI on where the real progress is happening, how new measurement and monetization models are emerging, and what a fully “enlightened” video ecosystem might look like over the next three years.

title: GTM in the technical era
description:
A panel on what it actually takes to sell to engineers, technical founders, and product-led teams.

Hosted by Exa and Lightfield during NY Tech Week, we’re bringing together founding GTM leaders to share how they’re navigating a shift where the buyer is increasingly technical, skeptical, and self-serve by default.

Technical buyers don’t respond to traditional sales. They read docs, try the product, and form opinions before ever talking to you. The old playbook breaks here.

We’ll dig into:
- How to sell when your buyer is an engineer or technical founder
- What “GTM” looks like in product-led and AI-native companies
- Where traditional sales still works — and where it doesn’t
- How founding GTM teams are adapting in real time

If you’re building or selling to technical users, this is the shift you need to understand. Come for the conversation, stay for the people — founders, operators, and builders navigating this shift in real time.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

About Lightfield: ​​​​Lightfield is an AI-native CRM that remembers everything, then acts on it. It automatically captures and organizes every customer conversation across email, meetings, and notes, creating your team’s collective customer memory. Backed by Greylock, Lightspeed, and Coatue, Lightfield helps companies learn from every interaction and move faster toward repeatable growth.

About Exa: Exa is the search engine for AI. It finds the most relevant content on the web using neural search, powering the retrieval layer behind AI agents, research tools, and intelligent applications. Companies use Exa to keep their AI products grounded in real-time information - from competitive intelligence and lead research to news monitoring and deep web retrieval. Backed by Benchmark, Lightspeed, YCombinator, and NVIDIA.

title: Roundtrip Capital
host: IncentivesPRO, ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals for America)
hosts: ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals for America)
description:
Hosted by IncentivesPRO, ALPFA Puerto Rico, and the National Puerto Rican Chamber of Commerce.

Capital doesn't move in one direction. The strongest economies, the ones that actually compound, run on a roundtrip: human capital, financial capital, and network capital flowing back and forth across borders, generations, and balance sheets until each one strengthens the next.

Puerto Rico has been running half that loop for a generation. People leave. Money comes in transactionally. The networks stay siloed. The roundtrip never closes. This event is about the founders, operators, and early employees who are closing it.

They came of age inside the turbulence: the 2008 collapse, Hurricane María, Verano del '19, the pandemic. And they chose to build anyway. Some stayed on the island and built from San Juan, Ponce, and Mayagüez. Others left, learned the playbook in New York and beyond, and are now routing capital, talent, and deal flow back home. Raised in crisis, raising rounds, employing teams, and assembling the infrastructure the rest of the capital stack hasn't caught up to.

These are Puerto Rican founders who have raised capital and built across the sectors that will define the next decade of the island's economy: regulation technology, fintech, AI, HR, energy, and healthcare. Alongside them, the diaspora investors, operators, and allies already moving capital across the corridor. The conversation unfolds across two panels.

Between Two Shores: San Juan & New York

Builders who operate across both cities, pivoting careers, crossing industries, and moving capital, talent, and ideas in both directions. The space between two shores is where the opportunity lives.

Carmelo Cintrón, Owner of Grupo Marullo and Head of Capital Formation at Sygnus Capital Puerto Rico. Pedro Cruz, Co-Founder of MeshMap Labs. Gabriel Ginorio, Senior Growth Manager at Rippling. William Rodríguez, Founder and CEO of an energy and AI startup. Moderated by Laura Camila Rivera, Founder and CEO of CAMI Studio and President of ALPFA Puerto Rico.

Made in Puerto Rico: The Island Advantage

Founders raised on the island who turned it into their unfair advantage: the network you can't buy, the cultural fluency you can't fake, and a home-field edge that compounds over time. Homegrown was never the ceiling. It was the moat.

Gustavo Díaz Skoff, CEO and Co-Founder of IncentivesPRO. Rodrigo Lloveras, Co-Founder and CEO of Hospitri. Jonathan Days, Founder and CEO of Puny.bz. Juan Suárez, Co-Founder and CTO of Equiply. Moderated by Ashley Rivera Mercado, Founder and President of Mujeres in Marketing.

This is a call to action. The producers are already here. The capital is already moving. The networks are already forming. What's missing is the room where we agree to close the loop on purpose.

We close with a networking reception. Salsa, plena, merengue. Because we do business, and we dance.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Aurelian Labs: Prototypes & Pours @ NY Tech Week
description:
You can go from idea to demo software in seconds: you can describe a product, generate code, spin up agents, automate tasks, and create the illusion of instant execution; but your workflow fragments as soon as you pass demo and try to go live.

You're suddenly managing coding agents, orchestration layers, terminals, cloud consoles, API keys, Git workflows, deployment scripts, security questions, and broken handoffs between tools that were never designed to operate as one system.

Come to hear about bridging the gap between AI-generated software and production-ready execution.

We'll explore why the next major opportunity in developer tooling is not just another coding agent, model wrapper, or chat-based IDE forked from VS Code.

See how to use a unified environment where AI-native software is planned, built, validated, shipped, and governed. 

Ask → Explore → Edit → Validate → Install → Push → Deploy

We'll cover:

* Why AI has compressed idea-to-code, but not idea-to-production
* Why the current developer stack is becoming more powerful but more fragmented
* Why agents need a control layer, not just more autonomy
* How to unify coding, validation, Git, install, deployment, and local/cloud AI workflows
* Why native performance, privacy, and workflow completion matter in the next IDE category

The session will close with a brief introduction to Aurelian Labs, the NYC based venture studio and first backer behind LingCode: a native AI development environment; and its broader thesis on building infrastructure companies for the AI-native economy.

This is for you if you're a founder, developer, investor, engineer, operator, or builder trying to understand what comes after AI code gen or who wants to stop using 50+ tools to make one software.

Because generating software in 30 seconds is impressive, but shipping it reliably is the only unlock that matters.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Still Human: AI x EQ
description:

Everyone’s building AI. Very few are building for humans.

As products get more powerful, they’re also getting flatter-optimized for output, not experience.

title: Music Ate AI’s Homework: Why Music Tech Is Writing the Rules for Everyone Else Description Every Industry
The Business of Christian Technology
description:
A night of conversation, founder pitches, networking, and thoughtful discussion exploring the intersection of faith, technology, AI, startups, and human flourishing in New York City.What does it look like to build technology that genuinely serves people well?

Faith-driven technology is no longer sitting on the sidelines. From prayer apps and AI tools to church infrastructure, Bible engagement products, donor platforms, and community-building software, Christian technology is becoming a real and growing part of the broader tech ecosystem.

Companies like Hallow and Gloo are demonstrating both meaningful demand and real opportunity in the space, while advances in AI are opening new possibilities for discipleship, ministry workflows, education, translation, and community-building.

But this night isn’t just about markets, trends, or business models.

It’s about asking deeper questions around ethics, formation, creativity, stewardship, and what Christians should build differently in a rapidly changing technological world.

The evening will include:

🎤 A live panel with founders, investors, and ministry leaders

⚡ Lightning pitches from builders creating in the faith-tech space

🤝 Networking with founders, engineers, creatives, operators, investors, and others from across NYC tech

🍸 Drinks, conversation, and community

Community + Collaboration

Christians*inTech exists to create welcoming spaces where believers and seekers can connect around faith, vocation, creativity, and technology in New York City.

Our hope for this gathering is simple:

To bring together thoughtful people building in technology and create space for conversations around faith, innovation, ambition, and human flourishing.

title: How to Get Paid by OpenAI: An Expert's Guide to AI Training Data
description:
Tech Week drinks for experts who want in on the AI training data gold rush, primary research market, and the future of expert work.

Frontier labs are quietly paying domain experts $100-300/hr to train their models. Mercor, Surge, and Scale have built billion-dollar businesses on this. At the same time, consulting firms, hedge funds, and PE shops pay experts $300-1,500/hr through networks like GLG, AlphaSights, and Guidepoint for primary research calls. Most experts have no idea how to break into either market, or which platforms are worth their time.

Come for a drink, meet other experts, and trade notes.

Topics of conversation will be around:
- How the AI training data and expert network markets actually work
- Who's paying, what they're paying for, and how to get on their lists
- What separates a $50/hr task from a $300/hr engagement (and a $1,500/hr expert call)
- The platforms worth joining vs. the ones wasting your time
- How to position your expertise to maximize rate and deal flow across both markets

title: Financial Services in the age of AI - FinTech Panel and Networking
description:
Cabaka is hosting an official NY Tech Week panel discussion on June 4 at 6:00 PM at Savills’ New York headquarters, focused on the convergence of blockchain, stablecoins, digital-dollar banking, and AI in financial services.

The panel will bring together leaders from Glean, N3XT Bank, Nightfall Network, and NYU to discuss how blockchain and AI are beginning to reshape financial infrastructure, payments, banking, and enterprise operations. The conversation will explore topics including regulated digital-dollar banking, stablecoin use cases, programmable payments, enterprise AI adoption, AI-enabled knowledge management, and how financial institutions can prepare for a more real-time, automated, and data-driven future.

Confirmed panelists: 

> Jeffrey Wallis, CEO of N3XT Bank
>Paul Brody, CEO of Nightfall Network, former EY Global Blockchain Leader, and Consensus Miami speaker
>Glean, who will discuss the enterprise AI knowledge management.
>Frederic Siboulet, NYU professor of financial blockchain and former EY machine learning leader

The purpose of the event is to create an executive-level forum for discussing the future of financial services at the intersection of blockchain and AI, while giving participating partners direct visibility with a relevant audience of financial services and technology decision-makers.

Think:
• reconciliations that never quite reconcile
• onboarding workflows across multiple portals
• reports that consume half your Monday
• compliance processes buried in PDFs and spreadsheets

We’re opening up the beta for Zomma Agent for this event.

title: AI Infra Night
description:
AI compute has become one of the most valuable and contested resources in the economy. Understanding what goes into a GPU hour — the chips, power, land, capital structure, and operational expertise — is essential to understanding where AI goes next. But understanding the stack is only half the story. The other half is how that capacity actually gets priced, contracted, and delivered to the teams building on top of it.

Join us for an evening with cloud operators, investors, infrastructure leaders, and the marketplace and procurement layer that connects them. The conversation will cover today's deployment bottlenecks, how the financing and capital stack shape what actually gets built, and how the ecosystem can evolve to unlock compute for a broader range of offtakers — from frontier labs to enterprises, startups, and researchers.

Co-hosted by TowersTeam, EverGrid.ai, and Compute Exchange — bringing together the operators, the capital, and the procurement marketplace behind today's GPU economy.

This is an intimate, invite-only gathering kept small to keep the conversation real. Please RSVP — admission is subject to host approval.

Thank you Wix, we're thrilled to host this event at their stunning New York headquarters!

title: IRL SOCIAL - founders and investor meet-up
description:
⚡ IRL or Nothing: A Meetup for People Building Human Connection in the Age of AI - in partnership with organisers @Resortpass and @WomenintechorgUK

NYC Tech Week · Founders · Investors · Builders

We’re in a strange moment. AI is everywhere. Screens are smarter. And somehow, people have never felt more alone.

If you’re building apps that get people off their phones and into the same room, that use technology as a bridge not a barrier, that believe proximity, serendipity, and real human chemistry still matter — this is your people.

We’re bringing together IRL social founders, investors, and innovators for an evening of honest conversation, big ideas, and the kind of connections you can’t make in a DM.

Expect a breakout session, expect good chat, expect to meet someone who’s grappling with the exact same thing you are.

What you’ll discuss:

• What’s actually broken about social media — and what IRL apps can fix

• Building for human connection in a world that keeps optimising for engagement

• The investor lens: where does this space go from here?

• Living the mission: how do you build community when community itself is the product?

This isn’t a pitch night. This isn’t a networking event where you collect business cards and forget everyone’s names. It’s a room full of people who genuinely believe the next great shift in tech isn’t about making AI smarter — it’s about making humans closer.

Come ready to talk. Come ready to listen. Come ready to leave with at least one person you actually want to stay in touch with.

Capacity is intentionally small. RSVP if this is your thing. 🧡

* Part of NYC Tech Week

description:

Join M13 for an intimate, off-the-record evening exploring how founders, investors, and technical leaders navigate building ambitious careers with ADHD. title: How We AI? NYC Edition
description:
How We AI: NYC, Inaugural Edition · #NYTechWeek

We're in the middle of a Cambrian explosion. Not the one from half a billion years ago - the one happening right now, in every team trying to figure out what AI actually means for the way they work.

The creatures that survived the first one weren't the biggest or the fastest. They were the most adaptive. The octopus is still here because two-thirds of its neurons live in its arms, not its brain. Each arm senses, decides, acts. That's the model - not just for how AI should work inside a company, but for how companies themselves need to evolve.

How We AI: NYC is a room of forty people figuring this out in real time. Operators, founders, marketers, and business leaders - some inside large enterprises, some building startups, some growing emerging brands - all already in the work, all with skin in the game.

The evening is built around candid conversation: a panel with people who've rewired how their teams operate, live demos of the workflows actually running, and time to compare notes with the others in the room.

Intelligence is human.

This is the first gathering of the people building what that looks like.

Forty seats. The founding cohort.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Rippling x Superhuman Mail x The Varo: Founders at Every Stage
description:

title: Pydantic at NY Tech Week
STRATEGY AT THE SPEED OF INFERENCE: What Happens When AI Analysis Is Free
description:
Strategic advantage has long rested on the quality of analysis: who could synthesize the most information, find the sharpest insight, and turn it into a defensible plan. AI has collapsed the cost and time of that work. Market intelligence that took six weeks now takes an afternoon, and is often stale by the time leadership convenes to discuss it.

This is not a productivity story. It is a structural one. When analysis is cheap, the activities that defined the strategist's role for fifty years (data gathering, framework application, deck construction) lose their economic value. What remains is judgment, narrative, political navigation, and the ability to commit an organization to action under uncertainty. Those skills are scarcer, harder to teach, and largely absent from how strategy talent is currently developed.

Join us on June 4 as this panel examines what that means for the people who buy strategy work, sell it, fund the firms disrupting it, and train the next generation expected to practice it.

Core Questions
What does decision-making look like at the speed of inference, and where does it break? How are PE operators and corporate boards repricing strategy services now that the underlying analytical labor has been commoditized? What is the new moat for consulting firms when generic models replicate most of a junior associate's output? If AI handles the apprenticeship work, how does the next generation of senior strategists develop the judgment AI cannot replicate? And where has this transformation underdelivered, and what are firms quietly walking back?

Panelists
● Jenny Fielding, Co-founder + Partner, Everywhere Ventures
● Kes Sampanthar, Founder & CEO, Centaurian AI
● David Thomson, Founder & CEO of Inevitable
● Iliya P. Zogovic, Founder & CEO, DBD Investment Bank
● Moderator Christine Alemany, Founder, Thrv Advisors (Women’s Circle Board Member)

https://www.cbsacny.org/events/EventDetails.aspx?id=2061030&group=

What's defensible, and what's hype?

title: Fund It or Kill It: The CFO–CIO AI ROI Lab
description:
AI ROI Lab is a hands-on workshop for CFOs, CIOs, and cross-functional leaders who need to decide where AI can create real business value - and where it won’t. Attendees will bring real workflows and pressure-test them live using a practical ROI framework focused on business value, implementation friction, repeatability, feasibility, and expected return.

This is not a panel or a theory session. It’s a live working lab for people who need to make the case, own the budget, evaluate implementation tradeoffs, or drive adoption across the business. Participants leave with a reusable framework to prioritize AI use cases with more rigor and less hype.

Hosted by Andra Vaduva, Founder and CEO of SafeSpace, a New York–based AI Adoption Lab with global clients, helping companies turn AI into practical workflows across operations, finance, and growth, in partnership with Catalin Briciu, CEO and Co-Founder of Linnify, a global technology partner that helps U.S. mid-market companies plan and execute digital and AI initiatives through proven, low-risk frameworks.

Together, they combine expertise in AI adoption and implementation to help U.S. mid-market companies move beyond the hype and deploy AI in ways that drive real, measurable business value.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Reset + Build: Overcoming AI Anxiety by Women in AI
description:
AI is moving fast — and for many people building in it, that pace can feel like noise, pressure, or uncertainty.

This is a space to slow down, reset, and move back into action with more clarity.

We’ll begin with a grounding session, hear from builders working across AI, and then move into hands-on experimentation with tools in the room.

Format
• Guided grounding / reset session
• Conversation with AI builders & operators
• Hands-on build + experimentation
• Networking w/ drinks & bites

Speakers
Emily Leahy is the founder of InPhase, an AI-powered platform helping women optimize performance and leadership through physiology-informed insights. With 15+ years building companies and coaching women in high-pressure careers, Emily works at the intersection of AI, women’s health, and the future of work. She is an ICF-certified coach through Brown University and a speaker on leadership, performance, and building systems that work with women’s biology, not against it.

TeLisa Daughtry is an award-winning serial entrepreneur, conscious technologist, educator, and global advocate for gender equity in technology. A recognized voice in AI accessibility and responsible innovation, she has spoken at institutions including the United Nations and has led workshops helping non-technical founders and leaders navigate AI adoption with greater confidence. Her work focuses on technology, education, entrepreneurship, and building more inclusive futures in AI.

Katherine Brough is the founder of NuPath AI, an AI platform focused on supporting mental health, physical health, and everyday wellbeing through intelligent systems. With a background in software engineering and full-stack product development, Katherine has spent years building mobile apps, scalable backends, and AI-driven tools designed to improve people’s lives. Her work sits at the intersection of AI, wellness, and human-centered technology.

Stephanie Farquhar is the founder of Equitable Data Solutions and a former leader at Google, the White House Office of Management and Budget, and the NYC Health Department. Her work focuses on helping leaders — especially women and historically marginalized professionals — build confidence with data and AI in high-stakes environments. Stephanie specializes in responsible AI, leadership communication, and making technical conversations more accessible, empowering, and actionable.

Nichole Acosta is digital health product manager who doesn’t just learn about AI—she builds with it, and she builds fast. She joined Women Defining AI in December and completed the foundations courses, and within days, she’d taken what she learned and shipped an Agenthon team matcher that matches people who want to solve similar problems across Mayo Clinic. Within hours of building it, her leadership team—including AI experts and physicians—were impressed enough to roll it out to 1,800 colleagues. She enjoys solving problems that reduce mental burden and is here today to help you build your daily operating system to do just that. 

​Moderator
Taylor Borde, Head of Marketing & NYC Chapter at Women in AI

Thanks to our Sponsor:

Vonage turns powerful communications APIs into real-world experiences, whether it’s connecting people through voice, video, or messaging, or building apps that tap into next-gen network capabilities. Vonage is built for developers who want to experiment, create, and scale seamless communication experiences fast.
https://www.vonage.com/

​About the Hosts:

​​Women in AI connects, empowers, and elevates female founders, investors, and engineers shaping the future of artificial intelligence. Through community, mentorship, and global events, we’re building a more inclusive AI ecosystem where women lead and innovate. ​​
https://www.womeninai.club/

Women Defining AI Inc. is dedicated to educating the public on artificial intelligence through curated learning programs, educational content, research, and community events. We advance this educational mission with a specific focus on closing the gender-tech gap and empowering women and underrepresented groups to lead in the AI era.
https://www.womendefiningai.org/

title: Agent-Led Growth: The CMO Briefing
description:
Marketing has a new audience. It's not human.

AI agents are recommending brands, comparing products, and building shortlists for your buyers right now. They're the first touchpoint in the buyer's journey — and they don't sleep.

Every marketing team has heard of AI search. Few are running it as a revenue channel. Knowing a channel exists and knowing how to operate it are two different problems.

On June 4th at 6:30 PM, Limy is bringing together a curated group of senior marketing leaders for a private briefing on Agent-Led Growth, the shift reshaping marketing, and what running AI Search as a revenue channel actually looks like in practice. 

You'll hear directly from the marketers running Agent-Led Growth inside Tiffany, P&G and Retrofête— what changed for their team, what's working, and what they wish they'd known a year ago.

🌟Eduardo Barbaro CDO at Tiffany
🌟Yana Ernazarova  - Head of Digital at P&G
🌟Gina Lombardo - VP Marketing at Retrofête
🌟 Oded Bachar - VP Marketing at IL Makiage

🌟 Aviv Shamny, Founder & CEO of Limy, the Agentic Marketing Stack. Framing the shift and the framework for Agent-Led Growth.

We'll also get into the new job description for marketers in the agentic era — how the role is being rewritten, and what's coming next.

Looking forward to seeing you there!

title: Wine & Einstein: Could you be an NPC? + Unlimited Gaming
description:
‘Could you be an NPC?’
We all think we are the main character of our story but how do you know you aren’t an NPC in someone else’s simulation? Is there any way that we can know whether we are in a simulated world? Would a simulation of your brain have conscious experience? If yes, could you then upload your consciousness into a computer? And how can you be so sure it hasn’t happened already?

Join us for a night of gaming, learning, and discussion with a lecture by Dr. Richard Brown, Philosopher, CUNY
Area of Specialization: Philosophy of Mind & Cognitive Science
Areas of Competence: Logic, History of Philosophy, General Psychology, Cognitive Neuroscience

Doors open at 6:30 PM
Lecture starts at 7:00 PM
7:50 PM Socialize (and game) with fellow guests.

title: Rebuilding the Government's Front Door
description:
Join us at the Kaizen HQ for drinks, bites, demos, and a room full of people who believe beautifully designed software shouldn't be a luxury in government.

Essential public services have been stuck in 1998 for too long. Americans deserve better than the digital experiences they're handed for renewing a passport, filing taxes, or filing for public benefits. Kaizen exists to fix that.

We build modern, AI-native software for local, state, and federal agencies. Today, our platform reaches 40 million residents across 50+ agencies in 17 states. Founded in 2022 and based in New York City, Kaizen has raised $35 million from NEA, a16z, Accel, 776, and Carpenter Capital.

description:
You won’t see any other event at New York Tech Week even a little bit close to this one. 

And that’s exactly why we need it. 

A magnetic presence. 
Confidence in self-advocacy.
An aura that says “I respect myself”, so they respect you back. 

As ambitious women in tech, we need these qualities to thrive in our careers.

Yet too many of us are trapped in our heads, for too long. 

Doubting our brilliance. 
Dimming our own light. 
Underselling ourselves. 

To get out of our own way and step into our power, we need embodied, authentic confidence and a presence that exudes authority, effortlessly. 

Power in Motion: Heels Dance Workshop for Women in Tech is an introduction to the dance style that has captivated women across the world for the way it emboldens and empowers us to reach our highest potential. 

Izzy Becker is a dancer, consultant in data & technology and coach from London, UK, based in NYC. 

In this unique event, you will:
🎤 Hear Izzy’s personal story of transformation
💃 Learn basic heels dance technique and a short piece of choreography

title: AI for HR: Simplifying the H1B Process with AI Support
description:
Immigration strategy shouldn't be opaque. But for most HR teams, it is. You submit paperwork, wait months for status updates, have no visibility into where things stand or what your options actually are. Meanwhile, your hiring managers are frustrated, your candidates are confused, and you're explaining delays you don't fully understand yourself. Manifest Legal built a tool to change that. They track H1B statuses in real time and, more importantly, they surface the alternative pathways available to you - the visa options, the timing strategies, the creative solutions that most HR teams never even know exist. This dinner brings together HR leaders who are ready to stop guessing and start understanding what immigration actually looks like for their organizations.

We'll talk through how visibility into your pipeline changes everything. What alternatives exist when H1B doesn't work. How to plan hiring strategy around visa timelines instead of being surprised by them. What conversations to have with your legal team, your hiring managers, and your candidates when you actually understand the landscape. Manifest will walk through their tool - how it tracks, how it communicates, how it opens up options. And we'll talk honestly about what this means for your organization's ability to compete for global talent.

You'll leave knowing what you're actually working with, what options you've been missing, and how to move immigration from a compliance headache to a strategic lever in your hiring. And you'll do it over dinner with HR leaders dealing with the exact same friction.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Stop Making AI Guess: When Your Codebase Becomes the Spec
description:
If AI should read codebase structure rather than guess at it, why doesn't it?

AI coding tools work by reading source files and inferring architecture. The result is "almost-usable" code that requires manual refactoring. 

The deeper problem isn't the tools — it's that most frameworks weren't designed to be read structurally.  There's no layer for AI to reason against, so the tools are stuck guessing.

SpyneJS is an open-source framework designed for AI-assisted development.  It exposes a structural codemap that gives agents a deterministic understanding of application structure.

Refined over six years in enterprise environments, the framework is designed so generated code respects architectural boundaries from the first prompt.

Join us for a live demo of frontend codegen that holds up under peer review.  We'll show how agents interact with SpyneJS to produce structurally sound features, then discuss what becomes possible when the codebase itself functions as a working specification.

For developers, founders, and anyone evaluating where serious frontend development goes next.

spynejs.com

description:
Win a Mac Mini. Pitch in front of investors. Get real feedback live.

Fundraising Tactics Panel + Pitch Competition
June 4 | Evening

A structured event built around two things: learning what’s working in fundraising right now, and putting it into practice live.

How the night works:

1. Tactical Panel (30–40 mins)
Investors and operators break down what they’re actually responding to right now across:

What makes investors take a first meeting
Outreach that gets replies
What moves a deal forward vs stalls it

2. Live Pitch Competition (45–60 mins)

10-15 founders pitch (short, tight format)
Investor judges give immediate feedback after each pitch
Audience votes alongside judges

3. Awards + Next Steps (10–15 mins)

Grand Prize: Mac Mini and 3 months of Raisi Campaigns ($5,700 Value)

4. Guided Networking (rest of evening)

Founders raising now
Investors actively deploying
Structured intros


About Raisi

Raisi is a fundraising operating system that helps founders identify the right investors, run targeted outreach, and manage their raise end to end.

Since 2024, once every 50.1 minutes, a founder has met with an investor interested in their company through Raisi. The platform has facilitated 21,000+ positive connections between founders and investors and is powered by up-to-date signals from millions of live interactions across the private markets.

About Forecastr

Forecastr helps founders build investor-ready financial models and projections. From early-stage planning to growth-stage forecasting, Forecastr gives you the clarity and structure investors expect, so you can walk into conversations with confidence and back up your story with real numbers.

title: Run Tech Club | Signal Run x Wildwood Ventures
description:
Shared motion. TechWeek ritual.

3 miles. Early morning. Post run gather at Fabrik Tribeca for coffee and breakfast. Founders, technical builders, and investors who are actually in the trenches — compounding momentum together before the week kicks off.

Part of the Signal Run series. This event will feature a guest partner from one of NYC's most active early-stage venture firms. Announced before the run. Worth showing up for.

Founders who share what they're building get indexed in the Signal pipeline. Real venture visibility. Showcase selection starts here. 

This is where the next rep begins.
→ Sign up here to secure your spot.

→ Apply to showcase here.
https://runtechclub.typeform.com/apply

———
About Run Tech Club

​Run Tech Club is the operating platform for early-stage founders who prioritize clarity over noise. A recurring system of weekly proof intervals, structured experimentation, and critical roadmapping — designed to close the gap between conviction and market proof. Founders who build through RTC surface real signal. The best of that signal earns visibility at Signal Showcase — quarterly, across LA, NY, Seattle, and SF.

Join Run Tech Club → runtechclub.co

———
About Wildwood Ventures

Wildwood Ventures (Backed by VF Corp. Fortune 500 owner of iconic brands like The North Face, Vans, Timberland, Altra, Smartwool) is an early-stage venture capital firm backing bold founders who are building the future of healthy and active lifestyles. Their mission is to address America’s health and wellness crisis — driving greater physical, mental, social, and spiritual wellbeing.

title: GNOMI GenZ Hackathon: Make Wall Street Go Viral
description:
GNOMI Gen Z Growth Hackathon — NYC HQ | June 5–6
During a16z Tech Week

We are opening GNOMI’s New York HQ to a small group of exceptional growth minds, product thinkers, creators, creative strategists, and culture-native builders.

GNOMI has raised $30M+ to build the world’s first AI News Agent — turning global news, markets, filings, earnings calls, and live events into real-time intelligence across 189 countries and 17+ languages.

On June 5–6, participants will compete to make GNOMI impossible to ignore for the next generation.

The defining question:
How do we make GNOMI the intelligence layer for a generation that wants to understand the world before the world tells them what to think?

Participants will compete across two tracks:

Best Content Creation
Create the most compelling, original, and culturally relevant content concept for GNOMI. This may include short-form video, social campaigns, creator-led formats, memes, storytelling concepts, launch moments, or content franchises that make GNOMI instantly understandable, shareable, and impossible to ignore.

Best Growth Strategy
Build the smartest and most scalable strategy to drive GNOMI adoption among Gen Z and millennials. This may include viral loops, ambassador programs, campus growth, referral mechanics, creator partnerships, community strategy, product-led growth, or entirely new distribution ideas.

This is not a traditional marketing brainstorm.

We are looking for executable ideas that can move culture, drive adoption, and turn GNOMI into a product people talk about, share, and use daily.

Prizes
Each track will have its own winners:
🥇 1st Place: $4,000 + interview opportunity at GNOMI
🥈 2nd Place: $2,000 + interview opportunity at GNOMI
🥉 3rd Place: surprise awards and recognition

We are looking for rare builders and creators who understand the intersection of AI, finance, media, culture, behavior, product, and growth — people who can turn insight into strategy, strategy into execution, and execution into measurable adoption.

Content. Growth. Product. Creative. Social. Community. Campus. Finance. Data. AI. Culture.

The next frontier is not more information.

It is building the intelligence layer the next generation trusts, uses, and shares.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Tea: Founder/CEO Self-Mastery - a Dialogue
description:
**Founder/CEO Self-Mastery: A Facilitated Group Dialogue**  

At the center of every company is a founder learning to lead themselves. This intimate, facilitated dialogue is designed for founders and CEOs who are actively working on their own self-mastery—navigating emotional regulation, ego, identity shifts, burnout, and the inner game of leadership. 

In a trusted space with peers who understand the weight of the role, we’ll explore what it takes to lead with clarity, resilience, and integrity. This is not about hacks or hustle—it’s about becoming the kind of leader your company truly needs. Come ready to reflect, be challenged, and connect at a deeper level.

The session will be facilitated by post exit founder, Peter Corbett who is widely know as an expert facilitator of retreats, workshops, and small groups (YPO forums / Hampton etc.).

The experience will take place in the format of a Tea Ceremony inside a 15’ Lotus Belle tent! 🎪 inside the Domino Sugar Factory.

title: Sanctum Digital: Global Mindful Movement
description:

The experience begins with a guided light-flow session—an accessible introduction to the SANCTUM method. Through a curated sequence of movement, breath, and music, you’ll awaken the body, sharpen mental clarity, and expand your state before the day begins.

Following the session, join the founders of SANCTUM for a breakfast and intimate conversation on rethinking human performance, building a global wellness brand, and launching their digital app. The discussion will explore how immersive, mind–body experiences are evolving into scalable platforms—and what it means to bring transformation into the tech space.

Come to move, reset, and connect—before the noise of the day sets in.

title: NET Worth - A padel morning
description:
NET WORTH
A private padel morning on the East River. Hosted by Lyzr during NY Tech Week.
Compound your social interest.
Before Tech Week's last day starts, a small group is heading to Domino Park for three hours on a private padel court — Manhattan skyline on one side, East River on the other, and the kind of crowd that makes the warm-up conversations as good as the game.
Mixed pairs, rotating doubles. No tournament, no rankings, no one keeping score too hard. If you haven't played padel before, you'll pick it up in ten minutes — it's easier than tennis and twice as fun. If you have played, you already know.

title: Building with the Bard: Remixing Shakespeare
description:
The best way to learn how to build with AI is to build something through, end-to-end in a single session.

​For New York Tech Week, Build First brings you Breakfast with the Bard: Remixing Shakespeare. Join us on Friday, May 15th, from 9:30 a.m. to 12:00 p.m. for a morning of innovative creation.

​First, we'll download the complete works of William Shakespeare, then use it as our creative input into Claude Code, and harness the power of AI to transform them into something new and exciting. Think of it as a creative laboratory where classical literature meets state-of-the-art technology.

​We'll provide a few starting ideas to spark your imagination, but the possibilities are endless:

- ​Shakespearean Chatbot: Create an AI-powered character chatbot.

- ​Remixed Play: Generate a modern-day interpretation of a classic plot.

​- AI-Generated Sonnets: Train an AI model to write new sonnets in his iconic style.

- ​Interactive Visualizations: Build interactive visualizations that explore themes and characters.

​For inspiration of what to build, see "Punk Shakespeare: here: https://punk-shakespeare.vercel.app/

​More ideas will be shared during the workshop, but the real magic will come from your unique vision and creativity. This is a chance to push the boundaries of AI, experiment with data, and discover new ways to interact with classical literature.

​Target Audience:

​- Writers and poets
- ​Developers and data scientists
- ​Artists and designers
- ​Anyone curious about AI and creativity

​We will provide breakfast, but bring your curiosity and laptop! Limited spots are available, so register now.

​—

​Bethany Crystal is the founder and CEO of Build First, an AI learning lab that teaches anyone how to build with AI.

​https://buildfirst.ai/

​—

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: C10 Labs x Wells Fargo x NY Robotics — Pitch the Product
host: C10 Labs, Wells Fargo, New York Robotics
description:
Most pitch events train founders to impress VCs. This one trains you to close customers.

C10 Labs, NY Robotics, and Wells Fargo are hosting a live pitch workshop for deep tech and robotics founders who sell to blue-collar and industrial buyers — plant managers, procurement teams, logistics operators, and regional decision-makers across Middle America.

Five selected founders will pitch live and receive a real-time teardown from a panel of experts: a marketing SME, a VC, and operators who actually buy this stuff. Feedback focuses on whether your story resonates with people who run shifts, manage crews, and keep systems online — not just people who write term sheets.

You may present:

A sales deck used with operators, plant managers, or procurement teams

An investor deck, if it's the primary tool driving pilots or enterprise adoption

Each selected founder gets:

A live pitch opportunity + deck walkthrough

Direct, candid feedback from a potential buyer, a marketing agency SME, and an investor

Practical guidance to make your story clearer and more credible in the field

Open application — submit your pitch to be considered. 3 slots reserved for C10 Labs cohort companies, 2 open to the broader community. Presenters announced onsite.

Breakfast and coffee provided by Wells Fargo.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Real-Time Sprint: Build It Live
description:
Description

A fast, high-energy morning sprint where builders create and demo real-time experiences in just a few hours.

No slides. No long hackathon. Just show up and build something that happens live.

We’re exploring what the internet feels like when interaction is immediate—voice, chat, AI, collaboration, and presence.

⸻

Time

Friday, June 5 | Morning Session
10:00 AM – 2:00 PM (4 hours)

⸻

Format

* 10:00 – 10:15 | Kickoff + prompt drop
* 10:15 – 12:15 | Build sprint (solo or teams)
* 12:15 – 1:45 | Live demos (2–3 min each)
* 1:45 – 2:00 | Voting, shoutouts, wrap

⸻

What you can build

Anything that feels real-time:

* live AI assistants you can talk to
* voice-first or conversation-based apps
* real-time collaboration tools
* interactive social experiences
* presence-based or “in-the-moment” systems

(Some teams may explore voice, AI, or communication layers using real-time APIs.)

⸻

Why come

You’ll leave with:

* a working prototype in a few hours
* immediate feedback from other builders
* ideas shaped by real-time interaction, not theory

⸻

Core idea

What if everything you built had to happen live?

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Open for Ops

description:

Walk through the gen 2 Four Seasons Holodeck (responsible for over $500M in presales) and get hands-on with mixed reality and XR demos from Agile Lens, StorySplat, and WanderLens Lab. Experience immersive tech delivering blue-chip value in 2026.

• Now celebrating 10 years of excellence in spatial design, Agile Lens was born from a mission to redefine storytelling through immersive technology, merging design, architecture, and XR to create transformative experiences that delight and surprise audiences around the world. https://www.agilelens.com/

• DBOX is an international creative communications agency that develops strategic campaigns for luxury real estate and hospitality, combining global expertise and local insight to build brands, visualize unbuilt projects, and drive sales across the full project lifecycle. https://dbox.com/

• WanderLens Lab is a NYC/LA-based creative tech startup focusing on the application of Gaussian splatting and physical AI in real estate, and how Snap Spectacles are shaping the next generation of location-based entertainment. https://www.wanderlenslab.com/

• StorySplat is a web platform that lets users create and share interactive 3D tours using Gaussian splatting, with waypoints, spatial audio, and hotspots—all exportable as simple, shareable web pages. https://storysplat.com/

This is a two day event - if this date doesn't work, sign up for Day 1 here (Mon June 1) : https://partiful.com/e/6bUok8RK79B927TfqX7F

host: Northwestern Mutual Life Insurance Company, Cloudonix, Inc
description:
