title: 3D & 4D Data: World Models, Robotics & Physical AI
host: New York AI Labs, SearchParty, Hue, Halespan
description:
What data takes physical AI from the lab to the real world?

​Robotics and AI labs know the problem: physical AI has no internet-scale data corpus to learn from — a $73B data drought keeping robots stuck in the lab, bodies without brains, starved for the scalability and diversity needed to learn the physical world.

​We’re putting on this high signal NY Tech Week event so teams can finally deploy autonomous machines that see, think, and act in our world.

​10:00 — Doors open. Coffee n’ networking

​10:30 — Volumes (15 min)

​10:45 — Panel: What data takes physical AI from the lab to the real world?

​11:15 — Coffee n’ networking

​12:00 — Close

​About the hosts:

​Volumes — the most accurate spatial training data for physical AI, across every format, source, and sensor modality. volumes.cloud

​Techstars — the world’s most active startup accelerator, turning over 4,000 early-stage founders into market leaders who have collectively raised over $32 Billion. techstars.com

​New York AI Labs — NYC’s community of frontier AI researchers, founders, and labs.

​Surprise panelists announced soon. ;)

​For researchers, founders, and investors backing physical AI.

​🎟️ Very limited capacity. RSVP early.

​This event is part of #NYTechWeek 2026 — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at tech-week.com.

title: She's Selling: Building the $3.3T Future of Female Exits with Fintech
description:
Women represent nearly half of all business owners in America, yet only 1% will ever exit. That's not just a founder problem. It's a $3.3T market opportunity that fintech has yet to fully build for.

The resources to start and scale are available. Meanwhile, the infrastructure to exit is still being built.
What's holding women back isn't just financial literacy. It's the psychosocial, emotional, and identity dimensions of the female exit that make her experience categorically different and categorically underserved. Most women have never seen the process clearly enough to picture themselves in it.
This session exists to bring together the female founder and the fintech world to have a conversation about the gap and the opportunity. 

Co-hosted with Baton, the modern business brokerage platform, we walk through what selling actually looks like — step by step, in plain language — so the platform feels approachable and the female founder can see herself in the process.

title: We Solved the Chatbot Misinfo Problem. Here's How.
description:
When chatbots need to be most accurate—when people turn to them during breaking news to understand what’s going on in the world—that’s when they fail most often, and most egregiously. They spread false claims and even cite foreign propaganda.

At NewsGuard, arming news consumers with tools for assessing reliability is what we do.

Misinformation in AI is solvable, and it doesn't require exploiting the publishers whose work the LLMs are trying to source. And now we’ve proven it.

This event will announce and demo the tool that can do it.

If you care about where AI and news are headed, come on over — we'll have bagels and coffee.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: How AI is transforming the workforce model – and what comes next for talent strategy
description:
For decades, workforce strategy followed a predictable rhythm: define roles, forecast headcount, hire, repeat. AI has broken that model. It is not just a tool for efficiency; it is a lasting shift in how work gets done, how roles evolve and how value is created. As AI takes on routine tasks, talent strategy must move beyond static, job-based planning toward a dynamic, skills-first approach grounded in human judgement and adaptability. 

Moderated by LinkedIn, this discussion with Nickle Lamoreux, IBM SVP and CHRO, and other industry executives will explore how leading organizations are redesigning roles, redefining higher-value work, and aligning hiring, learning and performance around skills to build continuously evolving workforces. The session will also highlight examples of how AI is reshaping work, what can be automated and where human expertise matters most. 

This event is on a first come, first served basis. Please arrive early to claim your seat. Registration will open at 10:30 AM ET.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Ekoparty: Argentina’s Cybersecurity Ecosystem, Talent, Startups, and Regional Expansion
host: Ekoparty Security Conference, Consulate General of Argentina in New York
hosts: Consulate General of Argentina in New York
description:
Born in Argentina more than 20 years ago, Ekoparty has become a key part of the country’s cybersecurity ecosystem and one of the most recognized community-driven platforms in Latin America. What started as a technical conference evolved into a space that connects researchers, startups, companies, founders, and emerging talent, helping showcase the depth of Argentina’s cybersecurity culture and technical expertise.

Today, Argentina represents one of the most distinctive cybersecurity ecosystems in the region, combining highly skilled professionals, a strong research and hacker culture, and a growing number of companies building globally competitive products and services. From independent researchers to established firms, the country has become an important source of innovation and talent for the international cybersecurity industry.

This session will explore how Argentina built this ecosystem, the role community platforms like Ekoparty played in fostering collaboration and visibility, and why the country has become a strategic opportunity for U.S. companies, investors, and organizations looking to connect with Latin American cybersecurity talent and innovation. It will also cover how Ekoparty expanded beyond Argentina to strengthen regional and international connections, including its recent launch in the United States with Ekoparty Miami.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Before You Raise: What Investors Actually Check
description:
This event is designed for tech founders and investors navigating fundraising and growth. Most companies enter investor conversations assuming their structure is solid and find out during diligence that it’s not.

We’ll break down what tech investors actually verify, where structural issues tend to surface, and how to approach ownership, equity, and governance early so they hold when capital is deployed.

Who this is for:
- Tech founders preparing to raise or actively in investor conversations
- Teams making decisions around equity, ownership, and early structure
- Tech or AI Investors evaluating early-stage companies

What we'll cover:
- What investors actually verify during diligence in high-growth tech companies
- Where cap tables and legal records diverge and how that impacts ownership and control
- How equity, governance, and decision-making authority evolve as companies raise and scale
- The structural issues that tend to surface when companies move from building to raising

Hosted by Ad Hoc Founders Counsel (Bagchi Law PC). Advising founders on structure, ownership, and capital as companies grow and raise.

title: AI-Native Services: Founders Panel
description:
Services markets — legal, finance, insurance, compliance — represent over $1 trillion globally. For decades, software made those service providers faster, but the margin stayed with the firms. AI is collapsing that distinction. Companies are no longer selling tools to service providers; they are owning both the intelligence layer and the delivery itself.

This panel brings together a curated group of founders building in that category, alongside the investors backing them, to work through the questions that are top of mind in the space right now: Where does AI replace the service, and where does it only make the service faster? And what are the pitfalls, technical and otherwise, that founders building AI-native services most commonly encounter?

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Scaling Founder-led Sales on LinkedIn
description:
Scaling Founder-Led Sales on LinkedIn

A practical workshop on building and operationalizing founder-led LinkedIn outreach

📅 Friday, June 5th, 2026
⏰ 12 PM ET
📍 Virtual (Zoom)

A lot of founders are doing outbound inconsistently or are trying to automate before the messaging is actually working.

And honestly, a lot of LinkedIn outreach right now is bad... we all know why!

People are over-automating, sending generic messaging, and scaling outreach that was never positioned well to begin with.

This workshop is focused on how to actually structure founder-led outreach in a way that still feels personal while being operationally manageable long term.

I’ll walk through the systems, messaging philosophy, campaign organization, and automation workflows I use across consulting clients to manage LinkedIn prospecting and outbound.

We’ll also talk about where founders tend to get stuck, common mistakes with automation tools, and how to think about outbound as an evolving process rather than a one-time setup.

What we’ll cover

1. Founder-led sales on LinkedIn: what actually works

-Why founder outreach performs differently
-Common messaging mistakes founders make
-The mindset shift from “selling” to supporting

2. Building and organizing your prospect list

-How to curate higher-quality LinkedIn prospect lists
-Why ICP alignment matters before automating outreach
-What founders often get wrong about volume

3. Structuring scalable outreach systems

-How to operationalize LinkedIn outreach workflows
-Cadence structure and campaign organization
-Connection requests, follow-ups, and sequencing philosophy
-Why campaign maintenance matters more than people think

4. Messaging that gets responses

-Why “you-focused” messaging performs better
-How to position around the buyer’s goals and problems
-Clear calls-to-action that convert into meetings

5. Automation tools and real-world workflow examples

-How I use automation tools like Dripify within outbound systems
-Common automation mistakes that quietly hurt results
-Why bad messaging scaled is still bad outreach

The tool mentioned in this workshop is called Dripify (but you can plug these tips into your own tech stack too). You can sign up with my affiliate link for a free account and 7-day trial here (no card needed):
https://try.dripify.com/krcg

6. Optimizing over time

-Why outreach systems should evolve every few weeks
-How to evaluate what’s working
-Real examples of improving outbound activity and engagement across client accounts

Who this is for:

-Founders leading their own sales efforts

-Early-stage operators building outbound systems

-Consultants and fractionals supporting GTM execution

-Anyone trying to create more consistent pipeline through LinkedIn outreach

What you’ll walk away with:

-A clearer framework for founder-led LinkedIn outreach

-A more scalable way to organize outbound efforts

-Messaging principles that improve response quality

title: The Autonomous Enterprise: How AI Will Redefine Company Operations, Leadership, and Talent.
description: Come join us for a discussion on how companies are being rebuilt in the age of AI. We’re at a moment where it’s no longer just about automation — it’s about rethinking the entire operating model. As AI agents begin to take on more execution, the real question becomes how everything is orchestrated across the business. I’ll be joined by a set of panelists who bring hands-on experience from leading AI transformation, building the infrastructure behind it, and working through the realities inside a P&L. We’ll explore what the “autonomous enterprise” actually looks like in practice, where it breaks down, and what it takes to make it work. If you’re thinking about how to move beyond experimentation and into real transformation, this should be a great conversation.

title: Building AI Agents for Science
description:
Building AI Agents for Science: From Theory to Laboratory

Artificial intelligence is undergoing a paradigm shift, moving beyond simple chatbots to autonomous agents capable of complex reasoning. Nowhere is this transformation more critical than in the sciences, where AI agents are beginning to accelerate the pace of discovery, from drug development to material science.

Hosted by GRAIL, this session explores the "Autonomous Science" movement—a new frontier where PhD-level agents partner with researchers to automate the scientific loop. This two-part event features a high-level panel of academic and industry leaders followed by a hands-on technical workshop.

During the workshop, participants will be introduced to ApexClaw, GRAIL's flagship platform for building advanced science agents. Built on the open-source foundations of OpenClaw, ApexClaw provides the specialized reasoning and rigor required for scientific research.

What You’ll Learn
Participants will explore the architecture of scientific agents and the shift toward autonomous R&D, including:

#The Future of Research: A panel discussion with higher-education leaders and AI experts on how agents are reshaping the university lab.
#The Agentic Loop: Understanding how AI systems handle hypothesis generation, experimental planning, and data interpretation.
#Introduction to ApexClaw: How to leverage specialized frameworks to go beyond general-purpose LLMs.
#Live Implementation: A guided session on deploying your first autonomous agent for a scientific task.

Who Should Attend
This event is designed for:
#Founders and Researchers looking to integrate autonomous workflows into their R&D.
#PhD Students and Academics interested in the future of digital laboratory assistants.
#Engineers and Developers exploring the intersection of AI, OpenClaw, and specialized scientific domains.
#Investors interested in the "AI for Science" vertical.

What Participants Receive
All attendees will receive exclusive free credits for ApexClaw, allowing you to implement your own agent during the workshop and continue building your own digital researcher after the event concludes.

The location will be provided to registered attendees.

Duration
2-hour

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com. This event is sponsored by The Front Yard at Pace University.  Learn more about their entrepreneur program at https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-front-yard-at-pace-university

title: Money in 2036: The Future of Finance and How We Get There
description:
In 2036, a repair shop owner in Nairobi, Kenya gets a $1,200 loan in 17 seconds. Three competing offers, best rate wins, money moves before he closes the tab. No human involved.

The infrastructure for that doesn't exist yet. Some of it is being built right now, in this city, by people in this room.

We're putting together a panel featuring leaders in traditional finance, DeFi, compliance, and the founders working on the underlying rails — to get into what's actually happening and where the gaps are. Networking after.

Hosted by CreditBridge and NYU Blockchain Club, as part of #NYCTechWeek.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Diasporic Impact Tech: Revitalizing Millenary Solutions to Redefine How We Live and Interact
host: GLOBAL PORTAL INSTITUTE, THE PORT GLOBAL
description:
Humanity is on the move, there have never been more people living outside of the country of origin in modern history. Between the world's oldest regions from Greece to the Amazon Rainforest and the Himalayas, existing solutions can meet modern technology to ensure proper longevity. Today, amidst this very special period of transition, most of the people may not have access to their bank account, but they have a connection. A digital relay framework makes it possible to scale these initiatives in order to be implemented across the world. Solutions are disseminated more rapidly than ever through diasporic impact across regions and continents. The next major arena facing humanity is the digital and physical architecture of a regenerative Diaspora and its design, leveraging modern-day technologies to enable significant and scalable contributions redefining how we live and interact, regardless of where you are from and where you are going. This Tech Week 2026, the Global Portal Institute and The Port Global invite you to another session exploring vital millenary intelligence brought to our lives through technology. 

Following Tech Week NY 2025 (https://www.globalportal.in/nytechweek2025), this following event will be joined by another lineup of significant international leaders.

---

title: The Oasis @ NY Tech Week Day 5 - Emerging Technology
host: Boardy, Vibe Space, Flowstate, The AI Collective
description:
Tech Week can be chaotic. Running between panels, pitching, networking, and feeling overstimulated. The Oasis is a daily retreat: a place to belong, breathe, and actually co-create.

We're building a persistent home base across the week where builders, founders, and creatives show up every day to flow together, have real conversations around meaningful topics, and leave refreshed and inspired. The series blends daily 'co-flow' drop-in sessions with curated collaborative programming: roundtable discussions, fireside chats, and mentor office hours. Each day seeds Flowstate Sparks, structured artifacts that keep the conversation going long after the week ends.

Day 5: Emerging Technologies

Our fifth and final day turns toward the frontier. We'll dig into the new developments, ideas, and signals that surfaced throughout the week. What's genuinely exciting, what's hype, and what threads are worth pulling on next. Expect candid roundtables, sharp questions, and a closing space to consolidate what you've gathered before heading back into the world.

With thanks to our venue partner, BELONG NYC 

The Oasis is hosted at BELONG NYC (@belong.nyc), a hospitality-driven workspace and urban sanctuary in the heart of SoHo. Think airport lounge meets coworking: a high-design, high-service environment purpose-built for the in-between moments of life in motion. Beyond hosting our series, BELONG offers Flowstate members on-demand access to private meeting rooms, phone booths, and hot desks, plus showers and changing facilities, secure luggage storage, and rentable lockers throughout Tech Week. If you're juggling flights, workouts, dinners, and back-to-back meetings, it's the place to reset and keep going.  This event is part of #NYTechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem.

title: $14M for Faces That Don’t Exist Yet
description:
Why would investors put $14M behind faces that don’t exist yet, and make it one of Europe’s biggest pre-seed bets?

Because avatars are no longer just a visual layer. They’re becoming products, performers, and scalable businesses.

Join us for a look inside Europe’s biggest avatar studio and see what it actually takes to build real-time digital humans people want to watch, interact with, and come back to.

We’ll cover:

* the tech stack behind live, responsive avatars
* how they become products and revenue streams
* what it takes to move from prototype to deployment
* and what happens when these faces don’t start from zero, but already come with followers, fandom, and reach

No fluff. Just a talk about what’s already happening, what’s coming next, and why this space is moving faster than most people realize, delivered by our CEO & Founder, Vlastimil Venclik.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Live Events Are Becoming A Real-World Testbed For Web3 Infrastructure
Too Young to Lead? How to Win in Tech Anyway
description:
Everyone told Vini Kaul she was too young. Too inexperienced. Not ready.

She went on to negotiate multi-billion dollar technology deals, become one of the youngest tech executives in enterprise sales globally, graduate Harvard Business School, and co-found an AI startup.

In this one-hour session, Vini breaks down exactly how she navigated age bias in high-stakes tech environments, built credibility before she had tenure, and converted skepticism into billion-dollar trust. She will also share what she is building next and why she is betting on AI to redefine what growth leadership looks like.

This is not a motivational talk. It is a tactical playbook from someone who has operated at the highest levels of tech, and won.

Key Takeaways:

>> How to build executive credibility before your resume earns it
>> The specific moves that convert age bias into competitive advantage
>> What closing multi-billion dollar deals taught her about trust, timing, and positioning
>> A first look at AI startup Smadlytics and the global growth behind it

title: Structuring for Global Capital: How Argentina's Digital Zones Can Compete with Dubai and Estonia
host: Andén Tech, Consulate General of Argentina in New York
hosts: Consulate General of Argentina in New York
description:
Argentina has studied the playbook. Dubai built a jurisdiction from scratch. Estonia digitized its state. Now Argentina is betting that its first Digital Zone — in Mendoza — can do both: offer the legal clarity of a special economic zone and the operational simplicity of a digital-first infrastructure.
 
Co-hosted by the Consulate General of Argentina in New York, this session brings together founders, investors, and officials to pressure-test that thesis openly. What would it actually take for Argentina to compete — and win — global capital at that level?

title: The Briefing: What No One Told Her About Exiting — A Conversation for Founders and Their Advisors
description:
Only 1.37% of businesses that sell a majority stake are female-founded. The women who do exit are navigating one of the most consequential financial and personal transitions of their lives — often without the advisory infrastructure to match.

This briefing brings together exited female founders and the wealth managers, RIAs, family offices, and exit advisory professionals who serve them, for an honest conversation about what the exit actually looks like from the inside. What worked. What was missing. What advisors were never trained to ask.

Moderated by Alisha Pennington, multi-seven figure exited founder and creator of Exette, this is not a panel about how to sell your business. It's a conversation about what happens when you do — and what the advisory world needs to understand to serve this client at the moment it matters most.

Alisha's Social Connection Links:
https://www.instagram.com/itsalishamp
https://www.linkedin.com/in/alisha-m-pennington

title: Marketplace and Community Led Growth
description:
The fastest-growing B2B companies are no longer scaling alone. They are leveraging marketplaces, partner ecosystems, and curated communities to access enterprise customers, accelerate deal cycles, and unlock non-dilutive growth.

This session is designed for founders and investors who want to understand how this actually works in practice. Through a candid fireside chat, you will hear real examples of how companies are using cloud marketplaces, partner programs, and high-trust communities to generate pipeline, close larger deals, and expand into new markets.

We will go beyond theory and break down what it takes to get started. From activating marketplace listings and co-sell motions, to tapping into the “dark funnel” of executive communities, you will leave with a clear view of where the leverage is and how to access it, and also hear from partners at White & Case on how to protect it.

The conversation will be followed by a curated networking session with founders, investors, and ecosystem leaders actively building through partnerships. This is a high-signal room for people who want to move from experimentation to execution.

If you are building or investing in a SaaS or AI company and looking to scale faster, access enterprise buyers, and build durable growth through ecosystems, this is where you start. Join us for snacks, drinks and revenue acceleration on June 5, 3-5pm and supercharge your partnerships journey.

title: Steeped in Tech: A Tea Reception Exploring AI, Taste & Human Experience
description:
Step away from the rush of Tech Week and into a room designed for better conversations.

Join Tea Drunk for Steeped in Tech, a tea reception for founders, investors, builders, and curious minds interested in the intersection of AI, culture, education, and the senses. Over exceptional Chinese top-terroir teas brewed gong fu style, we’ll gather for thoughtful networking, guided tasting, and open conversation around one central question:

"What role can technology play in helping us understand, teach, and articulate deeply human experiences — like taste, aroma, and sensory memory?"

Tea Drunk comes to this conversation with curiosity rather than conclusions. As a traditional craft business rooted in sensory education, cultural heritage, and human connection, we’re using this evening as a living case study: how might AI and emerging tools support growth, learning, and discovery without flattening what makes the experience meaningful?

Expect beautiful tea, light conversation prompts, and plenty of space to meet founders, investors, operators, AI builders, product thinkers, educators, and fellow sensory nerds.

This is a different kind of Tech Week room: slower, more human, and more memorable.

No alcohol. No hard pitch. Just exceptional tea, sharp minds, and real conversation.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

At the center of every company is a founder learning to lead themselves. This intimate, facilitated dialogue is designed for founders and CEOs who are actively working on their own self-mastery—navigating emotional regulation, ego, identity shifts, burnout, and the inner game of leadership.

title: NYC AI/ML Hackathon
description:
The Explainable ML Prediction Challenge is a two-day, technically rigorous event hosted by Intuit during NYC Tech Week 2026. Participants work on a shared, challenging dataset and are evaluated on the quality of their causal reasoning, forecast accuracy, engineering efficiency, and written methodology — not slide decks or stage presence.

The challenge sits at the intersection of causal forecasting, intervention analysis, and uncertainty quantification applied to large-scale multivariate data. You'll submit a recovered causal model, intervention forecasts, calibrated uncertainty estimates, and a short technical writeup explaining your approach. Automated scoring against held-out ground truth handles the objective evaluation; expert practitioners review your reasoning.

Teams of 2–4. Pre-formed teams welcome.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Finance Agents Demo Night with JP Morgan, LangChain, Nebius & Tavily
description:
​Join Tavily, LangChain, Nebius and J.P. Morgan for a hands-on session where you’ll learn how to build a real-time market research agent that combines structured financial data with live web intelligence.

​Using Tavily’s purpose-built search API, the agent retrieves high-signal, up-to-date insights, including news, regulatory updates, and emerging risk factors, to enrich traditional financial analysis.

​By the end of the session, you’ll have a working agent capable of deeper, more dynamic market research, going beyond static datasets to surface timely opportunities and risks.


​📅 Event Details

​Friday, June 5th

​4:00 – 6:00 PM ET

​J.P. Morgan Office (details shared after RSVP)

​
​What to expect

​⚡ A hands-on walkthrough of building a market research agent

​🧠 Learn how to combine structured financial data with real-time web intelligence

​🛠️ Practical insights into agent workflows and tool orchestration

​💬 Time to ask questions and connect with the Tavily & J.P. Morgan teams

​
Who should attend:

1) ​Engineers and builders interested in AI agents
2) ​Founders and operators working with data-driven products
​3) Investors and analysts exploring new research workflows
​4) Anyone curious about real-time, AI-powered market intelligence

Please note: This event is for 21+ and approved guests only (valid photo ID will be required upon check-in).

​
What is J.P. Morgan Innovation Economy Banking?

​J.P. Morgan Innovation Economy Banking is dedicated to supporting the next generation of builders — bringing together founders, investors, startups, and high-growth companies. With technology that powers growth, J.P. Morgan enables early-stage and venture-backed companies to make secure, real-time, and efficient financial decisions.

​
​What is Tavily?

​Tavily is a purpose-built search API for AI agents. It provides fast, structured, and relevance-optimized access to real-time web data, enabling developers to build agents that retrieve high-quality, up-to-date information with minimal noise.

What is Nebius?

Nebius is a purpose-built AI cloud platform designed for training, fine-tuning, and deploying modern AI models at scale. It provides high-performance GPU infrastructure, managed AI services, and developer-friendly tooling that enable teams to build and run AI workloads efficiently, reliably, and with full scalability.

What is LangChain?

LangChain is a purpose-built framework for developing applications powered by large language models (LLMs). It provides modular tools for orchestrating prompts, connecting models to external data sources, managing memory, and building AI agents, enabling developers to create intelligent, context-aware applications with minimal complexity.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com..

title: How AI Gets You to AI
description:
Organizations are rapidly discovering that AI is not only the destination of enterprise strategy, but increasingly the mechanism used to build and refine the strategy itself. 

This boardroom-style discussion explores how organizations are using AI to help create a virtuous cycle where generated code, contextual insight, operational intelligence, AI and cost governance, and reusable capabilities continuously reinforce one another. The conversation will focus on how enterprises can leverage AI to accelerate discovery, prototype solutions, identify patterns, improve data foundations, and uncover the operational intelligence needed to guide broader AI adoption and investment decisions. Leaders will discuss practical approaches for aligning cloud, data, analytics, governance, and AI into one connected strategy that evolves alongside the business rather than becoming another disconnected technology initiative. 

Designed as an interactive executive dialogue, the session emphasizes shared perspectives, real-world experiences, and the organizational shifts required to turn AI from a collection of tools into a continuously compounding enterprise cap

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: AI Happy Hour
description:
Join founders, engineers, investors, researchers, and AI-curious builders for an evening of drinks, conversations, and new connections during #NYTechWeek.

Whether you're building frontier models, launching AI products, exploring agent workflows, or just excited about where the space is heading, this is a relaxed place to meet people shaping the future of AI in New York and beyond.

Expect:
-Great conversations with AI builders & operators
-Founders, VCs, researchers, and creatives under one roof
-Drinks, music, and low-pressure networking

Come solo or bring your team — the goal is simple: good people, good energy, and meaningful connections.

Space is limited, so RSVP early.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

About Us:
​You.com is the leading AI search infrastructure — built for developers.
​Our APIs let you build intelligent agents and applications that use live, factual data from the web, news, and research, in real time.
​With just a few lines of code, you can power your LLMs with up-to-date, citation-backed results instead of static training data.

Descope is a no-code identity provider for customer apps and AI agents. Thousands of organizations use Descope to easily create and modify authentication and authorization journeys for customer apps, AI agents, and MCP servers without writing custom code.

One gives AI agents a single CLI (or MCP) spanning 350+ integrations and 62,000+ verified agentic actions, with fully managed authentication and access control. Customers either use us to build internal or personal agentic workflows or embed us into their product to ship agentic solutions to their users.

Want to see it live? Check it out at withone.ai or grab time here: https://calendly.com/jacob-withone/one-demo-1

Forge by Opsera (softwareforge.ai) is the first intent- and context-aware Software Factory that transforms raw ideas into enterprise-ready code at AI speed. It evolves the traditional SDLC into a true AI-SDLC- so whether you're launching AI products, building new AI-coded applications, or modernizing legacy software- teams can go from concept-to-cloud in hours, and turn decades of legacy debt into a competitive advantage.

​Founders Bay is a membership community of 150,000+ founders, executives, investors and operators coming together to grow their market through curated weekly events, expert-led virtual workshops, mentorship programs and educational resources. Proudly woman-owned and operated by Mariane Bekker, we are dedicated to empowering a diverse and inclusive startup ecosystem. 

Event Terms & Consent
​By registering for and attending this event, you agree to release and hold harmless Founders Bay Inc., its affiliates, employees, and partners from any and all liability, claims, demands, or causes of action arising out of or related to your participation in the event, to the fullest extent permitted by law.

​By registering, you consent to receive communications from Founders Bay, including newsletters, event updates, and related resources. You may unsubscribe at any time.

​By registering, you acknowledge and agree to Founders Bay’s Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, including how your information may be collected, used, and shared.

​This event may be photographed, recorded, or filmed. By attending, you grant Founders Bay the irrevocable right and permission to use your name, likeness, image, voice, and participation in any media now known or later developed, for promotional, marketing, and editorial purposes, without compensation.

​By registering, you agree to:
​-Photo and video may be captured at the event and used for marketing by You.com.
-Organizers reserve the right to refuse entry at their discretion.
​-Alcohol service is 21+ with valid ID.
​-Sessions and conversations may not be recorded without participants' consent.
-​Your registration data is shared with the co-hosts (You.com and Founders Bay) for event communications related to this event.

title: Billion Oyster Project Field Trip
description:
Join us for early evening field trip benefiting Billion Oyster Project, a nonprofit dedicated to restoring oyster reefs to New York Harbor. 

You will travel via ferry to Governors Island where we will meet Billion Oyster Project's CEO, Pete Malinowski, at the restaurant and bar Six Coasts. 

The best oysters are fresh oysters. We guarantee these will be the best oysters of your life. At sunset. On an island most of you haven't been to before. Learning about local ecology and an optimistic future for New Yorkers. What an adventure. 

Tickets start at $85. All proceeds will be donated to the Billion Oyster Project. Sponsored by Ambrook and Dandy.

There's a lot of tech in oysters. Read more about the intertwining roles of oysters, humans, and cities: https://ambrook.com/offrange/environment/the-new-shell-collectors

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Who Benefits from AGI Abundance
description:
AI is creating and concentrating wealth at an unprecedented pace. This carries enormous societal ramifications that could exacerbate existing inequities.
   
What could optimizing for justice and eudaimonia (human flourishing) look like? What institutions and groups are capable of and responsible for shaping that future?

There are no simple answers. Chris Russo (CEO of Savas Labs), will host a conversation spanning academia, business, nonprofit and policy perspectives. Audience questions encouraged in advance and live.

Foundational context: https://savaslabs.com/blog/just-society-after-agi/
  
This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring the tech ecosystem together. Learn more at http://www.tech-week.com.

title: The Room Is the Algorithm
host: Mega Exhibit, Inc, Mannarino Designs Inc
description:
An interactive gathering for founders, investors, designers, technologists, and operators curious about how space shapes human performance, regulation, and connection.

Tech Week is in full swing. This is your pause.
Consider a different kind of technology: the environments we move through every day.

We optimize everything in our stack: sleep, HRV, glucose, focus. But almost no one has optimized the environment itself.

Gather for an intimate evening built around a simple premise: the spaces around us may be doing more than we think.

Part conversation, part shared experience, this curated gathering explores the relationship between environment and human response, and how design may shape how we focus, restore, connect, and feel.

From light and acoustics to sound, rhythm, and materiality, the evening invites conversation around how the spaces we inhabit may be influencing us in ways we rarely stop to consider.

Designed for unexpected connections, fresh perspectives, and a few thoughtfully layered moments that unfold in the room.

For those curious about the intersection of space, technology, and human-centered environments. And what happens when the environment itself becomes part of the experience.

About the Hosts:

Anna Maria Mannarino is an award-winning interior and event designer and founder of Mannarino Designs, known for creating human-centered environments at the intersection of spatial design, wellbeing, and human experience. Her work spans residential, hospitality, and experiential spaces, with a practice grounded in how environments can support restoration, resilience, connection, and the emotional experience of space.

Christina Kolokotroni is the Principal of Mega Exhibit, an experiential agency and production studio creating environments that shape attention, behavior, and emotion. She helps bring ideas into physical environments from concept to execution. Recent work includes activations for CES, NBA All-Star Weekend, and CJ Cup Byron Nelson, exploring how space can shape human response.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: AI x Consulting
Bare Metal Happy Hour: Autonomous Systems & Agent Fleet Orchestration
description:
Are you an engineer or builder working on autonomous systems or agent fleet orchestration?

We're bringing together practitioners who are moving beyond individual AI tools and into autonomous, continuously running systems — whether you're maxing out managed API plans or running open source models on your own hardware.

Join us if you're building toward AI as a scalable workforce: agent loops that run for hours, parallelized workflows, and the infrastructure decisions that make or break production systems.

Expect to talk about things like:
↳ Scaling agent fleets on managed APIs vs. self-hosted inference
↳ Open source models and where local compute actually makes sense
↳ State persistence, monitoring, and reliability for long-running agents
↳ The real tradeoffs between cost, latency, and control at scale

Whether you're orchestrating agents through Claude or running Llama on bare metal, come ready to talk implementation details with people who are building the same way you are.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: PHL vs NYC Founder Draft
description:
Event Description

>_local host NYC and Seera Studios are bringing Philly and NYC head-to-head for Founder Draft, a live pitch competition during NY Tech Week by a16z inside Brown Rudnick’s office with some of the best NYC skyline views.

description:
​Pioneering Minds AI - The Demo

​Signal is the real currency. The next decade of tech won’t be won by the loudest model, but by understanding workflows, and the most intuitive integration of AI and human behavior.

​As part of #NYTechWeek, we are bringing together a hand-picked cohort of builders who are building the edge of vertical agents, formulating killer usage and redefining the interface of intelligence.We’ve bypassed the "wrappers" to curate founders solving hard problems with proprietary data and novel moats.

​Who Should Attend?

​Pre-Seed - Series A Founders: Builders who have moved beyond prototyping and are ready to deploy and make a real impact.

​Institutional & Angel Investors: Ideal for VCs, family offices, and active angels looking for high-signal deal flow.

​The Builders & Operators: AI engineers, product architects, and full-stack researchers who are obsessed with balancing cutting-edge LLM capabilities with rigorous product design and behavioral psychology.

​Strategic Ecosystem Partners: Innovation leads and corporate development executives from firms looking to integrate frontier AI solutions into professional services or enterprise workflows.

​Join us, shape the future, and be part of something truly different. We will be prioritizing active investors and founders to ensure the highest density of talent in the room.

​Demo Application: https://community.pioneeringminds.ai/forms/b9be7873

​Sponsorship: https://community.pioneeringminds.ai/forms/c50df13a

About BrainStation:
BrainStation is the global leader in digital skills learning and workforce transformation. They empower businesses and professionals to succeed in the digital age by equipping them with the necessary skills to create value in fields such as AI, data, design, marketing and product.
​​
About Pioneering Minds AI:
At pioneeringminds.ai, we grow AI native pioneers who build, deploy, and govern AI responsibly and put humans at the front seat. We help YOU - our AI pioneers - to truly understand AI and create real value out of AI.

Our mission is straightforward:

​​Accelerate Innovation: Equip participants with cutting-edge AI tools to deliver rapid prototypes.

​​Foster Collaboration: Unite diverse teams—students, engineers, and civic leaders—to tackle urban challenges head-on.

title: Impact Room: Building The Future We Want
description:
A room for the conversations we can’t afford to avoid.

AI is changing how we work, create, lead, and understand our own value. Beneath the speed of innovation is a deeper question:

How do we build a future we actually want to be part of?

The Impact Room is a raw, intimate, and interactive conversation that moves beyond AI hype and into the human, cultural, and organizational challenges shaping adoption.

Together, we’ll address the elephants in the room: Who is AI really impacting? What futures are being sold to us? What bottlenecks are preventing real adoption? How do we build cultures people can trust? And how do we lead ourselves and others through this shift?

Our panel brings together experts across ethics, governance, technology, and adoption to explore how we move from hype to real capacity through experimentation, human-centered guardrails, and coalition-building.

After the discussion, join us for the Impact Room Mixer with great drinks, great food, and the chance to connect with some of the most innovative minds at NY Tech Week.

Because the future is not inevitable. It is built by the people willing to shape it.

title: Taiwan Founder Night
host: LANDED Accelerator, Taipei Economic And Cultural Office In New York
hosts: Taipei Economic And Cultural Office In New York
description:
Taiwan Founder Night brings together Taiwan-linked founders, investors, operators, and ecosystem partners for a curated networking experience built around real conversations, not pitch decks. 

Most startup events make founders perform while everyone else sits and listens. Taiwan Founder Night is designed differently: we give guests just enough context to know who is in the room, then create the space for meaningful introductions to happen naturally.

The evening will open with remarks from Taipei Economic and Cultural Office (TECO) in NY and LANDED, followed by a concise look at Taiwan’s startup ecosystem and why Taiwan is increasingly becoming a world-class startup lab: rapid prototyping, advanced manufacturing, AIoT hardware-software integration, deep tech talent, and critical supply-chain capabilities.

We will feature startups in our event catalogue. At the start of the event, each featured startup will join a short Roll Call, where they introduce what they are building and who they hope to meet. No long pitches. No panels. No demo-day. Just enough signal for meaningful connections.

The rest of the night is dedicated to networking, giving founders visibility while keeping the focus on connection. Guests will also have access to a featured startup catalogue, making it easier to discover companies, follow up after the event, and make the right introductions.

title: Founder Feud: Lessons from 100+ Founders Who've Been Through It
description:
👨🏻‍💻 vs 👨🏻‍💻
Two teams of founders go ⚡️head to head⚡️ on stage in a Family Feud-style game show but every question is about the real challenges of building a company.

We surveyed 100+ founders on the things that actually matter when you're scaling from $1M to $10M+. The questions hit on topics like runway management, hiring mistakes, fundraising red flags, and the decisions that make or break your company at the growth stage.

The answers aren't made up. They're what founders actually think (not what they post on LinkedIn). Some are funny. Some are uncomfortable. Most are things you'll wish you knew earlier.

🧠 Why come?
Whether you're watching from the crowd or competing on stage, you'll walk away with a real look at how founders at your stage are thinking about the same problems you're facing. It's founder journey disguised as a game show fast, competitive, and way more useful than another panel.

🍸 Drinks and networking before and after. Come meet the people building alongside you.

title: Speaker Panel: Are We Building Better Lives?
description:
This conversation brings together engineers and strategists working at the forefront of AI development  and a political philosopher to examine the trade-offs AI introduces across the core pillars of human flourishing: health, work, creative expression, human connection.

Speakers will offer accessible reflections to frame key issues, then we'll open it up for Q&A and group discussion. Come ready to share your own experiences and questions about AI. Your perspective matters.

Ticketing for this event is managed on Eventbrite. Please visit the link to save your spot. 

Seniha Koksal is a computer scientist and artist exploring AI as both a system and a cultural force. She teaches at Parsons and has worked across startups and companies like Microsoft, building large-scale intelligent products. Her current work centers on multi-agent systems and creative AI, investigating how autonomous systems can generate, interact, and evolve as forms of expression.

Evan Dorsky is a robotics engineer and critical technologist. He has worked on autonomous systems at Apple and taught tech ethics with Stanford University and Olin College. He believes that no technology is neutral, and that if we learn to see the values embedded in technologies, we will be better able to mitigate their negative effects, while benefitting from the positives.

Dr. Benjamin Shapiro is a scientist and engineer working at the intersection of AI, biomedicine, and product development. He earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Johns Hopkins University and has built and applied machine learning methods across biomedical research and industry, including his most recent work in drug target discovery at GSK. He has a passion for translating technology into meaningful improvements to human health and life.

Geoff Gibbins is a strategy consultant and product designer who is obsessed with Human-AI collaboration. He has developed AI strategy for large corporations such as Walmart, Mastercard, Nestle and Pfizer, and built AI-enabled products for enterprises and consumers. He writes and publishes research on critical thinking in the age of AI, and has recently built Corrix, an AI collaboration assessment tool.

Dr. Ece Tekbulut is a political philosopher and the founder of Thinking Through. She earned her Ph.D. from Columbia University. Through Thinking Through, she creates spaces for people to become better thinkers. Her work brings rigorous philosophical inquiry into everyday life, exploring themes relevant to modern society.


This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Speak Easy: Rooftop Pitch Lab
description:
Your ideas are good. The question is: do they land?

Tech Week is full of conversations that matter, the ones that open doors, shape decisions, and move things forward.

This is your practice room.

Speak Easy: Pitch Lab is a hands-on evening for founders and ambitious professionals who want to sharpen how they pitch themselves, their ideas, or their next big move.

No lectures. No awkward spotlight. Real skill-building in a setting that doesn’t feel like a workshop.

How it works: Order a drink and pick up your Pitch Lab Passport, a self-guided practice card developed by Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk, executive communication expert and founder of Speech & Mind. As you mingle, experiment with techniques like Strategic Pausing, Vocal Authority, and Grounded Presence. Complete your Passport and walk away with sharper communication, stronger presence, and practical tools you’ll use the very next day.

WHAT TO EXPECT
✦ Curated, growth-minded crowd
✦ Actionable tools you’ll use immediately
✦ A research-backed mini guide to take home
✦ Bring your pitch to get feedback from Dr. Ozturk

📸 Photos and videos may be taken during the event for promotional use.

About the Host | Dr. Sumeyra Ozturk helps executives and founders communicate with clarity, authority, and presence when the stakes are high. She holds a PhD in Psychology specializing in nonverbal communication and completed executive coaching training at Harvard. Her approach combines performance psychology and neuroscience to help leaders think clearly, communicate with precision, and stay composed under pressure.

title: Multimodal Hacks: build the interface for agents
description:
Multimodal Hacks: Build the Interface for Agents

On June 6, 2026, join Forever 22, Google DeepMind, Pinecone, LangChain, Cursor, Clerk, and Betaworks for a full-day, hands-on hackathon focused on one idea: the interface layer for AI agents is expanding far beyond the command line.

We’re bringing together engineers, designers, and builders working across agents, LLMs, voice, generative media, developer tools, and real-time experiences to prototype the next generation of agent interfaces.

Whether you’re building voice-first agents, multimodal copilots, creative tools, workflow assistants, or new ways for people to interact with AI, this is a space to build something ambitious, useful, and demo-ready in a single day.

You’ll spend the day building alongside other top builders. Look forward to sponsor activations, an AMA, hacking time, demos, food, and prizes.

We’ll have up to five submission categories — and five winning teams.

Teams of up to 3 people. To keep things fair and fresh, all projects must be started from scratch on the day of the hackathon.

We’re accepting people on a rolling basis.
 
MC: Mari Zumbro(Filament) 

Judges: Marc (Danger Testing), Tommy (ClawCon), Yiliu (Collaborator AI), and Kaya Jones(Forever22 / Betaworks)

AMA Speaker: Vince Trost (CEO, Plastic Labs)

ABOUT OUR SPONSORS:

Google DeepMind is Google’s leading AI research lab, building frontier models like Gemini and pioneering breakthroughs across science, robotics, and generative media. 

Pinecone is a managed vector database that gives AI agents fast, scalable long‑term memory for search, RAG, and recommendations. 

LangChain is an open‑source framework for building AI agents, with tooling and integrations that make it easy to orchestrate models, tools, and data.

Cursor is an AI-native code editor and coding agent that helps developers plan, write, and ship software faster with deep codebase understanding.

Clerk is a modern authentication and user management platform that provides drop‑in sign-in, orgs, and billing-aware user flows so teams can focus on product, not auth.

Run of show (Saturday, June 6)

9:00 AM – 9:30 AM: Doors open (check-in, coffee, and bagels; sponsor booths + popups)

9:30 AM – 10:30 AM: Introductions and sponsor presentations

10:30 AM: Building begins

12:00 PM: Lunch served

12:30 PM – 1:00 PM: AMA with Plastic Labs CEO Vince Trost

3:00 PM – 5:00 PM: Demos

5:00 PM – 6:00 PM: Winners announced and doors close

title: Agents Hackathon by Lightning AI & Validia
description:
​What happens when Lightning AI's frontier training infrastructure meets Validia's agent expertise?

​A hackathon where you build smarter, ship faster, and deploy agents in a secure manner.

​We're excited to host our second hackathon right here in Manhattan, where we'll challenge you and your team to build autonomous agents that work the way you work - faster than ever before.

​What You'll Build With:

​You'll have full access to two powerful resources working in tandem:

​Lightning AI Studios provides the infrastructure to build, train, and deploy your AI agents with the same tools used for frontier model development. Access to serious compute, seamless workflows, and OpenClaw - the framework for building personalized autonomous agents that understand your unique context and workflows.

Attendees will also have exclusive access to GraphN, an AI platform from Lightning AI for building and deploying agent workflows. Think of it as the layer between the model and a working product. It handles workflow orchestration, model routing, tool execution (in sandboxed micro-VMs), knowledge base search, guardrails, and the compute to run it all.

You bring the idea and the data; GraphN gives you the scaffolding to make it actually work end to end.

We'll have starter templates ready so you can go from sign-in to a running agent in about five minutes, whether you're building a healthcare assistant, a multi-step research pipeline, a code analysis tool, or something we haven't thought of yet. 

​The Challenge:

​Build a personalized autonomous agent using Lightning AI's OpenClaw studio or GraphN that adapts to how you and your team actually work - and does it safely. Your agent should solve a real problem and demonstrate what's possible when you combine cutting-edge AI infrastructure with genuine security built in from the ground up, not bolted on after the fact.

​Think: agents that learn from your unique work patterns, automate repetitive workflows specific to your domain, and orchestrate complex tasks — all while staying resilient against the threats that make real-world AI deployment risky. Can your agent handle adversarial inputs without breaking? Does it know when it's being manipulated or probed? Can it maintain context about how you operate without leaking sensitive information or being distilled by a bad actor?

​The best submissions won't just be the most capable agents. They'll be the ones that are hardest to break.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Kevin Esherick on making hidden rules visible through latent painting, Charlotte Kent on the art history of generative systems, Anatola Araba on Afro Algorithms and bias as dataset, and others.

Then, build real-time audiovisual systems in TouchDesigner with Woody Poulard, construct a collective internet portrait with spencer chang's live browser extension, and more.

NYU's Interactive Telecommunications Program (ITP) is a graduate program at the intersection of art, engineering, and design.

title: Hardware Hack
description:
📋 Event Description

Hardware Hack NYC is a one-day, in-person sprint for builders who'd rather solder than ship a vibe-coded app. Everyone and their grandma is hosting a hackathon but this is the one where you build something physical. Robots. Sensors. Connected devices. Real hardware that runs in the real world.

The Format

This is a race-to-win style competition. Root Access drops a live challenge at kickoff. The first three teams to solve it take home the prizes. Every team gets a SparkFun Inventor's Kit on arrival - bring your laptop, your wits, and your soldering instincts.

The Challenge
Root Access is dropping a deeptech challenge live at kickoff. You won't know what it is until the clock starts. What you can plan for: you'll be working with microcontrollers, sensors, and the SparkFun Inventor's Kit for MicroPython. What it could touch:
- IoT / sensors / connected devices
- Physical/digital interaction
- Anything that runs on hardware, not just in the browser

The kit: SparkFun Inventor's Kit for MicroPython - get familiar before you show up.

What to Expect
- Cool demo of a Humanoid bot by AGI Bot.
- A live race-to-win challenge from Root Access (drops at kickoff)
- DevKits provided to every team
- 4 hours of focused build time
- Team formation on-site or come with your crew
- Breakfast & lunch will be provided
- Cash + credits + perks for the winners

Judge
Petra Vincent is a technology executive and engineer with enterprise experience spanning McKinsey & Company, PwC, Intel, and Novartis. She currently serves as CTO of ConnNext, a professional networking platform, and runs Handshake & Code, a revenue operations and sales enablement firm. With a background in Computer Engineering from NJIT, Petra brings both technical depth and strategic leadership to the intersection of hardware, circuits, and software.

title: AI Agents in the Wild: A Candid Conversation on Trust and Accountability
description:
We’re deploying AI agents faster than we can verify them. 

As AI agents take on increasingly consequential roles, the question of trust has never been more urgent. What does it actually mean to trust AI agents and how do you build responsible infrastructure? 

We’re convening the best group of industry, product, and thought leaders alongside VCs, founders, and academics to explore the following topics: 

1. Defining trust and how companies/frontier labs currently evaluate trustworthiness
2. What is the gap between current governance frameworks and deployment?
3. How are high-stakes industries, like finance and healthcare, approaching AI agent deployment and what can others learn from them? 
4. What are early agent adopters learning in regulated settings? 

There is “responsible AI” as a principle. But this is a session on something more concrete: how to move from intuition-based trust to system-level guarantees - and what needs to exist, technically and institutionally, for that shift to happen.

Join us for an exciting chat with Bryant McCombs (OpenAI) and Diane Chang (Google) as well as roundtable discussions and networking to follow.

SCHEDULE
11:00 – 11:45 AM | Panel Discussion
11:45 AM – 12:45 PM | Lunch + Networking

title: UGLY TALK: What Healthtech Founders Break on the Way to Revenue
description:
💡 What’s the Ugly Truth

Healthtech GTM is its own beast. The buyer is not the user. Regulations shape every channel. Pilots drag for months before you kNow if anything is working.

Founders burn 18 months chasing a hospital system that was never going to sign. They pour budget into a payer pilot that stalls in legal. They confuse a signed LOI with revenue.

The honest version of healthtech GTM does not look like a SaaS playbook. It looks like figuring out which procurement contact actually has budget. Killing a pilot at month 4 instead of month 12. Picking one buyer and refusing to spread thin across all of them.

🎯 Why You Should Attend

- Hear what actually moved the needle across provider, payer, and employer channels
- Get a real read on how long to run a pilot before calling it
- Learn the GTM mistakes founders wish they had caught 12 months earlier
- Meet healthtech operators who will tell you what worked and what broke
- Leave with a sharper view of which buyer to chase first and why

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room

- Healthtech founders, pre-seed to Series A
- GTM and  operators inside healthtech companies
- Healthtech investors who read GTM motion as a signal
- Builders deciding between provider, payer, and employer as their first buyer

🎤  Who’s Taking the Stage
Kanika Agarwal - Founder of MindPeers

Kanika is the Founder of MindPeers ( Series A backed, global company with four offices) and a leading voice at the intersection of mental health and AI. She has built a predictive intelligence system used by 600,000+ individuals and brings both lived experience and data-backed insight into how people perform under pressure. A serial entrepreneur, she scaled her first business to a multimillion in Singapore by age of 26 and sold to America’s biggest advertising firm. She has been recently recognized for getting on LinkedIn’s Top 20 Companies, winning a four-shark deal with double investment in Shark Tank and a Forbes Impact Leader.

Her special note on – Leading While Breaking, challenges the myth of resilience—showing how today’s leaders can grow even while navigating internal chaos. Known for her grounded yet powerful storytelling, she equips organisations to build leaders who don’t just cope—but compound strength.

Thivaher (Thiv) Paramsothy - Co-Founder & CEO of Hera Fertility


Moderator:
Aman Desai - Founder of Scalpels & Spreadsheets

Aman Desai is the founder of Scalpels & Spreadsheets, a global healthtech community that brings together founders, investors, and operators through curated events and content. His work focuses on building high-impact ecosystems that accelerate innovation across healthcare and technology.

Aman began his career as a physician, later advising private equity firms and large pharmaceutical companies on M&A in North America, Europe and Asia.

He is currently also the founder and CEO of Dossier Health, and an active angel investor in emerging healthtech and biotech companies.


💬 What’s On the Table

- How to pick the right buyer when the user is not the buyer
- When to pivot channels and when to keep digging
- How long to run a pilot before killing it
- The GTM mistakes founders wish they had caught earlier
- What changes when regulations shape every channel you touch
- Where founders waste 18 months chasing the wrong customer
- What a signed LOI is actually worth, and what it is not

🤝 Who's Hosting It
Ugly Talk HQ

Scalpels & Spreadsheets

Scalpels & Spreadsheets is a health media organization that brings together the world’s healthtech and life sciences founders, investors, operators, and experts through engaging events, content, and podcasts.

Now with over 5000+ members and events being hosted in 7+ cities, Scalpels & Spreadsheets is the go to spot for partnerships and collaboration.

description:
At a time when AI, automation, and constant acceleration shape how we work and think, The Innovator’s Spark offers a rare invitation to step out of the city and reconnect with the source of creativity, clarity, and original insight.

Hosted at Sunning Hill Retreat, a secluded 28-acre estate in Old Brookville, Long Island, this immersive experience invites participants into a setting that is itself part of the experience. Surrounded by open lawns, wooded paths, historic architecture, and a quieter rhythm of life, Sunning Hill offers something increasingly rare during Tech Week: space to slow down, breathe, reflect, and reset.

Centered around A Hymn to Life, the session introduces Art Meditation — a gentle practice that blends visual awareness, mindfulness, and intuitive reflection. Rather than focusing on artistic skill, participants are invited to engage with color, form, and texture as pathways into deeper presence and inner awareness.

The afternoon will be offered in two intimate small-group sessions, each designed for approximately 10–15 participants to preserve quiet, focus, and depth. Each group will include a guided Art Meditation experience followed by time for networking and sharing.

Beyond the exhibition itself, guests are invited to enjoy the designated retreat areas under the guidance of the Sunning Hill Retreat team, take in the natural beauty, and feel the contrast between the intensity of the city and the stillness of this historic retreat environment. For everyone’s safety and to help preserve the retreat environment, guests are kindly asked not to enter restricted, wooded, or unmarked areas, or explore the property without guidance from our team.

In a world where machines increasingly replicate logic and execution, this experience reconnects participants with what remains deeply human: intuition, reflection, emotional clarity, and the ability to generate truly original ideas.

This session also offers a glimpse into Sunning Hill Retreat’s broader Art of Mindful Living programs, with future opportunities to return for deeper experiences and gatherings.

This experience is hosted by Sunning Hill Retreat and co-hosted by New York Youth Roundtable and Youth Civic Service, bringing together mindfulness, creativity, youth leadership, civic engagement, and meaningful community connection.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: The Make & Market Hackathon
description:
You have 3 hours. An engineer. A creator. A stack that can actually ship something. Go.

Make & Market is a special-edition hackathon for builders and creators who want to make something real — not just talk about it. No all-nighters. No decks. No judges in suits. Just a curated afternoon, good drinks, and the quiet thrill of putting something into the world.

Here's how it works: engineers and creators are paired. 
The engineer builds a consumer app using Lovable, ElevenLabs, and Tavily. 
The creator shapes the story and makes it land. 

By 5pm, your product is live and your marketing is posted. The room votes. One week later, we check real traction. Most downloads, clicks, views - that team wins.

Come hungry. Leave with something shipped.

→ Spots are capped at 50. Apply with your role and a link to your work. 

We'll suggest your match - you have the first 30 minutes to swap if the vibe's off.

The Make & Market Hackathon is part of a16z Tech Week, and brought to you by The FOMO Group, the builder community behind FOMO Hack and FOMO Club, and Verci, NYC's home for the creator economy. Two communities, one room, one afternoon.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Duct Tape and GPUs: How AI is Being Used to Stitch PE Rollups Together
description:
Private equity firms are acquiring companies faster than anyone can integrate them. The result is portfolios full of mismatched tech stacks, siloed data, and workflows that were never designed to talk to each other. AI is increasingly being dropped into that chaos as the fix.
This event brings together engineers, builders, and founders to have an honest conversation about what that actually looks like in practice. What works, what breaks, and what it means for the people building these systems. If you have ever inherited a mess and been asked to make it intelligent, this one is for you. Hosted by Tallaris Partners.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Meet Me in the Middle
description:
Meet Me in the Middle

Volley & Vibe × Growing the Middle

You know what's better than networking? Networking where you're actually doing something.

Meet Me in the Middle is a pickleball social for mid-career professionals who are done with stiff networking events. We're going to keep it simple — a few quick pointers on how to actually work a room, hit some balls, and then close it out with a real networking session where you can put it into practice.

Come to play. Leave with connections that actually go somewhere.

Here's how the day flows:

All skill levels welcome. Volley & Vibe has you covered on the court — just show up.

title: Beyond the Chair: Building Human-Centered Workplaces from the Ground Up
description:
The workplace of the future isn't built in the cloud — it starts where people do: the chair they sit in.
Hosted by Libernovo, pioneers in intelligent ergonomic solutions, this half-day experience brings together workplace leaders, technologists, and people-experience professionals to explore a simple but powerful idea: that smart, human-centered furniture is the foundation of a high-performing workplace.
From dynamic support to data-driven wellbeing, join us to discover how the intersection of technology, health, and comfort is reshaping the future of work — one workspace at a time.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Run One With Me: Plan Your First Tech Event in One Session
description:
Most workshops leave you with notes. This one leaves you with a shipped plan, an app that keeps you on track, and a date your future attendees can already RSVP to.

For 2 hours, 100 people log in together and plan their first tech event, live. Not in a Google Doc you'll forget about. In a custom app built for exactly this, with your own profile, your own plan, and automated follow-ups that won't let you ghost yourself.

How it works:

Before the session, you get access to your profile in the app. During the session, I walk you through each module on Zoom and you fill it in live: your city, your niche, your date, your venue shortlist, your first invite list, your sponsor ask. Peer breakouts between modules to pressure-test each piece.

After the session, the app does what most workshops can't. It emails you on day 3, day 7, day 14, day 30. Reminds you to contact venues. Hands you the templates when you need them. Tracks your progress against your first edition date. Shows you a live map of where the other 99 operators are shipping.

Why do this with me:

I run Tech Wednesdays, 157 editions in. Every Wednesday for three years. The app is built from the exact checklist and templates I've used to keep it alive that long. You get the tools, not just the talk.

What you leave with:

- A profile in the app with your plan filled in
- A first edition date on the calendar
- Venue outreach emails auto-drafted, ready to send
- Your first 20 invites named, with message templates
- A sponsor ask in your voice
- Automated follow-ups for the next 90 days so you don't drift
- A public dashboard of 99 other operators doing the same thing
- A 6-month check-in to see who actually ran one

Who it's for:

Anyone who has ever thought someone should run a tech event in my city and suspected that someone is them.

Format:

2 hours. Virtual. 100 seats. Cameras encouraged. App access (free!) for 6 months.

You don't leave with inspiration. You leave with an event that's half-built before you close the laptop.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: BUILT by Triple Eight Creative — Live Brand Build + Char Pro Public Debut
description:
You don't have a talent problem.
You have a clarity problem.

You've built your career through client work, word of mouth, and real experience. But when it comes to showing up online — explaining what you do, positioning your value, translating your work into a business that actually reflects what you know — it falls apart.

BUILT fixes that.

This is an invite-only, application-based working session where 5 featured builders use Char Pro — T8C's AI brand strategist, built specifically for beauty professionals, to build their brand system in real time. Not inspiration. Not notes. An actual output you leave with.

The session is structured around five builder types — because everyone in the room is at a different stage:

Starting Fresh — strong skills, no clear direction yet

Needs Refinement — has a brand but unclear positioning

Needs Structure — full presence online but the flow isn't converting

Ready to Scale — established and wants to expand or monetize

Multi-Skilled — does everything and needs to know what to lead with

You'll see your stage built live. Then you'll build it yourself.

In the Apply session, every attendee works through their own brand using guided prompts and Char Pro access — you leave with a real starting framework, not a to-do list.

This is also the public debut of Char Pro.

Builder Spots — $25 commitment fee

Limited to 5 featured spots. Application and 15-minute screening call required. The $25 fee is credited toward any Inner Circle Pro membership, Char Pro access, or T8C service purchased within 30 days of the event. No submission = no confirmed spot.

If more than 5 qualified applicants apply, everyone beyond the featured 5 will be offered a complimentary 1:1 consultation with Chardé — so applying is always worth it.

Observers are welcome and free — founders, investors, and operators who want to watch AI translate human expertise into real business structure in real time. 

This isn't AI replacing the human. It's AI amplifying what the human already knows — and building a brand that still centers around human connection.

Saturday June 6 · 2:00–5:00pm
The Shop Workspace · 420 Carroll Street, Brooklyn

This event is part of #NYCTechWeek — a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Dance Unbound: Dance for Every Body
host: Dance Unbound, YOUTH CIVIC SERVICE INC.
description:
Dance Unbound doesn't belong in a conference room — and we're not pretending otherwise.
While Tech Week fills rooms with panels and PowerPoints, we're clearing the floor. This is a drop-in event where the only thing you have to do is move.
Pick a dance clip from our platform — contemporary, jazz, hip-hop, ballet, or Chinese dance — learn it on the spot, film yourself, and let our AI scoring engine evaluate your technique, form, and rhythm in real time. A live leaderboard tracks the top performers, and the top five walk away with prizes. Everyone else walks away with something rarer: their first real dance moment.
Not feeling competitive? Our community dance and dance therapy sessions run alongside — dance as connection, not performance. No experience, no judgment, no dress code.
Our guest speaker is Shuqi Cong, (also known as Andy Cong) who is a professional Chinese contemporary dancer and choreographer based in New York. He bridges traditional Chinese dance, martial arts, and Western contemporary styles, and has performed with the prestigious Graham 2 and Shen Wei Dance Arts. He will talk about how the rise of AI has transformed the way people choreograph dance and the art in general, as well as the integration of the technology into the art form. 

What makes it special? Most Tech Week events ask you to sit and absorb. This one asks you to show up, take a risk, and feel something. The technology is the floor, not the stage.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
