title: Tech & Spirituality: Thriving in a Hyper-Digital World
host: One Energetics, Practice with Paola, Bhakti Marga NYC
description:
Tech & Spirituality: Thriving in a Hyper-Digital World explores how we can remain grounded, present, and spiritually connected in an age of constant notifications, algorithms, and digital noise. As technology increasingly shapes how we think, feel, and relate to one another, this event invites participants to reflect on how their inner life intersects with their online world. Through thoughtful conversation and shared insight, we’ll examine how technology can either fragment our attention, or become a tool for deeper awareness and intentional living.

This gathering brings together seekers, creatives, and professionals to explore practical ways to cultivate balance, meaning, and mindfulness in a hyper-connected world. From developing daily rituals that anchor your attention to reimagining your digital habits through a spiritual lens, attendees will leave with both inspiration and actionable tools. Whether you're rooted in a specific tradition or simply curious about living with greater presence, this event offers space to reconnect with yourself beyond the screen.

Agenda:
2.30pm Doors Open
3pm Panel Discussion
3.45pm Q&A
4.30pm Short Guided Meditation
Light Bites, Refreshments & Connection

Where:
Motto by Hilton
113 W 24th St, New York, NY
Time: Saturday, June 6th | 3-6pm
FREE RSVP

About Your Hosts: 
Benjamin Lang is born and raised in New York. He is the founder of One Energetics, an energy technology company innovating storage and deployment. The One Energetics mission is to lay the technical foundation for the next generation of electricity — where accessible, abundant energy helps elevate the human experience.
connect: www.energetics.one

Swami Revatikaanta is a Brazilian born monk. He has spent the past 20 years living with his spiritual teacher in Germany. Swami Revatikaanta is a highly knowledgeable and prominent speaker. He oversees the newly opened Bhakti Marga Center in New York City. He is the host of the Thinking Bhakti Podcast on Youtube.

description:
Vibe Space Demo Day is where the top founders from our AI-native accelerator, the ones with real traction and a clear distribution edge, step up to pitch live to investors and scouts who are ready to deploy capital, not just “take a look.”

In a fast, focused session, selected teams will share what they’ve built, what’s working in the market, and what they are raising for next, with investors in the room who know this is the moment to move.

title: Health Tech AI Investment for Physicians
description:
As healthcare AI accelerates, investors are increasingly realizing that the next generation of successful healthcare companies will not be built by capital alone - they will be built alongside the physicians who understand clinical reality, adoption, workflow, and patient trust.

InvestDoc was created by Dr. Amad Khan and Diane Yoo, to bring together doctors, healthcare AI founders, venture investors, and family offices who believe healthcare investing requires true domain expertise.

Featuring:

- Leading neurosurgeons in the digital space
- CEOs of some of the hottest health AI and tech companies
- Venture capitalists and top tier investors
- Sir Petros Kattou KStWM; Chief Neuromodulation Officer – SOZΩ Brain Center, EU HQ Cyprus 
- Dr. Amad Khan, MD; Neurosurgeon 


Join us for the official New York Tech Week launch of InvestDoc.
We will have a private discussion on the growing role of physicians as investors, strategic advisors, and influential voices shaping the future of healthcare innovation.

Discussion topics include:
• Why clinical insight is becoming a competitive advantage in healthcare AI investing
• The growing role of physician-led diligence, trust, and distribution
• How doctors are moving beyond technology adoption into ownership and strategic influence
• Emerging healthcare AI sectors investors are watching closely
• The InvestDoc vision for building a high-signal doctor and investor network around healthcare AI

Curated audience of physicians, accredited investors, and healthcare leaders.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title: Asian Founders' Night Market
South Asian Collective
description:

title: From Curb to Collar: AI Demos That Solve Real-World Problems
description:
From Curb to Collar: AI Demos That Solve Real-World Problems is a high-energy, interactive pitch and demo event showcasing how applied AI is transforming everyday life—from school pickup system to pet safety.
Designed as a hands-on alternative to traditional panels, this event brings attendees beyond slides and into live, working technology. You won’t just hear about innovation—you’ll see it, test it, and engage directly with the founders building it.
The evening features two standout demos:
🚗 CurbTech Live AI — an award-winning smart school pickup system using computer vision (YOLO + OCR) and edge computing to automate license plate recognition and streamline student dismissal. The system will reduce pickup time, improve safety, and offer scalable solutions for schools nationwide.
🐶 Smart QR Dog Tag — a simple yet powerful innovation enabling instant pet reunification. With a quick scan, anyone can access a pet’s information and contact the owner—no batteries or apps required—making it an accessible, real-world safety solution for pet owners everywhere.
Hear directly from Dr. Yan Wang (VP, TechMatte Inc.) and talented high school student Nina Zhang, creators behind these impactful technologies, as they share insights into building practical AI systems that serve real communities.
What sets this event apart is its interactive, participatory format:
•	🏁 Live Scan Challenge — test the QR dog tag in a real-time rescue simulation 
•	🚗 AI Pickup Simulation — experience live license plate detection in action 
•	🤝 Networking Bingo — a fun, gamified way to connect with founders, builders, and investors 

Whether you're a founder, investor, engineer, educator, or simply curious about real-world innovation, this event offers a rare opportunity to experience AI that works—today.
Come join us at NY Tech Week! 
June 6 6:30pm at the paramount building
1501 Broadway #1511, New York, NY 10036 
Humans, pet dogs, and curious robots are all welcome! 🤖🐶
Snacks and drinks will be provided! 

About TechMatte Inc: a fast-growing and innovative technology company invested in continuous improvement and the development of the most current, reliable, and affordable products on the market. TechMatte strives to meet evolving consumer needs through thoughtfully designed accessories, while actively developing custom designed products and supporting novel technologies to drive future innovation. Learn more at  https://www.techmatte.com/ 

About LinaX Tech: a start up builds intelligent AI-powered solutions that improve safety, efficiency, and operational flow for schools, parking systems, and smart infrastructure. We combine computer vision, automation, and real-time data to create practical technology with real-world impact.

description:
Cliqk Creator Event
Hosted by Cliqk x Ilias Events
Friday, June 6, 2026 | NYC Tech Week

About the Event

The creator economy is being rebuilt from the ground up, and Cliqk is at the center of it. Join us for the official Cliqk Creator Event during NYC Tech Week 2026, a curated evening bringing together investors, creators, agency owners, and social media professionals who are shaping the future of digital culture and brand growth.

This is the room where the people building the creator economy meet the people funding it. Whether you are a creator monetizing your audience, an agency scaling client results, an investor deploying capital into the next generation of marketing infrastructure, or a social media professional looking for an edge, this event was built for you.
Expect high-caliber networking, live performances from a violinist, saxophonist, and harpist, and an atmosphere that makes it easy to have the conversations that actually move things forward. Every guest in this room is here with intention.

Attendance is limited. The guest list is curated. Come ready to connect.

Live Entertainment

The evening features live musical performances from a violinist, saxophonist, and harpist, creating an elevated and immersive atmosphere from the moment you arrive.

About Cliqk

Cliqk unifies content, communication, and deals into one creator economy operating system, helping creators, agencies, and brands scale faster. Cliqk is the first AI-native talent agency, helping creators grow and monetize while giving brands instant access to a vetted creator roster. Creators get a personal AI manager that sends campaigns, growth tips, and brand opportunities directly to their phone. Brands set a budget and goal, and the AI allocates it across a highly vetted roster of creators and runs the campaign end to end. Cliqk

title: Women Shaping the Future of Fintech
description:
Women Shaping the Future of Fintech is a gathering by WomenX that brings together women working across finance, technology, startups, investing, and fintech.

As fintech continues to evolve, we believe women already play a critical role in shaping this space. We hope their voices can be heard, their experiences can be shared, and that they can build a community where they feel supported and connected.

At this event, we will invite women from companies such as Stripe, Goldman Sachs, PayPal, Apollo, Mastercard, and fintech startups. They will share how they work at the intersection of finance and technology, and how they see fintech evolving across payments, banking, investing, private markets, and AI-driven financial products.

After the talks, there will be a networking session where speakers and attendees can connect more freely and build meaningful relationships.

To keep the community thoughtful and relevant, attendance will be curated. We ask applicants to share materials such as a resume, portfolio, LinkedIn, personal website, or any platform that reflects their work or interests.

We hope this can be a space to meet, learn, and support one another.

title: HOW TO RAISE FUNDS TO FUND ANY BUSINESS USING DIGITAL PRODUCTS
The Future of Relocation: AI, Fintech & Logistics Converging
description:
Event Description

The global relocation and moving industry—worth over $100B annually—remains one of the most fragmented, opaque, and operationally inefficient markets today.

We’re bringing together a curated group of founders, operators, and investors to explore how AI, embedded finance, and marketplace infrastructure are transforming physical relocation into a scalable, data-driven category.

Hosted by MoveSwitcher, this private session will dive into the intersection of:

AI-powered logistics and dynamic pricing
Marketplace infrastructure for labor and fleet coordination
Embedded finance: payments, escrow, and insurance in relocation
Data as the foundation for next-generation mobility platforms

The evening will include a short, focused discussion followed by a live product demo of MoveSwitcher—an emerging platform designed to become the operating system for global relocation.

This is not a traditional networking event. Attendance is limited to ensure high-signal conversations and meaningful connections among participants building and backing the next generation of marketplaces and infrastructure companies.

title: Ask a VP of Sales: Live GTM Office Hours for Early-Stage B2B Founders (Virtual)
description:
​Building your sales and marketing systems can feel overwhelming. These office hours are an opportunity to ask questions, troubleshoot challenges, and get practical advice on how to grow your startup.

​Join Kat Rucker, Fractional VP of Sales and Marketing, for small-group office hours focused on early-stage go-to-market strategy.

​Common topics include:

​• Founder-led sales
• Building early pipeline and outreach
• Messaging and positioning
• Turning conversations into revenue
• Prioritizing sales and marketing when you have limited time and resources

​Bring your current questions or challenges, and we’ll work through them together.

​How the Sessions Work
​These are small group office hours designed to keep the discussion practical and interactive.

Host will message you with a recommended 15- minute time slot during the hour, but feel free to come and go as you please.

​Each time block functions as drop-in group office hours, so you’re welcome to join for the full session or come with a specific question.

​About Kat
​Kat Rucker is the founder of Kat Rucker Consulting Group and a Fractional VP of Sales who works with early-stage startups on go-to-market strategy, sales systems, and founder-led growth.

​She has over 10 years of experience in B2B sales across software and services and helps startups build their first scalable revenue processes.

Kat's LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kat-rucker/

title: Female Founders Bagels + Debrief
description:
Building a company is exhilarating, exhausting, and often lonely. Join fellow female founders and entrepreneurs to step away from the inbox, share what's actually going on behind the scenes, and connect with women who get it.

No pitches. No panels. No agenda. Just good bagels, real conversation, and the kind of community that reminds you you're not building alone.

Whether you're navigating a fundraise, a tough hire, a pivot, or just need a moment to exhale with people who understand the journey, you'll find a supportive space here to debrief, ask questions, and trade notes with fellow female founders.

The location will be sent out via blast 48 hours before the event.

We can't wait to see you there.

Your hosts: 
Anna is the founder of Narrativ by AK. She helps founders turn their story into revenue through personal branding, messaging strategy and coaching. Ask her about how to tell your story IRL and turn events into a pipeline for growth. 
IG: @founderconfidence 

Movement and Mindset Movement & Mindset is a women's strength training gym in the heart of Manhattan. We empower women to own the gym floor by building physical strength and mental confidence.
IG: movement.and.mindset

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

​By registering for this event you agree for your email to be shared with Narrativ by AK and Movement & Mindset 

​📸 Photography Notice

​We may take photos and video at our events to share across social media and other marketing materials. By attending, you're giving us permission to use those photos and videos in anything related to Narrativ by AK, Anna Kaplan's, or Movement & Mindset's promotion and outreach.

title: Christians*inTech Picnic
description:
🌳 Christians*inTech 2nd Anniversary PicnicNY Tech Week Edition

Come celebrate two years of Christians*inTech with a laid-back afternoon in Central Park alongside founders, engineers, designers, investors, operators, creatives, ministry leaders, and friends from across NYC tech.

Over the last two years, Christians*inTech has grown into a space where ambitious people can talk honestly about faith, vocation, startups, AI, creativity, community, and what it means to build a meaningful life in one of the most fast-paced cities in the world.

Now we’re gathering to celebrate.

Expect:🍕 Pizza, snacks, drinks, and refreshments provided🧺 Potluck-style community picnic — feel free to bring food, snacks, or desserts to share🌳 A relaxed afternoon in the park🤝 Genuine conversations and new connections🎲 Lawn games, hangouts, and community vibes📸 Anniversary photos and memories

Whether you work in startups, big tech, venture, engineering, product, design, media, nonprofits, ministry, or you’re simply curious about the intersection of faith and technology — you’re invited.

No panels. No awkward networking structure. Just good people, meaningful conversations, and a chance to meet others navigating faith and technology in New York City.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek — a week of events bringing together the broader tech ecosystem across New York City.


Community + Collaboration

This event is also supported by the Singapore Global Network, a global community connecting professionals and innovators around the world with Singapore at its heart.

PARK LOCATION

We’ll be gathering near Pinebank Arch, close to the Columbus Circle entrance to Central Park.

Map Link: https://maps.app.goo.gl/VMw9potsw6t4Lpe29

Supported by Christopher Edwards (of Reitler Kailas & Rosenblatt LLP), DoGood Inc, and Singapore Global Network (SGN), a division under Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB). Join SGN to become a member of our global network of professionals and friends with Singapore at its heart. Learn more at www.singaporeglobalnetwork.gov.sg.

title: Building a Future in the U.S.: Career and Talent Strategies of International Professionals
description:
Global talent plays a critical role in driving innovation, entrepreneurship, and economic growth in the United States. Yet for many international students, professionals, and founders, building a long-term career in the U.S. can be complex and difficult to navigate.

This panel brings together industry leaders, career strategists, immigration experts, and startup founders to discuss how international talent can successfully transition from education to employment, leadership, and entrepreneurship in the U.S. We’ll explore career pathways, workforce mobility, and the evolving opportunities available to global professionals across industries.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

title:
Networking @ 
 #NYTechWeek
description:
Looking to expand your network by participating in #TechWeek?

Recruiter-run curation of networking in small groups. 

This event is to help celebrate the kickoff of: https://trajectory-exp.lovable.app/

Virtual networking events often promise "meaningful connections" and then drop you into a random breakout where everyone's on mute. I'm a tech recruiter. I've been on both sides of this. I have a theory that hand-picking does better than random selections and this is the experiment to test out matching within a larger group.

Here's how it works:
> You RSVP with your LinkedIn URL

> If I find good matches for you, I'll match you in a curated room. If not, you'll join a small general breakout room.

> You will get ~ 20 minutes together then come back to the main room. 

Who? tech professionals — engineers, PMs, designers, founders, ops

Facilitator:
​​​Jovena Natal is the founder of Clutch Talent, a tech recruitment agency. She has supported technical staff in getting jobs at companies like Google, MongoDB, Bitly, The New York Times and Harry’s. She has been featured as an expert on PBS, Fox, Bloomberg, and Crain's. 

Clutch Talent is a boutique tech recruitment agency founded in NYC with a newly opened office in California. https://clutchtalent.com

This event is a part of #TechWeek, a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Sip & Yap + Vibes
description:
🌸 Picnic Social Sunday, June 7th. Part of NYC Tech Week :) experiential event! 

​"A social for burnt out people who want to romanticize life again, a tech and non-tech community picnic"-Ruben

​🌸 Why: I choose to be relentlessly kind and ruthlessly compassionate, that is why I fight to make this simple moment happen.

​🌸 Where: NYC° at Pepsi cola Sign Park - By the East River. Overlooking the views of Manhattan.

​🌸 When: Social picnic starts at 1PM until 8PM (I'll be there early to setup for anyone who wants to help too).

​🌸 What: This is a community event that is free to attend! I will be bringing Mimosas and tea to serve, along with a picnic setup including low picnic tables and blankets (Donations accepted to help cover costs of tea and mimosas).

​🌸 Who: For you silly! The burnt out toast. The tech people who are losing their jobs and need a break. People looking for support and even someone to listen to them, in this crazy world. For the person who just craves something simple, fun.

🌸 optional: feel free to bring a picnic dish too! I’ll be bringing basics like tea, mimosas, crackers, charcuterie, fruit. More of those or any shareable items are welcome! 


🌸 Views

Heading to NYC to setup a bohemian picnic outdoors, where the good views are

1) We’ll have Tea Infused Mimosa Drinks  🌸

2) Classic board games here for competition - poker cards, Jenga, & Uno 🌸

3) Views of Manhattan, blankets, picnic tables, and music 🌸


This is a Sip & Yap series. A social dedicated to having fun, sipping, yapping, and even a few board games! *For background, its a little idea I am starting across all my fav cities. Cuz life be crazy and more simple times are needed. 

Links to our partners & supporters! :D

Rubeskadoobs Linktree Calendar - https://linktr.ee/SocialsRubeskadoobs


Featured on Philly Tech Week Too :D

Philly Tech Week - https://luma.com/phillytechweek

JoinPhilly - https://www.joinphilly.com

title: Nutrinity: Data to Diet
description:
Nutrinity: Future of Nutritional Intelligence

Artificial intelligence is no longer just a supporting tool. It is becoming the decision-making layer across industries, replacing reactive systems with proactive intelligence. From finance to logistics, AI is no longer simply analyzing outcomes, it is shaping them in real time.

Prior to Nutrinity, nutrition was still in a cave without fire. 

Most platforms today act as intermediaries. They track calories, log meals, or provide static recommendations after decisions have already been made. Nutrinity is built on a different premise. AI should not sit in the middle. It should lead the process entirely. Instead of adjusting habits after the fact, Nutrinity uses real-time data, user goals, and environmental inputs to generate nutritional harmony from the outset. The result is a system that continuously optimizes for performance, health, and efficiency while removing the friction that defines current solutions. This is not an incremental improvement. It is a shift that will make existing tools irrelevant.

The event begins with a live demonstration of Nutrinity, showing how real-time inputs can be translated into fully optimized nutritional decisions. This is not a concept preview. It is a working system designed to redefine how individuals interact with food, performance, and health.

Following the demo, there will be a panel featuring Norman Ng, Founder and CEO of Fortune Metals, Gary Xing, a systems and software engineer from the same company. The discussion will focus on how technology and program is reshaping industries, how systems are being built to scale, and how intelligence-driven infrastructure is replacing traditional operating models.

After the panel, the event will transition into a networking session designed for founders, investors, engineers, and operators to connect in a more informal setting. Light refreshments and drinks will be provided.

To close the evening, Victor Pan and Ivan Li will host one on one demonstrations, giving attendees a more detailed look into the platform and how it functions in practice.

Currently partnering with Fortune Metals, led by Norman Ng, the company represents a modern approach to industrial operations built on efficiency and execution. With technical leadership from Gary Xing, their work reflects how structured systems and disciplined engineering can transform even traditional sectors into scalable, optimized businesses. Learn more here: https://www.fortunemetalrecycling.com/

Eternal Growth is a mission driven organization focused on long term impact through education, community initiatives, and resource accessibility. Its work emphasizes building systems that create sustained value rather than short term results, aligning closely with the philosophy of intelligent and scalable solutions. Learn more here: https://eternalgrowth.org/

Kyuramen is a fast growing restaurant brand known for combining traditional Japanese cuisine with a modern dining experience. With a focus on consistency, quality, and atmosphere, the brand reflects the importance of execution and experience in building something that scales while maintaining precision. Learn more here: https://www.kyuramen.com/

About Us

Nutrinity is an AI driven platform focused on transforming nutrition from a manual process into an intelligent system. By combining real-time data, user preferences, and external inputs such as food availability, the platform generates optimized meal decisions aligned with performance and lifestyle. Instead of acting as a passive tracker, it operates as an active decision engine that continuously adapts and refines outputs, reducing friction and making optimization the default. Learn more here: https://nutrinity.app

title: Hold The Phone! & Some Beers
host: SmarterPhone, Analog Kids, Park Slope Parents, The Balance Project
description:
Hold The Phone! & Some Beers is a traveling education series that partners with schools and parent communities to strengthen a shared line of defense for children’s mental health. Our programs reframe the current narrative around smartphones and social media—from inevitable
to intentional—by offering practical, sustainable alternatives grounded in research and real-world experience.

Light bites provided.  Cash bar.  This is a kids-free event.

Speakers include: 
Dr. Richard Freed, psychologist & author of "Better Than Real Life."

Anne Renga Weaver, LCSW, therapist, anxiety specialist, college social worker, and local parent of three who works at the intersection of mental health, parenting, and collective care.

Dan Fox, marketing executive at Light Phone, the Brooklyn-based alternative phone company.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

title: Happy Demo | Draper House Americas x NYC Tech Week
description: Join Draper House Americas and friends for Happy Demo — a one-of-a-kind Demo Day meets Happy Hour during NY Tech Week 2026. Early-stage founders from our global community will take the stage for rapid-fire pitches in front of a crowd of VCs, angel investors, and fellow builders. Expect bold ideas, great energy, and craft drinks — the best startup, selected by the crowd, wins a full scholarship to the Draper Founders Program. This is your chance to connect, pitch, and celebrate the future of tech in NYC.
